key_takeaways: A practical blueprint for an edge-deployed, multimodal control plane that augments clinicians in real time.
Why accelerated computing is essential for clinical-grade latency and deployment close to the device and patient.

How simulation and digital twins accelerate testing and validation for safe scaling of autonomous imaging.AI systems act as intelligent collaborators, reducing radiologist cognitive load and supporting more precise, data‐driven decisions.

speakers.name: Taha Kass-Hout
speakers.title: Global Chief Science and Technology Officer

title: Transform Content Delivery With Generative AI at the Edge
abstract:
As AI reshapes digital experiences, content delivery needs a major transformation. Traditional networks built for static caching can't meet real-time, personalized expectations. Modern applications require generative AI inference at the edge, close to users and data, to meet strict performance, privacy, and efficiency needs. Learn how edge AI redefines how content is created, delivered, and monetized. 

By combining generative, agentic, and future physical AI with NVIDIA accelerated computing and software, you can synthesize content in real time and tailor experiences to users and markets. Learn how advanced edge compute and intelligent data systems reduce data movement, maintain sovereignty, and deliver adaptive, low-latency experiences across augmented/virtual reality, internet of things, and autonomous systems. Join us to see how Gen AI at the edge unlocks new value and innovation.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, RTX GPU, DOCA, Ethernet Networking, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Understand how moving generative AI inference to the edge transforms content delivery and enables real-time, personalized digital experiences.
Learn how to leverage edge AI, intelligent data systems, and NVIDIA accelerated computing to improve responsiveness, reduce data movement, and enhance privacy.
Discover how advances in AI data processing at the edge are enabling faster insights, richer experiences, and new forms of real-time intelligence.

title: Nemotron Unpacked: Build, Fine-Tune, and Deploy NVIDIA's Open Models
abstract: Hear from two of NVIDIA's leading researchers the story of NVIDIA Nemotron-an open-source ecosystem for the full AI life cycle. NVIDIA Nemotron is a collection of multi-modal and reasoning models, datasets, and training techniques designed to leverage NVIDIA's experience in accelerated computing for each step of your AI journey.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Learn why NVIDIA, primarily known as a hardware company, invested in a world-class family of multi-modal reasoning models.
Understand the components of Nemotron and how you can use Nemotron tools with other models.
Hear about key uses Nemotron can be used to tackle with enterprise-grade accuracy: advanced reasoning, coding, visual understanding, agentic tasks, safety, and information retrieval.
Learn how NVIDIA Nemotron uses cutting-edge model architectures to speed inference performance while balancing leading accuracy to provide a model that is state of the art and practical to use.

title: Game Agent Models: Pick and Perfect the Right AI Models for Your Game
abstract:
As developer approaches to in-game AI features become more sophisticated, most off-the-shelf models simply don't cut it. Game devs often ask for our guidance on selecting and customizing the best models for their specific use, and our help with distilling the models down for optimal performance.

In this session, our experts will explain how we select and rank the best state-of-the-art language models for gaming ability. They will teach you how to use NVIDIA tooling to evaluate, train, and optimize AI those models with gaming skills.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: Find out how NVIDIA evaluates models for gaming skills, to populate our unique game-ready model leaderboard.
We'll explain how to prune, distill, and evaluate AI models, for games that need efficient AI in the cloud or on particularly device.
Learn best practices for creating small parameter models with state-of-the-art accuracy.
We will also highlight the importance of benchmarking to facilitate efficient deployment.

speakers.name: Jani Joki
speakers.title: Director, Benchmarking and GeForce Technical Marketing

title: Build Gigascale AI Factories With Next-Generation Rack-Scale Systems
abstract: The next generation of AI models is driving unprecedented compute consumption, exposing the limitations of traditional system architectures. Enterprises are now evolving data center designs to support new rack-scale architectures for modern inference, as well as large-scale training optimized for these rapidly escalating demands. Join this session to learn how NVIDIA is systemizing the new NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture to help customers rapidly deploy gigascale AI factories.
key_takeaways: Move beyond do-it-yourself platforms to pre-engineered, rack-scale systems to overcome performance bottlenecks and de-risk deployment.
Deploy a full-stack, optimized gigascale AI factory designed for the next generation of AI models and inference at scale.
Lower total cost of ownership and boost performance-per-watt with DGX systems powered by the Vera Rubin architecture.

title: Optimize Performance of Vision AI Models on the Edge
abstract: As vision AI continues to expand into real-world applications-from multi-modal systems like vision language models (VLMs) to depth-aware robotics using stereo vision-optimizing model performance on edge devices has become mission-critical. This talk dives into practical strategies for deploying high-performance vision models on constrained hardware. We'll explore techniques for customizing large-scale VLMs while maintaining multi-modal alignment, and optimizing depth estimation models for real-time stereo processing with limited compute and power budgets. You'll leave with a clear understanding of how to balance accuracy, latency, and resource usage when bringing advanced vision capabilities to the edge.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
nvidia_technology: Jetson, DeepStream, Metropolis, TAO Toolkit, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Customize and adapt large-scale VLMs for edge deployment while preserving multi-modal alignment and robust visual-language reasoning.
Optimize stereo depth estimation models and processing pipelines to achieve real-time performance under tight compute and power budgets.
Design and evaluate edge inference strategies that balance accuracy, latency, and resource usage for mission-critical vision applications.

title: Immersive Robotics Teleop 101: Standardize Large-Scale Data Collection and Processing Pipeline
abstract: Learn to build scalable data pipelines for training robot policies using Teleop Core, an open-source framework that unifies simulation and real-world teleoperation. We will demonstrate how to standardize integrations with human interface devices such as PICO headsets and controller to accelerate the creation of very large robotics datasets.
topic: AR / VR - AR / VR Development Tools
key_takeaways: Architect a horizontal data pipeline that seamlessly bridges simulation and real-world environments to train robust robotic policies at scale.
Leverage the open-source Teleop Core framework to standardize inputs from diverse human interface devices for consistent data formatting.
Integrate high-fidelity hardware, including PICO VR headsets and Manus haptic gloves, to capture precise manipulation data.
Deploy a unified reference design that enables the wider ecosystem to contribute to and benefit from massive, standardized robotics datasets.

title: From Idle to Optimized: Managing GPUs, Kubernetes, and Automated Compute at Scale (Presented by HPE)
abstract:
As AI workloads grow in size and complexity, infrastructure teams are under pressure to deliver high GPU utilization, predictable performance, and strong operational controls across a mix of Kubernetes and bare-metal environments. Limited visibility into GPU behavior can quickly lead to inefficiency, cost overruns, and governance gaps.

This session examines practical approaches to observing, provisioning, and optimizing GPU-accelerated infrastructure for AI workloads. We'll discuss strategies for end-to-end GPU monitoring, automation patterns for Kubernetes and bare-metal environments, and techniques for aligning performance optimization with policy and governance requirements. The focus is on repeatable, scalable practices that infrastructure and platform teams can apply in real-world AI environments.
key_takeaways: GPU Observability: Techniques for gaining actionable insight into GPU utilization, performance, and health across the AI stack
Kubernetes and Bare-Metal Automation: Patterns for provisioning and managing GPU resources using policy-driven workflows across heterogeneous environments

Compute Optimization and Governance: Approaches to improving resource efficiency while maintaining operational control and compliance for AI workloads, with the support of the NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE portfolio

title: Take AV2.0 to the Next Level With End-to-End Embodied AI
abstract: Discover how Wayve is evolving embodied AI foundation models from frontier research into production-ready driving intelligence that can scale across vehicle platforms, markets, and real-world conditions. Learn how AV2.0 techniques in end-to-end learning, fleet-scale training, and mapless operation enable safe, adaptable autonomy for automotive applications and robotaxi services.
key_takeaways: Understand how embodied AI foundation models learn robust driving behaviors from large, diverse fleets for scalable autonomy.
Explore techniques for adapting a single generalist model to multiple vehicle platforms and driving contexts.
Gain insights into the deployment pathway from assisted driving features to commercial robotaxi services.
Learn how the NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Hyperion 10 Architecture enables scalable AV2.0 development.

title: Solving the "Three Computer" Problem: Building the Cloud Infrastructure for Physical AI (Presented by Nebius)
abstract: Physical AI represents a fundamental shift in computing, demanding the seamless integration of three distinct workloads-high-fidelity simulation, large-scale model training, and low-latency real-world inference-which NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang calls the "Three Computer" problem. Join this panel featuring leaders from Nebius and pioneering robotics and simulation companies as they dissect what it takes to bring this architecture to life. Moving beyond theory, the discussion will focus on the concrete infrastructure, orchestration layers (including NVIDIA OSMO and Cosmos), and pipeline strategies required to close the loop between virtual worlds and physical reality.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, Blackwell
key_takeaways: The Architecture of Physical AI: How to architect a unified workflow that connects Isaac Sim, training clusters, and edge inference without friction
Overcoming Integration Challenges: Where the "breaks" typically happen between simulation and reality, and how to fix them
The Cloud Reality: How Nebius is productizing the "Three Computers" to accelerate deployment
The Future Roadmap: What effectively needs to be built next to move the industry from custom, fragile integrations to scalable, production-grade physical AI

title: Unlocking Data at Scale for AI Factories (Presented by DDN)
abstract: In next-generation AI infrastructure, the data layer determines how efficiently GPUs run, how much energy and cooling are consumed, and how fast retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines and multi-modal workloads can operate. This session reveals why storage and data architecture built from the NVIDIA AI Data Platform reference design-including NVIDIA AI Enterprise-are the key drivers for AI factory success. Learn the core design patterns and pitfalls that decide whether turnkey, large-scale AI runs at full speed or stalls.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, RTX GPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, DOCA, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, cuVS, Dynamo, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Discover why data, not compute, is the key to AI factory success.
Learn why a turnkey, integrated platform built on NVIDIA's AIDP reference design is the fastest way to AI business outcomes.
See a RAG pipeline in action with a demo featuring NVIDIA AI Enterprise.

title: Future of Enterprise AI: Infrastructure to Intelligence (Presented by Tata Consultancy Services)
abstract:
The future of enterprise AI is shaped by how intelligence is designed, deployed, and scaled across the enterprise. Next-gen computing platforms enable advanced models, including generative and agentic AI, to operate reliably in complex environments, allowing organizations to move beyond isolated automation toward AI-driven systems that reason, learn, and act in real time. 

This session examines how enterprises are designing AI-native architectures that combine high-performance compute, modern data platforms, and advanced models to support production-grade AI at scale. It explores how governance, trust, and resilience are embedded into AI systems from Day 1. With examples from industries such as banking, financial services, telecommunications, and media, the discussion highlights how accelerated AI is transforming enterprises from software-driven execution to intelligence-driven outcomes.
nvidia_technology: Riva, Omniverse, Metropolis, NVIDIA NIM, Cosmos
key_takeaways: How enterprises design AI-native architectures for intelligence at scale
The role of next-gen computing platforms in scaling production-grade AI
Why governance, trust, and resilience must be built in from Day 1
The shift from automation to intelligence-driven execution
How enterprise AI is applied consistently across industries

speakers.name: Sujatha Gopal
speakers.title: CTO of Communications, Media & Information Services

speakers.name: Amit Kapur
speakers.title: Chief AI and Services Transformation Officer

speakers.name: Babu Unnikrishnan
speakers.title: VP and Chief Technology Officer BFSI Americas

title: The Kaggle Grandmasters Playbook: Battle-Tested Modeling Techniques for Tabular Data
abstract:
Our workshop highlights proven strategies for tabular data developed by NVIDIA's Kaggle grandmasters, who have earned top honors in hundreds of international data science competitions. You'll practice rapid electronic design automation (EDA), large-scale feature engineering, model building, ensembling, and pseudo-labeling - all accelerated with GPUs for faster experimentation and better accuracy.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Basic Python knowledge and basic understanding of machine learning models and tabular data.
key_takeaways: Learn how Kaggle grandmasters consistently win tabular data Kaggle competitions.
Learn how to conduct fast experimentation to improve your model accuracy.
Learn how to explore and combine many diverse models.
Learn advanced techniques of hill climbing, stacking, and pseudo labeling.
Learn important EDA and feature engineering principles.

title: Harness Agentic AI and Reasoning Models to Accelerate Scientific Discovery
abstract: Learn how an AI co-scientist that combines powerful reasoning models with science simulations can expand the aperture for human scientists to achieve breakthroughs in domains such as fusion energy and targeted alpha therapy for cancer. This session delves into strategies for overcoming the challenge of data scarcity for training, for employing reinforcement learning leveraging physics simulations, AI surrogates, emulators, and building AI agents that can reiterate autonomously.
key_takeaways: Trade-offs with domain-adaptive pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and retrieval-augmented generation in data scarce regimes with open models
Strategy on including data from experiments or simulations for model alignment
Integrating and orchestrating AI agents running LLMs and physics simulations in an iterative workflow

title: Next-Generation Discovery: Agentic AI for Science, AI-Driven Simulation, and GPU-Accelerated Chemistry
abstract:
Accelerate your scientific discovery pipeline by connecting directly with experts spanning autonomous AI agents for science, AI for molecular and materials simulation, and high-performance traditional atomistic simulation. You will explore how to leverage the latest GPU-optimized tools - including NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit, cuEquivariance, VASP, and GROMACS - to maximize accuracy and computational throughput for your specific research challenges.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science
key_takeaways: Deploy autonomous AI agents to orchestrate complex workflows and accelerate discovery in energy, agriculture, and materials science.
Leverage the ALCHEMI Toolkit and cuEquivariance to accelerate machine learning interatomic potentials for high-fidelity molecular design.
Maximize computational performance for electronic structure calculations in VASP, Quantum ESPRESSO, and CP2K using the latest GPU optimizations.
Extend simulation timescales and improve sampling efficiency in classical molecular dynamics codes like GROMACS, NAMD, and LAMMPS.

title: LLM-Generated CUDA Kernels: Are We There Yet?
abstract: Dive deep into why current large language models struggle to generate performant CUDA kernels for accelerated systems, despite being capable of functional code. We'll explore the latest advancements in benchmarking, agentic frameworks, and industry efforts that are building the necessary infrastructure to close this performance gap and enable you to write "speed of light" AI-coded CUDA.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
nvidia_technology: CUDA, NSight Compute, NSight Systems, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: CUDA coding capabilities have lagged behind more generalist languages and frameworks-not just anecdotally, now demonstrably based on benchmarks like KernelBench and our own ComputeEval.
The gap is closing, but it's not the whole story-you write CUDA for performance, which has bad support in frontier models.
Tool calling, agentic frameworks, and execution environments are evolving fast, but integrating performance feedback is niche, secret sauce, and early.

You can vibe-code functional CUDA today, but the infrastructure isn't there for AI-generated performant CUDA, and there are as many approaches as there are accelerated computing problem domains and target architectures.
Efforts like GPUMode's KernelBot are pushing the state of the art in advanced techniques for performant AI-coded CUDA, with clear support from frontier labs.

speakers.name: Mark Gabel
speakers.title: Sr. Manager, AI Quality for Development Tools

title: AI Agents, Robotics, and Digital Twins: The Full Stack of Self-Driving Labs and Biomanufacturing
abstract: The pharmaceutical industry's quest for self-driving labs demands convergence of intelligent AI agents and precision robotics to create biology data factories and new medicine foundries. Multiply Labs provides the physical execution layer, using modular robotic clusters and digital twins to automate complex cell therapy workflows, reducing variability and setup time while capturing high-quality data. Thermo Fisher is delivering the lab of the future with industry-leading instruments, automation, and digital platforms that enable fully connected, AI-ready labs and end-to-end automated workflows. HighRes Biosolutions powers the labs of top TechBio and pharma through advanced automation, autonomous mobile robots, and agentic orchestration. This connected approach is the blueprint for truly self-driving bio and manufacturing labs.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, Isaac, NeMo, Cosmos, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: See how combining intelligent AI agents with precision robotics enables "self-driving" labs that tightly couple experimental design, execution, and data feedback.
Learn how Multiply Labs uses modular robotic clusters and digital twins to automate complex cell therapy workflows, reducing variability and setup time while improving data quality.
Understand how Thermo Fisher's instruments, automation systems, and digital platforms create fully connected, AI-ready laboratories and end-to-end automated scientific workflows.
Discover how HighRes Biosolutions' lab automation systems, autonomous mobile robots, and agentic orchestration agents power high-throughput experimentation, at scale.

speakers.name: Olga Ovchinnikova
speakers.title: Director of Product Management, Digital Labs

speakers.name: Stacie Calad-Thomson
speakers.title: Business Development Lead, Healthcare and Life Sciences, NALA

title: Reimagining the OR With Physical and Digital Robotics: Innovations, Challenges, and Opportunities
abstract: The integration of physical and digital robotics into operating rooms (ORs) is revolutionizing surgical care, driving precision, efficiency, and improved patient outcomes. Join us for an insightful panel discussion as we explore cutting-edge advancements in intelligent surgical robotics systems. We'll examine technological breakthroughs in digital robotic training through physics-based simulation, autonomous surgical assistance, and real-time edge AI deployment in the OR. We'll explore how these advancements empower surgeons with enhanced precision while reducing cognitive load. You'll hear unique perspectives on the future of the operating room and how physical and digital robotics enable unprecedented surgical excellence.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, Isaac, Omniverse, Clara Holoscan, IGX, MONAI
key_takeaways: Learn how the next generation of intelligent surgical robotic systems is reshaping precision, efficiency, and safety in the operating room.
Understand how autonomous and assistive capabilities, powered by real-time edge AI, help guide instrument motion, streamline tasks, and reduce surgeon cognitive load.
Gain perspectives on how physical and digital robotics together enable data-driven, intelligent decision-making that improves outcomes for both patients and providers.
Discover how leading institutions are translating these innovations into practical OR deployments, setting a new bar for surgical excellence.

speakers.name: David Niewolny
speakers.title: Sr. Director of Business Development, Healthcare/Medical

speakers.name: Robert Cohen
speakers.title: VP, Innovation and Technology, Orthopedic Group

title: Accelerate Cloud Platforms for the Next Era of AI
abstract: AI advancement depends on cloud infrastructure to deliver massive compute scale, high-performance, high resilience, and robust security. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is addressing these challenges through an innovative architectural approach featuring the Oracle Acceleron fabric integrated with NVIDIA BlueField technology. This session explores how OCI's accelerated networking, intelligent isolation mechanisms, and high-speed data movement capabilities collectively form a robust and secure platform for giga-scale. Speakers will share details of the Oracle Acceleron architecture and show how it enables the world's most demanding AI workloads. Gain a deeper understanding of the infrastructure patterns shaping modern AI platforms and how organizations can leverage them as they accelerate their AI journeys, securely and efficiently.
key_takeaways: Explore how accelerated, software-defined networking and strong workload isolation enable secure, predictable AI cloud performance.
Understand how high-speed data access and movement, powered by Oracle Acceleron and NVIDIA BlueField, support gigascale AI workloads.
Learn practical insights from OCI's architectural journey and how these patterns can support next-generation AI applications.

speakers.name: Pradeep Vincent
speakers.title: Executive Vice President & Chief Technical Architect, OCI

title: OpenUSD Asset Integration and Data Review With Clash Detection
abstract:
Integrate clash detection into your OpenUSD-based digital twin workflows using NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. In this lab, we'll ensure ingested assets are run through conflict analysis using the Clash Detection SDK. You'll create and execute collision detection queries, inspect and visualize results in an Omniverse viewport, and implement robust overlap detection in complex 3D scenes. We will learn the fundamentals of simulation for digital twins, engineering validation, and automated design verification workflows.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Familiarity with CAD workflows, 3D modeling concepts
Basic understanding of simulation concepts and collision detection principles
Experience navigating 3D viewports and interactive visualization environments
Intermediate scripting experience with Python
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Explore Omniverse Clash SDK, APIs and reference implementation for clash detection.
Perform interactive clearance and conflict checks and what-if scenario analysis on USD stages with engineering and factory data.
Validate layouts, visualize results, generate layout review reports and identify metadata for resolving clashes on complex stages.

title: Achieving Gigascale AI: Insights From Infrastructure Visionaries
abstract: For AI developers and infrastructure pioneers, achieving competitive advantage now demands more than raw compute-it requires unprecedented efficiency, scalability, and automation. This session brings together infrastructure visionaries advancing gigascale AI across leading organizations. They will share real-world architectures, optimization strategies, and operational best practices that enable faster training, smarter orchestration, and greater system autonomy at every layer of the AI stack.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, Infiniband Networking, Base Command Manager, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Learn how AI pioneers are tackling mission-critical use cases that demand gigascale performance.
Get infrastructure design insights for deploying an AI platform with unprecedented speed, efficiency, and scale.
Discover the operational best practices and autonomous intelligence needed to manage gigascale AI with maximum cost-efficiency using NVIDIA Mission Control.
Gain the infrastructure advantage once exclusive to hyperscalers-and apply it to your own AI innovation.

speakers.name: Chris Lesniewski
speakers.title: Associate VP of Enterprise Infrastructure Services

speakers.name: Kenji Motohashi
speakers.title: Head of the Global AI Office

title: From Prompt to Pull Request: Engineering Coding Agents into Your Stack
abstract: Large language models can now do far more than autocomplete code-they can act as real teammates. In this session, we'll walk through how to turn an LLM into an autonomous engineer that can plan work, call tools, and ship real features. We'll unpack the core loop behind modern coding agents: breaking down tickets, reading docs, editing code, running tests, and self-correcting when things fail. You'll see how to wire agents into your existing stack-IDEs, repos, CI, and issue trackers-using only standard developer workflows. We'll discuss patterns for letting agents safely debug their own mistakes, roll back bad changes, and open production-ready pull requests. Finally, we'll look ahead at "long-term context:" agents that remember whole work sessions, not just a single prompt, and what that unlocks for reliability, velocity, and real-world software teams of every size.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, DGX Platform, HGX, CUDA, TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, CUDA-X, cuDDN, NCCL, Triton, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Autonomous AI requires agents that can plan, execute, and self-correct using standard developer tools.
To solve complex tasks, an AI must be able to read documentation and debug its own code.
The future of software AI is long-term context, moving from single prompts to entire work sessions.

title: Shaping the Future of Semiconductor Manufacturing With AI
abstract: Join Samsung experts to explore how AI and digital twins are reshaping semiconductor manufacturing-from electronic design automation (EDA) and computational lithography to the design and operation of advanced fabrication plants. Learn how Samsung builds solutions with NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries, including cuLitho, to achieve up to 20x speedups in key manufacturing processes. Explore how they leverage NVIDIA Omniverse libraries to build large-scale industrial digital twins to conduct closed loop simulations and predict and optimize fab scheduling, maintenance, and yield. Discover how AI agents, built on NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models, are driving chip, process, and system-wide optimization and innovation. Learn how Samsung's AI Factory, powered by over 50,000 NVIDIA GPUs, makes all of this possible.
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, OVX, CUDA-X, cuLitho, Blackwell
key_takeaways: AI-Powered Acceleration in Manufacturing: Samsung is leveraging NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries such as cuLitho to deliver up to 20x performance gains across critical semiconductor manufacturing processes, from EDA workflows to computational lithography.

Digital Twins for Predictive Optimization: Through large-scale fab digital twins built on NVIDIA Omniverse, Samsung enables real-time, closed-loop simulations to optimize fab scheduling, maintenance, and yield across highly complex manufacturing environments.

Scaling AI Innovation with Massive GPU Infrastructure: Samsung's AI Factory, powered by over 50,000 NVIDIA GPUs, supports advanced AI agents using Cosmos world foundation models, driving system-wide optimization and end-to-end innovation in chip design and fabrication.

speakers.name: Yong Ho Song
speakers.title: Corporate EVP, Head of AI Center(DS)

title: Visual AI Agents for Real-Time Video Understanding
abstract:
The next generation of visual AI agents starts with NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization (VSS), which enables the creation of interactive analytical agents that reason through massive amounts of video, assess physical processes, and provide decision-making insights to streamline operations. The blueprint combines powerful vision language models (VLMs), advanced search capabilities with large language models, and Cosmos Reason VLM into a full suite of software for building,

We've made it easier to customize and deploy at the edge, on-premises, or in the cloud. Be the first to hear about the new features of VSS to accelerate your AI agent development.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
nvidia_technology: DeepStream, TensorRT, Metropolis, CV-CUDA, TAO Toolkit, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn how VLMs, LLM-powered search, and Cosmos Reason are integrated into a cohesive agent pipeline.
Be the first to learn about the latest capabilities to NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization.
Discover strategies for customizing, deploying, and iterating on VSS-based AI agents across edge, on-premises, and cloud environments.

speakers.name: Adam Ryason
speakers.title: Product Manager, NVIDIA AI Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS)

title: Reinforcement Learning in the Modern AI Stack
abstract:
Explore new solutions for navigating the expanding landscape of reinforcement learning (RL) - know when to use RL, which flavor fits your domain, and what tooling accelerates your path to production. Get practical guidance on stacks, environments, and implementation strategies from hands-on practitioners.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Understand when reinforcement learning adds value over supervised fine-tuning or prompt engineering, and when simpler approaches are the better choice.
Learn how to select the right RL flavor for your use case, including RLHF for preference alignment, RLVR for verifiable reasoning tasks, and other emerging techniques.
Discover which tooling stacks and simulation environments map to specific domains.
Get a practical roadmap for integrating RL into your workflows, from initial experimentation through production deployment.
Explore common pitfalls and debugging strategies that accelerate your path from RL prototype to reliable, scalable systems.

title: Deploy State-of-the-Art Gen AI on Your RTX PRO Workstation
abstract:
This session guides you through the end-to-end deployment of generative image models on NVIDIA RTX PRO workstations by leveraging ONNX Runtime and TensorRT-RTX. We'll demonstrate how to ship a multi-stage model pipeline across a wide set of hardware without sacrificing performance. This workflow will be powered by classic graphics APIs, ONNX Runtime, and the TensorRT-RTX Execution Provider. You'll leave with the skills to convert raw models into high-performance, offline-capable applications that run seamlessly across diverse hardware architectures.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Foundational knowledge of Python and GenAI models
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DGX Platform, RTX Virtual Workstations (vWS), Blackwell, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Deploying efficient cross-hardware AI pipelines
AI deployment with minimal packaging
Bring models from research to deployment

title: Deep Dive: Streaming Immersive Robotics Teleoperations
abstract: Join NVIDIA expert Jiwen Cai for a technical deep dive on architecting scalable teleoperation pipelines that bridge simulation and reality. Learn how to leverage NVIDIA CloudXR to stream high-fidelity, low-latency visuals to wireless headsets, unlocking operator comfort and precise dexterity for industrial robotics deployments and data collection at scale.
topic: AR / VR - AR / VR Development Tools
key_takeaways: Architect a unified "Sim & Real" teleoperation stack using NVIDIA CloudXR to stream complex robot data to lightweight headsets without tethering.
Implement low-latency streaming protocols that enable precise dexterity (finger/hand manipulation) and lower-body control for humanoid robots.
Optimize operator efficiency and ergonomics by offloading rendering compute to the cloud, resolving common fatigue issues in large-scale data collection.
Introduce wrist camera reference design and post processing for human data collection.

title: Build Cost-Effective, Scalable RAG Pipelines: From Ingestion to Response Generation
abstract: We'll walk you through the full life cycle of a RAG pipeline: data ingestion, chunking and embedding, retrieval orchestration, and response generation, emphasizing robustness techniques such as hybrid retrieval, fallback strategies, caching, and constraint-aware prompting, along with patterns for monitoring quality and detecting drift over time. You'll also explore scaling concerns, including index sharding, cost-aware architecture choices, and aligning RAG design with product requirements and service-level agreements. By the end, you'll have implemented and evaluated a RAG pipeline that balances answer quality, latency, and reliability, providing a template for deploying similar systems in production.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, cuVS
key_takeaways: Understand core RAG building blocks (chunking, embeddings, indexing, retrieval, generation) and how design choices in each stage affect relevance, latency, and cost.
Learn practical robustness patterns - such as hybrid retrieval, fallback logic, and evaluation loops - to make RAG systems more reliable under noisy, incomplete, or evolving data.
Gain hands-on experience implementing and instrumenting a scalable RAG pipeline that can be monitored, tuned, and extended for real-world production use cases.

title: A New Standard for Unified Power and Infrastructure Design With OpenUSD (Presented by Schneider Electric)
abstract: The convergence of AI and advanced simulation technologies is transforming how industries design, build, and operate both products and facilities. This session explores how NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD-based digital twins are enabling Schneider Electric to drive a new era of AI-driven simulations and smart buildings across all sectors-from manufacturing and energy to data centers and AI factories. Learn how these technologies create interoperable, scalable, and virtual environments that accelerate innovation, optimize workflows, and unlock unprecedented collaboration. Discover how AI factories and intelligent infrastructure can leverage real-time simulation to reduce costs, improve efficiency, and drive continuous improvement. Join us to understand why open standards and immersive simulation ecosystems are the foundation for the future of AI.
key_takeaways: The integration of AI with advanced simulation platforms like NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD is revolutionizing design and operations. These technologies enable real-time, photorealistic simulations that help industries innovate faster and reduce time-to-market.

Open standards such as OpenUSD create interoperable ecosystems where diverse tools and workflows connect seamlessly. This fosters cross-team collaboration, scalability, and flexibility-critical for building digital twins and smart infrastructure.

AI-driven simulation environments act as "AI factories," optimizing processes, reducing costs, and improving efficiency. Real-time insights from these virtual models empower organizations to continuously refine operations and adapt to changing demands.

title: 800 VDC and Modular Data Center Solutions: Powering Next-Generation AI Infrastructure (Presented by Delta Electronics)
abstract: This session explores the convergence of 800 Volts Direct Current (VDC) power distribution and modular data center solutions as essential enablers for high-density AI and HPC workloads. Learn how 800 VDC architectures reduce power conversion losses, improve efficiency, and support scalable operations, while modular designs accelerate deployment and provide flexible power and cooling strategies.
key_takeaways: Understand why 800 VDC is gaining adoption for AI- and HPC-driven data centers, including efficiency gains and reduced conversion losses.
Learn design, safety, and standards considerations for implementing 800 VDC in high-density environments.
Explore how modular data center designs enable faster deployment and scalable growth.
Learn best practices for power and cooling strategies that support flexible, high-density modular environments.
Strategies to support 800 VDC in legacy and mixed power data centers using 800 VDC sidecars

speakers.name: Ralf Pieper
speakers.title: R&D Director, Delta Custom Design BU

title: Accelerate Open Science: Incorporating CUDA Into the SciPy Ecosystem
abstract:
As GPUs become central to scientific Python workloads, SciPy-ecosystem projects-including NumPy, SciPy, and scikit-learn-face the challenge of adopting CUDA without sacrificing usability, portability, or community values. This panel brings together open-source maintainers and ecosystem partners to discuss how tools such as CuPy, Numba, and CUDA Python are being used to accelerate core numerical routines while preserving Pythonic APIs.

Panelists will share lessons from real-world integrations, including managing build and packaging complexity, handling optional GPU dependencies, and supporting diverse hardware environments. The discussion highlights how collaboration across open-source communities, hardware vendors, and scientific users enables sustainable GPU acceleration aligned with the long-term goals of the SciPy ecosystem.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
key_takeaways: Practical Pathways to GPU Acceleration: Learn how major SciPy ecosystem projects are adopting CUDA to accelerate core numerical routines while maintaining Pythonic APIs and cross-platform support.

Collaboration Across Communities: Understand how open-source contributors, hardware vendors, and scientific users are working together to align GPU integration with community values and long-term sustainability.

Lessons from Real-World Integrations: Hear concrete insights from maintainers who have navigated build system complexity, dependency management, and user adoption when introducing CUDA into established codebases.

title: The Blueprint for a Modern Resource-Responsible AI Factory
abstract:
Hudson River Trading (HRT) is at the forefront of automated trading, processing vast amounts of data at lightning speeds. But their most impressive metric might be their efficiency. In this session, HRT engineers reveal how they are leveraging NVIDIA infrastructure to build the ultimate "AI factory."

Discover how to break the trade-off between speed and sustainability. We will cover the end-to-end journey of deploying AI-driven trading models that are faster, smarter, and greener. Learn how innovative data center design, renewable power strategies, and NVIDIA-optimized workloads combine to create a trading infrastructure that is resilient, autonomous, and environmentally conscious.
key_takeaways: Learn how to build and scale AI factory environments that achieve world‐leading energy efficiency through innovative data‐center design and NVIDIA‐powered systems.
Optimize AI workloads for performance and sustainability by balancing low‐latency performance with renewable power and thermal efficiency in large‐scale trading operations.
Discover how HRT architects and deploys AI‐driven trading models on NVIDIA infrastructure to improve autonomy, speed, and resiliency while minimizing environmental footprint.

title: Scaling the AI Factory With Full-Stack, Digitally Orchestrated Infrastructure (Presented by Vertiv)
abstract:
As AI factories scale from megawatts to gigawatts, building around repeatable building blocks-including prefabricated modules for power, cooling, heat rejection, overhead infrastructure, and services-into a single, factory-assembled system designed to simplify execution and compress schedules by reducing on-site labor intensity and complexity is paramount. 

This session introduces the AI factory playbook, physics-driven design and digital-first strategy, as an innovative migration from static building information modeling to a dynamic digital twin environment, powered by Direct Universal Scene Description (USD) Export in NVIDIA Omniverse. This full-stack infrastructure evolves from disaggregated parts to a unified system that addresses the friction of traditional design and construction by fundamentally changing the physics of deployment.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, HGX, MGX, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Learn how full-stack power, cooling, structure, and controls as one digitally orchestrated system-validated in a high‐fidelity model before construction-reduces time, change orders, and schedule risk.
Demonstrate how speed equals revenue acceleration, reducing on-site work and commissioning by up to 50%, directly accelerating time-to-first-token.

Demonstrate how density equals asset efficiency, with up to 30% less space compared to traditional builds, enabling higher compute density, reducing infrastructure sprawl, and improving revenue per square foot.
How total cost of ownership (TCO) savings improves internal rate of return with up to 25% TCO savings by shifting labor off-site, reducing rework, and minimizing non-repeatable field costs and waste.

Understand how inherent future-ready infrastructure delivers financial optionality with scalable blocks with densities configurable up to 944kW per rack, expending infrastructure readiness for multiple compute generations ahead.

title: Scale Foundation Models With Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer (Presented by Google Cloud)
abstract:
As AI models evolve from billions to trillions of parameters, the infrastructure requirements have shifted from simple compute to complex, vertically integrated systems. In this session, Google Cloud product leaders will unveil the latest advancements in the AI hypercomputer architecture, designed for the NVIDIA Blackwell era.

We will dive deep into the performance benchmarks and architectural breakthroughs of our newest GPU instances, including A4X VMs (powered by NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 GPU), A4X Max (powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72) and G4 VMs (powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server edition).

Learn how Google Cloud leverages its third-generation liquid cooling, Jupiter network fabric, storage, and Titanium ML adapters to eliminate bottlenecks, reduce costs, improve GPU utilization, and provide the most scalable environment for the next wave of generative and physical AI.
key_takeaways: Large-Scale Implementation: How Google's custom liquid-cooling and power infrastructure optimizes thermal management for high-density GPU deployments
Networking and Offload: Utilizing Google's Jupiter fabric and Titanium adapters to offload host networking and maximize effective GPU bandwidth
System Integration: Strategies for leveraging the full AI hypercomputer stack to reduce latency and improve training performance at scale

title: Reinvent Omnichannel Retail With Physical AI: Connect Stores From Edge to Cloud
abstract: Retail is no longer a choice between online and in-store. Success now depends on physical and digital experiences operating as one. Instacart is reinventing retail with AI by connecting stores, shelves, customers, and digital experiences, from edge to cloud. This session explores how Instacart applies AI across the retail stack to build real-time understanding of physical stores-one of grocery's most valuable and least digitized assets. Instacart offers enterprise solutions designed to democratize AI for grocers of all sizes, including Kroger and Sprouts. Powered by NVIDIA Jetson at the edge and NVIDIA Dynamo-Triton in the cloud, signals are processed on GPUs to continuously build and update a real-time digital model of in-store reality and inventory.
key_takeaways: Learn how edge-to-cloud AI architectures enable unified omnichannel retail systems.
See how NVIDIA Jetson-powered edge inference unlocks real-time understanding of physical stores and shelves.
Understand how GPU-accelerated cloud models translate in-store signals into better ranking, ads, and personalization.
Explore how agentic AI workflows bridge online and offline retail experiences at scale.

speakers.name: Azita Martin
speakers.title: VP and General Manager, Retail and CPG

title: Accelerate Video Workloads Using NVENC/NVDEC Across Gaming, Pro-Viz, Streaming, and the Data Center
abstract:
Stop by our session to explore video workflows accelerated by NVIDIA's dedicated video encoders (NVENC) and decoders (NVDEC). GPUs with dedicated video codecs power AI video pipelines, training, and inference alongside gaming, professional video production, streaming, and data center applications. 

Product managers, engineers, solution architects, and DevTech experts will be on hand to dive into our video feature sets, troubleshoot pipelines, or answer any video-related questions you have.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
key_takeaways: Learn about video codec usage in training and inference pipelines. Load balancing between compute and encode/decode can be a critical issue.
Any computer vision, streaming, broadcast, or video transcoding-related discussions
Exciting new features planned for PyNvVideoCodec, NIMs

title: Fast-Tracking the Launch of L4 Robotaxis Across the Globe
abstract: Uber and NVIDIA are joining forces to accelerate the next generation of Level 4 robotaxi and autonomous delivery fleets, built on NVIDIA's end‐to‐end AI architecture. This fireside chat will unpack Uber's long‐term vision for deeply integrating robotaxis into its global marketplace to deliver safer, more reliable, and more sustainable urban mobility.
key_takeaways: Learn how Uber plans to bring thousands of NVIDIA‐powered Level 4 vehicles online through multiple OEM partners beginning in 2026, and how adopting a stack like NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion 10 hardware, DriveOS, and DRIVE AV L4 software lets Uber certify once and deploy across many vehicle platforms, sharply shortening the path from pilots to scaled, profitable autonomy.

See how the partnership is advancing physical AI via a joint data factory built on NVIDIA's Cosmos platform, where Uber's vast driving and robotaxi‐specific datasets are paired with powerful tools for data curation, search, simulation, and continuous improvement of the autonomy stack.

Finally, the session will highlight how this NVIDIA‐powered infrastructure, from in‐vehicle compute to cloud‐scale training and rigorous, safety‐first validation, along with the Hyperion ecosystem, is enabling Uber to rapidly develop, test, and commercially deploy Level 4 robotaxis for ride‐hailing, delivery, and logistics around the world.

speakers.name: Sarfraz Maredia
speakers.title: Global Head, Autonomous Mobility and Delivery

title: Unleashing AI at Scale: Meet the NVIDIA Networking Experts
abstract:
Join us for an interactive session and engage directly with NVIDIA's networking experts. We'll cover our full-stack networking platform, including NVLINK, Spectrum-X Ethernet, InfiniBand, and BlueField DPUs, and networking software like Cumulus. Learn how these technologies accelerate every phase of AI, from training to inference, and how tools like NVIDIA AIR can help you validate your infrastructure. This session is designed to answer your specific questions and provide the strategic insights you need to unleash your AI at scale.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Rivermax, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Cumulus, Magnum IO, Interconnect Networking, NCCL
key_takeaways: Get a practical look at NVIDIA's end-to-end networking stack - NVLINK, Spectrum-X Ethernet, InfiniBand, BlueField DPUs, and Cumulus - and how these pieces fit together to drive high-performance AI infrastructure.
The session is built around attendee questions, offering strategic guidance tailored to real deployment needs - helping you and your organization chart a confident path to AI at scale.

The experts will break down how each element of networking technology boosts training, fine-tuning, and inference efficiency, helping teams remove bottlenecks and scale their AI workloads more predictably.

speakers.name: Scot Schultz
speakers.title: Sr. Director, HPC and Technical Computing

speakers.name: Avi Alkobi
speakers.title: Sr. Director of Business Development and Technology Alliances

title: Pioneering Autonomous Science: Toward a Unified Cross-Lab Workflow
abstract: This session brings together leading labs to showcase how they are using autonomous AI and high-performance computing to build scalable, reproducible data pipelines from edge to HPC. See real-world demonstrations of agentic AI automating complex, multi-facility workflows, optimizing resources, and accelerating scientific discovery with NVIDIA's advanced frameworks. The featured labs will share insights on transparent resource management, rapid deployment, and collaborative experimentation that can be reproduced and scaled globally. Learn how these approaches unlock innovative science through seamless orchestration, portability, and reproducibility.
nvidia_technology: Omniverse, RAPIDS, Hopper, PhysicsNeMo, cuBLAS, cuDDN, NeMo, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Edge to HPC data pipelines
Agentic AI
Scalable resource management and deployment
Collaborative AI science

speakers.name: Adam Thompson
speakers.title: Principal Technical Product Manager - Sensor Processing

title: Push the Performance Frontier of Computer Vision Models With NVFP4 Reduced Precision
abstract: Reduced-precision formats like NVFP4 are shaping the future of AI by enabling faster training and inference without compromising model accuracy. In this talk, we share best practices, insights, and lessons learned from training and deploying convolutional neural network (CNN) and transformer models using advanced reduced-precision techniques while maintaining full accuracy relative to high-precision baselines. We'll explore how reduced precision, combined with optimized CUDA kernels, delivers significant speedups for both training and inference-pushing the performance frontier of computer vision workloads on modern GPUs. This methodology can be broadly applied across industrial use cases requiring highly accurate, low-latency computer vision models, including manufacturing, medical imaging, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and more.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, TensorRT, CUDA-X, NSight Compute, NSight Systems
key_takeaways: Learn the design and advantages of NVIDIA's advanced NVFP4 format
Understand techniques to maintain full model accuracy leveraging low-precision computation.
Learn how advanced reduced-precision formats accelerate training and inference for CNNs and vision transformers.
Learn the methodology for adopting highly accurate, low-latency reduced-precision models for your use cases.

title: Building the Future of Manufacturing
abstract: The manufacturing industry is entering a new era, driven by AI, robotics, and simulation. Join distinguished leaders from the semiconductor and electronics, industrial and process manufacturing, and robotics industries as they share their pioneering use cases and how these technologies enable them to innovate, reimagine production systems, and transform the design and operation of advanced manufacturing and warehouse facilities.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: Jetson, Isaac, Omniverse, Metropolis, OVX, IGX, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Cosmos, DGX Cloud
key_takeaways: Discover how manufacturing leaders are applying AI, robotics, and simulation to accelerate innovation and efficiency.
Learn how these technologies are reshaping the design, planning, and operation of next-generation manufacturing facilities.
Explore pioneering real-world use cases that demonstrate the power of intelligent automation and industrial digitalization in driving innovation and resilience.
Understand the critical role of AI infrastructure in enabling smarter and more sustainable manufacturing.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.title: VP of Omniverse and Simulation Technology

title: Beauty Reinvented with L'Oréal: AI-Enabled Formulation Optimization
abstract: A beauty product has billions of possible formulations, rendering exhaustive lab testing impossible. L'Oréal is partnering with NVIDIA to accelerate and improve accuracy of in-silico formulation property prediction.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science
key_takeaways: Learn how to build an AI-accelerated simulation workflow for beauty product formulation optimization.
Understand how NVIDIA ALCHEMI's atomistic simulation NIM and active learning workflow accelerate accurate property prediction by 100X
Explore how AI can cut formulation optimization from years to weeks, and what it means to the beauty industry.
Discover L'Oréal's partnerships and open innovation, and how these initiatives drive its product innovation.

title: Quantum and HPC at Riken: The Sum is Greater Than Its Parts
abstract: Discover how we realize the vision in quantum-HPC integration that "the sum is greater than its parts" by integrating the Fugaku supercomputer and NVIDIA GB200 clusters with superconducting and ion-trap QPUs in our JHPC-Quantum project. Our tightly coupled software stack, including the low-latency RPC middleware, orchestrates heterogeneous resources, and when combined with our novel quantum-HPC hybrid algorithms, we achieve utility-scale results in realistic problems in areas such as advanced quantum chemistry and computational fluid dyamics using hybrid algorithms-not just those that are advantageous to quantum. With rapid advances in both quantum and HPC systems, these will likely lead to true quantum advantages in real applications in the very near future.
nvidia_technology: Grace CPU, TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, MGX, cuQuantum, cuBLAS, cuFFT, NCCL, NVLink / NVSwitch, CUDA Quantum, Blackwell, CUDA-Q
key_takeaways: Achieve Hybrid Synergy: Solve intractable problems like ground state energy of [4Fe-4S] clusters by combining 77-qubit circuits with massively parallel classical diagonalization on Fugaku, yielding 100x speedups over classical-only methods.

Orchestrate Tight Coupling: Minimize latency between QPUs and classical resources-including NVIDIA GB200s-using custom asynchronous RPC middleware to enable iterative, high-priority execution that loose coupling cannot support.

Scale to Utility: Expand the computable domain beyond brute-force limits by utilizing over 150,000 HPC nodes on Fugaku for the exponentially expensive pre- and post-processing required for deep quantum circuits.

Leverage NVIDIA GB200 Infrastructure: Power AI for science and fast cuQuantum simulations within the hybrid loop using our new deployment of over 2,000 NVIDIA GB200 GPUs as a quantum computing simulator, as well as a classical GPU simulation and AI engine.

Unify Diverse Modalities: Integrate multiple quantum and classical resources in a unified fashon, including IBM superconducting and Quantinuum ion-trap architectures as well as HPC resources into a single, system-agnostic platform.

title: Racing to the Top: How We Scaled Kimi K2
abstract: Explore how we scaled Kimi K2 by pioneering the Muon optimizer to double token learning efficiency and maximize training throughput through Day 0 infrastructure co-design. Gain insights into AI-native training and linear attention architectures to help you unlock the potential of longer-running agents.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
key_takeaways: How Kimi pioneered the use of the Muon optimizer in massive-scale pre-training to replace Adam and double token learning efficiency
Strategies for achieving training stability at scale through the Day 0 co-design of model architecture and high-performance infrastructure
A paradigm shift toward AI-native training, where the model actively participates in its own data synthesis, evaluation, and evolution
Why Kimi is betting on next-generation linear attention architectures to unlock the possibility of longer-running agents

title: Transforming the Automotive Industry With Industrial AI and Digital Twins
abstract:
The automotive industry is undergoing a major shift as AI, simulation, and digital twins redefine how vehicles are designed, engineered, manufactured, and validated. As automakers move to AI-enabled, data-driven development pipelines, they're unlocking new levels of efficiency, quality, and innovation across the vehicle life cycle.

In this panel, leaders from NVIDIA, Hyundai, Lucid, and Schaeffler will explore how industrial AI, virtual development environments, large-scale digital twins, and advanced simulation are accelerating automotive innovation. They'll share real examples of AI-powered design, automated engineering, factory optimization, and robotics training converging into a unified end-to-end pipeline, along with insights on the technologies and collaboration driving industry transformation.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Understand how industrial AI and digital twins enable faster, more efficient design, engineering, and manufacturing workflows.
Learn how leading OEMs and suppliers are building AI factories that connect data, automation, and real-time simulation across the vehicle life cycle.
See how virtual development environments accelerate autonomous vehicle and robotics software validation, system integration, and fleet-scale performance testing.

speakers.name: Roberto Henkel
speakers.title: Senior Vice President Digitalization & Operations IT

speakers.name: Alpesh Patel
speakers.title: Vice President, Chief Innovation Officer
speakers.company: Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore (HMGICS)

title: The Digital Surgeon: How Generative AI and Simulation are Redefining Performance and Safety in Robotic Surgery
abstract: The next wave of surgical innovation lies in the intelligence behind robotic systems. Advances in accelerated computing-enabling performance gains on the order of thousands-are transforming surgical data into a powerful feedback loop for continuous improvement. This session will share how a massive leap in computational power-up to 10,000 times greater than before-is enabling continuous learning in the operating room. Real-time analytics, AI-generated technique insights, and digital twin simulations are reshaping how surgeons learn, collaborate, and refine their practice. Combined with remote connectivity and scalable compute, these technologies mark a shift toward smarter, more adaptive operating rooms and improved patient outcomes.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: Clara, Omniverse, RAPIDS, BioNeMo, CUDA-X, MONAI, NeMo, Triton, Blackwell, cuVS
key_takeaways: The Surgical AI Factory: Turning video into wisdom
The Digital Companion: Low-latency assistance at the edge
Closing the data loop with physical AI and simulation

title: Real-time Voice Transformation with GPU Accelerated AI
abstract: Modulate's VoiceWear enables real-time voice transformation, demanding high-fidelity audio and near-zero latency. This session details how Modulate and NVIDIA migrated critical VoiceWear workloads from CPU to GPU, significantly reducing latency within their streaming pipeline. We'll explore the process of identifying bottlenecks, the impact of precision and framework selection on performance, and how targeted CUDA and inference-stack optimizations delivered measurable gains. Attendees will gain practical strategies for accelerating sequential, real-time ML workloads for production.
key_takeaways: How GPU acceleration shifts data prep, training, and inference workflows from hours to minutes
How to identify high-impact CPU workloads with strong ROI for GPU acceleration
Practical guidance for measuring and operationalizing cost and latency improvements

title: Drive Down Costs: Enhanced Tokenomics and Smart Routing for High-Scale Enterprise AI
abstract:
The next wave of enterprise innovation hinges on successfully deploying large language models with predictable cost and performance. This session demystifies the token economy and highlights why the critical, yet often overlooked, network and security infrastructure layer, together with the accelerated Intelligent AI Proxy, has become essential for Gen AI success. Learn how this infrastructure layer enables two fundamental shifts:

*	Intelligent Traffic Management to Optimize Tokens, the Currency of AI Factories: How you can increase output token throughput by more than 50% and drive significantly higher return on investment from their AI factories. 
*	Disaggregated Inference, a Critical Architectural Shift for Token Efficiency: How you can significantly improve GPU utilization and enable a far more scalable enterprise AI factories, delivering up to 3X increase in inference performance.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DOCA, Ethernet Networking, NCCL, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Dynamo
key_takeaways: Learn how to implement a reference architecture with intelligent AI proxies to improve tokenomics, optimize inference, and accelerate enterprise AI factory performance.
Understand how intelligent prompt routing improves response quality, strengthens security and regulatory compliance, and minimizes cost and latency through optimal resource use.
See how disaggregated inference separates context understanding from response generation to maximize GPU utilization and enable efficient scaling across data centers.

title: Deep Dive on Gigawatt AI Factories
abstract:
As at-scale AI workloads for training and inference make use of gigawatt-scale multi-GPU systems, massive data transfer, and memory access capabilities of Vera Rubin NVL72 systems in AI factories, we will show how these large systems get architected and deployed in the data center, leading to a robust reference architecture embraced globally for its speed of deployment and performance.

This session will showcase internal deployments and the tools and software stack developed and leveraged on these platforms. You'll also have the opportunity to have deep dive views on how multi-node NVLink, system interconnects, and storage, as well as the latest liquid-cooled designs, are shaping the future of AI infrastructure.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, DOCA, Fleet Command, TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Cumulus, Magnum IO, MGX, Base Command Manager, cuBLAS, cuDDN, cuDF, cuFFT, cuGraph, cuML, cuOPT, Interconnect Networking, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Large-scale reference architecture for Vera Rubin NVL72 by NVIDIA engineering
Data center innovations in power, cooling, design for multi-node NVLink, system interconnects, storage, and the latest air- and liquid-cooled designs will also be covered.
Learn about performance outcomes from next-generation AI workloads and gain insights into the underlying software stack.

title: Bringing Physical AI to Smart Spaces
abstract:
Creating truly scalable AI solutions for cities is a massive challenge, one that requires many AI models and platforms (and tons of data) working in harmony to make it possible. Announced at GTC Paris, NVIDIA Blueprint for smart city AI leverages 3-computer to activate physical AI and apply to cities for a unified, real-time intelligence solution for creating a smart city. 

Learn about the latest workflow updates. Linker Vision will share how they've been able to apply the blueprint with U.S. cities, from training models with synthetic data and NVIDIA Cosmos to deploying video analytics AI agents using NVIDIA Metropolis Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) that leverages the fleet of camera sensors deployed in cities-an end-to-end approach to processing tens of thousands of camera streams for proactive automation of city operations, safety, and planning.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
nvidia_technology: DeepStream, Omniverse, Metropolis, TAO Toolkit, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Cosmos, Blueprint
key_takeaways: How to use to deploy video analytics AI agents that leverage existing city camera infrastructure at scale.
How Linker Vision trains and adapts models using synthetic data and NVIDIA Cosmos to handle complex, rare, and evolving urban scenarios.
Latest updates to Smart City AI Blueprint workflow to enable traffic management, incident response, and urban planning.

title: Scaling DuckDB Beyond Its Limits: GPU-Accelerated Analytics With Sirius
abstract: DuckDB has redefined what a lightweight online analytical processing database can do, but as an in-process engine, it faces inherent scale limitations. Sirius bridges that gap by bringing GPU-native execution to DuckDB-without changing how users write queries. In this session, we'll explore how Sirius offloads query workloads to GPUs using the open Substrait format, accelerating analytics by over 8x while enabling DuckDB to handle data sizes far beyond a single node. Learn how this new architecture combines DuckDB's simplicity with the power of GPU compute, unlocking interactive analytics on massive datasets without giving up the elegance of a single-node engine.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, CUDA-X, cuDF, NCCL, nvCOMP
key_takeaways: DuckDB on GPUs is incredibly fast
DuckDB can now scale to larger datasets
GPUs accelerate data processing

title: Agentic Edge AI: Nemotron Training & Alignment Best Practices
abstract:
Discover how NVIDIA developed the Nemotron family of edge-ready models, setting new benchmarks for accuracy and performance. This session explores standard pre-training to advanced pruning and distillation for edge-scale efficiency. We will also detail the alignment techniques used to create state-of-the-art multimodal reasoning models. Finally, learn best practices for domain adaptation and the deployment of customized Nemotron models for gaming and AI applications on GeForce RTX platforms.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: How can edge-ready models be developed?
What are key techniques for making Nemotron state-of-the-art agentic AI models game-ready?
What are key techniques for making Nemotron state-of-the-art agentic AI models more efficient for deployment on the gaming  and PC GeForce RTX platforms?
How can you evaluate the quality and efficiency boost provided by these techniques for the Nemotron edge-ready models?

speakers.name: Jani Joki
speakers.title: Director, Benchmarking and GeForce Technical Marketing

speakers.name: Negar Habibi
speakers.title: Sr. Manager of Technical Project Management

title: Telecom Special Address: The AI Grid-Intelligently Connecting AI Infrastructure
abstract: The unsatiable demand for AI computing is creating the need for many new kinds of AI infrastructure providers. As AI inference traffic scales, compute must move beyond a few centralized data centers to a vast, distributed AI grid. Telcos, with their nationwide networks and dense footprint of data centers, are uniquely positioned to transform existing infrastructure into this scaled AI grid. This talk explores how telcos can build an AI grid to power new AI services and applications, gives a first look at NVIDIA's reference architecture, highlights edge AI applications that run best on this grid, and shares announcements from partners already on this journey. It also offers a forward-looking view of how innovations in AI-RAN, autonomous networks, and 6G come together as workloads on the AI grid-creating a once-in-a-decade opportunity for telcos to redefine their role in the AI era.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, Aerial, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Interconnect Networking, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Understand the new AI grid opportunity and how it can become telcos' most important new business platform this decade.
See concrete examples of how applications providers, telcos, and partners are collaborating to realize the AI grid.
Learn how AI-RAN, autonomous networks, and 6G converge as strategic workloads on a shared AI grid.

title: Scaling Trusted AI: A Hybrid Playbook for Impact in FinServ (Presented by AHEAD)
abstract: Financial services leader Intercontinental Exchange and AHEAD share how the Aurora platform is scaling enterprise AI across cloud and on-premises environments. The session highlights how to apply a proven hybrid AI playbook combining NVIDIA accelerated platforms, AI software, standardized pipelines, proprietary data, and governance frameworks to move AI from experimentation to production. Learn how this approach is delivering measurable impact across Intercontinental Exchange while improving performance, cost control, operational efficiency, and trust.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: The Power of a Hybrid Architecture: A standardized hybrid architecture, anchored by NVIDIA accelerated platforms, is the critical enabler for seamlessly scaling AI workloads across distinct on-premises and cloud environments.

Operationalization Over Experimentation: Moving from disparate experiments to production-grade utility requires a reproducible playbook that integrates software and pipelines to drive rapid adoption in complex sectors like FIDS and mortgage technology.

Ecosystem Synergy as a Force Multiplier: Success in enterprise AI is accelerated by strategic partnerships (e.g., Intercontinental Exchange and AHEAD) that combine deep domain expertise with technical prowess to navigate the complexities of deploying proprietary data models.

Governance as a Performance Driver: Integrating robust governance frameworks with proprietary data is not just a compliance measure, but the engine that delivers cost control, operational efficiency, and the essential trust required for enterprise AI.

Measurable Business Outcomes: Effective AI strategy transcends technical metrics, focusing instead on tangible KPI improvements-specifically cost optimization and operational speed-to validate the investment in high-performance computing infrastructure.

title: Deploy Reliable Agents at Scale With Reinforcement Learning and High-Performance Inference (Presented by CoreWeave)
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is essential for developing reliable and autonomous agents that learn from experience, delivering enhanced accuracy and cost efficiency. In this deep dive session, learn how to use the technical architecture of CoreWeave's RL infrastructure and inference solutions, highlighting how to define model weights, use Weights & Biases with serverless RL and the deployment of high performance inference stacks. We will cover use cases to show how to leverage these tools on NVIDIA GPUs to build a unified stack that scales seamlessly, optimizes performance, and accelerates your time to production.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Learn how to leverage CoreWeave's high-performance inference stack to deploy RL trained agents efficiently.
Gain insights on how to build reliable agents that learn continuously with RL.
Streamline operations with serverless RL using Weights & Biases to simplify and scale model post-training.

title: Fraud Prevention in the Age of Agentic Commerce
abstract: Join a Stripe engineering lead for an inside look at how Stripe's AI‐powered fraud prevention solution, Radar, is built with NVIDIA hardware and software to detect and block fraudulent transactions at massive scale, including emerging risks and new fraud vectors from agentic commerce to free trial abuse prevention. Trained on patterns derived from more than $1.4 trillion in annual payment volume, Radar uses advanced ML techniques to surface high‐confidence signals that reduced fraud rates for its users by 17% year-over-year.
key_takeaways: How Stripe builds for new fraud risks that come with agentic commerce and designs our models to identify and detect emerging fraud patterns
How Stripe uses NVIDIA GPUs and CUDA and AWS infrastructure to train and deploy our fraud-prevention models
How Stripe incorporates new signals and data points to evolve and train our AI models

title: AI Success Stories: Insights From the Frontlines (Presented by Dell Technologies)
abstract: How will you unlock the true value of AI? That's the critical question organizations are addressing today. In this session, Honeywell and Southern California Edison will join us on stage to share the AI impact they're delivering, their challenges and outcomes, and how they turned concepts into production-ready solutions.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Infiniband Networking, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Gain real-world insights into what works-and what doesn't.
Accelerate your journey from concept to production with practical tips to avoid common pitfalls that stall or derail AI initiatives.
Streamline infrastructure delivery for secure, repeatable success and faster time-to-value.
Powered by a Joint Partnership: The solutions and success stories are built on the Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA, delivering a portfolio of products, solutions, and services tailored for AI workloads.

speakers.name: Mehul Patel
speakers.title: Vice President of Forge and Artificial Intelligence

title: OCI AI Infrastructure: Industry-Leading Scale, Performance, and Reliability (Presented by Oracle)
abstract: Zettascale10 is Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's AI supercomputing fabric that interconnects hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs across multiple data centers to form multi-gigawatt clusters delivering peak performance. Built on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Acceleron ultra-low-latency RoCE networking and NVIDIA full-stack AI infrastructure, Zettascale10 will enable unprecedented scale, industry-leading price performance, and reliable GPU-to-GPU communication for the largest AI training workloads. In this session, learn how OCI has developed and is delivering these new supercluster innovations-from networking and storage to accelerator management and beyond-building some of the world's largest AI supercomputers in the cloud to meet the requirements of the most demanding AI workloads.
key_takeaways: Get an overview of operational flexibility that helps reduce downtime and speed up feature rollouts through plane‐level maintenance and independent network operating system updates.

Learn how OCI delivers consistent performance with more uniform GPU‐to‐GPU latency by removing a tier versus traditional three-tier designs, improving predictability for large‐scale AI training and inference.
Discover how power‐efficient optics help cut network and cooling costs without sacrificing 400G/800G throughput. This allows customers to devote more of their power budget to compute.

title: Building Frontier AI Models for eCommerce with the NVIDIA AI Factory
abstract:
Large-model technologies are rapidly transforming ecommerce. We'll discuss how we successfully overcome major challenges in large-scale data processing and training efficiency to develop a 245 billion-parameter mixture-of-experts (MoE) model that achieves state-of-the-art performance across Southeast Asian languages and key ecommerce tasks-a critical milestone for AI-native ecommerce. 

See how our model powers traffic distribution, search, content generation, and customer service to enhance efficiency and user experience, while our AI Merchant Bot improves seller efficiency and drives tangible growth. We'll highlight breakthrough search capabilities of large models in ecommerce search, including query rewriting, attribute enrichment, semantic retrieval, and multi-modal search, and how they significantly improve search quality and user intent understanding.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
