key_takeaways: Learn practical strategies for building and optimizing large-scale, region-specific language models.
Discover how advanced AI models can transform ecommerce traffic distribution, enhancing the efficiency and user experience of search, recommendation, and advertisement.
Gain insights into how AI is helping ecommerce customer service, merchant workflows, and improving the efficiency of ecommerce operations.

title: Graduate Fellowship Fast Forward Talks
abstract: Join a special presentation from our 2025-26 Graduate Fellowship recipients to learn "what's next" from the world of research and academia. Sponsored projects involve a variety of technical challenges, including machine learning, humanoid robotics, computer vision, speech and audio, reliability, drug discovery, agentic AI and trustworthy AI. These minds lead the future in our industry, and we're proud to support the 2025-26 NVIDIA Graduate Fellows. We'll also announce the 2026-27 Graduate Fellows at this session. For more information on the NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship program, visit www.nvidia.com/en-us/research/graduate-fellowships
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation
key_takeaways: Learn about some leading-edge research in academia from 10 top Ph.D. students.
Become familiar with the students who are our 2025-26 NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship winners.
Have an opportunity to inquire further into the Graduate Fellows' research.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.title: Chief Scientist and SVP of Research

title: Build Industrial Digital Twins for the Era of Physical AI
abstract:
Want to learn how to get started building industrial facility digital twins with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD? Join this interactive support session with NVIDIA technical product managers and solutions architects to get direct help and surface your questions, issues, and ideas specific to your use case.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Get hands-on help starting industrial digital twins with NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD.
Ask NVIDIA product managers and solutions architects questions specific to your use case.
Learn how to use NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD to develop realistic, operations-focused digital twins for industrial facilities.

speakers.name: Florian Gantert
speakers.title: Global Business Development and Strategy for Omniverse

title: Physical AI for the Real World: A Vision From NVIDIA Robotics Research
abstract: One of the hardest problems in physical AI is transforming robots from task-specific machines to general-purpose, intelligent collaborators. NVIDIA Research is pioneering a unified AI- and simulation-driven approach to solve this challenge, working from theoretical advances all the way to high-performance deployment. Join this session to learn about research breakthroughs in sim-to-real, real-world learning, model architecture, and model training, as well as key milestones on the path to generalist embodied intelligence for real-world applications. Gain perspectives about where robotics is headed, what technical hurdles remain, and how the next wave of research innovation could unlock entirely new markets.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, Isaac, Omniverse, OVX, CUDA-X, Blackwell, Cosmos
key_takeaways: The future of robotics is a collaborative ecosystem where general-purpose humanoids and specialized traditional robots will coexist, driven by a shift from modular to end-to-end AI systems.
Scaling these intelligent robots depends on closing the "sim-to-real" gap until simulated training data becomes virtually indistinguishable from real-world data.
This advancement in physical AI will move robots from controlled settings into our daily lives, opening entirely new markets and applications.

title: Distillation Recipes and Synthetic Data Fine-Tuning for Algorithmic Trading
abstract: Explore how model distillation and compression techniques are redefining the role of large language models in quantitative finance. In this expert talk, we'll examine strategies for reducing cost, latency, and complexity while preserving accuracy for real-time alpha generation and risk prediction. You'll gain insights into the essential techniques behind efficient model design and discover how these methods drive scalable, high-performance deployment on NVIDIA's accelerated computing platform.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Transfer Learning / Fine Tuning
nvidia_technology: CUDA-X, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn the principles of model distillation, transferring knowledge from large teacher models to smaller student models, to achieve faster inference and lower resource consumption without sacrificing accuracy.
Understand how large language models are applied in quantitative finance for alpha generation, risk prediction, and automated analysis, and how to optimize for cost and latency.

Develop a deep understanding of how pruning, quantization, and knowledge distillation work together as the foundational techniques for building efficient distilled models, and learn to integrate them for optimal performance in financial workflows.

Gain a strategic perspective on the trade-offs between accuracy, latency, cost, and hardware constraints influence decisions in compressing and deploying models for production environments at the edge.

title: Multi-Modal RAG: Build Agentic AI Systems With Video, Audio, and Text
abstract:
Enterprise developers are moving beyond text-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to harness multi-modal data-video, audio, and text. This Connect with the Experts session explores best practices for designing and optimizing multi-modal agentic RAG systems. Learn to combine speech recognition, visual understanding, and LLMs to enable context-rich, dynamic enterprise AI applications. The discussion covers real-world implementation including vector indexing of multi-modal embeddings, latency optimization for large-scale deployments, and fine-tuning for domain-specific retrieval. Gain insights into integrating NVIDIA Nemotron models and cuVS accelerated vector search to create unified RAG pipelines that reason across diverse data types. Engage with NVIDIA experts and fellow developers to exchange design patterns and best practices for powering next-generation enterprise intelligence.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Learn how to design and implement a multi-modal RAG pipeline that unifies video, audio, and text for enterprise use cases, from ingestion to retrieval to generation.
Understand how to generate and store embeddings for different modalities, including best practices for vector indexing, latency, and cost controls in production.
See how to plug in tools for automatic speech recognition, vision models, and LLMs to build an end-to-end workflow that your existing applications can call via simple APIs.
Explore patterns for grounding model outputs in enterprise data, handling security and access control, and avoiding hallucinations in multi-modal scenarios.
Walk away with reference architectures, sample prompts, and practical tips you can immediately apply to your own RAG projects.

speakers.name: Maryam Najafian
speakers.title: Principal SWE, Agentic AI Tech Lead

speakers.name: Tanay Varshney
speakers.title: Developer Advocate Engineer for Deep Learning SW

title: Supercharge Tabular ML Models With GPU-Accelerated Feature Engineering
abstract:
Fast experimentation in feature engineering is essential to quickly discover the most valuable features that improve model performance. In this tutorial, we leverage NVIDIA cuDF and cuML libraries to accelerate the experimentation pipeline on GPUs with their zero-code change features, enabling faster feature engineering and quicker development of more accurate models.
 
Prerequisite(s):


Basic understanding of Python and tabular data.
key_takeaways: First, you'll learn feature engineering techniques using a publicly available product review dataset.
Then, you'll train gradient boosted trees and support vector classification models with engineered features to evaluate their impact.
Before you leave, you'll understand how engineered features can boost ML models' accuracy, and gain practical skills for real-world use cases.

title: Harness AI to Accelerate Innovation in Healthcare Robotics
abstract: The next era in surgery will be defined by the convergence of advanced technology and human-centered care. In this session, Neda Cvijetic, global head of robotics and digital R&D at Johnson & Johnson MedTech, will highlight the role of AI, simulation, and accelerated computing in Johnson & Johnson's mission to unlock the full potential of medical innovation for those who dedicate their lives to care.
key_takeaways: Simulation is the next frontier in surgical robotics.
AI-driven simulation has the potential to enhance differentiated technology development in support of delivering better patient outcomes.
Leveraging NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare, NVIDIA Omniverse, and NVIDIA Cosmos unlocks key AI-driven simulation capabilities, and ultimately physical AI.

speakers.name: Neda Cvijetic
speakers.title: Global Head, Robotics and Digital R&D

title: How Snap Saves Millions with Accelerated Apache Spark
abstract: We'll detail our journey, achievements, and insights gained from implementing the Apache Spark RAPIDS accelerator across Snap's most substantial data processing pipelines. A significant cost saving of 76% to 90% has been realized in comparison to utilizing CPU-only jobs. We are currently collaborating with Google and NVIDIA to further deploy the RAPIDS accelerator to a broader range of our production workloads.
key_takeaways: Application of RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark at petabyte scale
Case studies on certain Spark jobs that benefit from GPU acceleration
Managing and working with GPU resources at scale, from managed cloud solutions to self-managed Kubernetes clusters

title: Turbocharge Your LLM Inference: Expert Strategies for Lightning-Fast, Scalable AI Deployment
abstract:
Explore cutting-edge methods for accelerating large language model (LLM) inference with NVIDIA technologies. This interactive session dives into deploying and scaling models using TensorRT-LLM with PyTorch integration, alongside modern speculation and quantization techniques that boost efficiency. Learn from NVIDIA experts how to fine-tune inference pipelines, optimize GPU performance, and build scalable, high-throughput deployments across advanced systems like the GB200 NVL72.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Learn advanced LLM inference optimization techniques using TensorRT-LLM, quantization (FP8/NVFP4), and speculation methods like Medusa and Eagle.
Discover how to scale and orchestrate LLM serving with NVIDIA Dynamo and disaggregated architectures on platforms like GB200 NVL72.
Gain practical insights into maximizing throughput and minimizing latency with custom attention and GEMM kernels in frameworks such as vLLM and SGLang.

title: Configure and Tune Robot Assets With OpenUSD and PhysX
abstract:
Learn from NVIDIA experts about OpenUSD best practices and physics tuning of mechanisms, using the example of a robotic hand. Starting from a URDF file, see how to structure, assemble, and optimize USD. Then learn about the physics tuning process, to enable dexterous hands to behave realistically in simulation. We will focus on best practices that make complex hand models performant, controllable, and ready for downstream learning and control tasks.
 
Prerequisite(s):


Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.


Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.


Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.


Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).
key_takeaways: Explain the end-to-end process for importing, assembling, and preparing robot USD assets for simulation.
Apply best practices to optimize robot USDs (meshes, transforms, structure) for performance and stability.
Tune joint parameters and control gains using tools and techniques to achieve desired robot motion in simulation.

title: Agentic AI Using Open Source Models: Finetuning, Deployment and Chip Design Case Studies
abstract:
Open models have evolved from experimental alternatives to production-grade building blocks for agentic AI and enterprise applications. But deciding when to use open source models, and choosing and customizing them requires a clear framework. 

In this session, we'll walk through a practical decision framework for deploying open models at scale from picking the right model size for your agentic tasks to the software and practices for customizing, evaluating, and deploying them. We'll bring it to life with two case studies: how CodeRabbit runs open-source models in production, and how NVIDIA's chip design team turned early failures into a repeatable flywheel: using a decision framework to pick use cases, then fine-tuning open-source models for the patterns that stuck. Whether you're building agentic AI, internal copilots, or cost-sensitive services, this talk will give you the framework and tools to ship with confidence.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Strategic Selection: How to navigate the open model landscape and select the right model size and architecture for specific agentic tasks, versus general-purpose needs.
The Full Life Cycle: A step-by-step framework for the entire pipeline-customization (fine-tuning), rigorous evaluation, and efficient deployment at scale.
Cost and Performance Balance: Strategies for building cost-sensitive services without sacrificing production-grade quality.
Tooling Toolkit: Which software tools and best practices are essential for shipping internal copilots and agentic AI with confidence.
Real-World Flywheels: What CodeRabbit and NVIDIA's chip design team learned deploying open-source models-from picking use cases to fine-tuning the patterns that stuck.

speakers.name: Shraddha Sridhar
speakers.title: Product Lead, AI for Chip Design

title: Build the Future of AI Infrastructure With NVIDIA DOCA
abstract: AI-driven workloads are redefining data center design, challenging infrastructure developers to deliver higher performance, stronger security, and greater networking, storage, and compute efficiency. This session covers how the latest capabilities and announcements in the NVIDIA DOCA software platform power the next generation of AI clouds and factories. It introduces the latest DOCA advancements that power massive-scale AI factories and cloud infrastructure. Gain insight into the DOCA software stack, APIs, microservices, and developer tools that enable accelerated, programmable infrastructure. Learn how NVIDIA DOCA's unified capabilities deliver breakthrough networking performance while maintaining backward compatibility, and explore how to apply new capabilities and best practices to transform AI networking, storage, and security architectures.
key_takeaways: Understand the core concepts, components, and architecture of the DOCA software platform for accelerated infrastructure development.
Identify practical DOCA use cases across AI networking, storage, and security.
Gain actionable guidance to access and use DOCA microservices, reference applications, and frameworks.

title: From Cluster to Factory: Scale AI Infrastructure Operations With Software-Defined Intelligence
abstract: Be among the first to hear how software-defined intelligence is aiding enterprises to scale AI operations. Hear directly from an AI innovator as they reveal key learnings from deploying NVIDIA DGX, sharing firsthand insights into architecting and deploying high-performance AI infrastructure and orchestrating complex inference and training workflows. In this session, we'll also discuss the critical role of software for NVIDIA Rubin and how to simplify complex liquid-cooled deployments at scale, boost site reliability engineering (SRE) productivity, and enable enhanced AI-powered anomaly detection and remediation across the entire AI factory.
topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, Base Command Manager, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Learn how AI innovators optimize their infrastructure management and boost SRE productivity in their AI factory with software-defined intelligence.

Discover practical insights from deploying NVIDIA's latest architectures with NVIDIA Mission Control, including architecting high-performance AI infrastructure, orchestrating diverse inference and training workflows.

Explore how to maximize NVIDIA Rubin performance, simplify complex liquid-cooled deployments at scale, boost SRE productivity, and enable enhanced AI-powered anomaly detection and remediation across the entire AI factory.

speakers.name: Jon Klinginsmith
speakers.title: Executive Director of Cloud Engineering and High Performance Computing

speakers.name: Bijal Shah
speakers.title: Senior Manager of Product Management, Data Center Enterprise Products

title: Tabular Foundation Models for Financial Services
abstract:
Tabular data powers entire industries, but building state-of-the-art models for structured data at scale remains complex. In this session, we'll unveil a comprehensive workflow for building transformer-based tabular foundation models. Each stage of our reference architecture is GPU-accelerated: tabular synthetic data generation, feature engineering and tokenization, training large-scale tabular transformers, and deploying inference pipelines. We apply this workflow to the real-world problem of fraud detection in credit card transactions, highlighting best practices and NVIDIA's ecosystem for scalable tabular AI.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Participants should be comfortable with basic Python and familiar with core AI/ML and Foundation Model concepts (tokenization, pre-training, fine tuning).
Experience working with financial services data or workflows (e.g., banking, payments) is recommended but not required; the material is designed for technical practitioners such as data scientists, ML engineers, and developers.
nvidia_technology: RAPIDS, cuDF, cuML, NeMo, Triton, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn to leverage tabular foundation models for structured data across enterprise use cases.
Apply synthetic data generation to tabular data using NeMo Data Designer.
Train and scale transformer-based tabular models easily with NeMo Framework.
Run highly optimized inference using Dynamo-Triton.

title: Voice Agent Applications and Models Development
abstract:
Get direct answers to your implementation challenges. This open Q&A session is your chance to connect with the experts responsible for Nemotron Speech to discuss how to maximize the performance of automatic speech recognition (ASR) and text-to-speech (TTS) in your own projects. Whether you are building for a noisy industrial environment, need better multilingual support, or are trying to create intelligent voice agents, our team is here to troubleshoot and share actionable strategies.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation
key_takeaways: Solving Real-World Challenges: How to apply Nemotron speech capabilities-like noise robustness and zero-shot TTS-to fix specific pain points in your application
Strategic Customization: Best practices for fine-tuning Nemotron speech models to handle the unique jargon, accents, or requirements of your specific domain
Achieving State-of-the-Art Quality: Practical techniques you can adopt right now to boost accuracy and naturalness in your deployment

speakers.name: Negar Habibi
speakers.title: Sr. Manager of Technical Project Management

title: vLLM in 2026: Architectural Challenges and Performance Optimizations
abstract: As LLMs grow in size, context length, and architectural complexity, vLLM must evolve to meet new performance and scalability challenges. This talk presents key improvements in vLLM's core architecture, including a GPU-first design for zero CPU overheads and an architecture for cluster-scale serving deployment. It also highlights major optimizations in KV cache management and GPU kernels. Gain a detailed technical view of how vLLM is advancing to deliver next-level performance on NVIDIA GPUs at scale.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
key_takeaways: Evolution in LLMs poses new challenges in inference.
vLLM is moving to a GPU-first architecture to eliminate CPU overheads and unlock higher, more stable performance for large-scale LLM inference.
Cluster-scale serving is becoming a first-class design goal, enabling vLLM to efficiently scale across multi-node GPU deployments for production workloads.

title: Accelerating Rack-Scale Connectivity for NVIDIA Blackwell and Rubin Platforms (Presented by Astera Labs)
abstract: Learn how advanced rack-scale connectivity solutions are revolutionizing data center infrastructure for next-generation AI workloads. We'll explore the technical innovations enabling seamless multi-GPU scaling and the performance breakthroughs achieved when integrating Astera Labs' purpose-built connectivity solutions with NVIDIA's Blackwell and upcoming Rubin architectures.
key_takeaways: Discover how Astera Labs' PCIe and CXL connectivity solutions eliminate bottlenecks in multi-GPU NVIDIA Blackwell deployments.
Understand the technical architecture behind seamless GPU-to-GPU communication across rack boundaries, including real-world performance metrics from production AI training clusters.
Learn how open standards accelerate ecosystem innovation, allowing third-party connectivity solutions to unlock new performance and scalability capabilities across AI infrastructure.

title: Engineering AI for Reality: From Readiness to Runtime (Presented by IBM)
abstract:
Most AI initiatives don't fail at the model stage. They fail in production.
Latency, governance, security, and scale expose gaps that prototypes never reveal. This session explores what it truly takes to run AI reliably in real-world hybrid environments, simplify the end-to-end lifecycle, reduce operational friction, and accelerate build cycles while maintaining interoperability and performance at scale.

Hear from Liqiang Wang, BioHPC Director at UT Southwestern, on how the medical center is deploying next-generation AI infrastructure with IBM Fusion and NVIDIA's AI Data Platform to train larger models, process complex datasets, and power advanced research : from accelerated drug discovery to AI-driven clinical training and improved researcher productivity.
key_takeaways: Learn how IBM and NVIDIA work together to build solutions that help AI leaders and practitioners eliminate AI bottlenecks and reduce operational friction.
Discover how hybrid acceleration improves AI build cycles with faster iteration, consistency, and performance.
Understand how governed, interoperable production frameworks help scale AI with trust, security, and reliability.
See how simplifying the AI life cycle enables teams to focus on outcomes instead of managing infrastructure complexity.

speakers.name: Jason McGee
speakers.title: IBM Fellow, CTO IBM Cloud, GM, Platform and Common Services

title: Artist to Audience: The Developer Platform for Real-Time AI in Media
abstract: In this session, we'll show how real-time AI is becoming the new fabric of modern media-connecting artist workflows to studio pipelines to distribution at global scale. You'll learn the reference architecture behind an AI-native media stack: accelerated compute, low-latency networking, and developer-ready SDKs and APIs that let teams build, integrate, and ship AI into real production systems. We'll walk through practical examples across creation, live production, enhancement, understanding, and delivery-focusing on what it takes to move from demos to deployed workflows, with measurable gains in speed, quality, and cost.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DGX Platform, Blackwell, Cosmos, DGX Spark, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Learn how AI-powered tools and generative agents are transforming media production workflows, enabling faster, more creative, and cost-effective content creation across multiple formats.
Discover how AI-driven recommendation systems and dynamic content optimization are enhancing audience engagement and discoverability across platforms.
Gain insights into how AI enables more targeted ads, microtransactions, and tailored subscription services, driving more efficient and sustainable revenue streams.

title: High-Performance Classical Control for Scaling Quantum Computers
abstract: Large-scale, useful quantum computers will require the full machinery of fault tolerance, bringing a formidable challenge for classical control electronics. In this talk, we'll illustrate the importance of hybrid quantum-classical systems by outlining PsiQuantum's single-photon architecture for quantum computing, how it implements quantum error correction, and initial work with NVIDIA to accelerate this with GPU supercomputing.
key_takeaways: AI is critical for solving key workloads in scaling quantum computing, including decoding for quantum error correction.
Making open AI models available to researchers is a key step toward scaling all approaches to QPUs.
NVIDIA has released open AI models for key QC tasks like decoding.

title: Architect the AI Factory for the NVIDIA Rubin Era: Scaling is Key (Presented by GIGABYTE)
abstract:
As we enter the era of the NVIDIA Rubin architecture, the global demand for trillion-parameter models and agentic AI is redefining the modern data center. Infrastructure is no longer just about individual servers; it is about building the "AI factory"-a seamless, high-density environment capable of processing massive workloads with unprecedented efficiency. 

In this session, GIGABYTE (Giga Computing) will reveal how we are bridging the gap between cutting-edge silicon and a successful industrial-scale deployment with RIKEN QHPC project in running NVIDIA Blackwell products. We will explore the architectural evolution of our latest server lineup, specifically engineered to harness the power of NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, RTX GPU, HGX, Infiniband Networking, MGX, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Maximizing NVIDIA Rubin Platform's Potential: See how GIGABYTE's advanced liquid cooling, intelligent pod management, and co-engineered thermal innovations push NVIDIA Rubin to sustained peak FP4 performance, maximize efficiency, reduce the hassle of cluster management, and deliver reliable, large-scale AI training and inference.

The Blueprint of GIGA POD: Discover how our modular rack-scale solutions utilize NVIDIA NVLink, NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand, and the full stack of NVIDIA solution installations to deliver the high-density interconnects needed for next-generation generative AI, highlighted by the successful partner deployment at RIKEN.
Time to Market: Learn strategies for rapidly scaling from pilot projects to full-scale AI factories, ensuring that enterprises and cloud service providers can stay ahead in the competitive AI race.

title: Robotics Teleoperation From the Web
abstract:
Discover how to break the hardware bottleneck in industrial AI with CloudXR.js, the critical web-based extension of the NVIDIA CloudXR platform. This session will explore the plug-and-play nature of CloudXR.js, enabling effortless integration of diverse immersive devices-including Meta Quest and PICO-into high-performance streaming pipelines.
 
Learn how to operationalize this universal interface for large-scale robotics data collection and teleoperation. We will demonstrate how CloudXR.js bridges Isaac Lab simulations with wireless headsets to accelerate sim-to-real workflows. Beyond robotics, we will examine the platform's impact on enterprise extended reality (XR) streaming in the automotive and manufacturing sectors, where browser-native accessibility is redefining how teams collaborate in immersive environments.
key_takeaways: Implement a universal, web-native XR streaming architecture using CloudXR.js to support diverse headsets (Quest, PICO) instantly.
Scale robotics data collection efforts by removing the need for platform-specific native application development and device tethering.
Optimize industrial teleoperation by leveraging low-latency, GPU-accelerated streaming between Isaac Lab and wireless head-mounted displays.
Deploy frictionless immersive workflows for non-robotic enterprise use cases, such as collaborative design in the automotive industry.
Accelerate the creation of large-scale datasets for physical AI training through standardized, plug-and-play device integration.

title: Scale Inference Using Open Models: How Nebius Token Factory Delivers Control and Efficiency (Presented by Nebius)
abstract: AI is moving from experimentation to global production, and inference efficiency and quality are the defining competitive edge. This session dives deep into the engineering behind high-performance, open-model inference, covering the system decisions, runtime techniques, and architectural patterns that unlock predictable, real-world performance at scale. Learn how Nebius Token Factory and NVIDIA push beyond benchmark-driven results to deliver structural, compounding gains in latency, throughput, and cost.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, Infiniband Networking, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Real-World Performance: How we designed Nebius Token Factory to meet demanding customer service level agreements and deliver predictable performance for real, global-scale workloads beyond black-box benchmarks
Optimizing Performance: The practical trade-offs between cost, latency, throughput, and quality, and how NVIDIA and Nebius have engineered a structural advantage to continuously optimize each one
The Exceptional Economics of Open-Source Models: How to maximize the impact of your budget with open-source models in 2026
Advanced Inference Techniques: Cache-aware routing for repetitive patterns, speculative decoding for much faster token generation, and other performance enhancers

title: TACC Horizon System in the Converged World of HPC and AI
abstract: Learn about the role of the Horizon HPC-AI Supercomputer for the scientific community, and how Horizon will be a step-change technology system with all of the NVIDIA hardware and software platforms.
key_takeaways: Convergence of HPC-AI for Science
Modern Workflows
Energy Efficiency with Accelerated Platfroms

title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including AI for science, telco, quantum, agentic AI, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Clara, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Innovative Insights: Discover cutting-edge solutions addressing real-world challenges across industries.
Emerging Trends: Gain a front-row seat to the latest developments in AI and accelerated computing technologies.
Networking Opportunities: Connect with startups, industry experts, and potential collaborators in an innovation-focused environment.
Investment Potential: Identify high-impact startups with scalable solutions for potential funding or partnerships.
Inspiration and Ideas: Learn how startups are leveraging advanced tools and frameworks to drive growth and transformation.

title: Driving Factory Autonomy With Digital Twins: Hyundai's Virtual Commissioning Journey
abstract:
Hyundai Motor Group is advancing factory autonomy by leveraging NVIDIA Omniverse to build scalable, high-fidelity digital twins across its manufacturing operations.

In this session, we share Hyundai's real-world journey of implementing Omniverse-powered factory digital twins, with a focus on virtual commissioning as a critical enabler for reducing deployment risk, accelerating system integration, and improving factory startup efficiency. Gain insights into how Hyundai virtualizes diverse manufacturing workloads-ranging from robotics and automation systems to production line logic-within a shared, collaborative digital environment. We will also highlight how cross-functional collaboration between engineering, IT, and operations teams is transformed through a unified digital twin platform, enabling faster decision-making and continuous innovation.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Learn how Hyundai uses NVIDIA Omniverse to build a scalable factory digital twin that supports diverse manufacturing workloads across planning, engineering, and operations.
Understand how virtual commissioning helps reduce integration risks, shorten system commissioning timelines, and improve first-day factory readiness.
Explore how shared digital environments enable tighter collaboration between cross-functional teams, aligning engineering intent with operational execution.
Gain practical insights into how digital twins accelerate innovation cycles and support Hyundai's long-term vision for autonomous, software-defined factories.

speakers.name: Jong-ho Shin
speakers.title: Senior Manager, Manufacturing Solution Division, E-FOREST Center

title: Transform Massive Raw Data Into Diverse High-Quality Post-Training Datasets
abstract: Struggling to gather high-quality data for your vision language models? This hands-on training lab walks you through a complete synthetic data generation pipeline-from curation and augmentation to pseudo-annotation and quality control. You'll learn how to transform any data into a high-quality dataset to post-train your vision language models (VLMs) for your domain-specific use cases.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Hopper, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Learn to implement a complete synthetic data pipeline for vision tasks.
Understand effective data curation, augmentation, and pseudo-annotation strategies.
Gain hands-on experience preparing synthetic data into VLM fine-tuning workflows.
Explore the practical use of VLMs, LLMs, and diffusion models in creation of diverse datasets.
Leave equipped to generate tailored, high-quality data for your domain-specific applications.

title: Bringing Sovereign AI From Ambition to a Strategic Foundation
abstract: Join Anne Bouverot, special envoy on AI, government of France, and Calista Redmond, vice president of global AI initiatives, NVIDIA, for an engaging conversation on how nations can chart their own AI paths while staying connected to the global innovation ecosystem. Learn how governments and industry are building sovereign AI foundations, advancing policy and partnerships, and scaling innovation for broad economic and social benefit.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Understand how sovereign AI strategies align national priorities with global collaboration.
See how open, modular platforms and public-private partnerships drive trusted, scalable AI adoption.
Explore how sovereign AI initiatives fuel local talent, startups, and sustainable economic growth.

title: Design and Operate AI Factories at Hyperscale (Presented by Microsoft)
abstract: As AI systems transition from experimental clusters to always-on AI factories, infrastructure design and operational reliability become inseparable. This session shares real-world lessons from Microsoft Azure's experience designing, deploying, and operating large-scale NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GPU clusters in production, focusing on how systems behave once they scale across thousands of GPUs, multiple racks, and heterogeneous fabrics.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Learn how large-scale AI factories behave differently once deployed across thousands of GPUs in production.
Gain insights into key design trade-offs when translating NVIDIA GPU architectures into reliable, repeatable cloud platforms.
Understand why observability, telemetry, and orchestration are critical to interpreting system behavior at scale.
Identify common failure domains and upgrade-related risks that only emerge in always-on environments.

title: NVIDIA Vera: Reimagining CPUs for Agents, Reasoning, and What's Next
abstract:
As AI workflows expand across training, inference, analytics, and reasoning-driven applications, CPUs play a growing role in AI factory performance-from orchestration and application execution to compute- and memory-intensive processing.

This session introduces NVIDIA Vera, a next-generation CPU architecture built for these demands. We'll cover the workload trends shaping CPU design and key Vera technologies, including the custom Olympus core, high-bandwidth LPDDR5X memory subsystem, and NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric. Learn how Vera's architecture is designed to maximize CPU performance and efficiency across the AI factory.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, RTX GPU, MGX, NSight Compute, Blackwell, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Understand why AI workloads are creating new demands on CPU architecture across the AI factory.
Learn how reasoning models and agentic applications are reshaping CPU infrastructure requirements.
Explore key Vera technologies, including the custom Olympus core, LPDDR5X memory subsystem, and NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric.
See how Vera complements NVIDIA GPUs to maximize end-to-end AI factory performance and efficiency.

speakers.name: Ian Finder
speakers.title: Head of Product, Data Center CPUs

title: Building AI Factories for High-Assurance, Mission-Critical Environments (Presented by Astris AI)
abstract: As AI systems move from experimentation to operational deployment, organizations in aerospace, energy, manufacturing, and the public sector face a common challenge: how to deploy AI reliably in environments where failure, data leakage, or downtime is unacceptable. This session explores how AI factory architectures are being designed to support high-assurance, mission-critical workloads across on-premises, edge, disconnected, and regulated environments.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Drawing on lessons from Lockheed Martin's internal AI factory and its commercialization through Astris AI, we examine how NVIDIA-accelerated infrastructure, AI enterprise software, and hardened inference services come together to enable secure training, inference, and governance at scale.
Learn practical design patterns for operationalizing AI in environments that demand trust, repeatability, and resilience-from industrial systems to national-scale missions.
This session explores how AI factory architectures are being designed to support high-assurance, mission-critical workloads across on-premises, edge, disconnected, and regulated environments.

title: Eliminate the Data Bottleneck: Accelerate Enterprise AI at Scale (Presented by Elastic)
abstract: Successful enterprise AI deployments require context from your petabytes of unstructured, proprietary company data. Yet indexing and vectorizing this data at enterprise speed and scale remains one of the biggest bottlenecks to implementing AI in production, resulting in stalled deployments and high overhead costs. Elastic's GPU-accelerated search with NVIDIA cuVS now delivers 12x faster indexing speeds and enables enterprise-scale AI deployments that accelerate your path from proof of concept to production.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Discover why traditional methods of vectorizing unstructured data create barriers that prevent enterprise AI from reaching production.
Understand how 12x faster data indexing eliminates the bottleneck that stalls AI initiatives.
Learn how Elastic GPU-accelerated search reduces high overhead costs and accelerates your path to production-ready AI infrastructure.

Elastic is the No. 1 OSS vectorDB, and integration as part of this solution creates an end-to-end data→index→search offering with NVIDIA that is highly relevant to the next cohort of agentic and generative AI applications.
The future of search will combine logging, monitoring, storage, search, and indexing together to support lexical and semantic search across vast array of enterprise data types.

title: Build Smarter Robots Using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab
abstract:
Next-generation robots need the ability to sense, plan, and act with full autonomy. A simulation-first approach is key to building robots that are adaptable and efficient - supporting rapid development of robust policies and smooth transfer to new tasks across robot embodiments. Connect with NVIDIA experts to learn how NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab can accelerate your robotics pipeline. Explore how to generate synthetic data, train advanced robot policies, and run software-in-the-loop testing to validate your entire stack and close the gap between simulation and real-world deployment.

Please note: Karthik Sachdev, Aravindh Shanmuganathan, Lilla Torabi, Kelly Guo, and Asawaree Bhide will be joining us for the Monday 5PM session only. Akul Santosh, Juana Du, Hammad Mazhar, Gavrial State, and Edith Llopntop will be joining us for the Tuesday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend the session accordingly if you'd like to hear from an expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Learn how to accelerate autonomous robotics by adopting a simulation-first approach that enables rapid policy development and smooth task adaptation.
Explore how to use NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab to generate synthetic data, train robot policies, and streamline every stage of your workflow.
Discover how to validate your robot stack with software-in-the-loop testing that helps bridge the gap between simulation and real-world deployment.

speakers.name: Juana Du
speakers.title: 机器人解决方案架构师

title: A Deep Dive Into Omniverse Libraries for OpenUSD and Physical AI Development
abstract:
Join this interactive Q&A session to get direct, actionable advice from NVIDIA's core engineering teams on overcoming the critical developer friction points in building large-scale, physically accurate digital twins for robotics, autonomous vehicles, and manufacturing. This is your unique opportunity to discuss your specific use cases and technical challenges directly with the experts who develop and maintain the core Omniverse libraries and technologies for world simulation, including OpenUSD, PhysX, RTX, and more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Engage in deep-dive Q&A with the primary engineers behind OpenUSD, PhysX, and RTX to resolve specific implementation bottlenecks.
Obtain actionable technical solutions for performance and fidelity issues common in large-scale, physically accurate digital twin simulations.
Learn low-level strategies for integrating Omniverse libraries to better scale workloads for robotics, audio-visual, and industrial manufacturing.

speakers.name: Brian Harrison
speakers.title: Sr. Director of Product Management Omniverse

title: From Foundation to Full-Throttle: Expert Advice on AI Model Customizations
abstract:
Specialized models are becoming necessary to win in the enterprise. Come speak to our field team about your challenges with deploying specialized models. They can share real stories from their experience with customization strategies, their successes and their failures.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: Engage in Q&A with NVIDIA solution architects specializing in working with AI models in the field.
Learn strategies for data curation, synthetic data generation, model evaluation, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning.
Leave with actionable strategies for customizing and optimizing AI models for production.

title: How Virtual Twins are Shaping the Next Industrial Revolution
abstract:
AI-powered virtual twins, science-validated industry world models, and virtual companions are shaping the next frontier for industries.

Learn how Dassault Systèmes' industry world models, agentic 3DEXPERIENCE platform, and virtual twin technologies-combined with NVIDIA AI infrastructure, open models, and libraries-are establishing new ways of working and transforming  biology, materials science, engineering,  and manufacturing.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: BioNeMo, CUDA-X, cuDDN, NeMo, PhysX, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Discover how industry leaders leverage science-validated industry world models.
Learn how the 3DEXPERIENCE agentic platform, combining NVIDIA AI technologies and Dassault Systèmes' industry world models, powers virtual companions to supercharge Dassault Systèmes' users.
Meet LEO, the virtual companion for design and engineering, in action, for optimal parts generation.
Explore how to improve the performance of autonomous software-defined shopfloors with virtual twin of production systems powered by industry world models.
Learn how to discover new innovative materials by exploring and simulating millions of molecular configurations with industry world models for molecular dynamics.

title: MedTech: Physical AI for Imaging, Intervention, and Robotics
abstract:
Physical AI is redefining how next-generation medical devices perceive, decide, and act in the real world. From advanced imaging systems and autonomous interventional workflows to intelligent surgical robots, developers now require a unified stack that bridges data, simulation, and real-time runtime and control.  NVIDIA's platforms - such as Holoscan, Isaac for Healthcare, Omniverse, Cosmos  and Metropolis - deliver an end-to-end foundation for building and scaling physical AI: model training with domain-specific foundation models, high-fidelity digital twins for procedure-aware simulation, and deterministic low-latency deployment at the edge. Join NVIDIA experts to learn how to leverage our full-stack technologies to accelerate your development - from imaging and interventional systems to next-generation robotics - and bring safer, smarter MedTech to market faster.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Build deterministic real-time edge AI with Holoscan.
Develop robotic and interventional systems with Isaac for Healthcare.
Leverage Cosmos and Omniverse for high-fidelity synthetic data generation and simulation.

title: MLOps 201: Master Operational Excellence With Agentic AI
abstract:
The next leap in enterprise AI is industrializing intelligence, where automation and human input combine to improve agility, decision quality, and process efficiency. This session shows how NVIDIA applies lean manufacturing principles and agentic AI to its own AI operations through three core MLOps platforms: the Data Factory for synthetic data generation and labeling, the Eval Factory for model benchmarking, and the NIM Factory for inference optimization. 

From these real-world examples, we distill a practical playbook for representing any process in AI-native terms, identifying high-impact automation opportunities, embedding continuous improvement, and building a culture of operational excellence. The result is an AI-first organization with a repeatable model for AI-enhanced operations, one that extends beyond MLOps to transform the entire enterprise and its business model.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, HGX, CUDA, TensorRT, Hopper, NCCL, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Cosmos, DGX Cloud, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: See how NVIDIA designed and scaled its AI operations (MLOps) to support high-quality data generation and labeling, foundation model benchmarking, and inference performance optimization.
Equip your organization with a playbook for AI-first operations, enabling leaders and engineers to drive transformation beyond MLOps into core business processes.
Learn how to optimize and scale a business process with agentic AI via process decomposition, automation, feedback loops, and setting the right abstraction boundaries.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.title: Director, Generative AI and LLMOps Platform

title: Building, Measuring, and Using AI Scientists
abstract: Learn how AI scientists can automate generation and testing of hypotheses with experiments in a loop. See how to maintain long-running coherence required for their days runtime, and the nuances of benchmarking the process of discovery.  Explore their automated discoveries across domains like molecular biology, materials science, and chemistry.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Hopper, BioNeMo
key_takeaways: Benchmarking AI scientists has shown they can reduce discovery time from six months to one day.
AI scientists can write tens of thousands of lines of code and consider thousands of papers, making them hard to assess.
See how to train parts of an AI scientist with NemoRL and open-source LLMs.

title: CSO Insights: Driving AI-Optimized Energy Efficiency and Data Center Performance
abstract:
Join chief sustainability officers (CSOs) to learn how AI is transforming enterprise energy and infrastructure at scale. This session will include:
 
*	Insights on applying AI to optimize energy efficiency, grid-integrated operations, and enhance data center performance at scale. 
*	Concrete examples from leading companies-including Amazon, PepsiCo and Equinix-demonstrating measurable impact on operations and supply chains.
*	Executive perspective on translating sustainability and energy efficiency goals into bottom-line impact-exploring how AI efficiency translates into a win-win for operational and strategic advantage.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Grace CPU, AGX, DGX Platform, CUDA, DeepStream, TensorRT, HPC SDK, Riva, Omniverse, RAPIDS, Infiniband Networking, Metropolis, Hopper, LaunchPad, MGX, Base Command Manager, BioNeMo, CUDA-X, DALI, NeMo, NSight Systems, NVLink / NVSwitch, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, DGX Cloud, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Insights on applying AI to optimize energy efficiency, grid-integrated operations, and enhance data center performance at scale.
Concrete examples from leading companies-including Amazon, PepsiCo and Equinix-demonstrating measurable impact on operations and supply chains.
Executive perspective on translating sustainability and energy efficiency goals into bottom-line impact-exploring how AI efficiency translates into a win-win for operational and strategic advantage.

title: The AI behind Teammates, Ubisoft's Experimental Interactive Characters
abstract: Ubisoft recently unveiled Teammates, its first playable AI-based R&D project. This experience redefines NPC interaction by allowing players to lead a proactive, intelligent squad via real-time voice commands. In this session, the developers behind Teammates will detail their process for selecting and tuning AI models to maximize player immersion. They will also provide a technical deep dive into their deployment strategy for high-performance, real-time inference.
key_takeaways: Ubisoft developers will present their proven methods for evaluating and specializing AI models for gaming.
They will explain how they set up infrastructure in the cloud to support real-time, latency-critical inference, capable of scaling up to serve many thousands of players simultaneously.
Get insights into building tooling that allows designers to iterate quickly on AI behavior and game logic.
They'll show how similar models can be run on gaming-spec PCs to achieve comparable response quality and performance without cloud costs.

title: Next-Gen Digital Health Agents for Language, Speech, and Analytics
abstract:
The future of digital health is agentic. Join NVIDIA experts to discuss how AI agents are transforming the ecosystem by bridging the gap between perception and reasoning. Discuss the implementation of advanced pipelines that utilize conversational AI and speech for patient interaction, computer vision for diagnostics, and deep analytics for decision support. Whether you are a researcher or an enterprise developer, engage with our team to understand how NVIDIA NIM, blueprints, and SDKs can help you deploy robust, multi-modal agents that drive real-world clinical, research, revenue cycle, and administrative outcomes.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: TensorRT, Clara, Riva, RAPIDS, Clara Guardian, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, cuVS, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Rock the Multi-Modal Integration: Learn how to synchronize language, speech, and analytics models to create holistic patient, provider and payer-centric agents.
Learn to Solve Real-World Problems: Explore your questions with NVIDIA experts to address technical roadblocks in architecture, foundational models, scalability, and resilience.
Discover Agentic Workflows: Understand the techniques and gotchas for moving from static generative AI solutions to dynamic agents that execute complex health tasks.

abstract: Hear from a panel of leading financial services innovators on how they leverage agentic AI for production use cases. Panelists will also discuss what it takes to get to production with agentic AI in the highly regulated financial services industry, and how they effectively measure return on investment (ROI) for new use cases.

title: Master Immersive Real-Time Visual Process Understanding and Guidance
abstract:
Extended reality (XR) applications powered by agentic AI will revolutionize how frontline workers do their jobs. Join NVIDIA experts for a deep-dive discussion on how to design and train agentic AI systems for enterprise use cases. In this session, we'll share strategies and best practices for: leveraging the Nemo Agent Toolkit to build powerful, scalable, and optimized agents for XR use cases, optimizing agentic AI to accurately interpret and guide complex workflows, and deploying AI to the edge using NVIDIA Cosmos for low-latency feedback.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

key_takeaways: Learn how to leverage the Nemo Agent Toolkit to build powerful, scalable, and optimized agents for XR use cases.
Learn how to optimize agentic AI to accurately interpret and guide complex workflows.
Deploy AI to the edge using NVIDIA Cosmos for low-latency feedback.

title: AI Research Breakthroughs from NVIDIA Research (Hosted by Karoly of Two Minute Papers)
abstract: Don't miss this exciting live panel discussion, hosted by Károly Zsolnai-Fehér (better known as the creator of Two Minute Papers), featuring top researchers from NVIDIA Research. They'll dive into the latest breakthroughs in AI, spotlight the most promising emerging technical trends, and candidly explore the biggest open challenges facing the field today. Join us for an interactive, high-energy conversation packed with cutting-edge insights!

title: Building an AI Factory to Power the Next Era of eCommerce
abstract: Coupang is evolving from a digital retailer into an AI-native commerce platform by building an AI Factory in partnership with NVIDIA. In this session, we'll examine how large-scale GPU infrastructure enables end-to-end AI lifecycle management - from data pipelines and model training to real-time inference across billions of interactions. We'll discuss the system architecture, performance optimizations, and deployment strategies required to scale foundation models across search, recommendation, and merchant services.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, Base Command Manager, Interconnect Networking, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Build a centralized AI Factory with production governance and scalability
Full - stack integration with NVIDIA GPU and Software drives competitive advantage
Architect scalable agent-based systems on NVIDIA ecosystem that replace expensive closed-source models while increasing throughput by 10x

title: From Challenge to Competitive Advantage: How VC Portfolio Companies Leverage NVIDIA to Win Markets
abstract: AI venture funding reached $100.8 billion in 2024-37% of all venture capital (VC) investment. Yet, most promising AI startups don't fail due to weak markets or teams, but from challenges bridging research and production: scaling infrastructure, solving domain-specific problems, and building defensible technical moats. The focus is now on "doing AI right" for real impact. Top VCs now treat NVIDIA partnership as essential portfolio infrastructure-not just for compute, but as a strategic accelerator that helps founders solve hard problems, lead technically, and speed up R&D. This session shares how startups used NVIDIA's ecosystem to achieve breakthrough results.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: How to Bridge Research and Production: Learn strategies for scaling infrastructure, solving domain-specific challenges, and building defensible technical moats.
NVIDIA as a Strategic Accelerator: Discover how partnering with NVIDIA can help startups solve complex problems, establish technical leadership, and accelerate R&D.
Real-World Success Stories: Gain insights from portfolio companies that leveraged NVIDIA's full-stack ecosystem to achieve breakthrough results and meaningful impact.

speakers.name: Alyss Noland
speakers.title: DGX Cloud Dev Ecosystem Development - DevRel

title: Accelerated AI Networking with DOCA
abstract: As AI models scale, data center networking must provide ultra-low latency, high throughput, and programmable data paths so training and inference workloads remain efficient. With the NVIDIA DOCA software platform, developers can move critical data-path functions onto NVIDIA BlueField data processing units (DPUs) using DOCA libraries and microservices to offload, accelerate, and isolate cloud infrastructure while enhancing observability. We'll cover how DOCA enables centralized orchestration of BlueField‐accelerated services, dynamic traffic steering, and fine‐grained network and security controls that work together to deliver secure, high‐performance fabrics with predictable performance and efficient resource utilization for multi‐tenant AI environments at scale.
topic: Networking / Communications - AI Networking, Networking / Communications - Cloud Networking
key_takeaways: Understand how industry-leading partners create DOCA-accelerated applications with wire speed performance data paths.
Learn how to use DOCA's programmable networking APIs and data-path acceleration capabilities to customize and offload packet processing for large-scale AI clusters.
Learn practical strategies for tuning and operating secure multi-tenant, high-bandwidth AI networking environments using DOCA-enabled DPUs and SuperNICs.

title: Shatter the Memory Wall: Composable Building Blocks for Massive Scale Analytics
abstract:
As data demands scale to terabytes and petabytes, traditional GPU memory boundaries become critical bottlenecks. We introduce a unified strategy using composable, engine-agnostic building blocks to shatter these limits.

We'll dive deep into cuCascade, a library for memory reservation and topology discovery. It prevents out-of-memory failures by gracefully spilling data between memory tiers-from GPU to host to disk-while mapping hardware throughput. We also introduce Quentrace, a semantic telemetry layer for "always-on" profiling, allowing developers to visualize query plans and resource consumption across GPUs in real time. We'll demonstrate these tools powering "speed of light" analytics on NVIDIA's NVL72 systems (GB200) utilizing full NVLink. Learn to leverage these components to optimize engines, identify bandwidth bottlenecks, and achieve unprecedented TCO and performance.
nvidia_technology: CUDA, cuDF, NCCL, NSight Systems, nvCOMP
key_takeaways: Moving data to the GPU is difficult, and needs to be fast
Composable, foundational building blocks are needed for accelerated data processing on GPUs
Telemetry as a first-class citizen matters when building large distributed engines on GPUs

title: Inside Resilient AI Factories: How Predictive Analytics and IST are Driving Next-Level Data Center Reliability
abstract: AI factories are transforming AI workloads, but maintaining uptime in these GPU-powered environments is a critical challenge-where even a single fault can disrupt weeks of progress and cost millions. Learn how NVIDIA's advanced fault detection, predictive analytics, and innovative In-System Test (IST) facilitate rapid diagnostics, enabling organizations to quickly identify and resolve issues. By minimizing downtime and preventing costly interruptions, these technologies drive a significant reduction in total cost of ownership and deliver improved return on investment. This session will highlight how automated recovery and hierarchical diagnostics empower organizations to deploy and scale AI infrastructure with confidence and efficiency.
key_takeaways: Discover how proactive fault prediction and self-healing capabilities drive higher uptime and business continuity for AI factories.
Walk away with knowledge of why rapid diagnostics and automated recovery reduce operational costs and maximize return on investment.
Learn how intelligent resiliency features empower organizations to scale AI confidently, unlocking new levels of efficiency and reliability.

title: AI Grid for Robots: Applications and Infrastructure for Physical AI Powered by Telcos
abstract:
Physical AI is moving robots from labs into streets, offices, factories, and public spaces-where they must see, think, and act in milliseconds. Traditional cloud-centric architectures can't meet the latency, reliability, and resiliency demands of these real-world uses, making a distributed AI grid essential. 

Two leading telecom operators will showcase physical AI use cases, demonstrating how AI-enhanced radio networks and distributed computing let robots offload heavy perception and decision workloads while maintaining real-time responsiveness and safety. The session will outline key architectural building blocks of an AI-native network, explore its evolution toward 6G, and explain why telecom AI grids are poised to become the default fabric for the next wave of physical AI.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, Aerial, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Interconnect Networking, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Learn what physical AI and robotics apps need from networks, and why an AI grid is essential for meeting strict latency, reliability, and safety requirements.
Understand the core architecture and infrastructure needed to deliver this, and how NVIDIA's AI-RAN and AI grid components can be used together to enable it.
Hear how telcos can become the platform for physical AI by building AI-native networks that evolve into 6G, powering large-scale autonomous systems.

speakers.name: Ryuji Wakikawa
speakers.title: VP, Research Institute of Advanced Technology

title: Engineering Real-World LLM Inference: Bridging Open-Source and Production Systems
abstract: In production, LLM inference performance is shaped by model architecture, hardware, workload patterns, and service-level agreement constraints. This session presents a practical workflow for profiling inference stacks, identifying real bottlenecks, and evaluating optimizations across kernels, scheduling, and system design, showing how to leverage fast-moving open-source innovations while applying our own enhancements to achieve production-grade performance and stability, and how to feed lessons from production back into upstream development.
key_takeaways: Learn how to profile LLM inference stacks and identify real bottlenecks across kernels, scheduling, and system design.
Explore how to leverage fast-moving open-source innovations and apply custom enhancements for production-grade performance.
Discover how lessons from large-scale deployments can feed back into upstream development to continuously improve inference systems.

title: Accelerate Hybrid Quantum-Classical Research at Scale With NVIDIA CUDA-Q (Presented by Foxconn)
abstract: This talk explains why Hon Hai Research Institute's integrated strategy for advancing quantum science across the full stack requires an integrated strategy, developing both better hardware and applications algorithms together. We'll outline Hon Hai Research Institute's approach to this-including Foxconn's ion-trap roadmap-from first-generation blade-type traps to the establishment of Taiwan's first industry-based ion-trap quantum computing laboratory and the creation of multi-layer and chip-based architectures. We'll also highlight advancements from Hon Hai Research Institute in software and theory for areas including quantum error correction, efficient tomography and noise benchmarking, quantum machine learning, and quantum networking.
key_takeaways: Introduce the research focus at HHRI
Introduce the quantum research outcomes by HHRI
Highlight our achievement in quantum error-correcting code, quantum algorithms, and quantum simulation areas

title: How to Build Your Custom AI Advantage (Presented by Mistral AI)
abstract: Enterprises need AI that builds on their unique expertise and strengthens their competitive edge. In this session, Tim Lacroix (co-founder and CTO, Mistral AI), Kari Briski (vice president of Gen AI, NVIDIA), and Carlos Pereira (chief architect, Cisco Customer Experience) reveal how Mistral AI's open-source leadership-paired with a full-stack AI approach-delivers unmatched customization, efficiency, and control. We'll reveal new models, products and platforms from Mistral AI that help to unlock the ROI of AI in the enterprise.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Discover Mistral AI's latest small, high-performance models optimized for NVIDIA hardware (with real-world edge deployments from Cisco), plus specialized enterprise applications: DocAI for intelligent document processing, Voxtral for transcription and customer service, and Vibe, the most cost-performant, customizable coding assistant.
Learn how leading organizations leverage proprietary data to deploy AI their way-and why this is your moment to own your AI future.
(P.S. We'll be breaking news at this session-you don't want to miss it!)

speakers.name: Carlos Pereira
speakers.title: Fellow and Chief Architect, Customer Experience (CX)

title: Accelerating Enterprise AI with NVIDIA: How SAP Builds, Scales, and Runs Agentic Workflows (Presented by SAP)
abstract: In this session, SAP shares how it partners with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI from model development to production-grade agentic workflows. This session shows how SAP combines NVIDIA AI with deep business context from SAP applications to move AI beyond prototypes into real-world impact. Learn how developers build and adapt domain-specific models, operationalize them using SAP GenAI hub and NVIDIA NIM, and apply them through SAP Joule for Developers to tackle complex challenges like code explanation and cloud migration. The session closes with a forward-looking view on agentic and physical AI, where live business context connects digital intelligence to real-world execution.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Learn how enterprises transition from AI pilots to deploying intelligent agents that automate and optimize essential SAP business processes.

The session provides an in-depth look at the technical architecture behind SAP's Joule Agents, emphasizing integration with modern AI infrastructure and business data for reliable, scalable operations.
Gain practical insights into designing, orchestrating, and governing multi-step workflows using Joule Agents in procurement, finance, supply chain, and operations.
