Scalable Impact: Lessons learned in extending aerodynamic AI surrogates across other CAE functions at JLR

speakers.name: Nick Simmonds
speakers.title: CFD and AI Methods Technical Specialist

title: Build Fault-Tolerant Distributed AI Training at Scale
abstract: Learn how to build resilient distributed training pipelines using NVIDIA's Resiliency Extension (NVRx) on clouds like AWS so your long-running jobs automatically restart from fast, asynchronous checkpoints. We'll dive deep into practical integration patterns with PyTorch-based frameworks and show how you can simplify operations while confidently scaling to multi-node, multi-GPU clusters.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: Continuous Training, Zero Downtime: Discover how automated restarts in NVRx keeps distributed jobs running reliably-even through node or process failures.
End-to-end Resiliency Stack: Understand how NVRx pairs with AWS to deliver end-to-end recovery including automatic instance replacement, monitoring, and orchestration for cloud-scale AI training.
Developer-Friendly Integration: Learn how to add resiliency to your existing PyTorch based Deep Learning framework codebase with minimal changes, using open-source APIs.
Operational Simplicity at Scale: See how built-in automation replaces manual recovery scripts and complex DevOps workflows, freeing teams to focus on experimentation and model quality.

title: Robotic Automation in Neurosurgery Navigation With Isaac and Holoscan
abstract:
Join XRlabs and Olympus as they come together to showcase how NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare is transforming surgical innovation. This session will demonstrate how Olympus' advanced medical imaging and surgical platforms, combined with XRlabs' simulation and training expertise, leverage Isaac's AI-powered workflows to bridge the gap between digital and physical worlds.

You'll see how simulation, digital twins, and robotics come together to enable safer procedures, faster training, and smarter decision-making in the operating room. From modeling complex anatomy to validating robotic tasks in virtual environments before deploying them in patient care, this collaboration highlights the new standard for medical device development and precision surgery.
key_takeaways: Simulation + Digital Twins Are Becoming Core to Surgical Innovation
AI-Powered Real-Time Surgical Workflows Are Now Feasible
A Fully Integrated Pipeline From Training → Validation → Deployment

speakers.name: Ernst Jonscher
speakers.title: Senior Business Development Manager, Video Microscopy (Exoscopy)

title: AI Digital Twin of the Climate System
abstract: Scientists use computer simulations to understand how different parts of the Earth's system interact, or to explore how they respond to external forcing. These simulations have been carried out using numerical models (global climate models, or GCMs). Recently, modern machine-learning-based emulators have been used to reproduce the same dynamics using data. We will present the first generation of AI digital twin of the climate system, using data from state-of-the-art GCMs. These AI emulators can simulate the climate system 100x faster than typical GCMs with one GPU. We will discuss how we build, evaluate, and leverage these emulators to make discoveries about the climate system. For example, it offers strategies to improve simulations of extreme weather such as hurricanes, and forcing them with past ocean states reveals how and why hurricane counts have varied over the past 1,000 years.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
key_takeaways: AI emulators allow us to simulate trajectories of the climate system 100x than traditional numerical simulations.
AI emulators allow us to query for extreme weather events to simulate trajectories of worst-case scenarios.
AI digital twins of the climate system offer insights into past, present, and future weather and climate events.
AI digital twins of the climate system allows us to ask "what if" questions to better understand the dynamics.

speakers.name: Elizabeth Barnes

speakers.title: Keller Professor of Applied Mathematics and Professor of Mathematics and Data Science

title: Real-Time Science and Engineering With AI Physics and Kit-CAE
abstract: Physical engineering is moving from static reports and siloed workflows to real‐time, interactive insight. This session shows how AI physics meets simulation‐driven engineering by putting NVIDIA's GPU‐accelerated libraries and NVIDIA Omniverse open libraries first. We'll cover accessing common computer-aided engineering (CAE) formats via OpenUSD technologies in kit-cae; high‐fidelity field visualization with RTX and IndeX; Warp‐accelerated CAE/visualization algorithms; physics NeMo surrogates made from solver-generated ground truth; and application streaming for instant, hardware‐agnostic review and collaboration. We'll highlight current industry use cases and advancements in the area of digital twins for science and engineering. You'll leave today with an adoption path and a clear view of how AI physics and Omniverse libraries can accelerate your organization.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, Omniverse, PhysicsNeMo, CUDA-X, DLSS, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Omniverse Kit-CAE enables integrating CAE data into Omniverse digital twins
PhysicsNeMo and NVIDIA Apollo enable building AI physics surrogate models
Digital twins accelerate the process of scientific and engineering discovery

title: Accelerated Building Blocks for Next-Generation AI+HPC Workloads
abstract: Embracing next-generation technology like AI-infused scientific workflows involves blending familiar building blocks with innovation, timeliness, and purpose. Whether it's models pre-trained on petabyte-scale data for common forecasting tasks, or real-time digital twins, developers need building blocks to enable their vision. In this talk, we walk through developer software used to enable AI and HPC innovations, including recent enhancements that maximize the use of NVIDIA hardware and improve the developer experience.
key_takeaways: Discover the latest HPC SDK updates for increased flexibility and application performance examples.
Learn how HPC partners with AI to accelerate traditional simulations in computer-aided engineering, electronic design automation, and earth science.
Explore adding reduced precision and distributed math libraries for optimizing application performance.

title: Developing AI 2035: The Next Decade of Intelligence
abstract: As AI evolves into the engine of global innovation, its future will define how we work, create, and solve humanity's greatest challenges. This luminary panel brings together AI leaders to chart the decade ahead-where breakthroughs in large-scale compute, foundation models, and intelligent systems converge to expand human potential. Building on a decade of NVIDIA DGX innovation, panelists will explore how next-generation AI balances power and sustainability, fosters discovery, and advances responsible progress for all.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, Infiniband Networking, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Mission Control
key_takeaways: See how AI is evolving to power intelligence responsibly and sustainably.
Learn the leadership principles shaping innovation across compute, models, and ecosystems.
Discover how multi-modal and real-time inference capabilities are transforming industries and society.
Explore new frameworks for safety, governance, and global collaboration in AI's next decade.

title: Best Practices in Building Your AI Factory: Connect With NVIDIA MLOps and AIOps Experts
abstract:
As organizations shift from traditional data center to AI factories, building a full-stack AI infrastructure can be challenging. Connect with NVIDIA experts on how to quickly deploy your AI factories, and learn best practices for your ML/AI workflows.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
nvidia_technology: NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Explore best practices in developing and implementing your MLOps strategy.
Learn how NVIDIA IT is deploying AI factories.
Learn how NVIDIA's partner ecosystem is leveraging NVIDIA AI.

title: Unlock Efficiency for Financial Agents With Scalable Data Curation
abstract: Financial applications demand high precision and ultra-low latency. Learn firsthand from engineering leaders at Capital One how they're building distributed data pipelines to curate high-quality datasets that enable laser-focused foundation models for financial applications.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: Discover best practices for curating high-quality datasets that boost the accuracy and reliability of foundation models in financial applications.
Learn to deploy scalable, distributed data pipelines using RAY across multi-node and multi-GPU setups to handle massive datasets efficiently.
Learn how to drive substantial benefits through acceleration of distributed Ray Tune workflows on NVIDIA GPU from engineering leaders at Capital One.

title: Autonomous Networks: The New Brain of Telco Operations
abstract:
Automation is not the same as autonomy. Automated networks follow predefined rules; autonomous networks understand context, analyze data, reason, act, and learn continuously. Autonomous networks are the next frontier for telecoms, and progress is accelerating rapidly. 

This panel brings together leading telecom operators and solution providers to show how agentic AI and large telco models are transforming operations. Speakers will share how they unify data across operations and business support systems and network domains and deploy AI agents that self-configure, self-optimize, and self-heal networks while improving performance and customer experience. Hear real-world examples of capabilities already in production, the NVIDIA software and infrastructure behind them, and what the next 12 months of innovation look like as operators advance toward higher levels of autonomy.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Understand why automation is not the same as autonomy, and how agentic AI plus large telco models accelerate the journey toward higher levels of network autonomy.
Learn about the latest AI agents and real-world operations use cases telcos are deploying today, and their measurable impact on network and operational key performance indicators.
Hear how panelists have built their autonomous network initiatives, the role of NVIDIA software and infrastructure, and what innovations they expect in the coming year.

speakers.name: Lilach Ilan
speakers.title: Global Head of Business Development -Telco Operations

title: Redefining Vector DB Management: Uniting Massive Scalability With GPU-Accelerated Indexing (Presented by KIOXIA America)
abstract: As generative AI reshapes enterprise workloads, the rapid growth of vector databases introduces new challenges for scalability and performance. Kioxia Corp. addresses these challenges with AiSAQ, an innovative approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) algorithm that breaks traditional DRAM limitations by leveraging SSDs for search operations.  Now, AiSAQ adds powerful GPU utilization to vector indexing, transforming a time-consuming bottleneck into a highly accelerated workflow. In this session, discover how AiSAQ's combination of SSD scalability and NVIDIA cuVS dramatically boosts throughput for demanding generative AI applications such as retrieval-augmented generation and semantic search. Gain insights into the benefits of integrating SSDs and GPUs, enabling robust, cost-effective solutions to meet the demands of today's AI-driven workloads.
key_takeaways: The rapid growth of vector databases introduces scalability challenges for scalability and performance.
Kioxia AiSAQ, an innovative ANNS algorithm, breaks traditional DRAM limitations by leveraging SSDs for search operations.
The latest version AiSAQ adds powerful GPU utilization to vector indexing, transforming a time-consuming bottleneck into a highly accelerated workflow.

title: Innovating Liquid Cooling Solutions for New-Generation AI Data Centers (Presented by Aivres)
abstract: With drastically increasing power and thermal density in new-generation AI chips and servers, AI data centers face enormous challenges in effectively and efficiently cooling their AI computing infrastructure at all levels-node, rack, pod, and data center. In this session, we will share Aivres energy-efficient liquid-cooled AI server products and solutions based on NVIDIA GPUs that fully meet the needs and technical challenges of new-generation AI data centers. Building on the primary liquid-cooling loop design, we extend the discussion to the secondary cooling loops, coolant distribution units, power supply, and liquid-cooled server rack designs. Finally, we will showcase our reference solutions and deployments for new-generation liquid-cooling AI data centers.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, HGX, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Energy-efficient liquid-cooled AI server products and solutions based on NVIDIA GPUs that meet technical challenges of new-generation AI data centers
The discussion to the secondary cooling loops, coolant distribution units, power supply, and liquid-cooled server rack designs
Showcase the reference solutions and deployments for new-generation liquid-cooling AI data centers

title: Sovereign World Models: Scaling AI Memory, Data Gravity, and Governance (Presented by DDN)
abstract: World models demand long-horizon reasoning that rapidly outgrows GPU memory, while sovereign AI requires strict control over where data and models live. This session shows how a data intelligence architecture designed with native multi-tenancy, KV Cache, and hybrid cloud capabilities can extend the AI pipeline beyond the GPU to deliver ultra-low-latency, high-concurrency access patterns and policy-based governance to scale world models under sovereign constraints. You'll leave with practical design patterns for building NVIDIA-accelerated AI factories that maintain performance without sacrificing sovereignty or cost-per-token.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, RTX GPU, DGX Platform, HGX, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Learn how how a data intelligence architecture designed with native multi-tenancy, KV Cache, and hybrid cloud capabilities can extend the AI pipeline beyond the GPU.
Discover practical design patterns for building NVIDIA-accelerated AI factories that maintain performance without sacrificing sovereignty or cost-per-token.
Learn how intelligent AI data pipelines deliver ultra-low-latency, high-concurrency access patterns and policy-based governance to scale world models under sovereign constraints.

speakers.name: Shilpa Kolhatkar
speakers.title: Global Head of AI Nations Business Development

title: Vision to AI-Driven Software Engineering
abstract: This session explores how bold product vision can be translated into real, production-grade software using AI-driven engineering practices. We'll walk through the evolving role of AI across the software life cycle-from ideation and system design to coding, testing, deployment, and continuous improvement.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Hopper, CUDA-X, RTX Virtual Workstations (vWS), Blackwell, DGX Cloud
key_takeaways: Turn Vision into Execution Velocity: Discover how AI transforms every stage of development-from design to deployment-so your teams ship high-quality products faster than ever.
Engineer Smarter with Human-led, AI-Augmented Systems: Learn how to harness AI as a true partner, accelerating implementation and iteration while keeping human creativity and intent at the center.

Build Strong Foundations to Scale AI Successfully: Explore how robust architecture, clear interfaces, and disciplined feedback loops turn AI from a chaos multiplier into a force for innovation and impact.

title: Build Faster, Smarter AI Agents: Techniques for Scalable Reasoning and Real-Time Efficiency
abstract: As AI agents evolve from simple task executors to adaptive systems capable of reasoning and decision-making, efficient orchestration of compute and model execution becomes essential. In this session, two NVIDIA engineers will walk through the latest frameworks and techniques for accelerating AI agents-from optimizing memory and compute pipelines to managing multi-stage reasoning workflows. Learn how to design high-performance agent architectures that can dynamically plan, retrieve, and act in real time while scaling seamlessly across GPUs.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Learn how to design high-performance agent architectures that can dynamically plan, retrieve, and act in real time, while scaling seamlessly across GPUs
Discover practical methods to maximize throughput and reduce latency with live demos, and learn from performance insights.
Learn how to bring advanced agentic intelligence closer to production readiness.

title: Reverse Pitches: VC Pitches for Startups
abstract: This isn't a typical pitch session. Join us for a fresh perspective, where investors from innovative venture capital firms pitch their firm and their vision for the future of their industries. Get a firsthand look at what the next wave of startups could look like.
key_takeaways: Spotlight and deepen engagement with VCA members
Deal flow pipeline opportunities for VCA members
Promote VC to startup connections

title: Accelerating the Path to Production: The Evolution of Enterprise Storage to Deliver AI-Ready Data
abstract: Roughly 70% of AI projects fail to transition to production due to challenges related to data compliance and security. To address this challenge, enterprise storage systems are evolving from data delivery to knowledge and intelligence delivery systems. Join us to learn how NVIDIA is collaborating with leading partners from the industry on architectures that deliver AI-ready data to accelerate the path from proof of concept to production.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Data security and compliance can become key blockers for production deployment.
Enterprise data is the secret to unlock value from AI, but it also needs to be secured and governed.
Storage systems are evolving to deliver intelligence for the enteprise AI factories.

title: How to Build an AI Cloud: From Site Selection to GPU Clusters (Presented by IREN)
abstract:
Building an AI cloud starts long before GPUs arrive. Site selection, power access, networking, and construction sequencing now define how quickly and reliably AI workloads can scale.

In this session, IREN leaders share a practical view of how AI clouds are built from the ground up. The speakers cover how sites are chosen near fiber routes and grid interconnects, why power availability has become the primary constraint on AI growth, and how GPU clusters are configured to support high-performance training and inference. The talk focuses on real-world trade-offs and infrastructure decisions that shape performance, uptime, and scalability in modern AI cloud deployments.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Understand the factors that define the scalability of AI infrastructure.
Know the most important infrastructure decisions for building an AI cloud.
Learn from IREN's experience as a leader in AI cloud infrastructure.

title: Under the Hood of Building and Scaling AI-Native Applications (Presented by Together AI)
abstract: Building AI-native applications is fundamentally different from traditional software-and scaling them demands infrastructure designed for models, not just compute. Decagon builds AI agents for enterprise customer support across voice, chat, and email, backed by a research org that trains and fine-tunes specialized models to execute complex workflows through Agent Operating Procedures. As Decagon scaled, running autonomous agents reliably and economically became the defining challenge-especially for real-time voice, where silence reads as failure and latency is instantly perceptible. In this session, learn how Together AI, the AI-native cloud, helped Decagon turn a multi-model agent stack into a production system that meets sub-second latency targets and stays reliable through traffic surges.
key_takeaways: Why AI-native apps need AI-native infrastructure: training, fine-tuning, and serving purpose-built models at massive concurrency and token volumes
Scaling without breaking unit economics: continuously optimizing inference performance and cost-per-query so always-on, 24/7 voice agent deployments remain viable at scale
Keeping up with fast-moving research: Avoiding DIY and traditional cloud stacks that quickly become obsolete as models, inference techniques (like speculative decoding), and hardware evolve
From prototype to production-fast and reliable: supporting rapid iteration, safe rollout of new checkpoints, and production stability under unpredictable traffic spikes

title: Agentic AI II: Production Deployment, Safety and Scale
abstract: This session focuses on the deployment of agents in production, and hard engineering required to run models and agents at scale. Our experts will discuss the considerations of hosting open models using high-performance inference engines on your own infrastructure, as well as incorporating frontier models in your workflows. We discuss optimizing, inference speed, and reducing costs as well as implementing rigorous safety guardrails, along with evaluating and auditing models in production.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Best practices for self-hosting open models using tools like vLLM and NIMs
Leveraging frontier models as well as in-house models to offset latency and costs
Implementing input/output guardrails for safety
Auditing models in production

speakers.name: Neal Vaidya
speakers.title: Developer Advocate Engineer for Deep Learning SW

title: Building Sovereign AI: Scaling 100B+ Model Training on NVIDIA Blackwell Infrastructure (Presented by Lablup, Inc.)
abstract:
Dive deep into the real-world challenges of training 100 billion-plus parameter foundation models from scratch on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. We'll share hands-on experiences from a national-scale sovereign AI initiative, covering fault-tolerant orchestration, storage I/O optimization, and NVIDIA Collective Communication Library (NCCL) tuning strategies that keep massive training jobs running reliably across more than 500 B200 GPUs.

At this scale, GPU errors, NCCL timeouts, and storage slowdowns begin to appear. Learn how we built a resilient stack with automated failure recovery, resolved 10x storage degradation from NFS driver issues, tackled MXFP8 precision stability on Blackwell, and tuned RoCE and NVIDIA InfiniBand networks through cross-vendor collaboration.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, HGX, CUDA, TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, NCCL, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Explore the architecture of a national-scale sovereign AI training cluster using 500+ NVIDIA B200 GPUs with KubeVirt-based infrastructure as a service and container orchestration.
Learn fault-tolerant scheduling strategies that automatically recover multi-node training jobs from GPU, network, and storage failures without manual intervention.
Discover how to diagnose and resolve storage I/O bottlenecks caused by NFS driver misconfigurations that can slow data loading by 10x or more.
Gain practical insights into MXFP8 precision training on Blackwell architecture, including driver compatibility and stability considerations.
Apply NCCL tuning techniques and network optimization strategies for RoCE and InfiniBand environments to maximize distributed training throughput.

title: Scaling the AI Factory: Eliminating Power and Thermal Barriers With Two-Phase Cooling (Presented by ZutaCore)
abstract:
As AI shifts to continuous, real-time, agentic workloads, power and thermal stability-not model complexity-now limit AI factory scale. GPUs running large-scale inference require predictable, full-performance operation, yet air cooling introduces throttling and inefficiency.

This session presents Neuralwatt's evaluation of ZutaCore HyperCool two-phase direct-to-chip cooling. Results show zero thermal throttling and events, up to 30% lower peak GPU temperatures, and a 22.5% increase in tokens per watt. Learn how two-phase cooling enables higher compute density and throughput without adding power capacity, while supporting waterless, high-temperature, production-ready AI infrastructure.
key_takeaways: Why thermal and power ceilings-not model complexity-are the new bottlenecks for AI factory scale
How two-phase cooling maintains stable, full-performance GPU operation under real-world agentic workloads
Neuralwatt validation results of ZutaCore HyperCool: 30% lower peak temperatures, 22.5% more tokens per watt, zero throttling
How to increase compute density and throughput without increasing available power
Practical guidance for adopting waterless, high-temperature two-phase cooling in production AI facilities

title: オールフォトニクスネットワークとNVIDIA DynamoによるAIエージェント向けプラットフォームの最適化手法
abstract: 参加者は、Dynamo と LMCache、NIXLを用いて 1,000 km 超にわたり KV キャッシュを共有し、分散データセンター間で大規模言語モデル(LLM)推論をスケールさせる方法を学びます。複数サイトの GPU クラスタや LLM サービスを運用する参加者にとって、本セッションは、分散データセンター間のサイロを解消しつつ、低遅延かつ省電力なサイト間推論を実現する実践的なアーキテクチャを提示する点で重要です。
key_takeaways: 遠隔データセンター間でオールフォトニクスネットワークを介したディスアグリゲーテッド推論処理を実行するための要点を学ぶ
エンタープライズグレードのレベルで、Dynamoを効果的に管理する方法、具体的にはKVキャッシュの分散を制御し、プリフィルデコードのバランスを取る方法を学ぶ
サイト間の KV キャッシュヒット率を高め、プレフィル時間と TTFT(第1トークンまでの時間)を短縮し、複数データセンターの部分キャッシュを融合する仕組みを理解する
NIXL と DOCA オフロードが、多様な RDMA トランスポートを単一の低オーバーヘッドな経路に統合し、長距離リンク上でも GDS(GPU Direct Storage)の利点を維持する方法を学ぶ
統合アーキテクチャが 100 km 超の距離でも低遅延の LLM 推論を維持し、分離されたサイトに比べて約 2 倍のスループットと約 3 倍のエネルギー効率を実現し、分散データセンターをリージョン規模で統合できることを確認する

speakers.name: Kota Tsuyuzaki
speakers.title: イノベーションセンター テクノロジー部門 担当課長 エンジニアリングマネージャー
speakers.company: NTTドコモビジネス株式会社

title: Simply Build, Run, and Optimize Your NVIDIA AI Factories With Nutanix (Presented by Nutanix)
abstract: Learn how Nutanix integrates infrastructure orchestration, security, and AI platform services into a single, turnkey environment. This approach replaces fragmented point solutions with an optimized stack designed to accelerate the deployment of production-grade agentic AI applications while simplifying day-to-day operations.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Understand how Nutanix helps you manage all your AI inferencing in a single system.
Discover how Nutanix Enterprise AI with NVIDIA AI helps drive enterprise Gen AI across your business, always in your control.
Learn about the next-generation Nutanix Enterprise AI Gateway that helps you control costs and load balance Gen AI requests.
See a demo of the Nutanix Enterprise AI Gateway in action.

title: Ozaki Scheme: Addressing the Accuracy Challenge in the Era of Low-Precision Accelerators
abstract:
This talk explores the Ozaki Scheme, including its two variants, as a practical approach for recovering high accuracy from low-precision computations in numerical linear algebra.
Participation is important as it sheds light on how we can effectively respond to the accuracy challenges posed by next-generation accelerator architectures.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries
key_takeaways: The core concept of the Ozaki Scheme and its variants, and how high-accuracy results can be systematically recovered from low-precision arithmetic in numerical linear algebra.
How the Ozaki Scheme addresses the accuracy-performance trade-off, enabling reliable numerical results while exploiting the high throughput of low-precision accelerators.
Practical techniques for applying the Ozaki Scheme to matrix computations, including matrix multiplication and factorizations, on modern GPU and accelerator architectures.

title: Transforming Industry With Physical AI (Presented by Agile Robots)
abstract: Agile Robots is driving a shift in industrial automation-from systems that follow predefined rules to machines that perceive, learn, and act in the physical world through AI. This talk explores how Agile Robots builds on deep experience in industrial automation and combines it with advanced AI to realize physical AI as a new paradigm for designing work, manufacturing, and production, where intelligence is embodied in robotic systems, rather than confined to software. The presentation highlights how Agile Robots is developing today's and the next generation's robotic systems-including humanoid robots-by integrating NVIDIA Isaac open frameworks and libraries to bring AI-driven flexibility, autonomy, and collaboration to the factory floor, and outlines the technological trajectory ahead.
key_takeaways: Physical AI requires real-world data and experience to learn effectively. Physical AI cannot be trained in isolation. Deployment in real manufacturing projects, industrial production lines, and entire factories, combined with a diverse portfolio of in-house developed robots, provides high-quality data, and gives Agile Robots a unique advantage.

Manufacturing is becoming a scalable service. Agile Robots is providing manufacturing as a service, and it enables highly automated production anywhere. By combining advanced R&D, system integration, product design, and AI-driven software, production becomes faster to deploy, easier to adapt and operate, and scalable across the globe.

Humanoids have to prove themselves in factories first. Humanoid robots are advancing rapidly, but their value lies in solving real problems, rather than entertainment. Industry will be their first meaningful home, where structured environments and compliance enable reliable testing, learning, and scaling. Only once humanoids can handle unexpected situations with confidence will they move into homes or care settings. The factory is the training ground for the humanoids of tomorrow.

title: Transform Data Center Economics With NVIDIA Grace- and Vera-Powered Edge Compute Servers (Presented by MiTAC)
abstract: Dive into the MiTAC R1917GC, featuring the NVIDIA Grace C1 CPU and PCIe Gen 5 I/O in an MGX 1U form factor. Discover how its industry-leading energy efficiency significantly reduces total cost of ownership (TCO) for edge data center workloads.
key_takeaways: Understand why the shift to Arm-based servers (e.g., MiTAC R1917GC with NVIDIA Grace and Vera) is an essential, long-term industry trend for addressing soaring utility costs and achieving aggressive power usage effectiveness goals in modern data centers.
Understand how Grace's high-bandwidth LPDDR5 and fast on-chip fabric architecture delivers a superior TCO by reducing operating expenses while maintaining full system performance.

Learn how the combination of power efficiency and PCIe Gen 5 I/O prepares data center infrastructure for the next generation of accelerated cloud applications, ensuring long-term technological competitiveness.

title: Scale a Thinking Enterprise on BlueVerse (Presented by LTM)
abstract: This session introduces how BlueVerse, built on the NVIDIA stack and powered by Nemotron models, enables AI factories to reliably move from idea to impact. Scaling a thinking enterprise requires more than powerful models-it requires an architecture where intelligence, context, governance, and outcomes work together. Learn how agentic workflows use Nemotron‐driven reasoning, unified guardrails, and continuous evaluation to improve with every outcome-creating an enterprise that learns, adapts, and scales safely.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, LaunchPad, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn three proven process-to-outcomes patterns you can start in 90 days: agentic IT ops, finance ops, and Socratic learning agents.
Apply AI factory economics to real-world enterprise scenarios by linking performance to cost, latency, and business key performance indicators.
Create your blueprint by understanding how context, agents, tools, guardrails, and evaluation come together for reliable, scalable execution.
Discover "IT as HR" and manage your digital employee like a pro, from onboarding and permissions to evaluation and performance management.
Measure what matters, including latency, cost-to-serve, quality, safety, and drift tied to cycle time, resolution, and compliance.

title: Maximize Tokens-per-Watt With AI Factory Digital Twins, From Chip to Grid (Presented by Siemens)
abstract:
AI factories are becoming mission‐critical, gigawatt‐scale infrastructure that must be engineered as a single, connected system. This session presents a "chip‐to‐grid" workflow focused on two key performance indicators: time‐to‐token and watts‐per‐token. 

It begins at the silicon layer, where AI‐native, GPU‐accelerated electronic design automation that utilizes Fuse and NVIDIA Nemotron accelerates design cycles. The workflow then extends to the factory, where Siemens Digital Twin Composer with NVIDIA Omniverse enables decision‐grade digital twins across power, cooling, and layout before construction. The session will conclude with an operational reference architecture that improves efficiency, resilience, and uptime. Join us to explore how digital twins redefine AI factory design, construction, and operations-creating a new level of operational excellence.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, HGX, CUDA, Fleet Command, TensorRT, Ethernet Networking, Magnum IO, PhysicsNeMo, Base Command Manager, cuBLAS, CUDA-X, cuDDN, cuFFT, cuGraph, cuML, IndeX, NCCL, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, Omniverse Replicator, PhysX, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Nemotron
key_takeaways: AI factory time-to-token and watts-per-token chip-to-grid workflow
How to reduce design cycles and maximize performance
Enable decision-grade digital twins across power, cooling, and layout before construction.
Secure, connected infrastructure integrates chip design, data center operations, and the grid.
The session concludes with a reference architecture and workflow for building AI factories that are faster to design, safer to operate, and measurably more efficient per token.

title: Al + Quantum: Unlocking the Next Era of Scientific and Industrial Breakthroughs (Presented by Quantinuum)
abstract:
The convergence of AI and quantum computing is not a distant vision-it's unfolding now. Generative Quantum AI (Gen QAI) is at the forefront of this shift, creating a feedback loop where quantum computers generate novel data that can enhance classical AI models, while AI systems learn from and guide quantum exploration. Quantinuum has been using the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform to bring these two technologies together.

Their convergence unlocks more than technical achievements-it redefines what's possible. In pharmaceuticals, it means accelerating drug discovery by exploring chemical spaces classical computing cannot reach. In materials science, it promises breakthroughs in superconductors and batteries that will transform energy and mobility. In finance and logistics, it opens the door to solving optimization problems that define competitiveness.
key_takeaways: Industries are beginning to build roadmaps that harness Quantinuum and NVIDIA's work in AI + quantum as a co-pilot for discovery, design, and decision-making.
The AI + quantum convergence is the defining trend to watch in the next decade.
Integrated hardware, software, and new developer tools are accelerating scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computing for real-world industries.

title: NVIDIA NeMo を活用した半導体領域知識を組み込む小規模 LLM のファインチューニング
abstract: 本セッションでは、半導体製造装置の立ち上げ・保守作業などの支援を目的としたLLMのチューニング方法をご紹介します。NVIDIAが公開しているオープンソースの合成ペルソナデータセットであるNemotron-Personas-USA, Nemotron-Personas-Japanと合成データ生成ツールであるNVIDIA NeMo Data Designerを活用した独自データセットの構築から、NVIDIA NeMoを用いたモデルチューニングまでのプロセスを紹介し、製造業におけるAI活用の可能性を探求します。
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: AIエージェントへの人格埋め込みを、Nemotron Personasで簡単にできる方法を紹介
NVIDIA NeMo Data Designerによる、独自データをもとにした合成データ生成方法を紹介
NVIDIA NeMo RL によるモデルのチューニング方法を紹介

speakers.name: Kenya Nishiguchi
speakers.title: データサイエンティスト
speakers.company: 東京エレクトロン株式会社

title: インタラクティブCFD最前線:Omniverseで体験するデジタルツインと都市シミュレーション
abstract:
本講演では、CFD(数値流体力学)シミュレーションの新たな可能性として、Ansys FluentとNVIDIA Omniverseを統合し、リアルタイムで流体解析結果を高精度かつ直感的に可視化する実践的手法を紹介します。従来のCFD解析は膨大な計算時間と静的な結果表示に制約されていましたが、GPUアクセラレーションとOmniverseの仮想環境を活用することで、エンジニアや研究者は没入型の3D空間でシミュレーションを対話的に操作し、流れ場の変化を瞬時に確認できます。これにより、設計検討や意思決定のスピードが飛躍的に向上し、複雑な流体現象をより深く理解することが可能になります。
さらに、物理ベースのCFD解析とデジタルツイン基盤を組み合わせることで、製品開発や都市計画におけるシナリオ検証が容易になり、複雑な流体挙動を直感的に共有できるため、協調設計やチーム間コミュニケーションの質が向上します。教育分野では、物理の専門知識を持たない学生が視覚化やインタラクティブな学習を通じて計算工学に親しむための効果的な導入戦略を提案し、コンピュータサイエンス教育における新しい学習モデルを提示します。
加えて、東京工科大学のDGX B200スーパーコンピュータ「青嵐」を用いたGPU加速CFDによる東京都区内大規模流体解析の事例を紹介します。都市スケールのシミュレーションでは、膨大なメッシュデータ、境界条件の設定、並列計算の最適化など、技術的課題が山積しています。本講演では、これらの課題を克服するための最新手法と、国土交通省が主導するプロジェクト、PLATEAUのデータを使った都市環境における風況解析を現実応用へ拡張する際の将来展望についても考察します。
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, Omniverse, Infiniband Networking, cuBLAS, Interconnect Networking, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Ansys FluentとNVIDIA Omniverseを統合し、ダイナミックな流体シミュレーションをリアルタイムで可視化する手法を習得します。これにより、エンジニアや研究者は没入型環境でCFD結果を対話的に探索できるようになります。
物理ベースのCFDシミュレーションとデジタルツインプラットフォームを組み合わせることで、意思決定の高度化、協調的なエンジニアリング、複雑な流体現象の直感的なコミュニケーションが可能になります。
物理学の背景を持たない学生が、視覚化やインタラクティブな学習を通じて計算工学に親しめるよう、物理ベースのシミュレーションをコンピュータサイエンス教育に取り入れるための戦略を紹介します。
東京工科大学のDGX B200スーパーコンピュータ「青嵐」を用いて、東京都区内の流体力学をシミュレーションする大規模GPU加速CFD実験の事例を学び、都市スケールのシミュレーションを現実応用へ拡張する際の技術的課題と将来の可能性を考察します。
