speakers.name: Harsha Padmanabha
speakers.title: Global VP, Product & Engineering - AI & User Experience

title: Agentic AI 101
abstract:
Agentic AI is evolving rapidly, shifting AI systems from passive responders to active systems that can plan, reason, and take action. In this session, we break down what agentic AI is, how the space has changed in the last year, and how both frontier models and open models are enabling a new class of AI applications.

You will learn the core components of agentic systems, where different model types fit, and practical ways to get started building agents today. This session is designed to give developers and technical leaders a clear mental model for agentic AI and a concrete path from experimentation to real applications.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Understand what agentic AI is, and how it differs from traditional chat-based systems.
Learn how recent advances in frontier models and open models are accelerating agent development.
Explore the core building blocks of agentic systems, including planning, tool use, memory, and evaluation.
Identify common use cases where agentic AI delivers the most value today.
Get started with practical steps, patterns, and tools for building your first agentic AI application.

speakers.name: Erik Pounds
speakers.title: Sr. Director, Product Marketing, Enterprise AI

title: Distributed Inference at Scale Using Dynamo
abstract:
NVIDIA Dynamo is a distributed inference serving framework built to run LLMs efficiently, reliably, and at massive scale. It helps AI providers reduce cost-per-token, meet strict service level objectives, and scale deployments using Kubernetes across large GPU clusters. Learn how Dynamo delivers high-performance distributed inference across frameworks such as SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, and vLLM. We'll explore key capabilities - including disaggregated serving, KV-cache-aware routing, topology-aware scheduling, and intelligent memory and cache management - that make Dynamo a powerful foundation for production workloads. Whether you're evaluating Dynamo, experimenting it, or deepening your understanding of its capabilities and real-world impact, join us to ask questions, see how the system works under the hood, and get practical guidance from the experts who build and deploy it.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Practical insights for deploying, tuning, and scaling LLMs in real-world environments, informed by production examples
A deeper understanding of advanced inference techniques such as disaggregated inference, KV caching to storage, and topology-aware scheduling
Clear guidance on the tools and recipes available to help you get up and running quickly with Dynamo using your preferred inference framework - SGLang, TensorRT-LLM , or vLLM

title: Master AI Infrastructure At Scale: A DevOps Playbook
abstract:
Your AI factory is ready for action-now it's time to put it to work.

In this session, two NVIDIA senior solutions architects will walk you through the essential steps for deploying and managing your AI infrastructure. Aiming at teams beginning their AI factory journey, we'll focus on real-world deployment practices and insights drawn from leading customer environments. Together, we'll discuss how to streamline cluster onboarding, configuration, and monitoring, laying the foundation for efficient and resilient operations from Day One.
topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, DGX Platform, HGX, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, Interconnect Networking, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Best practices for initializing HGX/DGX clusters and configuring storage, networking, and compute fabrics
Techniques to reduce mean time between failures and accelerate time-to-first-token
Deep dives into GB200/GB300 NVLink architecture, system management, and tooling advancements

title: Automating AI Factory Infrastructure (Ansible, GitOps, etc.)
abstract:
AI factories are hard, but automation can make life easier. Join us to get your questions answered about how to use industry standard and product-specific tools to automate the deployment and operation of your AI factory.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
nvidia_technology: DOCA, MGX, Base Command Manager, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control
key_takeaways: Automate Your AI Factory: Learn the best practices for using automation to simplify the deployment and maintenance of complex enterprise AI factories, making your life easier.
Get your questions answered about using industry-standard tools like Ansible, Terraform, and Git to implement robust infrastructure-as-code (IaC) for your AI factory infrastructure.
Understand how to effectively manage and control infrastructure change using proven IaC methodologies to ensure reliability and stability in your AI factory operations.

speakers.name: Scott Ellis
speakers.title: Sr. Director, Solutions Architecture and Engineering

title: Profiling Python and AI workloads with Nsight Compute
abstract:
Learn how to use the Nsight Compute tool to profile CUDA and AI GPU kernels in the Python framework of your choice: Numba, Triton or PyTorch. Understand the optimization workflows provided by NVIDIA Nsight Compute UI and its ncu command line interface. Use the new scoreboard dependency analysis view and source page improvements like opcode to metric pipeline associations. Learn how to use the Nsight Jupyterlab Extension for profiling and streaming reports directly in Jupyter. Try the latest Nsight Python decorators for profiling from within any Python script.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Intermediate knowledge of CUDA
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis
key_takeaways: How to profile CUDA and AI GPU kernels in various Python frameworks
How to use the Nsight Compute kernel optimization flow and guidance
How to use the Nsight Jupyterlab Extension and Nsight Python

title: Global Governance and the Future of AI Safety Regulation
abstract: The pace of AI regulations is increasing, and the complexity of complying with different laws is challenging for global companies. For example, EU member countries are passing "companion" regulations for the Artificial Intelligence Act. Meanwhile, the U.S. federal government hasn't adopted any AI regs, but many states have, and the current administration's focus differs from that of the previous administration. At the same time, Congress is focused primarily on child safety. How can companies comply with laws in such a fragmented environment?
nvidia_technology: Maxine, Audio2Face, BioNeMo, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Understand the current state of AI regulations and governance frameworks across different countries and regions.
Gain insights into the core ethical principles that underpin AI governance, and learn how these principles are being integrated into global AI governance structures and practices.
Stay updated on the latest advancements and future directions in AI safety research.

speakers.name: Minesh Tanna
speakers.title: Global AI Lead and Partner, Disputes & Investigations

title: Accelerating LLM Training and Inferencing With Reduced Precision Format
abstract:
Large-scale LLMs require enormous computational resources, making efficiency crucial for training. Learn how cutting-edge reduced-precision formats such as FP8, MXFP4 and NVFP4 can dramatically accelerate pre-training, post-training, and inference while maintaining accuracy close to full-precision baseline models. You'll learn how NVIDIA's innovative NVFP4 format better captures dynamic range and reduces quantization error compared to MXFP4. We'll cover techniques including Random Hadamard Transforms, two-dimensional block quantization, stochastic rounding, and strategic placement of higher-precision layers to stabilize training. 
Gain practical skills with the software tools necessary for quantization-aware training and efficient inference deployment, helping your applications retain accuracy while benefiting from reduced precision.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Understanding of LLM training and inference
Basic understandings of reduced precision formats
Python, docker and shell scripting skills
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, Hopper, CUDA-X, NeMo, NSight Systems, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Large-scale LLMs require tens to hundreds of petaflops for training, making computational efficiency critical.
Reduced-precision formats like FP8 and NVFP4 enable faster pre-training, post-training, and inference while maintaining full-precision accuracy.
Learn training techniques that enable stable low-bit training.
Gain hands-on experience with quantization-aware training and deploying efficient inference pipelines that preserve model accuracy.
Understand the principle of reduced precision formats and their necessity.

title: Powering Specialized Agents: Architecting Synthetic Data Pipelines with NVIDIA Data Designer
abstract:
Building a truly performant LLM agent means fine-tuning for your unique use-case. This requires training data that span multiple specialized domains or tools; for most real-world applications, the data you need simply don't exist.

This hands-on workshop leverages NVIDIA Data Designer, our recently open-sourced, state-of-the-art framework, to bridge this gap. We'll cover the latest techniques developed at NVIDIA for producing high-quality reasoning data, including STEM and tool-calling workflows used to scale Nemotron models.

By the end of this session, you will gain the skills to speed up data experimentation and drive innovation in your own AI projects.
 
Prerequisite(s):


Basic Python and Jupyter notebooks


Training or fine-tuning LLMs (conceptually)


Using datasets for supervised fine-tuning and evaluation (classification, generation, or instruction-following tasks)


Optional: prior exposure to NVIDIA NeMo, Hugging Face datasets/models, or other LLM fine-tuning stacks.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Master the fundamentals - synthetic data generation, governance, and best practices.
Learn NVIDIA's playbook - apply expert techniques for data design and experimentation used to scale state-of-the-art models like Nemotron.
Build with Open Source - Get hands-on with NVIDIA Data Designer to generate high-quality training datasets from scratch.

title: Healthcare Reimagined: Bridging Digital Intelligence and Physical Autonomy
abstract: Robotics and AI are redefining healthcare, accelerating diagnostics, surgery, patient care, and operational efficiency. We'll highlight how NVIDIA technologies drive this transformation, from physical AI systems that perform precision tasks and address workforce shortages to digital AI agents that interpret multimodal data, predict patient needs, and guide decision-making. We'll dive into the latest NVIDIA technology-spanning cutting-edge hardware, AI factories, and next-gen simulation-enabling both physical and digital robots. You'll see how these technologies unify sensing, perception, planning, control, and generative reasoning into deployable devices. You'll also learn how medical technology developers are building on NVIDIA's ecosystem of tools, reference workflows, and partner solutions to accelerate R&D, improve system intelligence, and bring autonomous capabilities to life.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, Isaac, Omniverse, Metropolis, Clara Holoscan, IGX, MONAI, Cosmos, DGX Cloud, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Learn about three computer frameworks enabling physical AI in healthcare.
Learn the most recent advancement in bridging sim-to-real, from digital + physical integration to advanced simulation.
fearn the newest development in synthetic data generation frameworks and pipelines to bridge data gaps.
Discover how NVIDIA's newest runtime hardware and software enable deterministic edge AI deployment with faster development cycles.
Explore NVIDIA Healthcare's most recent open-foundation models democratizing Gen AI development for medical applications.

speakers.name: Prerna Dogra
speakers.title: Director Product Management & Developer Ecosystem - MedTech

title: From Isolation to Integration: Evolving Confidential Computing for a Scalable, Secure Future
abstract:
Generative AI is rapidly becoming the defining workload of modern computing, but securing these powerful systems without compromising performance is still a challenge for most enterprises. This session dives into how you can lock down your most valuable AI assets-models, data, and prompts-while continuing to push the limits on speed, scale and insights.

Google and NVIDIA will share how confidential computing on NVIDIA GPUs can protect your data and model using accelerated computing, so your information stays shielded even while in use.
key_takeaways: Discover how Blackwell and Vera Rubin NVL72 unlock cutting-edge confidential computing for large-scale AI.
Learn how workloads can run securely in the cloud, on premises, or in hybrid environments to meet diverse enterprise needs.
Hear how real customers are already using NVIDIA GPU confidential computing to move critical AI projects into production with confidence.

speakers.name: Nelly Porter
speakers.title: Director of Product Management, Trusted Cloud

title: AI on the Front Lines of Healthcare: Unlock Clinical, Operational, and Diagnostic Value
abstract: Learn from the digital health software leaders on the front lines of the agentic and ambient AI revolution. Panelists from a crosscut of the digital health ecosystem will share how they are deploying cutting-edge agents to solve critical healthcare challenges, such as transforming physician-patient conversations into structured notes, crafting diagnostic reports in real time, or interacting with caregivers to answer questions and guide them through receiving care.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Gain practical insights from industry leaders on successfully integrating agentic AI across the digital health ecosystem to solve critical operational challenges.
Learn strategies for deploying AI interfaces that actively support hospitals, practitioners, and patients.
See how autonomous agents accelerate clinical decision-making and discover how ambient AI technology streamlines clinical workflows.

speakers.name: Paul Lipton
speakers.title: Lead Product Manager, Rad AI Reporting

title: Adding Safety and Perception to Industrial Robots
abstract:
Learn to implement functional safety for robots using an "outside-in" approach. You will gain deep insight into building collaborative, AI-powered factory environments where vision systems and robotics converge to deliver operational intelligence and safety.
We will connect industrial robots to factory-wide sensor infrastructure using NVIDIA Metropolis to build intelligent systems dedicated to robot safety. Through practical exercises, you will create a video analytics AI agent that extends a robot's real-time perception. 

This agent will enable critical safety features, such as automatic speed adjustments (slowing down or speeding up) and risk mitigation, essential for safe co-bot environments. This lab showcases how NVIDIA Halos for Outside-In Safety Agents, powered by NVIDIA Metropolis technology, enable an outside-in approach to achieving advanced functional safety.
 
Prerequisite(s):


Intermediate experience with Python, including packages, virtual environments, and scripting


Familiarity with computer vision concepts, object detection, and AI inference pipelines


Experience with robotics workflows and sensor integration


Knowledge of data streaming, real-time analytics, and message-passing architectures
key_takeaways: Build an outside-in safety application for robots using NVIDIA Metropolis vision AI-based workflows to integrate sensors, AI inference, and real-time dashboards to optimize robot perception and responsiveness.
Implement a safety AI agent with NVIDIA Halos that enables factory sensors and robot sensors to safeguard workers, robots, and warehouse assets.
Deploy and scale robot safety applications across factory environments for comprehensive proactive responses for factory robots leveraging existing camera infrastructure.

title: Building the Architecture of Advertising Intelligence: How GPU-Accelerated AI Agents are Reshaping Digital Advertising
abstract: Digital advertising now rivals high-frequency trading in both speed and complexity: tens of millions of auctions occur every second, each demanding a decision in under a millisecond. AI is transforming the industry as agents autonomously plan, negotiate, and execute media transactions in real time. This session details how PubMatic re-engineered its global infrastructure using NVIDIA-accelerated computing to power some of the world's first agentic advertising transactions, offering a blueprint for how GPU-driven intelligence is reshaping not just programmatic advertising, but real-time digital markets of every kind.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, RAPIDS, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), Triton, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Examine the accelerated-computing architecture that enables PubMatic to process 45 million bids per second with microsecond-level inferencing.
Learn how NVIDIA Triton Inference Servers, RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark, and GPUs were optimized for streaming, low-batch, high-frequency workloads unique to digital advertising.
Understand how this infrastructure enables autonomous AI agents to transact via emerging open protocols, enabling true machine-to-machine negotiation for media buying.
Learn how to apply these architectural principles to any real-time industry as agentic AI begins mediating customer journeys across retail, finance, healthcare, mobility, and more.

speakers.name: Vasu Cherlopalle
speakers.title: VP of Real-Time Bidding and Analytics

title: How HBM4 Unlocks Efficient LLM Serving at Scale (Presented by SK hynix)
abstract: Explore how next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM4) improves large language model serving by addressing two critical inference constraints: memory bandwidth and capacity. Through detailed profiling and controlled scaling studies across modern LLMs and reasoning- and agent-based workloads, we show how HBM4's substantially higher bandwidth and increased capacity translate into lower latency and higher throughput in LLM serving systems, and we project the performance gains production AI systems can expect from GPU platforms equipped with HBM4.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NSight Compute, NSight Systems
key_takeaways: Why memory bandwidth and capacity have become the primary performance constraints in modern LLM serving systems, especially for long-context, reasoning, and agent-based workloads
How HBM4 alleviates the "memory wall" in LLM inference by scaling both bandwidth and capacity, enabling lower latency and higher throughput in real-world serving scenarios
What recent advances in high-bandwidth memory technology and the future HBM roadmap mean for GPU platforms and large-scale AI deployment

title: Wireless Network Digital Twins: Use Cases for 5G and 6G
abstract: Wireless network digital twins are a critical building block for AI‐native networks, especially as next‐generation 6G systems will be born first in simulation. NVIDIA Aerial Omniverse Digital Twin (AODT) creates realistic, physics‐based replicas of wireless networks that reflect radio conditions, topology, and traffic patterns, allowing operators and partners to model, test, and optimize 5G and 6G safely before touching the live network. In this session, NVIDIA and partners will show how AODT supports workflows from network planning and optimization to validating new AI‐for‐RAN algorithms and agentic AI scenarios, where agents explore actions in the twin first. Speakers will share real use cases and how integrating AODT helps them create and run more intelligent networks today, and why digital twins are becoming the default way to build AI‐native wireless networks of the future.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Learn about the capabilities, use cases, ecosystem solutions, and a roadmap of NVIDIA Aerial Omniverse Digital Twin for enabling 5G and 6G development.
Discover how operators and partners use digital twins to plan networks, validate AI algorithms, and de-risk changes before they go into production.
Gain practical guidance on how to get started with AODT-from integrating it into existing processes to using it for simulations to improve network performance.

speakers.name: CC Chong
speakers.title: Sr. Director, Head of Aerial Product Management

title: Build an AI-Ready Data Center: Practical Insights From YTL, Wiwynn, and NVIDIA (Presented by Wiwynn Corp.)
abstract: This session reveals how YTL partnered with Wiwynn and NVIDIA to build an AI-ready data center. We'll walk through the NVIS deployment methodology-covering site qualification, high-density GPU rack integration, and power/cooling validation under real-world stress. You'll also learn how to implement end-to-end observability and manage liquid-cooling risks. Finally, we share benchmarking results using MLPerf and AI workloads to confirm the environment meets AI-ready standards.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, Mission Control, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Step-by-Step Checklist: Build or upgrade your data center with NVIS guideline for infrastructure integration, focusing on stable power and water delivery, optimized pod layout, and successful rack-level L11/L12 acceptance.

Mission Control in Action: Implement cluster-level and workload-level management via Slurm/K8s integration, extensive observability, and Wiwynn UMS-based leak detection directly integrated with DC operations.
Performance Benchmarking: Use NeMo Megatron and MLPerf Training to rigorously test and validate scalability, efficiency, and power consumption of your AI clusters.
Reusable Blueprint: A proven framework for operators and solution providers to build high-performing AI factories.

title: Architect for AI Breakthroughs: Co-Design Memory + Storage for What's Now and Next (Presented by Samsung Semiconductor)
abstract: As AI workloads evolve toward complex agentic reasoning and large-scale physical intelligence, the integration between compute and the memory-storage hierarchy has become vital. This session explores collaborative architectural approaches co-designed to support the transition to the NVIDIA Rubin platform powering AI factories, alongside platforms powering AI at the edge and physical AI. We will discuss the technical integration of HBM4, HBM4E, and SOCAMM2 within accelerated CPU-GPU clusters to optimize system-level throughput and thermal efficiency, while also examining the role of high-performance PCIe Gen6 storage in emerging inference context management frameworks. Join us to explore how these synchronized memory and storage layers provide the robust performance and reliability required for the current state and next frontier of AI.
key_takeaways: Analyze the technical integration of HBM4, HBM4E, and SOCAMM2 within Vera Rubin systems to maximize bandwidth and thermal efficiency.
Evaluate the impact of PCIe Gen6 storage on emerging inference context management frameworks for persistent agentic AI.
Explore memory design standards that provide the deterministic performance and reliability essential for AI at the edge and physical AI.

title: Performance Without Compromise: How Memory Innovation is Central to AI System Effectiveness (Presented by Micron)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is driving unprecedented computational demands that rely on massive data access at low latency. Learn how advanced semiconductor technologies-high-bandwidth memory, low-power DRAM, and next-generation storage-have become critical to meeting these computing challenges in AI systems. We will spotlight how Micron's innovations reduce power consumption in large-scale AI training and inference systems while delivering the bandwidth to advance next-generation generative AI.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, RTX GPU, Hopper, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Understand how data bandwidth directly relates to smarter, more capable AI.
See how AI-driven improvements are accelerating semiconductor manufacturing.
Gain insights into Micron's view of future memory technologies.

title: AI Literacy at Scale: K-to-Career Access That Delivers Real Student Outcomes
abstract:
AI literacy has moved from a "nice-to-have" to a readiness requirement. The open question is execution: how schools build access that scales, supports educators, and reaches students across different learning paths, geographies, and resources.

This panel brings together education leaders and practitioners shaping that execution from four angles: classroom and school implementation, learning science and workforce-relevant skill-building, platform-level delivery at scale, and institutional systems that connect education to long-term opportunity.

Together, the panel will share practical approaches to expand AI literacy and access without turning schools into pilot sites. You'll leave with a clear view of what to teach, how to equip educators, how to deploy tools responsibly, and how to build pathways that connect early literacy to real-world readiness over time.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Implementation Models that Scale: What works in real K-12 environments, and how to avoid one-off pilots
Teacher Enablement: How to support educators with curriculum, professional learning, and classroom-ready resources
Access and Reach: Approaches that broaden participation across districts, student populations, and learning contexts
Responsible Use in Schools: Practical guardrails for safe, appropriate AI use without freezing adoption
Pathways to Opportunity: How early AI literacy connects to future-ready skills and long-term workforce readiness

title: Teach AI to Code in Every Language With NVIDIA NeMo
abstract: "Teaching AI to Code in Every Language with NVIDIA NeMo" is a hands-on training lab for machine learning practitioners, data scientists, and developers who want to build state-of-the-art coding language models on NVIDIA's platform. Participants will learn how to use NVIDIA NeMo and NVIDIA GPU-accelerated infrastructure to train multilingual LLMs for code, from dataset preparation and cleaning to large-scale distributed training and optimization. The session will cover key challenges of multilingual code modeling, such as balancing languages, handling noisy repositories, and designing evaluation strategies that reflect real-world developer workflows. Attendees will implement practical techniques for scaling training across large compute environments, apply best practices for performance tuning, and explore how these models can power developer assistants, code review tools, and enterprise codebase management solutions. By the end of the lab, participants will have a clear roadmap to experiment with, train, and deploy advanced multilingual coding LLMs using NVIDIA's end-to-end ecosystem.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: A practical understanding of how to use NVIDIA NeMo and NVIDIA infrastructure to train state-of-the-art coding LLMs
Insights into dataset preparation, multilingual training challenges, and strategies to overcome them
Knowledge of optimization techniques and best practices for scaling training across large compute environments
Awareness of real-world applications for multilingual coding LLMs, from developer assistance to enterprise codebase management
A clear roadmap for experimenting with and deploying advanced coding LLMs using NVIDIA's technology stack

title: AI-Powered Huge Ensembles for Portfolio Climate Risk Management
abstract: Discover how large‐scale AI‐accelerated ensemble forecasting is reshaping the way we assess physical climate risk in financial portfolios. This session dives into practical, data‐driven methods for quantifying asset‐level climate exposure-bridging advanced climate modeling with real‐world financial and energy use cases. You'll learn how massive ensembles help uncover cross‐dependencies among temperature, wind, and solar patterns that drive volatility in energy commodities, grid stability, and portfolio performance under extreme conditions.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
key_takeaways: Explore how AI‐driven ensemble modeling strengthens portfolio resilience against physical climate risks such as extreme heat, flooding, and storms.
Discover techniques for enhancing global climate model outputs with AI analytics to generate high‐resolution, asset‐level insights.
Gain practical strategies to anticipate and manage energy market volatility by revealing renewable generation risks, tail events, and grid stability challenges through ensemble forecasting.
Enhance commodity trading risk management

title: Best Practices in Building and Packaging CUDA Python Libraries
abstract:
Packaging CUDA‐enabled Python code can seem daunting. With many build backends, Python wheels, conda packages, and CUDA compatibility rules in the mix, even experienced developers hesitate. The key is separating Python packaging complexity from CUDA‐specific challenges.

This session starts with a deep dive in build backends and packaging formats like wheels and conda packages, then moves into compiled extensions and how tools like pip, uv, and conda ensure broad compatibility. We'll explore CUDA‐accelerated packages from pure‐Python wrappers to custom CUDA C/C++ kernels, emphasizing practical decisions and best practices for reliable installs.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
key_takeaways: Learn to apply standard Python packaging best practices.
Identify when existing CUDA Python libraries (e.g., CUDA Python, CuPy, Numba, nvMath Python) let you ship CUDA support without introducing CUDA-specific packaging complexity.
Understand how CUDA versioning affects binary distribution when your package relies on CUDA runtime or driver APIs.
Recognize when owning custom CUDA code introduces additional decisions around GPU architectures, PTX and LTO-IR inclusion, and build matrix design-and how to manage that complexity responsibly.

title: Inspecting Multimodal Data with Nemotron and Gemma 3
abstract: This workshop introduces a multi-agent approach to solving complex, visual-heavy enterprise tasks. You will learn to use Google Gemma 3 as an agentic orchestrator to plan tasks, call tools, and leverage Nemotron for high-fidelity visual reasoning.
key_takeaways: Understanding of NVIDIA Stack on Google Cloud: Learn how NVIDIA's optimized software stack runs seamlessly on Google Cloud to power low-latency agents.
Diving into Nemotron Family of Models: Dive into Nemotron models that excel at agentic planning and tool calling.
Deploying Nemotron in Google Cloud: Learn how to connect Nemotron with Google's native ecosystem.

title: Advancing Autonomous Vehicles With World Models
abstract: Simulation remains a cornerstone for accelerating robotics and autonomous vehicle (AV) development, enabling policies to be evaluated ahead of real-world deployment and drastically reducing development time. Achieving meaningful results requires simulators that capture both the environment and the world's response to AI agents with high fidelity, across diverse and challenging scenarios. NVIDIA Cosmos has been specialized for autonomous vehicles, incorporating novel architecture changes and optimizations to deliver real-time simulation for the first time. In this talk, we explore the breakthrough that powers closed-loop evaluation of AV policies, empowering developers to iterate faster, test more rigorously, and bring safer, more robust autonomous systems to the real world.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
key_takeaways: Learn how Cosmos world models create high-fidelity, dynamic environments that mirror real-world complexity for autonomous vehicles.
See how real-time simulation allows developers to test AV policies interactively, accelerating iteration and reducing risk.
Discover techniques for translating simulation results to real-world performance, enabling safer and more robust autonomous systems.
Gain insights into optimizing architecture and tools for faster policy evaluation and deployment in production environments.

title: MLOps 101: Platforms and Processes for Building AI
abstract:
As AI adoption accelerates across every industry, teams are discovering that model quality alone is not quite enough to deliver real value. Scalable and reliable AI systems depend on robust MLOps that streamline the entire life cycle, from data to deployment. At the same time, the field is undergoing a major transformation driven by agentic AI, advanced reasoning, multi-modality, and new patterns for large-scale, disaggregated inference. 

This session introduces the foundational patterns of MLOps while also exploring how these new capabilities reshape AI design and operational strategy. We'll examine several use cases to break down the process and ideal platform for MLOps in this new era. Whether you're launching your first AI initiative or modernizing your infrastructure, this session provides the technical blueprint and best practices you need to operationalize AI at scale.
nvidia_technology: NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Learn how to build a comprehensive MLOps stack, the layers involved, and which concrete solutions work at each layer.
Understand the MLOps pipeline, including feature engineering workflows, experiment tracking, pipelines, inference, and packaging and deploying AI at scale.
Learn best practices you need to operationalize AI at scale.

title: Connect With Cybersecurity, Agentic AI, and Threat Hunter Experts
abstract:
Join NVIDIA cybersecurity and agentic AI experts to learn how NVIDIA technology and agentic systems can be applied to strengthen your cybersecurity posture.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI
key_takeaways: Explore best practices in defending LLM agents from attacks.
Ask our GPU Confidential Compute team about new solutions for data privacy and edge security.
Explore more with our Networking DPU team for the latest and greatest in NVIDIA solutions allowing unprecedented scale-out and data integration.
Learn how threat hunters are applying the latest innovations in cybersecurity to bolster detection and response.

title: Optimize Mixture-Of-Experts for Low-Latency Inference Decode on Blackwell
abstract: This ninja-level tutorial will guide you through the techniques to build a low-latency inference kernel for Blackwell using CUDA and PTX. You will learn about techniques like programmatic dependent launch, prologue minimization, lightweight CGA parallelism, and operand swapping in the Blackwell Tensor Cores.
key_takeaways: Learn about challenges in parallelizing small GEMMs, and how Blackwell hardware features help overcome them.
Learn about key features of the Blackwell Tensor Cores.
Learn about the benefits of warp-specialization and latency hiding.

title: Optimize Biologics Design Workflow With Auto-Tuned Models and Multi-Agent Systems
abstract:
Applying AI to antibody therapeutics often presents significant engineering challenges-particularly in effectively linking structure prediction, molecular generation, and property optimization into a cohesive pipeline. Learn how breakthrough AI frameworks can be leveraged to architect a zero-code antibody design and optimization workflow. We'll demonstrate how multi-agent capabilities enable intelligent interpretation of complex R&D requirements-from de novo generation to multi-objective optimization-without the need for manual coding.

We'll also share key learnings and identified bottlenecks while addressing the industry-wide "ADA prediction gap" through the construction of a high-fidelity immunogenicity simulation framework that models human immune responses, including MHC binding, APC presentation, and T-cell activation.
key_takeaways: Multi-Agent Workflows: Learn how to utilize the AI framework to build zero-code pipelines where autonomous agents intelligently manage antibody generation, structure prediction, and optimization tasks.
Foundation Model Optimization: Discover how automated fine-tuning strategies can unlock massive accuracy gains.
Clinical-Grade Immunogenicity Prediction: Explore a technical framework for simulating complex immune interactions that predicts clinical anti-drug antibody (ADA) rates with substantial accuracy.

title: Climate Prediction and Smooth Navigation Use Cases
abstract:
This session will introduce two use cases of NVIDIA platform adoption-one in climate prediction, and the other in smooth navigation.

Climate Prediction: Accurate climate prediction relies on capturing small-scale processes, such as clouds and turbulence, which remain elusive in traditional models. This talk presents the CliMA solution: a physics-AI hybrid Earth System Model built in Julia to run natively on NVIDIA GPUs. Leveraging GPU acceleration, researchers generate large ensembles to drive a "calibrate, emulate, sample" workflow. 

Generalized Navigation: the CUDA-accelerated Delta-Corridor framework utilizes Delta-Corridor spatial modeling to achieve reliable and smooth generalized navigation with Isaac and Cosmos. By applying continuous safety corridors, it ensures reliable navigation for mobile robots, boats, and autonomous vehicles in dynamic environments.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
key_takeaways: Learn from top researchers how they are using NVIDIA software platforms to drive scientific breakthroughs.
Understand how NVIDIA is engaging with academic researchers, and how to connect with the right people.
Hear success stories from researchers spanning domains such as climate & weather and robotics.
Understand how Academic Grant Program can enable research outcomes.

speakers.name: Tapio Schneider
speakers.title: Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering

title: Accelerate GPU Scientific Computing With nvmath-python
abstract: In 2024, NVIDIA unveiled nvmath-python, a library designed to bridge the gap between Python scientific community and NVIDIA CUDA-X math libraries, and it's now generally available. You'll learn what makes nvmath-python a useful addition for GPU-accelerated computing with Python, along with how to use the library's unique capabilities to reach extreme levels of performance to solve cutting-edge scientific problems previously unimaginable in Python.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries
key_takeaways: Learn how nvmath-python's just-in-time kernel fusion allows achieving high computational efficiency.

Get familiar how to leverage Python just-in-time compilers, along with nvmath-python, for writing sophisticated device kernels, from cooperative general matrix multiplication algorithms to fast Fourier kernels, floating-point emulation, and more.
Scale a single-GPU problem to very large ones with minimum code changes using nvmath-python's multi-GPU/multi-node capabilities.
Learn how the library interoperates with the rest of the Python scientific computing ecosystem for getting richest experience.

title: Bridging the Real-to-Sim Gap with 3D Gaussian Splatting
abstract:
Advances in 3D Gaussian-based reconstruction are transforming simulation for autonomous vehicles and robotics. This interactive session offers an opportunity to connect directly with experts on cutting-edge techniques for indoor and outdoor physical AI simulation. Learn how Gaussian splatting enables real-time rendering of complex, photorealistic environments with greater computational efficiency than traditional 3D reconstruction methods. Whether you're a researcher, developer, or industry professional, this session provides the technical grounding and networking opportunities needed to apply 3D Gaussian splatting in simulation pipelines that accelerate autonomy development.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Learn about the latest advances and technologies in 3D Gaussian-based simulation.
Walk through practical workflows to take real-world sensor data into simulation platforms such as Isaac Sim and CARLA.
Connect directly with experts on any questions related to implementing 3D Gaussian-based rendering in your development pipelines.

title: An Introduction to Newton Physics Engine for Robotics
abstract: Robots learn faster and safer in simulation, but complex joints, balance, and contact-rich motions challenge current physics engines. Developers need high-fidelity simulation so virtual training transfers safely to real robots. This session with NVIDIA, Google DeepMind, Skild AI, and Disney Research introduces Newton, an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics engine built on NVIDIA Warp and OpenUSD to advance robot learning. Newton's flexible design and multiple solvers enable realistic simulations-like walking through snow, or handling cups and fruit-and reliable deployment in the physical world. Explore Newton's architecture, integration into robot-learning pipelines, and ways to develop new solvers for safer, more capable robots across diverse environments and tasks.
key_takeaways: The importance of differentiable physics for robot simulation
How developers can couple multiple solves for training robot policies
How they can extend Newton to develop their own customer solver

title: Scaling Trustworthy Multi-Modal Video Intelligence: A Life Cycle-Aware Approach for Domain-Specific Real-World Platforms
abstract: This session will demonstrate how to move beyond basic spatial-temporal modeling and implement domain-specific video intelligence using techniques inspired by NVIDIA VSS to build robust, life cycle-aware platforms for real-world applications like brand safety and contextual advertising. Explore new solutions for accelerating large-scale video ingestion with modern multi-modal tooling like NVIDIA NeMo Curator, and discover strategies for achieving high-throughput inference through embedding-level optimization and evolving to next-generation, end-to-end validation frameworks. We'll present proven methodologies with customer success examples from enterprises like Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, and Kenvue to help you deploy enterprise-grade, trustworthy AI systems that analyze video content at scale.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Metropolis, NeMo, Blackwell, Blueprint, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Moving Beyond Spatial-Temporal Modeling with Domain-Based Video Intelligence: Learn how domain-specific cues, such as content life cycle patterns, real-world context, and viewer intent, enable deeper, more accurate video understanding necessary for applications like brand safety and contextual advertising.

Apply Multimodal Structuring Techniques Inspired by NVIDIA VSS: Explore how NVIDIA's Video Search & Summarization methodologies, such as multi-modal boundary detection, semantic grouping, and hierarchical summarization, can strengthen production pipelines designed to handle audio-rich, motion-poor, or text-centric video content.

Accelerating Large-Scale Video Ingestion With Modern Multimodal Tooling: See how integrating tools such as NVIDIA NeMo Curator supports efficient preprocessing, scene segmentation, and summary generation for high-volume enterprise video datasets.

Customer Success-Implementing Enterprise-Grade Brand Suitability: Learn proven methodologies for deploying AI systems that analyze video content at scale, with case studies demonstrating how Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, BYD, Burger King, and Kenvue achieved their campaign goals.

Achieving High-Throughput Inference Through Embedding-Level Optimization: Understand how PYLER standardized inference tasks and introduced embedding-level fast inference, allowing over 90% of decisions to be made without full model passes, significantly improving throughput and cost efficiency.

title: PMPP Edition 5 Unveiled: Ask Dr. Wen Mei and the CUDA Team Your Questions
session_type: Connect With the Experts, Q&A With NVIDIA Experts
abstract:
Join Dr. Wen-Mei Hwu and NVIDIA experts for a live Q&A exploring key updates in the CUDA ecosystem since the last edition of his book Programming Massively Parallel Processors (PMPP). This session offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with Wen-Mei and his team of CUDA engineers, who will answer technical questions, share new features introduced in PMPP Edition 5, and discuss emerging topics under consideration for the sixth edition.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, NSight Compute, NSight Systems, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Understand how Programming Massively Parallel Processors (PMPP) is used by both academia and industry, and learn what to expect from PMPP Edition 5 as it becomes available around GTC 2026.

Learn about new and updated content in PMPP Edition 5, including coverage of LLMs, advanced matrix multiplication, multi-GPU programming, filtering, wavefront algorithms, compute and memory architectures, and performance optimization, plus an updated code repository and new hands-on labs.
Gain insight into the future of parallel computing and get your technical questions answered directly by NVIDIA architects and engineers.

speakers.name: Wen-Mei Hwu
speakers.title: Sr. Distinguished Research Scientist
speakers.company: Senior Distinguished Research Scientist and Senior Research Director at NVIDIA

title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics-Part 1: Simulation-First Design and Accelerated Development
abstract: Join Part 1 of a two-part curated session on how simulation-first design is changing the way healthcare robots are built. You'll see how teams are using NVIDIA Physical AI-spanning world foundation models, physics-based simulation, synthetic data, and real-time sensor-to-action deployment-to move faster with less risk. This session focuses on how simulation and digital twins are being used in practice to speed up development, test decisions earlier, and optimize systems long before they reach the operating room
key_takeaways: How Simulation-First Design Accelerates Healthcare Robotic Development: Learn how teams use digital twins, physics-based environments, and generative simulation to rapidly design, test, and refine robotic systems before they touch the real world.

How World Foundation Models and Synthetic Data Expand Capability and Coverage: See how specialized foundation models and SDG pipelines create rich scenario diversity, improve training efficiency, and strengthen early-stage algorithm performance.

How Developers are Building Next-Generation Devices With NVIDIA Physical AI: Hear from industry leaders using NVIDIA's simulation and modeling technologies to optimize planning, validate concepts, and shorten product development cycles.

title: Enhance Data Center Efficiency and Throughput by Co-Scheduling on NVIDIA CPU+GPU Systems
abstract: Coherent NVIDIA CPU-GPU systems provide a versatile platform to support a wide spectrum of workloads. However not all jobs require all capabilities at scale. Co-scheduling CPU-only, partially accelerated, and fully GPU-accelerated jobs on NVIDIA CPU-GPU nodes can boost throughput and energy efficiency, removing the need for multiple dedicated clusters. We discuss isolation mechanisms including CPU affinity via cgroups, cache and memory bandwidth partitioning through ARM's MPAM extensions, and InfiniBand QoS for network traffic separation. Learn to identify co-schedule candidates and assess scheduling policy design options and incentives for user adoption. We'll present workload studies aligned with NERSC's upcoming Doudna system, demonstrating projected improvements in utilization and efficiency while highlighting open challenges in enabling fine-grained resource sharing.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Unified Cluster Architecture: The need for separate CPU and GPU clusters can be eliminated by running CPU-only and GPU-accelerated workloads concurrently on a single co-scheduled Grace+GPU cluster.

Maximize Resource Utilization: Co-scheduling jobs with dissimilar resource requirements on the same nodes takes advantage of hardware resources (e.g., CPU cores) that might be left unused by a single job.

Performance Isolation: A combination of ARM's Memory Partitioning and Monitoring (MPAM), InfiniBand Quality of Service (QoS) and Linux cgroups enable fine-grained management of CPU memory capacity and bandwidth, CPU cache capacity and bandwidth, and network bandwidth to ensure performance isolation among co-scheduled jobs.
HPC System Efficiency: Our data demonstrate that co-scheduling improves both job throughput and energy efficiency.

Case Study: An analysis of jobs running at the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC) reveals that this complex scientific computing and AI workload is highly amenable to co-scheduling.

title: Decoding Quantum Errors With AI
abstract: Learn the basics of quantum error correction (QEC) and why decoding is a primary bottleneck for quantum computing. Using hands-on exercises, learn about key decoder metrics like accuracy, scalability, throughput, and latency and their implications for quantum computation. Lastly, implement your own AI decoder workflow using the CUDA-Q QEC library. We assume that you're familiar with the fundamentals of quantum computing (gates, circuits, measurement, etc.), but do not necessarily have experience with QEC.
key_takeaways: The basics of quantum error correction and the importance of decoding
Key metrics for assessing the practical aspects of decoder performance
How to use CUDA-Q QEC to implement a custom AI decoder model

speakers.name: Monica Van Dieren
speakers.title: Sr. Technical Marketing Engineer, Quantum and HPC

title: AI Performance and Inference Economics for Engineers
abstract: This technical deep dive analyzes the cost drivers for AI systems, focusing on the interdependent relationship between total cost of ownership (TCO) and performance. We will show how the influence of extreme co-design, spanning silicon, software, and the rack-scale system, unlocks new efficiency levers. The session will detail how these integrated optimizations fundamentally improve operational metrics like cost per token and token per watt in modern deployments.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Hopper, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Understand rack-scale performance/TCO drivers and influence of networking innovations like NVLink 6.
Learn strategies to minimize cost per token by deploying low-precision silicon and software-stack optimizations like wideEP, Dynamo, Sparsity, and NVFP4.
Understand the influence of long-context, agentic, and deep reasoning on inference economics and possible optimizations.
Discover how the different levers in our co-designed stack bring efficiency and optimize inference.

title: Large-Scale JAX Training With Pipeline Parallelism
abstract: We'll give an overview of the first practical system for enabling pipeline parallelism (PP) for JAX on NVIDIA GPUs. JaxPP accepts simple annotations on the training step functions, and then will introduce Multiple Programs operating on Multiple Data (MPMD). This system is a compiler that works on top of JAX and its XLA compiler. We will give an introduction to pipeline parallelism and how it works on training. We'll also describe our experience of using JaxPP effectively on Cohere's training models.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: Pipeline parallelism is an important method for distributed training and inference.
JAX for GPUs can support it out of the box without changes to underlying compiler.
JaxPP is a tool for PP on a large distributed cluster of GPUs.

title: High Performance AI-RAN Made Simple; Python to CUDA Signal Processing at the Speed of Light
abstract:
The Aerial Framework has been designed from the ground up to meet the needs of 3GPP radio access networks (RANs)-signal processing workloads with microsecond latency requirements. It is a platform that unites research, test beds, and production deployments to solve development challenges for real-time applications.  

The session will give a hands-on tutorial to design algorithms in JAX/Torch and instantly deploy to real-time GPU pipelines with inline networking.  

GitHub: https://github.com/NVIDIA/aerial-framework
key_takeaways: Show why Aerial Framework exists, who it is for, and what it can do.
Show how to leverage the CUDA-X ecosystem to prove it is possible to accelerate research in Python to real-time applications, demonstrate AI-native workflows, and demonstrate algorithm profiling.
Show what is coming next for Aerial Framework.

title: Close the Sim-to-Real Gap: Next-Gen Techniques for Synthetic Data Mastery (Presented by World Wide Technology)
abstract: Stop relying on limited real-world datasets. Join us to explore proven techniques to leverage NVIDIA Omniverse Replicator and NVIDIA Cosmos to create synthetic imagery for building robust computer vision models that recognize rare objects and challenging conditions. We will dive deep into synthetic imagery best practices, the NVIDIA hardware and software stacks that accelerate the process, and the critical decision points that maximize your synthetic data return on investment and accelerate your path to production-ready physical AI.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: Understand the decisive value of synthetic data augmentation for balancing datasets and increasing the performance of computer vision models on rare events.

Learn how we've leveraged synthetic imagery to help our customers across many industries, ranging from world-class entertainment venues to international utilities leaders and national manufacturing corporations.
Discover the best practices for synthetic imagery generation using NVIDIA Omniverse Replicator and NVIDIA Cosmos, focusing on maximizing domain randomization and minimizing the sim-to-real gap.
Explore how to identify when synthetic data is the appropriate solution and, critically, when a different training approach should be considered.
See the NVIDIA hardware necessary to accelerate synthetic data generation with NVIDIA Omniverse Replicator, including NVIDIA's RTX-capable GPUs.

speakers.name: Paige Reiter
speakers.title: Technical Solutions Architect II, Cloud and Infrastructure

speakers.name: Wes Long
speakers.title: Technical Solutions Architect II, Cloud and Infrastructure

title: The Physics of Long-Context Inference: Breaking the Memory Wall With NVIDIA Dynamo (Presented by VAST Data)
abstract:
Long-context AI conversations create a "memory wall" where GPU high-bandwidth memory becomes a bottleneck for scaling inference during agentic AI. This session shares a practical study of how NVIDIA Dynamo's KV Block Manager (KVBM) accelerates large scale inference.

We'll examine Dynamo's four-tiered architecture (G1-G4) that places data in the right tier at the right time to balance higher concurrency, throughput, and cost. We'll show how VAST Data as the G3 tier achieves maximum memory and storage efficiency to eliminate the memory wall for long-running agents. Finally we'll dive into the new NVIDIA Inference Context Management Storage platform, which provides a new class of AI-native storage infrastructure designed for gigascale inference. 

You'll leave with blueprints for architecting secure, persistent memory for the next generation of agentic AI.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, DGX Platform, HGX, DOCA, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, Magnum IO, MGX, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Dynamo, NIXL
key_takeaways: A Practical Study of Intelligent Offloading: Deep dive into the architecture of the KVBM to understand how it intelligently batches asynchronous writes and demands bulk synchronous reads, enabling GPUs to break through memory limits without expensive recompute operations.

The Arithmetic of "Infinite" Context: Learn the formula for accurately sizing infrastructure for large-scale inference deployments. We break down the relationship between concurrent users, context window size, and retention multipliers to plan for the massive storage footprint required by long-running agents.
Achieving Line-Rate Throughput: Review real-world data where VAST saturates 200Gbps links at ~99% line rate during retrieval, ensuring maximal efficiency and a 20x improvement in time-to-first-token.
Governing the "KV Cache Era": Address new data management challenges. We discuss best practices for encryption and handling sensitive user information contained within cached prompt history.

Establish an Optimized Context Memory Tier: Augment existing networked object and file storage by holding latency‐sensitive, reusable inference context and prestaging it to increase GPU utilization. Get AI-native data services while enabling 5x higher tokens-per-second with 5x more power efficiency compared to traditional storage.

title: Unlock Sovereign AI With Confidential RAG and Secure Inference for Regulated Data
abstract:
In this session, we showcase a real-world national deployment that enables confidential retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and AI inference on protected data-without exposing those data to cloud operators, infrastructure administrators, model providers, or third-party vendors.

Learn how NVIDIA Confidential Computing, along with the combined technology from OPAQUE's Confidential AI Platform and the TII, the applied research arm of the Advanced Technology Research Council (ATRC), establishes hardware-backed trusted execution environments that deliver cryptographic guarantees of data confidentiality, integrity, and verifiable governance across the entire AI life cycle. This architecture enables real-time AI inference and analytics directly on encrypted data, unlocking high-value AI use cases while preserving zero-trust security boundaries.
key_takeaways: Industries across the public and private sectors face a fundamental challenge with AI: extracting value from highly sensitive, regulated data while meeting strict privacy, security, and data-sovereignty requirements.
Learn how to securely deploy AI to deliver world-class insights and deliver better outcomes across public health, financial services, and other industries that leverage sensitive, private data.
Learn how to build cloud-scale confidential RAG and inference pipelines while maintaining complete control over sensitive data.
Enhance outcomes through improved delivery of public services, while securely enabling research into regional population data while preserving privacy across the data pipeline.

Understand the end-to-end architecture supporting sovereign large language models and domain-specific AI. Organizations retain full control of their encryption keys while securely collaborating on shared AI workloads, and cryptographic audit trails provide provable compliance for regulators and security teams.

title: Building Sovereign AI Systems Under Real-World Constraints (Presented by Radiant)
abstract: This session examines sovereign AI as a systems engineering challenge that emerges from the interaction of architecture, control planes, operations, and life-cycle ownership. Drawing on real-world experience designing large-scale AI infrastructure under persistent supply and operational constraints, the talk focuses on the long-term consequences of foundational design decisions and how they shape autonomy, resilience, and adaptability over the life of an AI system.
key_takeaways: Understand why sovereign AI cannot be reduced to geography, vendor choice, or configuration, and how sovereignty instead emerges from coherent systems design.
Learn which architectural decisions have the greatest long-term impact on autonomy and resilience, and which have the ability to evolve under hardware, power, and supply constraints.
Explore the role and limits of configuration in achieving sovereignty, and where configuration decisions matter most.
Examine how software and control-plane design bind infrastructure together, enabling operational control, life-cycle ownership, and adaptation as constraints change.
Gain a multidimensional framework for evaluating sovereignty, spanning jurisdiction, architecture, operations, and control, rather than any single technical lever.

title: Accelerate the AI Factory With NVIDIA-Certified Systems
abstract: NVIDIA-Certified Systems provide the essential foundation for the modern AI factory, offering performance-optimized hardware validated to run demanding workloads from the desktop to the data center and edge. This session explores how the program's rigorous testing-now including NVIDIA-Certified Storage for massive data demands-ensures enterprise-grade reliability, security, and scalability for generative AI and industrial digitalization. Join us to learn how these certified servers, workstations, and edge systems provide a trusted, best-practice pathway for enterprises to confidently deploy accelerated computing at scale.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Grace CPU, HGX, CUDA, Omniverse, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, CUDA-X, Interconnect Networking, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Cosmos, Blueprint, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control, Dynamo
key_takeaways: Trusted Hardware Foundation: NVIDIA-Certified Systems offer a validated pathway for enterprises to confidently choose hardware that is performance-optimized for accelerated computing workloads, from the desktop to the data center.

Comprehensive Performance Validation: Every certified system undergoes rigorous testing-including single-GPU, multi-GPU, and multi-node scenarios-to ensure it can handle demanding real-world applications like generative AI and HPC.

Expansion into Enterprise Storage: The program now includes NVIDIA-Certified Storage to meet the massive data demands of AI factories, ensuring high-performance and reliable data solutions for faster model training and inference.

Optimized for the Full Stack: These systems are specifically designed and tuned to run the NVIDIA AI Enterprise software platform, including NVIDIA NIM microservices, to streamline the deployment of production-grade AI.

Scalable and Secure Infrastructure: Certification ensures that systems support essential enterprise features like remote management, TPM security, and high-speed networking to enable seamless scaling as AI needs grow.

speakers.name: Steve Stein
speakers.title: Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Data Center Platforms

title: Deploy an AI Concierge for Every Customer at Global Scale
abstract: Learn how to design and deploy AI systems that operate directly in customer-facing scenarios, where accuracy, latency, and trust are critical. This session dives into the technical patterns, safeguards, and evaluation techniques needed to prevent hallucinations, support real-time interactions, and deliver AI experiences that feel natural and reliable in production. Drawing from real deployment experience, we share the system design principles, evaluation strategies, and safeguards required to operate AI on the front lines.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Understand the unique technical challenges of deploying AI in customer-facing, real-time environments.
Learn design patterns and safeguards that reduce hallucinations and enforce accuracy at scale.
Apply system architectures that balance low latency with reliability and consistency in production.
Evaluate customer-facing AI using metrics that reflect trust, correctness, and real task success.
Design AI interactions that feel natural to users while remaining controlled, predictable, and debuggable.

title: How NVIDIA Can Help You, as a Researcher, Accelerate Scientific Discovery
abstract:
Join this engaging session led by NVIDIA experts who collaborate closely with the global academic research community, supporting leading researchers in leveraging NVIDIA software technologies to drive scientific advancements across diverse fields. We'll provide insights into how various NVIDIA teams, representing different functional areas, can assist in accelerating your scientific discoveries at multiple levels. The team will outline their approach to working with individual researchers and groups, share success stories, and introduce a range of developer support programs, including the Academic Grant Program, DLI courses and teaching kits, etc. We'll also focus on aligning discussions with specific research domains such as robotics, biology, Gen AI, data processing, and climate science-while showcasing tools and resources available to help researchers enhance research outcomes.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
nvidia_technology: CUDA, DeepStream, Morpheus, TensorRT, Clara, Isaac, Omniverse, RAPIDS, BioNeMo, Clara Holoscan, CUDA-X, cuDDN, cuDF, cuML, cuOPT, Data Science Workbench, JetPack, MONAI, NSight Compute, NSight Graphics, NSight Systems, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Cosmos
key_takeaways: Learn how NVIDIA helps the academic research community adopt NVIDIA software technologies.
Learn how different teams at NVIDIA can assist in accelerating your scientific discoveries on multiple levels.
Learn about various supporting programs NVIDIA offers today for research communities.
Learn about various frameworks and tools that that can help researchers to accelerate their research outcomes across diverse fields of science.
Learn how to empower your lab quickly in AI knowledge and beyond.

speakers.name: Judit Szulagyi
speakers.title: Director of Higher Education and Research (EMEA)

speakers.name: Chelsea Cropper
