title: Harness the Potential and Navigate the Challenges of Agentic AI Across Colleges and Universities Worldwide
abstract:
Discover how to design, deploy, and scale agentic AI systems that transform research acceleration, student support, and campus operations while navigating governance, safety, and infrastructure challenges unique to higher education. Join NVIDIA's Higher Education and Research team to explore proven architectures, deployment patterns, and integration strategies that help you move from experimentation to production-ready AI agents across your institution.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Understand what agentic AI is and where it delivers the most value in higher education and research-accelerating literature reviews, lab workflows, student services, accessibility, IT support, and campus operations.

Learn the NVIDIA reference stack and patterns to build safely: NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NeMo and NIM microservices, Triton/TensorRT for optimized inference, RAG with vector databases, and Guardrails-plus how to integrate agents with LMS/SIS/LIMS and HPC resources.

Be introduced to deployment blueprints from prototype to production: agent orchestration and tool use, connectors to institutional data, monitoring and telemetry, GPU scheduling and cost control (on‐premises, cloud, or hybrid), and performance tuning.

Leave with actionable next steps: quickstart labs, sample workflows and case studies, success metrics and ROI frameworks, and pathways to NVIDIA academic programs and resources to accelerate adoption.

speakers.name: Zahra Ronaghi
speakers.title: Sr. Manager, HER and Gen AI Solutions

title: Measuring Brand, Fame, and Virality Using Spatio-Temporal Causal Graphs
abstract:
We introduce a GPU-accelerated system for decomposing engagement into Brand (persistent equity), Fame (spatial reach), and Virality (temporal dynamics). Standard analysis cannot separate these effects because they are mathematically entangled: observing any two determines the third. We address this by using spatio-temporal causal graphs that explicitly encode influence structure, with CUDA kernels for CSR propagation and distributed Shapley computation across GPU clusters.
Built on an Online Evolving Spike Neural Network distributed pipeline, the system uses variance-optimal stratified sampling for coalition evaluation and processes 100,000+ marketing time series at daily resolution via custom CUDA kernels and C++ simulation bridges. The framework enables teams to identify whether observed variation is driven by content quality, audience segmentation, or timing effects-separating forces that traditional attribution methods conflate. This turns engagement decomposition into actionable budget allocation grounded in large-scale causal inference.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Learn how spiking neural networks and entropy-based estimators identify rare events and directional influence in high-dimensional time-series data.
Explore GPU-accelerated approaches for building and analyzing spatio-temporal causal graphs using CUDA-X and cuGraph.
Understand how multi-exaflop compute on NVL72 SuperPODs enables the evaluation of every causal edge across billions of events in near-real time.
Dive into practical methods for running large-scale counterfactual simulations to model alternative futures and system behaviors.
See real-world applications of causal modeling in domains like marketing, finance, and operations, where correlation-based tools are lagging.

title: Workflow Revolution: How Doudna Drives HPC, AI, and Quantum Scientific Impact
abstract: This presentation will focus on how scientists can leverage the Doudna supercomputer, to be deployed in 2027 at the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC), to manage and execute complex workflows, particularly those involving data sourced from external scientific experiments. We'll explore the system's architecture, including the integration of Rubin GPUs, and detail how Doudna's advanced workflow-centric capabilities accelerate science. We will discuss the relevance of these workflows for cutting-edge fields such as AI, modeling and simulation, and quantum computing, and how these new workloads enhance throughput and support of novel scientific discoveries. Gain insight into what goes into designing an HPC system to support these diverse workflows.
nvidia_technology: Grace CPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Clara Holoscan, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, NeMo, CUDA Quantum
key_takeaways: Overview of leveraging Doudna at NERSC for complex, data-driven workflows from external scientific experiments
Understanding of Doudna's architecture, networking, GPU configuration for driving diverse workflows for scientific discovery
Guidance for supporting science users with new workflows and real-world examples of scientific impact

title: Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs
abstract:
Struggling to fully utilize GPU memory bandwidth on modern architectures? In this interactive session, NVIDIA engineers will work with you in real time to diagnose and optimize memory-bound kernels. Bring your own code, performance traces, or ideas and get hands-on guidance on loop unrolling, vectorization, prefetching, and asynchronous Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) pipelines. Learn to recognize common pitfalls, explore TMA patterns, and apply producer-consumer pipelines to maximize throughput. Walk away with practical, actionable solutions tailored to your workloads.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Get direct guidance from NVIDIA engineers on why your memory-bound kernels may not be saturating bandwidth on modern GPUs, and how architectural trends affect your performance limits.
Learn which optimization techniques-unrolling, vectorization, prefetching, and TMA-best fit your specific workload, with personalized advice on applying them.
Bring your own kernels or performance traces and walk through concrete steps to diagnose bottlenecks and move toward peak bandwidth.
Understand how to avoid common pitfalls in your own code, including loop peeling, misalignment, too-small kernels, and tricky TMA corner cases.

title: Don't Leave Tensors on the Table: Programming and Optimizing Tensor Cores
abstract: Explore practical techniques for maximizing Tensor Core performance in your CUDA kernels. We'll discuss CUTLASS-based coding patterns to unlock efficient pipeline utilization and streamlined data management. Together, we'll use Nsight Compute to analyze, optimize, and fine-tune your workflows for optimal Tensor Core performance.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis
key_takeaways: Discover the fundamentals of Tensor Cores on NVIDIA hardware, and learn how to harness their power using the CUTLASS library.
Gain practical skills in Nsight Compute for profiling and optimizing your CUDA kernels for Tensor Cores.
This talk is part of an ongoing series about optimizing CUDA kernel performance with Nsight Compute.

title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics-Part 2: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap and Real-World Deployment
abstract: Join Part 2 of this two-part curated session on what it takes to bring simulation-trained robotics into the real world. This session looks at how teams are translating behaviors developed in simulation into reliable clinical performance. We'll cover practical approaches to closing the sim-to-real gap, along with the real challenges of deploying safe, robust, low-latency sensor-to-action systems in live healthcare environments.
key_takeaways: How simulation and generative models accelerate readiness for real-world robotic behavior
Key sim-to-real challenges-variability, robustness, safety-and how leading teams address them
What it takes to deploy reliable, real-time perception and control systems in clinical environments

title: GPU Access to Unbounded Dataset Sizes Breaks Open Document Ingestion and Search
abstract: Vector databases with 1 trillion vectors cannot fit in memory, and VecDB ingestion and search requires the massive concurrency, memory bandwidth, and latency tolerance of GPUs. Come see new programming models, new storage server reference designs, new SSD offerings, and new customer-oriented scaled results.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, DGX Platform, HGX, CUDA, DOCA, RAPIDS, Infiniband Networking, Magnum IO, MGX, cuGraph, NSight Systems, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell, cuVS, Dynamo
key_takeaways: With a new SCADA API and infra, GPUs can effectively use O(100K) threads, HBM bandwidth, and latency tolerance to access storage much more effectively than CPUs.
Industry partners in Storage-Next are adding high-IOPs, power-efficient SKUs to their roadmaps.
We've launched cuGraph.WholeGraph and cuVS products based on SCADA GA that enable work on problems orders of magnitude bigger than possible before, with more than 2x better total cost of ownership.
Customer proofs of concept show tangible benefits for document ingestion and search.

title: Interactive Digital Twin for Fusion With Integrated Sensors and Agentic AI
abstract: The session will describe the development of the digital twin for the DIII-D Experimental Fusion Reactor. The talk will describe the development of the AI models and live sensor data integrated with the CAD data to develop an interactive digital twin.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, Clara Holoscan, NSight Systems, Omniverse Replicator
key_takeaways: Digital twins offer a new tool to help the community achieve commercial fusion energy.
AI/ML models are critical to enable interactivity with high fidelity.
The integration of live sensor data allows the digital models to be tested relative to the physical asset operation.
Omniverse provides a superior set of capabilities to allow for the development of the highly coupled digital twin.

title: Optimize NVFP4 Training and Inference at Scale Without Losing Quality
abstract:
NVFP4 and MXFP8 are the new numerical formats in Blackwell to significantly speed up LLM training and inference versus previous formats based on FP8, BF16, or FP16. In this session, we'll discuss how to achieve this speedup without compromising the stability of the training and model accuracy. We'll bring experts with deep knowledge on both the hardware and the software side, so that you learn how to enable NVFP4 and MXFP8 on your GPU to reduce memory footprint and accelerate training and inference.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Learn how lower-precision formats such as NVFP4 and MXFP8 compare to FP8, BF16, or FP16 to significantly speed up LLM training and inference.
Learn how NVIDIA training technologies such as TransformerEngine and Megatron-Core enable NVFP4 and MXFP8 stable training.
Learn how NVIDIA inference stack with Model Optimizer and TensorRT-LLM enable NVFP4 quantization without quality degradation.

title: Standardize Gen AI Service Evaluation: An API-Centric Benchmarking Approach
abstract: Evaluating Gen AI services across heterogeneous environments presents significant visibility gaps for developers, as traditional hardware-centric benchmarks often fail to reflect production-level API performance and accuracy. This session introduces MLPerf Endpoints, an open-source framework by MLCommons designed to address these challenges through transparent, reproducible, and vendor-neutral benchmarking. By shifting the evaluative focus to the API layer, this methodology enables developers to conduct iterative, on-demand measurements that mirror real-world user experiences and infrastructure optimizations. You'll analyze early cross-provider results and explore visualization tools designed to drive data-driven decisions in AI procurement and system design.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, AGX, CUDA, TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Data Science Workbench, Interconnect Networking, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, DGX Cloud, Dynamo
key_takeaways: Methodological Framework: Analyze the MLPerf Endpoints architectural framework and its role in defining standardized performance targets for production Gen AI.
Comparative Analysis: Evaluate initial cross-provider benchmarks using visualization tools to identify optimal price-performance and accuracy trade-offs.
Ecosystem Integration: Engage with the open-source MLCommons ecosystem to help define the technical roadmap for vendor-neutral measurement standards.

title: How AI Systems Evolve Into Long-Horizon Agents
abstract: Learn how an AI system can evolve from simple search into long-horizon agents that run for hours or days to complete complex, real-world tasks. This session walks through four stages of that evolution-AI search, asynchronous workflows, agents, and long-horizon agents-and examines the architectural patterns and technical trade-offs required at each step. We will cover practical approaches we learned building Genspark, including model routing, tool execution frameworks, validation of agent trajectories, and reliability strategies needed to operate agent systems at scale.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Understand the system evolution from AI search to asynchronous workflows, agents, and long-horizon agents.
Learn the architectural patterns and technical trade-offs required at each stage of building agent systems.
Apply practical techniques for model routing and tool execution in multi-step, long-running tasks.
Identify common failure modes in long-horizon agents, including partial execution and compounding errors.
Design reliability and validation strategies that keep agent trajectories on track at scale.

title: Operationalizing AI at Scale: NVIDIA's End‐to‐End Journey to an Enterprise AI Factory
abstract: Learn how NVIDIA's IT organization engineered an on‐premises AI factory to deliver agentic AI with enterprise‐grade security, reliability, and governance. We'll walk through the end-to-end technical stack behind NVIDIA's internal AI platform, from our on-prem enterprise AI factory infrastructure to containerized agentic applications. This includes scalable inference, secure enterprise data access, agent development and evaluation, standardized agent interaction, memory, runtime execution, and data flywheels for continuous improvement. You'll see how NVIDIA has deployed AI agents for the chip design, IT, supply chain, and developer and employee productivity domains.
key_takeaways: What key decisions will drive selection of components for an enterprise AI factory
How AI factory design is informed by use cases
How to measure success in AI delivery so you can address gaps and double-down on key value drivers
How to design a staged process that qualifies use cases from proof of concept to full-scale production, while minimizing risk
What skill sets are needed within an IT team

title: Life Sciences: Scale Multi‐Agent for Accelerated Clinical Operation Systems With NVIDIA and AWS (Presented by Cognizant)
abstract: Discover how life sciences leaders are transforming enterprise‐scale clinical operations through multi‐agent AI systems built on NVIDIA's accelerated computing and AWS' cloud‐native intelligence. This session brings AbbVie and Clario to the stage to share how organizations can strategically modernize workflows, unify data and automation platforms, and unlock new operating leverage-positioning their enterprises for faster innovation, responsible AI adoption, and measurable business impact.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: MONAI, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn how life sciences enterprises can accelerate clinical operations by adopting scalable multi‐agent AI architectures powered by NVIDIA's accelerated compute and AWS's cloud‐native ecosystem.
Understand the strategic considerations AbbVie and Clario applied to unify data, automation, and decisioning into a next‐generation enterprise AI platform.
Discover how executive alignment, platform governance, and strategic funding pathways (AWS + NVIDIA) enable faster AI adoption and measurable operational impact.
Gain insight into how multi‐agent systems elevate productivity across clinical, imaging, and operations teams through secure, governed, and enterprise‐ready deployment patterns.
Explore how organizations can modernize their technology foundations to drive future‐ready, AI‐enabled operating models at scale.

title: Private AI: Run Modern Models On-Prem Without Moving Sensitive Data (Presented by Cirrascale)
abstract: Explore why more enterprises are shifting AI workloads closer to protect sensitive company data and how to operationalize Private AI without sacrificing performance. In this session, we'll break down the real-world drivers behind on-prem and private deployments (data sovereignty, IP protection, regulated workloads, and risk reduction), then walk through a practical blueprint for running modern models with full control of data. Attendees will leave with a clear framework for model selection, secure deployment design, and operational controls so teams can confidently deploy high-performance inference in private environments while keeping sensitive data in place and minimizing exposure.
key_takeaways: Evaluate when on-prem private AI is the right choice, versus public endpoints.
Discuss large-scale use cases in a variety of industries.
Design a high-performance inference architecture using NVIDIA GPU-accelerated infrastructure that keeps sensitive data in place.
Operationalize private AI with enterprise-grade practices that help keep production stable.
Scale from pilot to production by standardizing repeatable patterns.

title: Making AI Agents Explainable for Manufacturing at Scale With Dataiku and NVIDIA (Presented by Dataiku)
abstract: As manufacturers accelerate toward autonomous AI agents to reduce costly unplanned downtime, trust, transparency, and safety have become the critical blockers to adoption in high-stakes environments. This session shows how Dataiku and NVIDIA, together with the Puffin Inspector framework from Dataiku's Open Source Lab, make AI-driven scheduling explainable, governed, and ready for real-world industrial orchestration today.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
key_takeaways: How the open-source Puffin Inspector framework enables transparent, auditable decisions from AI agents
How Dataiku LLM Mesh and NVIDIA Nemotron power scalable, production-grade maintenance scheduling assistants
How to embed governance, safety guardrails, and explainability directly into AI-driven operations

title: Make AI Factories Production-Ready: Inference, Networking, and DPUs (Presented by F5, Inc.)
abstract:
As AI factories move from experimentation to production, teams quickly learn that GPU performance alone does not determine inference capacity, latency, or user experience. Inference workloads produce long-lived, bursty, stateful traffic that strains traditional networking and security architectures, reducing token throughput, increasing time-to-first-token, and introducing instability at scale.

This session explores how AI factories built on NVIDIA accelerated computing benefit from BlueField DPU-accelerated networking and cloud-native traffic management. We examine the unique demands of AI inference, how NVIDIA DPUs offload and isolate critical data paths, and how inference-aware traffic control improves performance, reduces latency, and enables consistent multi-tenant inference services in production environments.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, DOCA, Ethernet Networking, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Understand why AI inference behaves differently than traditional application traffic.
Learn how DPUs support scalable, secure AI factory architectures.
See how inference-aware networking improves GPU efficiency and predictability.
Find out how distributed traffic management enables scalable inference across the AI grid.

title: Get NVIDIA-Certified at GTC for Free: Associate Certification
abstract:
Register separately to book an exam slot and take your chosen exam onsite. 

(Exam registration link coming soon)

Maximize your GTC experience by earning an industry-recognized Associate NVIDIA certification at no additional cost. Conference attendees can register to take an Associate-level certification exam onsite -choosing from Generative AI LLMs, Gen AI Multimodal, Data Science, and AI Infrastructure and Operations.
Advance registration is required to book an exam slot. We recommend preparing in advance using official NVIDIA study guides and the on-demand certification overview session which covers exam format, topics, and test-day tips.

Don't miss this opportunity to validate your skills and stand out in the fast-growing fields of AI and accelerated computing-at no cost during GTC.

title: Hands-On With Earth-2: Building Local and National Weather Resilience With AI
abstract:
We explore the life cycle of AI-based high-resolution regional weather forecasting applications, which are used by businesses and governments to achieve independent local weather and climate prediction capabilities at high accuracy and unprecedented speed. We'll start by creating a custom high-resolution model, guiding you through preparing a training dataset, training a downscaling diffusion model with NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo, and packaging it for real-time use. We then use NVIDIA Earth2Studio to integrate our custom model with a global AI-based weather forecasting model, such as FourCastNet 3, to build a full regional forecasting workflow, enabling sharper and more actionable local forecasts. We'll then discuss leveraging custom weather observations to improve the accuracy of the predictions, and deploying the workflow in operations so it can be productized as an inference service.

Prerequisite(s):

Previous DLI: https://learn.nvidia.com/courses/course-detail?course_id=course-v1:DLI+S-FX-31+V1
Basic Python and Deep learning experience.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
key_takeaways: Learn the principles of downscaling and nowcasting, why they are important for independent weather and climate forecasting capabilities, and how AI is revolutionizing these fields.
Prepare training datasets for efficient regional high-resolution AI weather forecasting.
Gain hands-on experience with using PhysicsNeMo to train a regional forecasting and downscaling models based on generative AI.
Package the trained model for inferencing and build an interface for it in Earth2Studio.
Combine the regional model with a global forecasting model such as FourCastNet 3 to build and deploy a regional forecasting pipeline.

title: Advance World Simulation With 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Environment Reconstruction
abstract:
Learn how to reconstruct a large scene for robotics testing using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec Gaussian-based reconstruction technologies to perform multi-GPU training, object segmentation, and extraction. This lab will walk through core concepts with a step-by-step workflow for data capture, reconstruction, and object level-integration for robotics simulation in NVIDIA Isaac Lab.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Omniverse NuRec, Isaac Sim / Lab
key_takeaways: Learn how to reconstruct a large scene with multiple GPUs to test robots using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec, fVDB, and Isaac Sim.
Understand core reconstruction and rendering technologies with hands-on experimentation with object segmentation.
Learn how to utilize data collection to train 3D Gaussian splats, extract 3D objects, and convert to USD for robotics simulation in Isaac Lab.

title: Train and Deploy Contact-Rich Robot Manipulation Skills With Isaac Lab and Isaac ROS
abstract:
Learn how to develop contact‐rich manipulation policies in Isaac Lab, and deploy them using NVIDIA Isaac ROS. In this hands‐on lab, you will walk through a gear-assembly workflow that uses Isaac Lab to train an insertion policy, and then deploy it in ROS 2, together with perception models (segmentation and 3D pose estimation) and cuMotion for GPU‐accelerated planning. You'll see how to configure an Isaac for Manipulation workflow that begins with a simulated UR robot and depth cameras for training the policy, and then tune to achieve robust, repeatable sim‐to‐real performance on contact‐rich tasks.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Basic understanding of ROS (Robot Operating System).
Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).
key_takeaways: Use Isaac Lab to train contact-rich manipulation policies.
Use Isaac ROS for high‐performance perception and manipulation packages and pipelines in ROS 2.
Deploy an Isaac for Manipulation gear‐insertion policy in simulation.
Plan and execute motions with CUDA‐X cuMotion for fast, collision‐aware manipulation.
Learn how to achieve sim-to-real zero shot deployment independent of the runtime framework.

title: How to use NVIDIA Warp to Build GPU-Accelerated Computational Physics Simulations
abstract: Discover how NVIDIA Warp enables the next generation of GPU-accelerated physics, geometry processing, and differentiable programming. This training lab introduces Warp's core capabilities and modules, then moves into a hands-on notebook where participants build a high-performance physics solver entirely in Python. This is demonstrated through a practical computational fluid dynamics example based on a 2-D Navier-Stokes formulation. You'll leave with an understanding of what Warp is designed for, how its core building blocks work, and how it can be used alongside deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and JAX in modern computational engineering workflows.
key_takeaways: Unified Python Framework for Physics and AI: NVIDIA Warp enables developers to build GPU‐accelerated physics solvers directly in Python while integrating smoothly with deep learning, bridging computational physics, geometry processing, and deep learning.
Interoperability with DL Frameworks: Learn how Warp complements frameworks such as PyTorch and JAX while interoperating with them through zero-copy data sharing and gradient exchange.
Warp Tile-Based Primitives: Gain hands-on experience using Warp's tile-based programming model for various matrix operations, making GPU programming more accessible.

Rapid Development of High‐Performance Solvers: The lab will demonstrate how Warp's modular, Python-native, and expressive design allows fast prototyping of physics solvers, using 2-D Navier Stokes solver as an example.

title: Accelerate Video Frame Extraction and Labeling
abstract:
Learn how to reduce the cost associated with frame extraction for computer vision post-processing using free and publicly-accessible American Sign Language data.  Use a novel, open-source framework and contribute to the world's largest dataset.
 
Prerequisite(s):

Have GitHub Account
Understand Python and Poetry
Have AWS Command Line Interface Configured (for S3 Operations)
key_takeaways: Access and use a novel and open-source pipeline for extracting and processing key frames and landmarks.
Build using Super Annotate's integrated environment to visualize, manage, and label data.
Contribute to the world's largest dataset for American Sign language.

title: Train and Test End-to-End Autonomous Vehicles with Alpamayo
abstract: This workshop will help you get started with the Alpamayo suite of models, frameworks, and data for building reasoning-based autonomous vehicles. Learn how to run neural reconstructions on the AlpaSim simulator with the NVIDIA Physical AI Open Datasets and connect it to the Alpamayo 1 end-to-end stack. By the end of the session, you'll have hands-on experience running neural simulations and will be more familiar working with end‐to‐end stacks.
key_takeaways: Gain hands‐on experience running a neural reconstruction simulation in AlpaSim.
Learn how to integrate AlpaSim with the Alpamayo 1 end-to-end stack.
Practice manipulating agent positions and trajectories within the simulation environment.

title: Liquid Cooling Readiness for Vera-Rubin Reference Architecures (Presented by nVent)
abstract: Vera-Rubin deployments extend the performance envelope of direct-to-chip liquid cooling while remaining compatible with the same core liquid-cooling architecture used for Grace-Blackwell systems. This session defines what it means to be "liquid-cooling ready" for NVIDIA SuperPOD reference architectures by examining coolant distribution unit (CDU) and technology cooling system (TCS) network design across Grace-Blackwell and Vera-Rubin platforms. A properly designed TCS manifold with common CDU and rack-drop interfaces enables scalability through CDU count and rack-drop configuration, allowing systems to scale from GB200 to VR300 without changes to the underlying TCS architecture.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Defining "liquid-cooling readiness" through scalable CDU deployment and a properly designed TCS manifold from Blackwell to Rubin for NVL72 reference architectures
Defining fault domains and the CDU as the IT system boundary
Power architecture: different levels of redundancy and their effects on the power resiliency of the overall SuperPOD support
Covering the importance of multi-point controls, monitoring, and integration with the CDU and the facility BMS

title: Designing Modular Infrastructure for the AI Factory Era (Presented by Intelliflex)
abstract:
AI workloads are scaling faster than traditional data center models were designed to support, particularly as power density requirements increase and expansion timelines compress.

In this session, we explore the infrastructure requirements of AI factories-including power density, modular deployment approaches, and direct liquid cooling readiness-and examine architectural strategies that enable phased scalability while avoiding the constraints of retrofitting legacy, low-density facilities for accelerated computing at scale.
key_takeaways: Recognize why traditional data center models struggle to support the power density and deployment pace required by modern AI workloads.
Understand how modular infrastructure architectures enable phased scalability without the constraints of legacy facility designs.
Evaluate the role of direct liquid cooling readiness as a core requirement for GPU-dense, accelerated computing environments.
Identify the risks and limitations associated with retrofitting existing low-density data centers for AI factory workloads.
Apply infrastructure design principles that align power, cooling, and scalability with the needs of AI factories and accelerated computing.

title: How AI-Infused Platforms Redefine Modernization, Delivery, and Value (Presented by Wipro)
abstract: In the AI era, competitiveness will be determined not by how fast enterprises modernize once, but by how effectively they institutionalize change itself. AI platforms illustrate this shift by productizing modernization intelligence, embedding AI across the software life cycle, and enabling enterprises to reduce the cost of change while accelerating AI readiness.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Omniverse, LaunchPad, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Learn how executives must break the project mindset and adopt platforms that make modernization sustainable and AI at scale a reality.

Recognize that AI won't deliver exponential returns if it's layered on top of a fragmented core. This isn't about faster delivery, it's about fundamentally changing how change flows through the enterprise.

Understand that only when modernization becomes a platform capability will enterprises steadily pay down legacy debt, ensure AI-readiness, and move beyond pilot theater to AI that genuinely reshapes how the business runs.

speakers.name: Sorabh Singhal
speakers.title: Global Service Line Head of Industry Cloud and Digital Business

title: From Smart Factory to Lights-Out Factory: Operationalizing Industrial AI With LTTS and NVIDIA (Presented by LTTS)
abstract:
Manufacturing is advancing automation, yet factories still depend on human input for planning, quality, and operations. The next step is autonomous, self-optimizing factories powered by industrial AI.

L&T Technology Services (LTTS) introduces its Lights-Out Factory (LoF) solution, built on NVIDIA's Industrial AI platform, Omniverse, Isaac, and CUDA-accelerated AI. LoF integrates digital twins, robotics, and AI-driven decision-making into a closed-loop system, enabling factories to move from assisted automation to autonomy.

This session shows how LTTS combines manufacturing expertise with NVIDIA's AI to create learning factories. Discover how virtual twins simulate workflows, AI adapts to real-time conditions, and robotics improve yield, safety, and throughput. Real-world results include reduced cycle times, improved yields, and faster ramp-ups, with a roadmap for scaling LoF deployments
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, PhysX, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: How Lights-Out Factory moves beyond traditional automation to autonomous manufacturing
Reference architecture for industrial AI using NVIDIA Omniverse, Isaac, and accelerated AI
Role of digital twins in enabling closed-loop, self-optimizing factory operations
Practical steps to move from proof-of-concept to production-scale Industrial AI

speakers.name: Umashankar KA
speakers.title: Senior Architect, AI Digital Twin Solutions

title: Get NVIDIA-Certified at GTC for Free: Professional Certification
abstract:
Register separately to book an exam slot and take your chosen exam onsite. 
(Exam registration link coming soon)

Maximize your GTC experience by earning an industry-recognized Associate NVIDIA certification at no additional cost. Conference attendees can register to take an Associate-level certification exam onsite -choosing from Generative AI LLMs, Gen AI Multimodal, Data Science, and AI Infrastructure and Operations.
Advance registration is required to book an exam slot. We recommend preparing in advance using official NVIDIA study guides and the on-demand certification overview session which covers exam format, topics, and test-day tips.

Please note:

Adding this session to your calendar is a reminder only and does not reserve an exam seat
You must click here to register separately to secure an exam slot
Exam seating is limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis
You will have 2 hours to take the exam
If your preferred time is full, walk-in slots may be available

Don't miss this opportunity to validate your skills and stand out in the fast-growing fields of AI and accelerated computing-at no cost during GTC.

title: IGX and Jetson: Autonomous Robots and Physical AI Applications
abstract: NVIDIA IGX and Jetson are the world's leading computing platform for AI at the edge. It offers high-performance compute and advanced NVIDIA AI software stack, as well as an extensive ecosystem to help you create and take products to market. Join with NVIDIA engineers and experts to ask your questions and join discussion on topics ranging from hardware design to system software, from the latest AI models to application frameworks like Metropolis, Isaac, and Holoscan.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, Isaac, Metropolis, IGX, JetPack
key_takeaways: Developer Journey on Jetson Thor and IGX Thor
System design and AI model performance at the edge
Demystify application development on IGX and Jetson.

title: Solve the Day 1 Challenge of Landing LLM Inference in Enterprise
abstract: Alibaba Cloud ACK RBG is a management tool for the full life cycle of large model inference deployment on Kubernetes, designed to address the challenges of large-scale deployment and operations of prefill-decode (PD) disaggregation architecture within Kubernetes clusters. It not only provides features such as multi-role management/collaboration, auto service discovery, topology-aware placement, elastic scaling, and atomic rollout/failure recovery, but also tightly integrates with NVIDIA Dynamo's AIConfigurator and Planner to achieve performance profiling and smarter scaling in complex PD disaggregation and mixture-of-experts (MoE) scenarios. This aims to further improve inference efficiency and reduce costs while meeting service level agreement (SLA) requirements, representing one of the effective ways for large-scale practical deployment of NVIDIA Dynamo.
key_takeaways: Understand key technical challenges of landing LLM inference service in real enterprise production environment. Share the case study and best practice of how Alibaba Cloud help clients to deploy LLM inference services in Kubernetes at scale, and manage those with high performance and efficiency.

Learn to use SGLang community project - RoleBasedGroup (RBG) - to manage the entire life cycle of Dynamo inference service just as easy as deploy, rolling update, failure auto-recover, monitor and orchestrate micro services.

Integrate RBG with Dynamo AIConfigurator and Planner to autoscale the prefill-decode disaggregated and MoE inference instances based on model specific metrics to continuously balance the serving SLA (TTFT, TPOT) requirement and cost.
Leverage Mooncake, Fluid such distributed cache technologies to reduce large model service cold start time 10X.
Extend Kubernetes Gateway API (GIE) with prefix cache and KVCache awareness and LoRA affinity routing policy to reduce LLM serving latency over 40%, increase cache-hit 15%.

title: How to Build End‐to‐End Physical AI Systems for Humanoid Robots
abstract:
Physical AI brings intelligence to machines, industrial arms, autonomous mobile robots (AMRs), and humanoids, enabling them to perceive, reason, and act in the real world in real time. These robots go beyond scripted behavior to make intelligent decisions on the fly: moving materials, assembling parts, and collaborating safely with people in dynamic environments.

In this session, experts will walk through practical, end-to-end workflows for physical AI development using NVIDIA Isaac and Omniverse technologies. You'll learn workflows spanning data collection and curation, digital twin creation, model training and scaling, high-fidelity simulation, and deployment to real hardware. We'll highlight proven toolchains and real-world examples that developers can apply to accelerate the path to smarter, more adaptable robots.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, Isaac, Omniverse, OVX, CUDA-X, Blackwell, Cosmos, DGX Cloud, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: End-to-End Physical AI Pipeline: Building intelligent robots requires integrated workflows-from data collection and curation to model training, simulation, and deployment on real hardware.
Digital Twins for Scalable Development: High‐fidelity digital twins enable safe, repeatable, and large‐scale training and validation of robot behaviors before real-world deployment.

Accelerated by NVIDIA Isaac and Omniverse: These platforms provide the toolchains for simulation, synthetic data generation, and deployment, reducing the gap between research prototypes and production‐ready robots.

title: Hardware-Efficient and Lossless Video Generation at Scale: Native Sparse Attention and Mixed-Low-Bit Quantization
abstract: We present a unified, hardware-aware framework for scaling large video generation models-such as Diffusion Transformers (DiT)-without compromising visual fidelity. Learn how production-grade model compression techniques jointly eliminate the quadratic cost bottlenecks that traditionally limit long-horizon video modeling. The session covers domain-specialized sparse attention for video sequences, robust low-precision training pipelines including low-bit attention mechanism, and mixed-low-bit inference strategies alongside adaptive sparse attention. With industrial-scale results demonstrating 2x end-to-end acceleration from sparse attention and 60-70% savings via low-bit computation-all with near-zero accuracy loss-this talk provides a practical blueprint for building efficient, lossless, and deployable video generative models at scale.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation
key_takeaways: Learn how domain-specific sparsification and supervised token selection reduce attention cost while preserving visual quality for ultra-long video sequences.

Understand how MXFP8/INT8 quantization, smooth K/V transforms, randomized Hadamard transforms, and stochastic rounding jointly eliminate quantization bias and stabilize training in attention mechanism.

Gain practical guidance for deploying video generation models using mixed-low-bit (e.g., FP4/FP8) kernels and lossless quantization workflows, and how ScalingAttention choose the optimal sparsity pattern for each workload, achieving maximum acceleration at minimal cost to fidelity.

title: Parameterized CUDA Graph Launch in PyTorch: CUDA Graphs Without the Pain
abstract: Modern GPUs have gotten so fast that CPUs cannot launch new work to the GPUs fast enough, which reduces overall efficiency. CUDA Graphs can eliminate CPU overhead completely, but have traditionally been hard to use, so let's break down the problems and fixes for them, focusing on PyTorch in particular.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks
key_takeaways: CUDA Graphs are hard to use because launching them depends upon global state, but CUDA Graphs don't know this state on their own. The user must track this manually, which is error-prone.
Global state errors can be classified into several categories: consistency problems between device and host, use-after-free errors, uncaptured work, and stale memory buffers.
Debugging tools in PyTorch built on CUPTI and the CUDA API can be used to identify several of these errors.
Users can be relieved of thinking about these problems if they make their model work with torch.compile(), via a feature called "parameterized CUDA graph launch."

title: Ask the Experts: Applications Built for Scale With cuPyNumeric
abstract:
Want to understand how Python scales efficiently across GPUs and clusters? Join NVIDIA experts for a live, classroom-style Q&A on cuPyNumeric, the technology that extends NumPy to distributed GPU environments. In this session, you'll have the opportunity to ask questions and discuss how cuPyNumeric maps familiar array operations onto distributed memory layouts, task schedules, and communication patterns.
 
Our engineers will walk through real-world examples from the Linac Coherent Light Source, the Vera Rubin Observatory, and Australia National University, illustrating how these teams built and tuned distributed GPU applications from prototype to production. Bring your questions and dive deep into the engineering that bridges Python productivity with raw GPU performance.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Hopper, cuBLAS, cuFFT, Blackwell, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Understand the Architecture: See how cuPyNumeric translates NumPy operations into distributed memory partitions, task scheduling, and inter-GPU communication.
Learn from Real Systems: Explore engineering lessons from large-scale deployments, from I/O bottlenecks to optimizing data locality and overlapping communication.
Engineer for Scale, Not Rewrite: Discover how to extend single-GPU Python code into high-performance distributed applications without abandoning familiar NumPy patterns.

title: AI Coding for the GPU: Build a Coding Agent to Help GPU Developers Write Speed-of-Light Code
abstract: Discover the evolution of AI-assisted development for accelerated computing as we move beyond general-purpose coding assistants to specialized agents designed for high-performance CUDA optimization. While frontier models have improved at syntax correctness, they often lack the domain-specific context required to utilize the latest hardware features and achieve "speed of light" performance. This session details NVIDIA's "CUDA Intelligence" ecosystem-a transition from simple retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines to advanced agentic workflows using the model context protocol (MCP) and agentic orchestration. We will demonstrate how we are democratizing state-of-the-art code transformation by integrating cloud profiling, Nsight tools, and expert-level optimization skills into a constellation of AI services that can run locally or in the cloud.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: CUDA, cuBLAS, CUDA-X, cuDDN, CV-CUDA, NSight Compute, NSight Systems, CUDA Quantum, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, CUDA-Q, DGX Cloud, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Understand why general-purpose LLMs struggle with high-performance CUDA code, and how NVIDIA bridges this gap by injecting proprietary knowledge of new technologies (e.g., CUDA Tile) and libraries.
Learn how the architecture is evolving from naive RAG to sophisticated agent orchestration using MCP services and reasoning models.
Explore how "CUDA Intelligence" utilizes cloud profiling and micro-profiling reinforcement learning to not just generate code, but iteratively optimize it for memory footprint and execution speed.

See how AI inference services are being built as foundational layers across the Nsight ecosystem to create an expertise flywheel that turns profiling data and expert workflows into actionable code improvements.
Gain insights into deploying these capabilities via NVIDIA NIMs or local blueprints (e.g., on DGX Spark), ensuring data privacy while maintaining access to state-of-the-art reasoning capabilities.

speakers.name: Matt Frazier
speakers.title: Director Software Engineering, AI Technologies for Developer Tools

title: Authorship Reimagined: AI, Art, and the Future of Creative Expression
abstract: A new era of storytelling is unfolding, where artists and intelligent systems co-create, challenge tradition, and push the boundaries of expression. This panel brings together leaders at the intersection of art, education, and technology, including the creative visionaries shaping the next frontier of cinema, design, and digital experience.  From generative animation tools built for Hollywood to AI frameworks informing visual storytelling, these panelists are not asking whether AI belongs in the creative process but how to wield it with intention, imagination, and integrity. Together, they will explore what authorship means when machines become part of the medium, how education is evolving to prepare tomorrow's creators, and why protecting creative agency matters more than ever in an AI-powered world.  This is a conversation about design and disruption, culture and code, where the future of art is not automated but amplified.
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, Audio2Face, CUDA-X, DALI, RTX Virtual Workstations (vWS), Video Codec SDK, Video Storage Toolkit (VST), NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Understand how artists and designers are using AI not simply to automate tasks, but to expand the boundaries of creative thinking, form, and process.
Explore how generative technologies are challenging traditional ideas of authorship, originality, and ownership in the arts-and what this means for creators and institutions.
Learn how schools like California College of the Arts are adapting curricula and pedagogies to prepare the next generation of artists and designers to engage critically and intentionally with AI.
See how artistic perspectives are influencing the design and application of AI systems, offering new ways to think about ethics, aesthetics, and human-machine collaboration.
Gain insight into how AI is being used to preserve, remix, and reimagine cultural narratives-while raising important questions about voice, agency, and representation in digital media.

speakers.name: Louis Stewart
speakers.title: Head of Strategic Initiatives, Developer Ecosystem

title: Build, Optimize, Run: The Developer's Guide to Local Gen AI on NVIDIA RTX AI PCs
abstract:
The AI PC ecosystem is exploding. Developers are now running local, high-performance AI workloads. This technical session dives into how NVIDIA accelerates the top open-source software stack-from the different quantization techniques, training recipes, as well as inference frameworks and tools like Ollama, ComfyUI and custom pipelines. 

Crucially, we will move beyond simple inference to address the architecture of reliable, local agentic workflows. Join us to learn about the technical intricacies and considerations to develop robust, local AI workflows on NVIDIA RTX.
key_takeaways: Overview of different quantization schemes for generative AI models
Maximizing inferencing performance on RTX AI PC with top tools and frameworks
Building generative AI workflows for Agentic AI and Creator Workflows

title: Accelerate Your AI Infrastructure at Scale
abstract:
This session will be an extension to the session led by NVIDIA senior solutions architect Kristof De Brouwer, titled "Mastering AI Infrastructure At Scale - a DevOps Playbook." You can ask questions about your scaled AI deployments of our technical experts, explore deployment strategies in depth, and engage in discussions on optimizing NVIDIA AI infrastructure for your specific environments.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA's products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, HGX, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, Hopper, Interconnect Networking, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Best practices for initializing HGX/DGX clusters and configuring storage, networking, and compute fabrics
Techniques to reduce mean time between failures and accelerate time-to-first-token
Deep dives into GB200/GB300 NVLink architecture, system management, and tooling advancements

title: Top-K Selection at the Speed of Light
abstract: Explore new techniques for top-k selection, a critical operation for accelerating mixture of experts and scientific computing that efficiently extracts the most relevant items from massive datasets. Dive into the design of our state-of-the-art GPU algorithm, AIR Top-K (now centralized in CUB), and learn how leveraging these primitives can accelerate high-throughput workloads by over 30x compared to previous methods.
key_takeaways: Discover the AIR Top-K algorithm design, which utilizes kernel fusion and adaptive buffering to maximize throughput on the GPU.
Learn how to leverage cub::DeviceTopK to deploy optimized, device-wide ranking in your applications without the need for custom kernel maintenance.
Examine real-world case studies demonstrating over 30x speedups in high-throughput workflows.
Gain insights into the broader applicability of top-k algorithms for critical workloads, including mixture of experts.

title: Build a Software-Defined Multi-Tenant NVLinked Cluster
abstract:
AI workloads are scaling into full production for training and inference. Standing up these clusters for R&D is challenging on its own, but production brings complexity: stricter security requirements, operational rigor, and a broader user base demanding stability, flexibility, and mature features.

The solution is a single, enterprise-grade, software-defined NVL GPU cluster built to dynamically adapt to changing workloads, support rapid deployment, and simplify life-cycle management.

In this talk, we'll examine considerations across the infrastructure, platform, and software layers. We'll look at network automation, virtualization, and different orchestration platforms. We'll focus with a concrete example based on our reference architecture, integrating Run:ai, Base Command Manager, vCluster, and Netris to create a secure, scalable, production-ready multi-tenant cluster.
topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, NVIDIA Run:ai, Mission Control
key_takeaways: What are the different layers  of consideration when building a multi-tenant GPU cluster?
What are the different security and feature considerations at these different layers of the stack?
How do I make platforms like Kubernetes or NVIDIA Run:ai multi-tenant?
What is an example stack I can deploy to get a fully multi-tenant deployment in my private cloud?

title: What's in Your Developer Toolbox? CUDA, AI, and HPC Profiling, Optimization, and Debugging Tools
abstract:
Several experts from the tools development and management teams will be available to talk about getting started, best practices, and advanced techniques for application debugging and optimization with NVIDIA developer tools. You can ask questions about anything from high-level concepts to specific tools details, and also discuss plans and suggestions for improving the tools in the future. We can provide a brief overview of the NVIDIA Nsight family of design, profiling, and debugging tools, including an introduction to the latest features and copilot integrations for developing CUDA compute, AI, and HPC applications on the newest platforms.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis
key_takeaways: Best-known methods for profiling and debugging AI, CUDA, and graphics workloads
Tips and tricks for identifying performance issues using built-in tools expertise
Learn how to extend and customize tool functionality with recipes, scripts, and cloud features

speakers.name: Rafael Campana
speakers.title: Sr. Engineering Director of CUDA Developer Tools

speakers.name: Matt Frazier
speakers.title: Director Software Engineering, AI Technologies for Developer Tools

title: Break Communication Barriers: Scale AI and HPC with NCCL, NIXL, and NVSHMEM
abstract:
Learn how to eliminate communication bottlenecks with NVIDIA Communication stack. Over the past year, each library has evolved to address specific bottlenecks: NCCL introduced Symmetric Memory support and Copy Engine collectives, enabling zero-SM communication. NVSHMEM enhanced GPU-initiated operations in the Python ecosystem. NIXL emerged as the inference-focused solution, providing async, noncontiguous transfers across memory tiers (GPU HBM, CPU DRAM, local/networked storage) with backends including GDS, UCX, and S3-essential for disaggregated inference architectures.

Explore developer optimizations: custom collectives in NCCL, KV cache and storage innovations in NIXL, and expert parallelism patterns. Learn how Spectrum-X plugins extend capabilities across multi-site deployments for scale-across connectivity.
nvidia_technology: CUDA, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, Blackwell, NIXL
key_takeaways: Learn how to build application-specific collectives with NCCL and overlap communication with computation. Tight fusion of compute and communication eliminates bottlenecks and maximizes GPU utilization.

Leverage NIXL's asynchronous transfers to move KV cache efficiently across GPU memory, CPU DRAM, and storage tiers. Learn how optimized data movement strategies reduce latency and improve throughput in disaggregated inference architectures.
Expert parallelism can be expressed differently depending on your workloads. Discover how to implement these patterns for optimal performance.

title: Orchestrate Next-Generation AI Workloads With Open-Source Slurm
abstract: Scaling next-generation discovery requires more than increased computational power; it also depends on flexible, reliable workload orchestration at large scale. This session examines how Slurm can be used to manage traditional HPC jobs, Kubernetes-based AI workloads with complex topology requirements, and converged environments in a single ecosystem. We will outline the current architecture, recent enhancements, and ongoing community-driven work that target higher efficiency, portability, and interoperability for supercomputing workloads over the coming decade.
key_takeaways: Understand recent Slurm and Slinky updates that improve scheduler scalability, throughput, and cluster utilization.
See approaches for using Slurm and containers to manage diverse HPC and AI applications without operating separate clusters.
Review key open-source characteristics-transparent code, community-driven development, and cost-efficient deployment models-and how they affect long-term operations in HPC environments.

speakers.name: Tim Wickberg
speakers.title: Director, System Software, Slurm and Slinky

speakers.name: Danny Auble
speakers.title: Head Director for Slurm and Slinky

title: Expression About Expression: When AI Looks in the Mirror
abstract: Despite fears of replacement, we may be entering a new wave of human creativity. AI systems increasingly generate content that comments on themselves or reveals emergent behavior. What happens when human judgment and machine intelligence blend together? This panel looks at emerging forms of expression that arise from human-AI collaboration, including outputs that feel unfamiliar or self‐reflective.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation
key_takeaways: Gain a clear understanding of how AI is reshaping creativity and what this means for artists across film, music, design, and new media.
Explore concrete examples of new artistic forms emerging in an AI‐native world.
Discover how creators are adapting to and thriving in this transition, with personal experiences from the panelists.

speakers.name: Shari Reich
speakers.title: Manager of Developer Relations for Audio and Music

title: Transolver and Transolver++: Accurate PDE Solvers on Million-Scale Unstructured Geometries
abstract: We present Transolver & Transolver++, showing you how to scale partial differential equation (PDE) solvers to million-scale unstructured meshes on NVIDIA GPUs with near-linear multi-GPU speedups. We explore efficiency techniques, including physics-aware tokens and eidetic-state attention, that let you parallelize point-wise ops with minimal overhead. We dive into practical accuracy, demonstrating consistent state-of-the-art error reductions on all standard benchmarks and real engineering cases. Both models show incredible industrial engineering applicability. We share new solutions for automotive and aerospace workflows so you can plug these models into your design loops to accelerate your design process from days to minutes. Together, we show how you can train, evaluate, and extend Transolver within the NVIDIA CUDA ecosystem and deploy it in production.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, PhysicsNeMo, cuBLAS, cuDDN, cuML, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, Triton
key_takeaways: AI models can only deal with less than 100,000 mesh points, while numerial computational fluid dynamics models suffer from great computational complexity.
To reduce that, you need to get rid of superficial and unwieldy meshes, learning intrinsic physical states underlying geometries.
By projecting the mesh points to physics-aware tokens and applying physics-attention among these tokens, the model can learn intrinsic physical interaction among these massive mesh points.
Physics-Attention is equivalent to learnable integral on input domain, which demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across all benchmarks and applications.

title: Next-Generation Storage Architecture With Compute-Storage Separation Based on DPUs: Redefining Data Center Storage
abstract: This poster demonstrates how to address the high coupling and performance bottlenecks inherent in traditional compute-storage systems by implementing a compute-storage separation architecture based on data processing units (DPU). It showcases practical applications delivering efficient solutions across cloud storage CSAL, XiRaid software-defined RAID, virtualized environments, and various mainstream distributed storage systems.
key_takeaways: Achieving compute-storage separation through DPU enables dedicated cores for computing, storage, and networking resources, enhancing system efficiency and driving architectural innovation.

DPU eliminates replica synchronization traffic, boosting network bandwidth utilization to 100% - a threefold improvement over traditional architectures - effectively overcoming performance bottlenecks.

Deep integration of JBOF and DPU liberates storage performance constraints, supports flexible software-defined configuration, dynamically adapts to all AI scenarios, optimizes cost-effectiveness, and maximizes hardware investment returns.

speakers.name: Liang Guo
speakers.title: Chair of the New Technologies and Testing Working Group; Chief Engineer
speakers.company: China Academy of Information and Communications Technology Cloud Computing and Big Data Research Institute

speakers.name: Long Chen
speakers.title: NVIDIA 网络市场开发总监

speakers.name: LINA Xie
speakers.title: Deputy Director, Data Center Department
speakers.company: China Academy of Information and Communications Technology Cloud Computing and Big Data Research Institute

title: Detect Windshield Damage Using Physically Realistic Synthetic Data
abstract: Explore a practical pipeline that uses Omniverse synthetic data and Cosmos-Transfer to measure windshield damage with pixel-level masks and produce clear repair versus replace calls. You gain tools to iterate faster, boost model quality without real data, and harden claims with trusted, auditable outputs.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
key_takeaways: Build an end-to-end Omniverse SDG pipeline with automatic pixel-wise labels so you can create balanced windshield datasets on demand.
Use Cosmos-Transfer to control domain variables and improve Sim2Real transfer so you get precise masks and millimeter-level damage measurements.
Produce clear repair-versus-replace decisions with auditable evidence and spatial metrics that plug into your claims tools.
Speed dataset generation by about 40 percent with improved Replicator randomization so you iterate faster with near zero real data.

title: Online Reasoning Video Segmentation with LLMs and Just-in-Time Digital Twins
abstract: Reasoning segmentation (RS) identifies and segments objects based on implicit text queries. RS is thus a catalyst for embodied AI agents, enabling them to interpret high-level commands without requiring step-by-step guidance. However, current RS approaches rely heavily on the visual perception capabilities of multi-modal LLMs, leading to several major limitations. We therefore propose an agent framework that disentangles perception and reasoning for online video RS without LLM fine-tuning. Our innovation is the introduction of a just-in-time digital twin concept, where - given an implicit query - a LLM plans the construction of a low-level scene representation from high-level video using specialist vision models. We call this approach to creating a digital twin as "just-in-time'' because the LLM planner anticipates the need for specific information and only requests this limited subset.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: We replace token sequences with a digital twin that explicitly encodes objects, attributes, and relations in an interpretable form.
We introduce a just-in-time twin method built only when a query demands it, activating relevant vision models and adapting the temporal scope dynamically.

Unlike traditional digital twins that maintain comprehensive representations, our approach selectively generates and updates only the information required by specific queries, reducing computational overhead while bridging the gap between high-level reasoning and low-level visual perception.

title: LESS: Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-Supervised Learning for Speech Foundational Models Using In-the-Wild Data
abstract: Although state-of-the-art speech foundation models (SFM) can produce high-quality text pseudo-labels, applying semi-supervised learning (SSL) for in-the-wild, real-world data remains challenging due to its richer and more complex acoustics. We therefore introduce LESS (Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-supervised Learning), a versatile framework that uses LLMs to correct pseudo-labels generated on in-the-wild data. In LESS, pseudo-labeled text from automatic speech recognition (ASR) or automatic speech translation (AST) of the unsupervised data is refined by an LLM, and further improved by a data filtering strategy. Across Mandarin ASR and Spanish-to-English AST evaluations, LESS delivers consistent gains, with an absolute Word Error Rate reduction of 3.8% on WenetSpeech, and BLEU score increase of 0.8 and 0.7, achieving 34.0 on Callhome and 64.7 on Fisher test sets, respectively.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Audio / Speech Foundation Models
key_takeaways: We introduce a novel approach that combines SSL techniques with LLMs for hypothesis correction to improve pseudo-labels generated by state-of-the-art SFMs and address challenges posed by noisy and diverse audios directly sourced from YouTube.

Experiments are carried out in Mandarin (ZH) ASR, achieving a significant 3.8% Word Error Rate (WER) reduction on the WenetSpeech testset. We'll discuss the choice of LLMs, WER Prompting, and optimal filtering thresholds, offering new perspectives on applying LLMs to speech processing tasks.

We validate LESS framework on Spanish-to-English (ES-to-EN) AST, attaining state-of-the-art BLEU scores of 34.0 and 64.7 on the Callhome and Fisher test sets respectively, demonstrating LESS's ability to generalize across languages, tasks, and domains.
To ensure fully reproducibility, all SFMs and LLMs used are publicly available and the recipe is open sourced.

title: LightRV: An End-to-End Lightweight Training Solution for Open-Source Reasoning Vision-Language Models
abstract: We introduce a fully open-source, end-to-end training solution for reasoning-capable vision-language models, enabling efficient, low-cost construction of such VLMs without building from scratch. Through collaborative explorations with Zeekr, the pipeline has demonstrated promising applicability to cockpit entertainment scenarios.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Hopper, NCCL, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Build reasoning-capable VLMs end to end with an open-source pipeline that you can reproduce and adapt quickly.
Transfer strong textual reasoning to multi-modal tasks using a dual-distillation strategy.
Practical value suggested through collaboration with Zeekr in cockpit entertainment-related explorations.

title: Next-Gen Hadoop Storage: BlueField-3 and CSAL for Performance and Resilience
abstract:
CSAL is an open-source user-mode FTL, cache, and I/O trace component accelerating big data and AI storage within SPDK (upstreamed), designed by Solidigm and commercially released on CSP Cloud. 

Traditional Hadoop storage systems face bottlenecks in high-concurrency, throughput, and low-latency demands for large-scale analytics, AI training, and real-time processing due to share-everything design. BlueField-3 + CSAL optimizes data paths for breakthrough throughput, offloads storage tasks to BlueField-3 (reduces CPU overhead by 70-80%), and redesigns HDFS with computation-storage separation for superior resource efficiency.

Triple replication, erasure coding, and RAID deliver multi-layered redundancy, ensuring high reliability, data integrity, and enterprise security across failure scenarios. Supports large-scale analytics, AI model training/inference, real-time streaming, etc.
key_takeaways: Breakthrough Performance: The solution, powered by BlueField-3 and Solidigm CSAL software, optimizes data paths to deliver exceptional throughput, overcoming traditional Hadoop storage bottlenecks.
Efficient Resource Utilization: By offloading storage tasks to the BlueField-3, the solution significantly reduces server CPU overhead, boosting overall system efficiency.
Robust Data Protection: Triple replication, erasure coding, and RAID ensure high reliability and security, safeguarding data across diverse failure scenarios.
Versatile Application Support: Designed for large-scale analytics, AI training/inference, real-time processing, and edge computing, it meets varied enterprise needs.
Future-Ready Scalability: Seamlessly integrates with cloud-native and hybrid cloud environments, enabling flexible, scalable data architectures for digital transformation.

speakers.name: Long Chen
speakers.title: NVIDIA 网络市场开发总监

title: Dual Engines of Thought: A Dual-Engine Architecture With Breadth-Depth Coordination for Open-Ended Reasoning in LLMs
abstract: Dual engines of thought (DEoT) - an adaptive reasoning framework that dynamically orchestrates breadth and depth reasoning modules to enable stable, efficient, and context-sensitive inference in large language models. DEoT employs an entropy-regulated controller to judiciously navigate the reasoning space, mitigating both informational sparsity and computational overhead. This dynamic coordination facilitates robust multi-source integration and enhances the model's capacity for deep causal inference. Empirical results on the open-ended News-to-Question benchmark reveal that DEoT significantly outperforms standard LLMs, delivering responses that are not only more analytically nuanced, but also more logically coherent. Our architecture demonstrates broad applicability across high-impact domains, including biomedicine, economics, geopolitics, industrial systems, and advanced technologies.
nvidia_technology: CUDA, TensorRT, RAPIDS, NeMo, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Dual-Engine System: Proposes the Dual Engines of Thought framework, which dynamically coordinates breadth and depth engines to balance exploration and elaboration, enabling efficient open-ended reasoning across domains.

Multi-Agent Modular Analysis: Implements a multi-agent architecture with specialized analytical roles and clear task traceability, improving reasoning stability, interpretability, and system extensibility.

Information-Theoretic Metric - Introduces Effective Reasoning Information Rate (ERIR), a principled metric that combines logical validity and novel information gain to objectively quantify reasoning efficiency.

Experimental Validation: Demonstrates 85.5% overall win rate on the five-domain News-to-Question (N2Q) benchmark and consistent performance gains over six prompting baselines, supported by ablation studies confirming dual-engine effectiveness.

speakers.name: Teng-Ruei Chen
speakers.title: CTO
speakers.company: Taiwan Intelligence Research and Development Co., Ltd.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Follow-the-Leader Robotic Endoscopic Navigation via Synthetic Depth Data
abstract: Autonomous navigation is essential for both medical and industrial endoscopic robots, as it enables safe and efficient exploration of narrow, often tubular, environments without continuous human intervention. We propose a follow-the-leader endoscopic robot designed to minimize contact between the central body of the endoscope and the intestinal wall during navigation, thereby reducing patient discomfort. To achieve this, we construct a realistic intestine-robot simulation in NVIDIA Omniverse for training autonomous navigation policies. Furthermore, we generate thousands of synthetic intraluminal images within Omniverse to fine-tune the Depth Anything model, enabling dense 3D perception from a single monocular camera.
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, Isaac, Omniverse, Omniverse Replicator, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Endoscopic Navigation
Reinforcement Learning
Depth Estimation
Synthetic Data Generation
Fine-tuning

title: Accelerate Topological Property Simulation of SSH-Hubbard Model via DMET-VQE With CUDA-Q
abstract: We demonstrate how the Density Matrix Embedding Theory with Variational Quantum Eigensolver (DMET-VQE) can be applied to uncover topological properties in the one-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger-Hubbard (SSHH) model, and how its computational performance can be enhanced through asynchronous simulation across multiple GPUs using NVIDIA CUDA-Q. In our simulations, the open-boundary edge-state signatures - that is, the signals of the topological states splitting away from half filling to one-and-three-quarters filling as onsite interaction increases - depend on the fragment size, which determines the number of qubits, revealing the significance of computational acceleration in high-qubit simulations.
key_takeaways: Asynchronous observation enables simultaneous execution, substantially improving simulation speed and efficiency for large, dense workloads.
Increasing fragment size enhances topological accuracy, yielding higher precision for critical regions and greater GPU-acceleration benefits.
For more complex problems, DMET-VQE provides a potentially stable and scalable path on noisy quantum devices in the future through a divide-and-conquer approach.
Achieves 1,420× multi-GPU speed-up via asynchronous CUDA-Q observation.

title: Neural-Based High-Resolution Weather Localization in Thailand: Downscaling and Forecasting
abstract: Explore new AI techniques for high-resolution local weather simulation in Thailand. Global weather models like GFS provide broad forecasts, but often miss local extremes. Regional models such as WRF-ROMS capture more detail, but require hours of computation, limiting use to one or two runs per day. Accelerated by NVIDIA technology, we present two AI-powered solutions for fast, high-fidelity, and energy-efficient local weather prediction: (1) Weather Downscaling, which refines GFS outputs to 3×3 km resolution, and (2) AI Weather Forecasting, which generates high-resolution 24-hour, 3×3 km forecasts in seconds on a single A100 GPU.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, PhysicsNeMo, cuDDN, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Two neural-based approaches improve weather simulation in Thailand - fast and high-resolution.
GFS downscaling with DEM and QDM using CorrDiff improves local detail and accuracy.
AFNO-StormCast hybrid model enhances regional weather forecasting.
Deep learning and GPU acceleration on NVIDIA A100 enable fast, energy-efficient, and low-cost downscaling and forecasting.
Bridges the gap between global coverage (GFS) and local precision (WRF-ROMS)

title: Pioneering AI-Driven Engineering: Agentic Optimization for FEA-Driven ISO Standard Validation
abstract: New solutions for the time-consuming and error-prone process of safety-critical engineering validation are here: we demonstrate an agentic AI system that delivers a 7,200% engineering speedup and a 75% error rate reduction in validating complex ISO standards and finite element analysis (FEA) simulations. Explore how this pioneering, end-to-end system integrates AI agents with proprietary engineering workflows to enable faster iteration and increase the reliability of your high performance compute-driven design cycles.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: We built the first end-to-end agentic AI system for compliance and simulation, connecting AI agents to design workflows and software to intelligently infer changes.
The system achieved a 7,200% engineering speedup over human benchmarks for the end-to-end task speedup to report generation.
The agent acts as a "second set of eyes," delivering a 75% error rate reduction in safety-critical applications and increasing trust in HPC simulation results.
The agentic pipeline was tested on a complex problem involving a 200-plus page dense document and a complex FEA simulation on an NVIDIA H100 system for a safety-critical engineering application.

title: AI-Orchestrating Platform for Drug Repurposing
abstract: We'll introduce a multi-model AI-orchestrated platform that integrates structure-based screening, knowledge-graph reasoning, and gene-expression analysis to accelerate systematic drug repurposing. By unifying multi-modal data and diverse AI models, the system enables cross-domain reasoning from molecular binding prediction to disease-gene inference. A case study on Bremelanotide illustrates how this approach identifies new therapeutic indications through end-to-end computational orchestration.
nvidia_technology: BioNeMo, cuGraph, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Gain practical insights into how AI orchestration can be applied to real-world drug repurposing challenges, and leave with a clear view of how such frameworks can accelerate the translation of computational predictions into experimentally testable therapeutic hypotheses.

We'll present two complementary workflows that illustrate different reasoning paths. The Drug-Target Driven workflow traces discovery from drug to disease through structure-based screening and AI-powered binding prediction for small molecules and proteins.
