The Disease-Gene Driven workflow moves from disease to drug via AI agents operating on knowledge graphs and retrieval-augmented generation, supported by gene-expression analysis.

Learn actionable strategies for designing integrated, data-driven pipelines that connect molecular modeling with systems-level understanding, and see how multi-model AI can enhance interpretability, scalability, and hypothesis generation in drug discovery.

title: S5-TTS: Low-Latency Streaming T5-based Text-to-Speech Synthesis With Minimal Lookahead
abstract: Discover S5-TTS, a streaming T5-based text-to-speech model that delivers low-latency, natural, intelligible speech with minimal lookahead, generating speech incrementally as text arrives. By combining a controllable lookahead mechanism with auxiliary attention and interleaved multi-source distillation, S5-TTS achieves high-quality synthesis, comparable to full-context TTS models, while significantly reducing response latency. We dive deep into the impact of lookahead and distillation through comprehensive ablation studies, showing that a small lookahead combined with distillation provides the best trade-off between intelligibility, naturalness, and efficiency. You'll gain insights into how the design of S5-TTS bridges the gap between quality and latency, paving the way for more responsive conversational AI applications.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NeMo, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Learn how S5-TTS generates speech incrementally, producing natural and intelligible speech without waiting for the full text.
Understand how the lookahead window size affects intelligibility, speaker similarity, and naturalness.
See how interleaved multi-source distillation further improves speech naturalness under limited lookahead.
Discover the performance and latency advantages of S5-TTS compared to conventional full-context TTS models.
Gain practical insights into designing responsive TTS systems suitable for real-time interactive applications.

title: AI-Driven Rapid Diagnosis of Lymph Node Metastasis During Cancer Surgery
abstract: We developed an advanced intraoperative lymph node diagnostic framework that integrates rapid fresh pathology imaging with an AI-assisted analytical pipeline. The demonstrated approach enables accurate intraoperative diagnosis of lymph nodes, markedly improving diagnostic sensitivity and reducing the false-negative rate of intraoperative frozen section analysis.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
key_takeaways: AI-Assisted Intraoperative Diagnosis: Integration of rapid fresh pathology imaging with an AI segmentation pipeline enables rapid and accurate diagnosis of lymph node micrometastases during cancer surgery.
Reduced False Negatives: The proposed method lowers the false-negative rate of micrometastasis from ~59.8% to under 10%, significantly reducing reoperation rate.
Rapid Workflow: Achieves reliable lymph node diagnosis within 30 minutes, improving intraoperative diagnosis efficiency and reducing the pathology workloads.
Scalable and Efficient Pipeline: Combines MONAI, cuCIM, and CUDA acceleration for high-throughput deployment on multi-GPU systems.
Transformer-Based Segmentation Innovation: Adapts VISTA2D and Segment Anything Model for precise tissue segmentation on rapid fresh PATHOscope images.

title: Learn Skill Abstraction From Action-Free Videos
abstract: Learning from videos is a path toward generalist robots by leveraging diverse visual data beyond traditional datasets. Videos often contain recurring skills (e.g., grasping, lifting) across tasks and environments. While skill-based methods can learn reusable behaviors, they usually require clean, action-labeled data. Conversely, existing video-learning methods often train monolithic models or focus on single-step dynamics, hindering skill composition and long-horizon planning. We propose Skill Abstraction from Optical Flow (SOF), which learns skills directly from action-free videos. SOF uses optical flow as a surrogate for action and adapts skill learning to operate on flow-based representations. It plans in skill space and translates these plans into actions. SOF improves multitask and long-horizon performance, enabling skill acquisition and composition directly from raw visual data.
key_takeaways: SOF introduces a new framework for learning reusable robotic skills directly from action-free videos by using optical flow as a surrogate for action signals.
The method bridges the gap between single-step and trajectory-level learning, enabling the discovery of mid-level, composable skills that transfer across tasks and environments.
By adapting existing skill-learning algorithms to operate on flow-based representations, SOF extracts structured motion patterns without relying on curated, action-labeled datasets.
The framework plans in a learned skill space and translates flow-based plans into executable actions, supporting multitask learning and long-horizon planning.

Experiments demonstrate that SOF outperforms prior video-learning approaches in both generalization and efficiency, highlighting the power of optical-flow-based skill abstraction for scalable robot learning.

title: Accelerated Quantum Supercomputing: Build a Multi-GPU, Multi-QPU, Multi-User Environment for Hybrid Algorithms
abstract: Quantum computers can run some calculations faster than classical computers, but integration with classical computing is required. Our work is the first-ever demonstration of a multi-QPU and multi-GPU environment fully integrated into a data center.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Ethernet Networking, cuQuantum, CUDA Quantum, Blackwell, CUDA-Q
key_takeaways: We deployed multiple ORCA PT Series photonic QPUs into an existing multi-GPU, multi-user HPC cluster.
We extended CUDA-Q to support photonic quantum computers.
We use slurm to schedule both classical and quantum resources.
We use the deployment as a testbed for developing hybrid quantum-classical algorithms.
Our work provides a blueprint for high performance computing/quantum computing integration.

title: Make Federated Learning Robust When Devices Join and Leave
abstract: This work introduces the first open-source framework for benchmarking federated learning (FL) under dynamic client participation, demonstrating that this practical scenario causes significant performance degradation. To address this challenge, we propose knowledge-pool federated learning (KPFL), a generic plugin that uses a shared knowledge pool and generative knowledge distillation to improve model robustness and prevent knowledge loss.
key_takeaways: We introduce the first open-source framework specifically designed to benchmark FL models under dynamic client participation.
We propose KPFL, a generic plugin that mitigates learning instability and knowledge degradation caused by DP.

We conduct extensive experiments showing both the significant challenges posed by decentralized personalized federated learning and the effectiveness of KPFL in improving performance across a wide range of FL methods.

speakers.name: Cheng-Kuan Lin
speakers.title: Associate Professor
speakers.company: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University (NYCU)

speakers.name: Ming-Lun Lee
speakers.title: Graduate Research Assistant
speakers.company: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University (NYCU)

speakers.name: Yu-Chee Tseng
speakers.title: Chair Professor
speakers.company: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University (NYCU)

title: Accelerating High-Fidelity Generative Models: Entangled Quantum Walks With CUDA-Q
abstract:
We introduce a Quantum Walk-based Adaptive Distribution Generator (QW-ADG), a hybrid quantum-classical framework designed for high-efficiency generative modeling. The key is using an entangled two-qubit coin within a variational quantum walk, generating complex 2D spatial correlations for tasks like image creation.

Implemented on the CUDA-Q platform and accelerated on an NVIDIA GPU, our model demonstrates a significant performance advantage over alternative quantum approaches. In a direct benchmark generating 8x8 MNIST digits, our QW-ADG is over 10x faster than a standard quantum generative adversarial network (QGAN) while achieving comparable or superior accuracy (L1 Error).

We establish the QW-ADG as a highly efficient and practical framework for near-term quantum generative modeling, highlighting the power of structured quantum dynamics combined with high-performance simulation.
key_takeaways: Novel Quantum Walk Method: We introduce a Quantum Walk-based Adaptive Distribution Generator (QW-ADG) that uses a novel entangled two-qubit coin in a hybrid quantum-classical loop to generate complex 2D patterns with high fidelity.

10x Faster Than QGANs: In direct benchmarks generating 8x8 MNIST digits, our QW-ADG is over 10 times faster than a standard quantum generative adversarial network while achieving comparable or superior accuracy (L1 Error).

Enabled by CUDA-Q: The entire framework is built on the CUDA-Q platform and accelerated by an NVIDIA T4 GPU, enabling the rapid simulation and benchmarking required to demonstrate a significant practical advantage for our quantum algorithm.

title: Generative Antibody Factory: GPU-Accelerated Diffusion Model and Protein Language Model for Nanobody Discovery
abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated generative AI pipeline that designs and evaluates nanobody candidates in days instead of months - combining diffusion-based structure generation with protein language models to improve developability and experimental success.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, BioNeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, Blackwell, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Learn how GPU-accelerated diffusion and protein language models enable end-to-end nanobody design within days instead of months.
See how fine-tuned protein language models predict and filter low-polyreactivity VHH sequences, improving expression yield and manufacturability.
Discover how multi-GPU training on Taipei-1 achieved up to 159× speedup for structure remodeling and evaluation.
Understand how integrating diffusion modeling with developability prediction increases experimental success rates.
Explore how generative AI pipelines can scale to 100 k-design campaigns for next-generation biologics and diagnostics.

title: Create Noninvasive Diagnoses: Adapting Digital-Twin Model for Hemodynamic Simulation
abstract: We are developing noninvasive diagnostic solutions for CAD to replace risk-bearing, high-cost invasive angiography with safe, efficient, and data-rich alternatives. By combining coronary computed tomography angiography (CCTA), AI-based lumen segmentation, and hemodynamics simulation, clinicians can assess both the anatomical and functional significance of coronary lesions - improving patient safety, healthcare efficiency, and clinical precision.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: CCTA as the first-line noninvasive diagnostics is highly recommended by medical societies.
Our approach composed of digital-twin model and hydrofluid simulation is straightforward in terms of clinical application.
Comparing conventional intervention with digital-twin simulation shows promising results to go beyond.

title: Quantum-Inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Scalable and Efficient LLMs
abstract: Discover how quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (QKANs) unlock new levels of efficiency and scalability in large language models. By embedding Data Re-Uploading Activations (DARUANs) as quantum variational activation functions (QVAFs), QKANs achieve exponential parameter reduction and enhanced expressivity compared to classical multilayer perceptrons (MLPs). The resulting hybrid QKAN (HQKAN) architecture serves as a drop-in replacement for transformer feed-forward blocks, enabling smaller, faster, and more energy-efficient LLMs. Accelerated through cuQuantum-optimized Tensor contractions on NVIDIA GPUs, HQKANs compress GPT- and Gemma-class models with significant parameter reduction while maintaining strong generative performance, paving the sustainable path toward next-generation AI.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, cuQuantum, cuBLAS, cuDDN, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Quantum-Inspired Foundations: QKANs use DARUAN-based QVAFs to represent complex nonlinearities with exponentially fewer parameters.
HQKAN Integration: Drop-in replacement for MLPs in transformer architectures - improving scalability and efficiency.
cuQuantum Acceleration: Tensor-network formulation optimized for NVIDIA GPUs boosts throughput and memory efficiency.
Massive Compression: Achieves significant model size reduction in GPT- and Gemma-class benchmarks with minimal loss.
Toward Sustainable AI: Combines quantum computing with GPU innovation for greener, high-performance generative models.

speakers.name: Hsi-Sheng Goan
speakers.title: Director
speakers.company: Center for Quantum Science and Engineering, National Taiwan University

title: Continuous-Time NODE in Transformers and Physics-Informed Loss as Soft Physical Constraints in Weather Forecasting
abstract: We develop a novel approach to integrate continuous-time Neural ODE dynamics to transformers and implement physics-informed loss as soft physical constraints to learn the spatio-temporal mapping of weather variables in the embedding space of the vision transformer. As climate volatility grows, this approach offers timely improvements in forecast quality without sacrificing transformer's learning capacity.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
key_takeaways: Explore physics-informed loss as soft constraints in data-driven weather forecasting.
Integrate continuous time layers in discrete spatio-temporal models.
We leverage the continuous learning paradigm to effectively learn the complex spatio-temporal changes, even from weather data recorded at coarser resolution.

title: Polar Deep Learning on GPUs: Drone-Satellite Multispectral Fusion for Vegetation Mapping
abstract: We present a GPU-powered deep learning workflow that fuses drone and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery to map Antarctic vegetation at 1 meter resolution. This approach improves large-scale ecosystem monitoring and reveals links between vegetation dynamics and hydrological change in polar environments.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Sensor Fusion
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, cuDDN, cuML
key_takeaways: Drone-satellite data fusion enables high-resolution mapping of Antarctic vegetation.
GPU-based deep learning with PyTorch accelerates model training and prediction.
The method identifies vegetation-hydrology interactions across multiple seasons (2017-25).
Demonstrates a scalable workflow for ecological monitoring under climate change.
Highlights NVIDIA GPU applications in remote sensing and environmental research.

title: AI-Powered Digital Twin Transforms 7-Eleven Retail Store Design and Training
abstract: See how interactive 3D digital twins reduce retail store setup time by 70% and display costs by 40%, replacing expensive physical mock-ups with fully virtual environments. This self-improving platform integrates real-time inventory management, AI-driven planogram optimization, and demand forecasting - enabling over 7,200 stores to validate layouts, train employees, and test "what-if" scenarios safely before physical implementation.
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DeepStream, TensorRT, Isaac, Omniverse, Metropolis, Hopper, Audio2Face, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NeMo, Omniverse Replicator, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Cosmos, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Learn how to reduce retail setup time by over 70% and cut display costs by 30-40% through interactive 3D digital twins that replace physical mock-ups with physics-based virtual environments.
Discover techniques for generating synthetic training data that achieved 99.58% planogram recognition accuracy using 50% real and 50% simulated images.

Explore self-improving AI systems where every user interaction enhances model performance, continuously optimizing layouts, reducing manual counting by 50-70%, and lowering restocking errors by 10-15%.
Understand how to integrate real-time inventory, demand forecasting, and planogram optimization into unified digital twin platforms serving 7,200+ stores.
Master hybrid 3D asset creation pipelines combining photogrammetry, AI generation, and manual modeling for rapid retail environment digitization.

title: BrainRAD: Dementia Diagnosis and Prognosis Prediction With Retrieval-Assisted Vision-Language Framework
abstract:
Dementia affects over 55 million people worldwide and is expected to double by 2050. Diagnosis and prognosis are challenging due to reliance on expert interpretation of neuroimaging and clinical data. Large vision-language models (VLMs) offer a promising solution by integrating MRI images and textual information to generate clinically meaningful image captions and diagnostic reasoning, but their potential in brain imaging is limited by the scarcity of large-scale, paired MRI-report datasets. While public datasets such as ADNI [2] provide extensive imaging and clinical data for Alzheimer's disease study, they often lack detailed radiological reports linking visual and linguistic features. 

We develop Brain Retrieval-Assisted Diagnosis (BrainRAD), an AI-driven framework designed to advance precision diagnosis in dementia through integrating quantitative MRI biomarkers and large VLMs.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, Clara, cuDDN, NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, DGX Station, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Dementia diagnosis and prognosis prediction is achieved based on the fine-tuned vision-language model.
Integrating quantitative measurements and original images can provide enhancement for vision-language model performance.
Retrieval-assisted prediction improves model robustness and prediction accuracy.

speakers.name: Chenyu Tim Wang
speakers.title: Associate Professor, Director of Operations
speakers.company: The University of Sydney, Sydney Neuroimaging Analysis Centre

speakers.name: Geng Zhan
speakers.title: Research Associate
speakers.company: The University of Sydney, Sydney Neuroimaging Analysis Centre

title: Solving Sidewalk Autonomy Through Large-Scale Urban Simulation
abstract: We explored sidewalk autonomy solutions with scalable simulation, scalable scenario generation, and new learning frameworks. We'll show how we accelerate safe, human-like navigation in real-world urban sidewalk environments.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, CUDA, Isaac, Omniverse, cuDDN, DLSS, NCCL, Omniverse Replicator, OpenGL, PhysX, Triton, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Scalable Urban Simulation
Sidewalk Autonomy
Reinforcement Learning
Navigation Models

title: GPU Efficiency Optimization for Multi-Trillion Video Recommendation Models
abstract: Large-scale recommendation systems, critical for Facebook Video, face significant computational and memory challenges due to inherent scale, imbalanced sparse embeddings, and increased dense computation. We've advanced multifaceted optimizations for GPU resources. Specifically, we implement 60-plus custom forward and backward kernels with 2-100X speedup, including rowwise FP8 for GEMM, custom Flash Attention masks, feature encoder operators, and Tensor memory/compute operation on jagged Tensors. We also explore various techniques to eliminate stream sync points and CPU blocking operations and demonstrate the effectiveness of overlapping communication with compute, distributed inference/caching, and AOTI lowering. These combined optimizations result in >4X QPS gain for both training and inference, significantly lowering down infra cost and enabling model innovations.
nvidia_technology: CUDA, cuDDN, NSight Compute, NSight Systems
key_takeaways: Unlike LLMs, large-scale recommendation systems exhibit greater heterogeneity in sparse/dense feature inputs as well as GPU/CPU computing and memory architecture, necessitating multifaceted optimization strategies for improved GPU utilization.

We addressed technical challenges and presented comprehensive optimization techniques for training and inference of multi-trillion industrial ranking systems that power Facebook video recommendations for billions of users.
Our combined optimizations have resulted in significant queries per second and latency performance gains, substantially reducing infrastructure costs and fostering new model innovations.

title: LeHome: A Simulation Environment for Deformable Object Manipulation in Household Scenarios
abstract:
Our goal is to deliver a visually realistic and physically authentic simulation that enables robots to handle daily tasks in ordinary homes (especially deformable objects), and promote large-scale home adoption of robots by bridging the sim2real gap. Challenges include interacting with diverse, non-standard objects and adapting to dynamic environments; expensive and labor-intensive household data collection; the complexity of modeling diverse deformable objects; and the high cost, large size, and expertise of industrial robots.

We contribute diverse high-fidelity assets across four room types via NVIDIA Newton; accurate, fine-grained, and causally interactions with objects through Isaacsim Features; support for multiple robotic embodiments, especially low-cost robots; and efficient and extensive teleoperation methods for data collection with Leader & Follower, Xbox, etc.
key_takeaways: Newton
IsaacSim
Manipulation
Simulation
Deformable Object

title: Fast and Accurate Virtual Drug Screening Powered by GPU-Annealer and CUDA-Q
abstract: This work presents a fast and accurate GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening pipeline. In the first stage, a GPU-annealer was built to simulate a quantum annealer and used for molecular docking, achieving faster performance with competitive AUC and EF1. In the second stage, CUDA-Q VQE refined binding energies for reliable ranking and better EF1. During the entire pipeline, GPU delivers up to four orders of magnitude speedup over CPU execution, accelerating early drug discovery.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, CUDA Quantum, CUDA-Q
key_takeaways: Large-Scale Virtual Drug Screening Support: Provides a solution that combines high accuracy and high speed for evaluating protein-ligand interactions in the early stages of drug development.
GPU-accelerated Docking by Quantum-inspired Algorithm: Demonstrates high-speed performance in virtual screening while maintaining competitive AUC and EF1 accuracy.
CUDA-Q VQE Refined Ranking: Improves bond energy estimation using quantum variational energy estimation (VQE), providing more reliable molecular ranking results.
Significant GPU Acceleration Benefits: The CUDA-Q VQE module achieves a four-order-of-magnitude performance improvement, while GPU annealing operations are an order of magnitude faster than CPUs.
Accelerating the Drug Discovery Process: Combining GPU-Annealer with the CUDA-Q architecture creates a scalable and practical AI drug design platform.

title: BenchOverflow: Measuring Overflow in Large Language Models via Plain-Text Prompts
abstract: We propose BenchOverflow, a new benchmark that exposes how large language models silently waste GPU time, energy, and cost by generating unnecessarily long outputs during normal use. We show how to measure, compare, and mitigate overflow, helping you build faster, more sustainable, and compute-efficient AI systems.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU
key_takeaways: Introducing BenchOverflow: A new benchmark revealing how normal prompts can cause excessive text generation without jailbreaks or adversarial inputs.
Unified Evaluation Protocol: Standardized 5k-token tests measure overflow severity through output length, cap-saturation rates, and model-to-model correlations.
Cross-Model Insights: Analysis of nine leading open- and closed-source models exposes consistent overgeneration patterns and heavy output tails.
Practical Mitigation: A simple conciseness reminder significantly reduces overflow across diverse prompting strategies.
Open and Reproducible: All datasets, evaluation tools, and metrics are publicly available to accelerate research on efficient LLM behavior.

title: Disaggregated Inference Experiments With NVIDIA Dynamo for Telecoms Reasoning, AI Agent Use Cases
abstract:
We explore how disaggregated inference optimizes GPU resource utilization in real-world reasoning and AI agent use cases within telco environments. We also analyze the workload characteristics of Wikipedia-based AI agents, which increase prefill inputs. To handle such large inputs, we experimented with the NVIDIA Dynamo framework on NVIDIA H100 GPUs in a disaggregated setup. Our results show an approximate 16% overhead in time-to-first-token and confirm superior stability under high loads, compared to aggregated inference.

From a telecom perspective, the disaggregated inference could represent a significant breakthrough to enable flexible, on-demand GPU allocation across multi-data center architectures and supports scalable AI agent deployment. Future work will focus on developing cost models for geo-distributed GPU resources to ensure efficient resource planning for our customers.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NVLink / NVSwitch
key_takeaways: Analyzing the internal behaviors of real AI agents and why AI agents require disaggregated inference
How disaggregated inference works, and the key benefit of disaggregation
Case study on disaggregated inference and insights for future resource allocation planning to ensure latency SLA
Strategy of GPU utilization optimization on disaggregated inference, including telecom use cases

speakers.name: Kota Tsuyuzaki
speakers.title: イノベーションセンター テクノロジー部門 担当課長 エンジニアリングマネージャー
speakers.company: NTTドコモビジネス株式会社

speakers.name: Sotetsu Suzugamine
speakers.title: イノベーションセンター テクノロジー部門 Software Engineer

title: Interactive 2D-3D Visualization Interface for Digital Pathology
abstract: Explore an interactive GPU-accelerated interface that links 2D level-by-level images with 3D component renderings, making it easier for researchers and clinicians to navigate complex 3D spatial structures in dense tissue datasets, such as digital pathology.
topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Rendering Engines / Pipelines / Tools
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, Clara, CUDA-X
key_takeaways: Learn how a GPU-accelerated pipeline enables efficient preprocessing and visualization of large 3D pathology datasets.
See how 2D level-by-level slide views are seamlessly linked with 3D component renderings for intuitive spatial navigation.
Discover a 3D visualization framework that can be potentially extended to other large-scale biomedical image datasets.

title: Agentic AI for Medical Discharge: Combining Machine Learning with Language Models for ICD Code Prediction- Department of Computer Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, Mahidol University
abstract: We show a new AI system that helps hospitals code medical records automatically. Our system combines machine learning with language models to make coding more accurate and save doctors' time.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, cuDDN
key_takeaways: Our AI agent system is 55% accurate, which is better than using large language models alone (52.6%).
Short medical documents need AI filtering, but long documents work better with machine learning only.
Our system costs less to run than big language models, but gives better results.

title: GreenMind: A LLM for Complex Reasoning - Chain-of-Thought Data and Self-Correction Cycles on NVIDIA NEMO
abstract: We introduce GreenMind, a large language model designed for complex reasoning through chain-of-thought data and self-correction cycles. By leveraging the GRPO strategy from NVIDIA NEMO for fine-tuning, we guide the model to generate logically coherent responses, deliver informative answers, and offer intuitive explanations. These features provide value not only to end users, but also to ongoing research aimed at improving data quality and sampling techniques.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, cuBLAS, cuDDN, cuDF, cuFFT, cuGraph, cuML, NeMo, CUDA Quantum, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Pipeline to create chain-of-thought data from supervised data, tuning GRPO on NVIDIA NEMO
Self-correction cycles through fine-tuning pipeline, with well-designed reward function
Effectively addressing questions that require intermediate-level reasoning
First Vietnamese LLM on NIVIDIA NIM

title: AI Solves GPS Outages: Making Vehicle Navigation Robust
abstract: Kalman filters (KF) are the most successful tracking algorithm for vehicle navigation. Its accuracy drops when the process noise covariance matrix Q is fixed. Conventional approaches for updating the Qmatrix have stability issues due to aggressive updates. We propose a reinforcement learning (RL)-guided adaptive Q-update for robust vehicle navigation. The proposed RL-guided KF achieves stable state estimation under GPS loss and tough terrain.
key_takeaways: 94% reduction in position error compared to the conventional KF with a fixed  Q matrix
Maintain robustness to different routes and motion patterns
More stable and lower update rate than existing adaptive Q methods

title: Glider: A Unified Platform Architecture for Resource Placement and Coordinated Scheduling of Dynamic AI Workloads
abstract: Dive deep into new solutions for the complex challenge of scheduling dynamic AI workloads. We present Glider, a unified platform that improves resource utilization by using workload-centric orchestration, fragmentation-aware placement, and native gang scheduling to efficiently coordinate parallel jobs.
key_takeaways: Learn how workload-centric orchestration and fragmentation-aware placement strategies like bin-packing and consolidation mitigate GPU fragmentation and maximize resource utilization.

Discover how Glider applies distinct, optimized scheduling policies for different AI workload types, prioritizing rapid placement for latency-sensitive tasks and consolidation for throughput-oriented training.
See how a hierarchical Fair-Share Scheduling and quota system provides enterprise-grade fairness and guarantees quality of service in multi-tenant clusters.

title: MANGO : Thai Multi-Modal Adaptive Neural Generative Orchestrator
abstract: We explore new techniques for fine-tuning open-source multi-modal models like Qwen, LLaMA, and Gemma to understand Thai language and culture. By combining curated Thai data from the internet, university courses, and volunteer contributions, we aim to improve model performance and enable AI applications for education, travel, call-center services, and research
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, NeMo
key_takeaways: Learn how we fine-tune open-source multi-modal models (Qwen, LLaMA, Gemma) to better understand Thai language, culture, and context.
Explore how curated Thai datasets gathered from the internet, in-house engineers, volunteers, and university courses enhance model accuracy and reasoning.
See how Thai academic benchmarks (TCAS exams) are used to measure Thai comprehension and multi-modal performance.
Understand how these efforts create AI systems ready for real-world Thai applications in education, tourism, call-center services, and research.

title: Interactive Digital Twin of the Left Atrium Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
abstract: We created an interactive digital twin of the heart's left atrium that allows researchers to simulate and study atrial arrhythmias, and practice treating them through virtual ablation. This gives physicians the ability to test ablation techniques without performing these invasive procedures on live patients.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, NSight Compute, NSight Graphics, NSight Systems, OpenGL
key_takeaways: This model enables researchers to define specific tissue properties and precisely control ectopic triggers to study atrial arrhythmias.
Simulated ablations within the model allow users to test different lesion patterns and evaluate their effectiveness in terminating arrhythmias.
Using this model, we can minimize invasive procedures by offering a reproducible and risk-free environment for testing ablation strategies.
This model also could be suited for educational use, helping clinicians and trainees visualize the impact of ablation decisions in near-real time.

title: Accelerate Minimal Residual Disease Detection: Real-World Performance and Clinical Impact
abstract: We implemented an minimal residual disease (MRD) detection pipeline optimized with NVIDIA Parabricks to accelerate computation and enable faster, scalable molecular analysis without compromising accuracy.
key_takeaways: GPU-accelerated MRD pipelines delivered significant performance boost, enabling scalable, rapid molecular insights for adjuvant therapy decision making.
GPU results showed high concordance with CLIA/CAP-validated CPU pipelines, maintaining clinical-grade reliability and reproducibility.
The acceleration on unique molecular identifier processing will enable same-day MRD reporting, directly improving research productivity and clinical decision timelines.

title: GPU-Accelerated Multi-Agent Large Language Models  for Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) Protocols
abstract:
ERAS protocols need real-time, personalized support; CPU rule engines have high latency and low throughput. We quantify performance and clinical utility of GPU-accelerated LLM inference for ERAS decision support by building a multi-agent LLM system on a single RTX 5090 with hybrid RAG, benchmarked against a CPU rules baseline across three ERAS use cases: PONV prevention, POD checklists, and chest-tube removal. Evaluations included microbenchmarks, concurrency stress tests, and task-level accuracy.

Results: Mean latency fell 2.9× (1900→650 ms); P95 fell 2.4× (2050→850 ms); max throughput rose 7× (0.5→3.5 QPS). Under 16-patient concurrency, P95 remained ~2.1 s with GPU utilization 60-80% and VRAM 16-18 GB. Task accuracy reached 95%.

A single RTX 5090 enables bedside, sub-second responses and supports 8-16 concurrent patients, offering a practical solution with a favorable cost profile.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, Isaac, Blackwell, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Real-time AI assistance supports 8-16 concurrent patients, transforming workflow from static rules to dynamic, context-aware recommendations
95% clinical accuracy achieved through multi-agent reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)-grounded LLM inference, validated across PONV, POD, and drain management tasks.
Projected 20% reduction in postoperative complications PONV/POD with $175,000 annual savings demonstrates strong clinical and economic value.
Affordable consumer GPU deployment with four-month return on investment offers practical alternative to expensive cloud APIs ($18,000/year)

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.title: Engineer
speakers.company: Department of Information and Communications Research Laboratories, Industrial Technology Research Institute

speakers.name: Hsing-hsien Wu
speakers.title: Deputy Director
speakers.company: Division of Thoracic Surgery, Tainan Municipal Hospital, Taiwan

title: Robot Learning for Automated Electric Vehicle Battery Recycling: Sim-to-Real for Dexterous Manipulation
abstract: At SBTC, we aim to automate electric vehicle (EV) battery dismantling to boost recycling efficiency and support a sustainable circular economy. Conventional robotics struggles with these tasks, which demand dexterity, haptic feedback, and robustness to visual localization inaccuracies and diverse battery designs. To address this, we developed a sim-to-real pipeline where dismantling skills are trained in simulation using Isaac Lab and transferred to real robots with ROS2, CuRobo, and computer vision technologies. This approach enabled the development of key dismantling skills, such as an (un)screwer engagement skill, capable of intelligently locating and detecting screw or bolt engagement, and a cable-cutting skill, which allows industrial robots to remain compliant while safely cutting high-voltage cables.
key_takeaways: Demonstration of a sim-to-real pipeline that enables training robotic manipulation skills in simulation and deployment on real robots
Presentation of the reinforcement learning workflow highlights showing how trained robotic manipulation skills overcome the limitations of conventional robotic approaches
Development of an (un)screwer engagement skill, allowing robots to intelligently detect and engage with screws, nuts, and bolts
Development of a compliant cable-cutting skill, enabling industrial robots to safely handle and cut high-voltage cables in EV battery packs
Contribution toward a sustainable circular economy by increasing the efficiency and safety of EV battery recycling through intelligent robotics

speakers.name: Özhan Özen
speakers.title: Sr. AI Scientist and Project Leader
speakers.company: Swiss Battery Technology Center - Switzerland Innovation Park Biel/Bienne

title: Scaling Gaussian Splatting: Large-Scene 3D Reconstruction in the Wild
abstract: Explore new techniques for scaling Gaussian splatting to reconstruct large, complex 3D scenes in the wild. We introduce a framework that enables efficient and accurate reconstruction across wide indoor and outdoor environments by addressing challenges in memory scalability, geometry consistency, and lighting variation. Our approach bridges the gap between compact Gaussian representations and large-scale scene capture, providing a practical pathway toward real-world 3D reconstruction at unprecedented scales. This poster highlights how scalable Gaussian splatting can empower researchers and creators to reconstruct immersive, photorealistic environments from everyday images.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation
key_takeaways: Learn how to scale Gaussian splatting for large, real-world 3D scenes without compromising visual fidelity or performance.
Discover new optimization strategies for handling memory and computational efficiency across wide indoor and outdoor environments.
Understand techniques to ensure geometric consistency and lighting robustness in unconstrained, in-the-wild scene captures.

title: Hybrid Inference With SLMs on Devices and NVIDIA NIM Microservices in the Cloud
abstract: We explore how small language models (SLMs) running on small devices integrate with NVIDIA NIM microservices running in the cloud or local GPU environments to enable real-time, hybrid AI inference. Our hybrid local-cloud inference approach combines on-device processing for instant interaction with GPU-accelerated reasoning in the cloud. This solution achieves latency under 200 ms and is expected to significantly reduce bandwidth usage - potentially by up to 10× - while scaling across industries from consumer electronics to healthcare and retail. Built with NVIDIA technologies including NeMo, TensorRT, and Triton, we demonstrate how hybrid inference brings AI closer to the user, powering natural, multi-modal experiences directly on devices.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, OVX, NeMo, TAO Toolkit, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, cuVS
key_takeaways: Learn how small language models run directly on small devices to deliver real-time, low-latency AI experiences.
Discover how NVIDIA NIM microservices, combined with TensorRT and Triton, enable scalable GPU-accelerated inference for hybrid edge-cloud systems.
See how NVIDIA NeMo, DeepStream, and cuVS work together to power multi-modal intelligence - merging speech, vision, and reasoning.
Explore a real-world augmented reality retail use case that demonstrates <200 ms interaction and explores the potential for up to 10× lower bandwidth consumption.
Understand how this hybrid inference approach scales across industries - from consumer electronics to healthcare and manufacturing.

title: Multilingual Benchmarking and Evaluation of Safety Guardrails in Low-Resource Languages
abstract: Safety guardrails remain biased toward high-resource languages, leaving low-resource communities vulnerable to harmful model behavior, cultural misalignment, and legal inconsistency. We introduce UbuntuGuard, a new multilingual benchmark for safety evaluation across 11 African languages. We generate contextualized, policy-grounded dialogues using NVIDIA Nemo-Curator, with policies authored via GPT-5. Policies and dialogues are translated into languages such as Yoruba, Swahili, and others to create a diverse Multilingual Policy-Dialogue dataset. We evaluate leading guardian models, including NVIDIA Nemoguard-8B, uncovering significant multilingual safety gaps, especially under cross-lingual settings. Results show alignment between dialogue and policy language is critical, highlighting the need for adaptive, culturally grounded guardrails that operate reliably across linguistic boundaries.
topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI
key_takeaways: Today's safety AI guardrails work well for English, but may fail to reliably protect users in many African languages.
We built UbuntuGuard, the first benchmark to evaluate multilingual safety guardrails using policy-based dialogues.
NVIDIA technologies (Nemo-Curator + Nemoguard-8B) help generate and assess culturally aware safety scenarios at scale.
Guardian models struggle when policies and conversations are in different languages, revealing major cross-lingual safety risks.
This work highlights the need for safety systems that adapt to cultural context, because safety standards are not universal.

title: In-Storage ETL: Fast In-Place Dataset Transformation for Machine Learning
abstract:
Most extract, transform, load (ETL) workflows pull data out of object storage into client machines or servers for preprocessing, augmentation, or custom transformations. This lacks scalability, and unnecessary data movement often degrades performance significantly. Unlike other ETL tools, in-storage ETL performs transformations on the same machines where your data lives, minimizing redundant transfers by exploiting data locality.

We introduce an in-storage ETL system that runs I/O-intensive transformations close to the data. It supports inline transformations for real-time training, and offline batch processing where results are stored in a destination bucket. We'll explore its architecture and benefits, highlighting linear scalability and high performance. Learn how to accelerate ETL pipelines, streamline workflows, and scale seamlessly from a real-world example of data preprocessing.
key_takeaways: Understand how in-storage ETL executes data transformations directly on storage nodes to minimize data movement.
Discover how containerized, language-agnostic design enables flexible user-defined ETL development across runtimes.
See a real-world example demonstrating streamlined ETL workflows and efficient data preprocessing at scale.

title: Agentic UI in XR Solutions With NVIDIA and Apple Vision Pro
abstract:
We're at a pivotal moment to reimagine how we interact in extended reality (XR), shifting from complex, traditional interfaces to intuitive, conversational experiences powered by voice and AI. This evolution lets us focus on creativity and exploration, not learning mechanics. 

The Agentic UI architecture reshapes the traditional interaction model by introducing intelligent, conversational interfaces accelerated by NVIDIA's AI and rendering stack using the following design pillars:  

*	Natural Language Interface: Replace traditional user interface (UI) controls with conversational AI 
*	Context Awareness: Maintain rich understanding of user intent and application state 
*	Proactive Assistance: Anticipate user needs and provide intelligent guidance
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CloudXR, Riva, Omniverse, Blackwell, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Reimagining XR Interaction: Discover how voice-driven, AI-powered interfaces replace traditional panels, buttons, and controllers to create natural, conversational interaction in XR.
Intelligent, Context-Aware Agents: Learn how NVIDIA's NeMo Agent Toolkit enables XR applications to understand user intent, maintain context, and provide proactive, goal-oriented assistance.
Seamless Apple Vision Pro Integration: Explore a reference implementation that unites NVIDIA Riva speech services, spatial audio, and RealityKit rendering for immersive, multi-modal interaction.
Scalable, Enterprise-Ready Architecture: See how the Agentic UI supports enterprise-scale deployment with optimized performance, context management, and real-time 3D collaboration.

title: Agentic Engineering Development: Planning, Coding, Deployment
abstract:
Our Prometheus framework for agentic engineering coordinates planning, coding, and deployment agents to automate software development. Explore the unification of human oversight and AI agents, in which we offer a new approach to the software development life cycle. Discover the benefits of our framework and the lessons we learned while working on it. 

The system consists of a multi-pronged approach. Planning involves gathering project requirements, business insights, and creating epics and tasks. We combine this with coding, frontend, backend and testing efforts, deployment, and verification.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Agentic engineering in Prometheus structures AI development across planning, coding, and deployment for faster, more reliable delivery.
Automation in requirement generation and code creation reduces rework, accelerates cycles, and improves alignment between teams.
Feedback-driven deployment agents enable continuous improvement and quality assurance.
Human oversight and structured validation keep agentic systems aligned with business goals.

title: Persona-Aware AI: Fine-Tuning Language Models With Reinforcement Learning From Human Feedback (RLHF)
abstract:
RLHF Persona Adaptation is a practical way to fine-tune foundational AI models so chatbots can perfectly match specific user personas and domains. 

From a cold start (or with some synthetic dialogues to get going), we tune the model using human feedback. Once it's good, we register it and put it into action. As people interact with it, we collect both explicit and implicit feedback. If we spot any changes, that triggers a scheduled retraining, creating a continuous loop of personalization. 

Accelerated by NVIDIA NIM, this system has a consistent tone, style, and content, while keeping everything governed through its model registry and data store. When tested on datasets for business, technical support, and customer service, our RLHF-tuned versions consistently beat the base model. They performed best in their specific target domains, improving response quality and user engagement.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, DGX Cloud, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) helps models adapt to changing user preferences and contexts over time.
Fine-tuned models produce outputs that are more accurate, natural, and relevant to the situation.
Persona-aware interactions improve user satisfaction and make communication smoother.
With NVIDIA NIM and NeMo Curator, feedback collection and model retraining are automated.

title: Scaling Trust: Multi-Tenant Compliance-Aware RAG on NVIDIA H200-Powered AI Pods
abstract: Discover how we use NVIDIA H200 GPUs to power a multi-tenant, multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that automates compliance comparison and enterprise knowledge access. Our approach reduces regulatory review time from hours to minutes, achieving high accuracy across complex technical documents, tables, and diagrams. Discover how GPU-accelerated AI turns compliance into a scalable, multilingual service for modern enterprises.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NVIDIA NIM, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Learn how NVIDIA H200 GPUs and Cisco UCS AI servers enable real-time, large-scale AI compliance analysis across multiple organizations.
See how a multi-tenant RAG architecture delivers scalable, low-overhead deployment with ~90% automation.
Explore how AI-driven rule extraction and comparison reduce compliance review time by over 99%.
Understand how multi-modal and multilingual AI improves accuracy across complex technical text, tables, and diagrams.
Learn how secure multi-tenancy and role-based separation protect sensitive data.

title: Qwen3-Swallow: Continual Pre-Training of Japanese-English Thinking LLMs With Megatron-LM
abstract:
While state-of-the-art LLMs achieve remarkable reasoning in English, their Japanese capabilities often lag. We present Qwen3-Swallow - a family of Japanese-English LLMs developed through continual pre-training (CPT) of open-weight Qwen3 models using LLM-refined corpora and FP8-accelerated training.

To construct CPT data, we employed TensorRT-LLM for large-scale, cost-efficient LLM inference, accelerating dataset rewriting and synthetic data generation for Japanese, mathematical, and code domains.
During training, TransformerEngine with FP8 blockwise scaling improved computational efficiency by 20% in FLOP/s per GPU with less than 0.4% loss deviation compared to BF16. The resulting models achieved +2.5-point average improvements in Japanese instruct and few-shot tasks while preserving English proficiency, providing a scalable blueprint for sovereign AI-aligned bilingual model adaptation.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: TensorRT, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, NCCL, NeMo
key_takeaways: Continual pre-training enables efficient bilingual adaptation of open-weight LLMs.
FP8 blockwise scaling via TransformerEngine improves training throughput by 20% with minimal loss deviation.
TensorRT-LLM accelerates LLM inference for large-scale dataset rewriting and synthetic data generation.
Qwen3-Swallow achieves +2.5-point gains in Japanese performance while maintaining English capability.
Qwen3-Swallow demonstrates a reproducible framework for cost-efficient, sovereign AI-driven bilingual LLM development.

title: A Collaborative Multi-Agent AI System for Scalable High School Transcript Processing
abstract: College admissions offices face a major challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts with varying formats, grading systems, and layouts. In this paper, we present a multi-agent AI system that automates this process through intelligent coordination among specialized agents. The architecture includes a Pattern Recognition Agent, Semantic Analysis Agent, Visual Intelligence Agent, and an Orchestration Agent that manages communication and result integration. Using GPA extraction as a coordination signal, the system ensures reliable collaboration and prevents information loss. Tested on 40 real-world transcripts from 13 U.S. states, it achieved 96.7% accuracy versus expert review, processing each document in 45 seconds. This demonstrates a scalable, collaborative AI solution that preserves human-level accuracy while greatly reducing processing time.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, cuBLAS, cuFFT
key_takeaways: Multi-agent AI systems
Agent coordination
Document automation
High school transcript processing

title: A Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework for Reliable Estimated Time of Arrival Prediction
abstract: Explore how we leverage deep learning to build a scalable, multi-task estimated time of arrival (ETA) prediction system that improves delivery time accuracy and reliability across millions of DoorDash orders.
key_takeaways: We developed a multi-task deep learning framework that jointly predicts multiple ETA types to improve overall accuracy and consistency at scale.
Our architecture combines DeepNet, CrossNet, and Transformer encoders to capture complex spatial, temporal, and categorical relationships across orders.
We align predictive performance with business objectives using a decision layer that tailors ETA outputs for different use cases.
The deep learning architecture delivers a +185% improvement in consistency and a +123% boost in accuracy compared to our tree-based model baseline.

title: Recursive Transformer for Vision-Language Models
abstract: We propose a novel recursive transformer for vision-language models. The idea is to recursively reuse a certain part of parameters in the transformer to pursue better parameter efficiency and reduce memory footprint. Our methods use (1) a Multi-layer Connector that maps the output feature space to the input embedding distribution to alleviate misalignment; (2) a Multi-modal Expert - an independent connector expert for vision/language tokens; and (3) Multi-step Loss to enable a flexible number of recursions. This early attempt at a recursive vision-language model achieves a consistent 4% accuracy gain in vision-language tasks on multiple datasets under the same parameter size. It thus presents an effective way to boost the performance of a vision language model by reusing its parameters. It also shows potential for building more-efficient vision language model architectures.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, CV-CUDA, JetPack, NVLink / NVSwitch
key_takeaways: An early attempt in recursive transformer for vision-language model, which is an important topic for both academia and industry
The proposed method effectively alleviates the feature misalignment issue in recursive transformer.
It achieves promising performance on multi-modal tasks, e.g., a consistent 4% accuracy gain under the same parameter size.
It presents an effective way to boost the performance of a vision language model by reusing its parameters.
It shows potential to build more-efficient vision language model architectures.

title: Cut Engineering Time With a Digital Twin Framework: Case Study on Vision Inspection and Depalletizing
abstract: Since industrial robots in real factories perform a variety of tasks - including welding, inspection, and pick-and-place - the recently developed digital twin framework should be applicable to all tasks for practical on-site deployment. In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to verify the scalability of the digital twin framework by applying it not only to the welding shop line, but also to vision inspection and depalletizing tasks. The motion planning results for each task were deployed onsite, and it was verified that the engineering time can be reduced by over 88% without compromising productivity. These results confirm that the proposed framework can quickly adapt to diverse robotic workflows, improving performance and scalability across real production lines.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Explore how you can utilize a digital twin framework to expand its application across diverse robotic tasks in manufacturing domains.
In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to verify the scalability of the digital twin framework by applying it to vision inspection and depalletizing tasks.
The motion planning results for each task were deployed onsite, and it was verified that the engineering time can be reduced by over 88% without compromising productivity.

title: GPU-Accelerated Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials With cuEquivariance and MILAP
abstract: Explore new techniques to extend the spatial and temporal limits of machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIAP). Leverage MLIAP for distributed inference in molecular dynamics and cuEquivariance for accelerated performance, achieving 26× faster training and 12× faster inference.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Hopper, NCCL, NSight Systems
key_takeaways: 26× faster training with cuEquivariance - train larger MLIP models efficiently
13× faster inference, 6× larger systems with MLIAP + cuEquivariance
12× faster molecular dynamics, enabling longer and more complex simulations

title: Deploying 8B Models on Edge GPUs: 1.58-Bit Quantized LLM on CUDA Environment
abstract: Explore our edge-optimized quantization approach that enables full 8B-scale reasoning on Jetson Orin NX. By combining 1.58-bit and INT4 quantization with custom CUDA kernels and fused attention/Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) layers, we achieve 8B inference in just 2 GB VRAM - bringing large-scale generative AI fully offline.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Achieved full 8B-parameter LLM inference on Jetson Orin NX within only 2 GB VRAM through mixed-precision (1.58-bit + INT4) quantization
Developed custom CUDA kernels for INT4 embedding and fused QKV/Gate-UP layers to improve latency and efficiency
Enabled cloud-free, real-time reasoning on embedded GPUs - bringing large-scale generative AI to edge devices

title: Minimize Sim-to-Real Gap: Leveraging a Digital Twin Framework for New Vehicle Manufacturing Line With 28 Robots
abstract: When deploying robot motions generated with a digital twin, a large sim-to-real gap can lead to significant onsite engineering time. In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to assess the onsite deployment feasibility of the digital twin framework by applying it to a welding shop line for new vehicle manufacturing. By minimizing sim-to-real gap, the collision-free motion trajectories for each robot were deployed onsite within 18 minutes of engineering time per robot. These results highlight that the proposed framework is applicable even to large, line-scale factory environments, and our calibration method is effective for rapid onsite deployments of trajectories generated by the digital twin framework.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: Explore how you can deploy the result of digital twin framework in large, line-scale factory environments by minimizing sim-to-real gap.
We collaborated with Hyundai Motors to assess the onsite deployment feasibility of the digital twin framework for new vehicle manufacturing.
By minimizing sim-to-real gap, the collision-free motion trajectories were deployed onsite within 18 minutes of engineering time per robot.

title: AI-Powered Digital Twin for Rapid Verification in Robotic Manufacturing: Accelerate Motion Planning With Isaac Sim
abstract: We present an AI-driven offline programming (OLP) and digital-twin framework for factory-level automation in automotive manufacturing. By integrating GPU-accelerated motion generation with NVIDIA Isaac Sim for simulation-based validation, we demonstrate how manufacturers can rapidly verify safety, cycle time, and precision before physical deployment, accelerating the digital transformation of production lines.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
key_takeaways: We automated the labor-intensive task of reprogramming robot motions for new product launches using an AI-driven motion generation system.
By leveraging GPU-accelerated AI, we generated collision-free, efficient robot motions and deployed them in Kia's manufacturing line for measurable productivity gains.
We validated safety, reachability, and cycle time through NVIDIA Isaac Sim, ensuring that simulated results aligned with real-world operations.
Comparisons between simulated and actual execution confirmed high reliability, proving the simulator's strong applicability to industrial use cases.
This study demonstrates a scalable framework that integrates AI motion planning, simulation validation, and robot control - advancing factory-level digital transformation.

title: Scaling Cross-Site LLM Inference: Low-Latency KV-Cache Sharing over >1,000 km With NIXL and DOCA
abstract: Explore new solutions for cross-site LLM inference: we raise KV-cache hit rates by locating partial caches across data centers and blending them during prefill, while NIXL unifies remote direct memory access (RDMA) transports and offloads pacing/retries/queues to the DPU to keep GPU-direct storage unblocked and cut per-transfer overhead. You can improve tokens per second, time-to-first-token, and energy efficiency over hundreds to thousands of kilometers, making regional deployment practical now; in our tests at ~1,000 km we achieved ~2× higher throughput versus the baseline and ~3× better energy efficiency.
key_takeaways: NIXL unifies multiple RDMA protocols behind one control plane and API, reducing per-transfer overhead and improving cross-site scalability.
Consistent hashing across data centers plus CacheBlend merging of partial KV blocks raises cache hit rates over prefix hashing and lowers time-to-first-token.
Under long-haul conditions, NIXL with DOCA and GPU-direct storage matches or beats TCP-based sharing in tokens/s across hundreds to thousands of kilometers.
Understand how offloading retries and pacing to the DPU preserves GDS performance at long RTTs.
Together, these choices deliver higher throughput and better energy efficiency than isolated sites or prefix-hashing baselines at regional scale.

title: Glasses-Free 3D Smart Educational Training and Decision Support Platform for Radiation Oncology Treatment
abstract: We present a light field-based 3D planning platform that enhances visualization, precision, and workflow efficiency in radiation therapy planning. The system integrates advanced eye-tracking, light-field rendering, and GPU-accelerated computation to provide real-time, glasses-free 3D visualization of anatomical structures and radiation dose distributions. By performing automated image segmentation and continuous ray-tracing calculations, the platform enables clinicians to instantly identify underdosed or overdosed regions and make rapid adjustments. Clinical trials demonstrate significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency, reducing the experience-based planning gap from 67 minutes to 16 minutes. Integrating powerful GPU computing not only ensures real-time performance, but also establishes a new standard for intelligent, interactive, and precise radiation therapy planning.
key_takeaways: "Floating image module" uses light-field tech for glasses-free 3D, improving depth, reducing discomfort.
Module offers air-touch interaction, enabling finger manipulation, reducing load, and boosting therapy efficiency.
Module integrates AI segmentation, 3D reconstruction, processes DICOM data, and enhances dose accuracy.
GPU acceleration cuts segmentation time from hours to seconds, aiding research.
Trials show 50%+ planning time reduction, better perception, fewer SBRT errors.

speakers.name: ShengYao Huang
speakers.title: Director of the Department of Radiation Therapy

speakers.name: Ren-Jun Hsu
speakers.title: Cancer Biologist & Director of Radiobiology and Cancer Center
speakers.company: Hualien Tzu-Chi Hospital, Busshist Tzu-Chi Medical Foundation

title: End-to-End FP8 Training for Large Language Models: Higher Accuracy at Lower Cost
abstract:
Explore a plug-and-play FP8 training recipe that lets you train reasoning LLMs faster and with less memory-while matching BF16 accuracy-so you can scale context, batches, and experiments on the same budget. See how you can adopt it across pre-training, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning (RL) to unlock more results per GPU hour. 

Our end-to-end FP8 pipeline improves speed and memory use without changing your model or schedule. Learn how to apply it today to do more with fixed compute budgets and deliver stronger downstream results.

We'll also share an FP8-first workflow that maintains accuracy while cutting compute and memory overhead. You get practical steps to drop it into your pre-training and post-training stacks.

Dive deep into an inclusive, end-to-end FP8 approach that turns training efficiency into real task gains.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, CUDA, Hopper, NCCL, NVLink / NVSwitch, Triton
key_takeaways: See exactly where we use FP8, versus BF16/FP32, so you can adopt the recipe without changing your model or schedule.
Learn how the FP8 pipeline cuts step time and memory use, letting you run larger batches or longer context on the same GPUs while maintaining near-BF16 accuracy.
Walk away with a clear migration checklist for pre-training, fine-tuning, and RL-including optimizer states, scaling, and logging-that you can apply immediately.

speakers.name: Zhen LI
speakers.title: Executive Director, Associate Dean, Professor, and Director

title: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap in Physical AI With Real-Time Control Stack
abstract: We explore a real-time execution stack that unifies NVIDIA Isaac Robot Foundation Models, ROS2, and software-based motion control to achieve sub-millisecond, deterministic performance - enabling precise, synchronized actions in the real world and closing the sim-to-real gap in physical AI. We also introduce sensor-driven fine-tuning methods to improve real-world adaptability through residual policy learning and LoRA-based adaptation.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, TensorRT, Isaac, Omniverse
key_takeaways: Real-time ROS2/motion control stack enables sub-millisecond EtherCAT motion cycles on Jetson Orin NX.
Demonstrates a deterministic execution layer that enhances Isaac Manipulator's trajectory-tracking performance and responsiveness in real-time operation.
Comparative test with a 6-DOF arm controller shows 85% lower tracking error, confirming improved accuracy.
The experimental result highlights the importance of tightly coupled real-time execution in deploying foundation model-driven physical AI to real-world systems.
Outline a blueprint for sensor-driven fine-tuning for Isaac GR00T using LoRA and residual policy modules to adapt the foundation model on edge devices.

title: AI-Accelerated Materials Discovery: High-Throughput Virtual Screening of Novel Materials With NVIDIA ALCHEMI
abstract: Leveraging NVIDIA ALCEMI, which confers unprecedented AI-enabled batched simulation capabilities, we performed ultra-fast high-throughput virtual screening of catalysts for hydrogen generation and immersion coolant for GPUs. Our newly discovered material candidates are poised to unlock the potential for hydrogen-driven and AI-driven societies.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science
key_takeaways: NVIDIA ALCHEMI
Ultra-Fast High-Throughput Virtual Screening
Materials Discovery
Catalyst
Immersion Cooling Fluid

title: Autonomous AI Agent for End-to-End Component Data Extraction
abstract: Improve component data extraction performance by 6x (from two hours to 20 minutes) and achieve 100% accuracy using an autonomous generative AI agent. We detail our "Intelligent Processing Pipeline" and the "Data-Oriented Hybrid Cloud" (Wiwynn OCP Grand Teton servers + 8x NVIDIA H100s) that powers this solution.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)
key_takeaways: By implementing a fully autonomous, end-to-end agent, we reduced single-component processing time from two hours to 20 minutes (an 83% reduction), freeing specialized engineering talent to focus on high-value innovation.

Achieve 100% accuracy in complex technical data extraction by combining Parent Page Retrieval (to preserve critical context) with Context Engineering (to embed specific engineering terminology intent) before querying the LLM.

Build a secure-first "Data-Oriented Hybrid Cloud" by running an on-premises cluster (Wiwynn OCP servers with 8x NVIDIA H100s and NVAIE) for confidential data, while intelligently escalating high-demand, non-sensitive workloads to public LLMs.

title: CudaqToEinsum: Efficient Tensor-Network Conversion From CUDA Quantum for Large-Scale Quantum Circuit Simulation
abstract: We present CudaqToEinsum, a high-performance framework for converting quantum circuits into Tensor-network representations. Integrated tightly with CUDA Quantum, our method achieves 30-50× faster translation than the Qiskit + cuQuantum CircuitToEinsum route on 85,000-qubit BPS circuits. With native support for batched contraction, it processes over 150,000 samples simultaneously, achieving up to 200-300× speedups, paving the way for large-scale quantum simulations on HPC platforms.
key_takeaways: Fast Conversion: CudaqToEinsum converts CUDA Quantum circuits into Tensor-network form up to 50× faster than Qiskit-based workflows.
MLIR-Based Parsing: We directly extract circuit semantics from CUDA Quantum's MLIR, eliminating the need for manual topology handling.
Batch Tensor-Network Support: Our method enables batch contraction for large datasets, delivering up to 200-300× speedups in multi-sample inference.
Backend Agnostic: The output is standardized and can be plugged into cuQuantum, PyTorch, or other contraction libraries without modification.
HPC-Ready: Designed for multi-GPU and multi-node execution, paving the way for scalable quantum circuit simulation on HPC platforms.

title: Bioactivity Prediction via Spatial Emptiness Representation of Protein-Ligand Complex and Uniting Multiple Pockets
abstract:
Discover how spatial intelligence and pocket integration advance bioactivity prediction. We present DTIGN + LigoSpace, a next-gen framework that combines GeoREC - a geometric representation of spatial emptiness - and the Union-Pocket, a unified 3D structure merging multiple binding pockets of the same protein. Together, they capture how ligands truly fill and influence complex protein environments.

By integrating GeoREC for spatial representation, Union-Pocket for global context, and a pairwise learning strategy for accurate ligand ranking, our model delivers significant gains in bioactivity prediction. It learns not only if a ligand binds, but how completely it occupies its target, revealing the link between pocket filling and real-world potency. Accelerated on NVIDIA H200 GPUs, this approach achieves up to 96% faster inference.
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, Ethernet Networking, BioNeMo, cuDDN, cuGraph, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NCCL, NeMo, OpenGL, Triton, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: New Spatial Paradigm: Learn how GeoREC quantifies 3D spatial emptiness within protein-ligand complexes to capture how completely a ligand occupies its target site.
Unified Structural Context: Explore how the Union-Pocket merges multiple binding pockets into one global 3D frame, improving model understanding of full protein environments.
Ranking-Based Learning: See how pairwise loss improves ligand ranking accuracy and reflects realistic differences in bioactivity.
Stronger Generalization: Discover performance gains across diverse datasets, achieving higher correlation, lower root mean square error, and more consistent prediction of true ligand potency.
GPU-Accelerated Discovery: Experience how NVIDIA H200 GPUs cut inference time by 96%, enabling rapid, geometry-aware bioactivity prediction at scale.

title: Enhance Production of Synthetic Radar Images From Geostationary Satellite Observations With Generative Diffusion Models
abstract: Explore how generative diffusion models can transform geostationary satellite and lightning observations into realistic, high-resolution radar reflectivity imagery. This work introduces a probabilistic approach that improves key metrics and reproduces fine-scale storm structures, helping extend radar-like coverage to regions without operational radar systems.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, NCCL, Triton, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Introduces a generative diffusion model to synthesize radar reflectivity from Himawari-8 satellite and lightning data.
Demonstrates a 25% improvement in the Fractions Skill Score (FSS), with finer storm structures and spatial detail.
Provides probabilistic radar ensembles that represent uncertainty in satellite-to-radar transformation.
Bridges satellite and radar observations to extend severe-weather monitoring beyond existing radar networks.
Highlights the potential of diffusion models for AI-driven remote sensing and geophysical data generation.

title: Emotion-Aware Voice Agent
abstract: Our goal is to develop emotion-aware voice agents capable of understanding and responding with appropriate emotion. Traditional agents, relying solely on text-based sentiment analysis, miss critical emotional cues conveyed through speech. Our research integrates multi-modal sentiment analysis, processing both the semantic content (text) and the prosodic features (speech) to create a holistic emotional understanding. This allows for more natural, empathetic, and engaging human-computer interactions.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, Riva, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Multi-modal Sentiment: Parallel speech + text analysis with ensemble fusion
LLM/Text-to-Speech Awareness: Inject emotion feature into prompting and prosody controls
Switchable Text Sentiment: DeBERT, Speech LLM-based
Latency-First Design: Streaming speech-to-text, async sentiment sidecar, non-blocking feedback.

title: A Multi-Agent Debate Framework for Automated NVIDIA Cosmos Optimization in Surgical Robot Training
abstract: We present HMARCF-HILFOF, the first multi-agent agentic AI system integrated with NVIDIA Cosmos Physical AI for automated surgical robot training. By combining multi-expert debate reasoning with physics-based world simulation, we automate scenario design while ensuring clinical quality. HMARCF coordinates four agents - Thoracic Surgery, Robot Learning, Cosmos Engineering, and Imaging/Radiology - through structured debates to produce auditable, versioned Cosmos configurations (angles/tissue/dynamics/physics). HILFOF provides human-in-the-loop quality assurance with quantitative metrics (VACS >0.85, SBF >0.90) and feedback-driven regeneration. In preliminary phantom-based validation of thoracic needle insertion, the system expanded scenario diversity from 10 (manual baseline) to 30+ (5 angles × 3 tissues × 2 dynamics) while reducing prompt engineering time from hours to minutes.
key_takeaways: Multi-agent debate (four roles, including Radiology) systematically converts expert constraints into auditable Cosmos configs, not ad-hoc prompts.
Scenario diversity rises 3× (≥30 vs. 10; angles × tissues × dynamics) while prompt/parameter time falls from hours to minutes.
QA loop (HILFOF) enforces VACS > 0.85 and SBF > 0.90, gating acceptance and driving automatic regeneration.
Reproducible and Traceable: Fixed seeds, YAML configs, scenario matrix, debate logs, and config hashes for artifact review.
Clinically Oriented Path: Phantom results today; IRB plan with target gates (error < 2.0 mm, VACS > 0.90, failure < 2%) toward multi-site standardization.

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
