title: Design and Optimization of GPU-Aware MPI Allreduce Using Direct Sendrecv Communication
abstract: Explore new techniques for accelerating GPU-aware MPI Allreduce using direct send/receive communication with throttling. Our design improves scalability and overlaps computation with communication, achieving up to 7% faster nanoGPT training while maintaining strong performance across diverse workloads.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries
nvidia_technology: Grace CPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Interconnect Networking, NCCL
key_takeaways: Learn how a Direct Send/Receive Allreduce design eliminates bottlenecks in traditional ring-based communication.
See how throttling enables scalable GPU communication across hundreds of nodes without overloading the network.
Discover how computation-communication overlap and kernel fusion jointly boost efficiency for medium message sizes.
Understand how our design achieves up to 7% faster nanoGPT training compared to NCCL on large GPU clusters.
Explore how this approach extends to real workloads, improving end-to-end performance across diverse systems.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.title: Professor and Distinguished Scholar of Computer Science

title: AI-Based Software Quantifies Fatty Liver Disease From MRI-PDFF Images Using Feature Extraction to Reduce False Positives
abstract: Our system integrates neural networks with SVM-based feature extraction to automatically detect liver regions in MRI-PDFF images while minimizing false positives. By employing whole-liver quantification instead of traditional ROI sampling, it objectively computes mean PDFF values for more accurate and consistent fatty liver assessment and follow-up.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
key_takeaways: Integrates neural networks with SVM-based feature extraction for automatic liver detection in MRI-PDFF.
Automatically computes mean liver PDFF to quantify steatosis severity.
Employs whole-liver quantification instead of ROI sampling to eliminate inter-observer variability.
Enables objective tracking of liver fat changes during treatment.

title: Secured Multi-Agent Earth Observation Systems with NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
abstract: This poster presents a Ridge-i project that integrates NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails into a multi-agent Earth Observation (EO) chatbot to improve the safety, accuracy, and maintainability of on-premises AI systems used for satellite-data analysis. The team replaced custom prompt-based guardrails with NeMo's framework to enforce bidirectional input/output safety, jailbreak detection, and content moderation. Benchmarks on 224 EO queries showed faster customization (-50%), smaller codebase (-66.7%), and higher multi-turn jailbreak accuracy (+71.9%) with only a small latency increase. Overall, NeMo Guardrails strengthened security and efficiency for government-grade EO deployments while simplifying development and maintenance.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: Ship Faster: ~33% less initial build time and ~50% less new-project customization; ~66.7% smaller guardrail codebase.
Stronger Safety: +71.9% accuracy on complex multi-turn jailbreaks with only ~12% latency overhead; legitimate EO queries run ~35% faster end-to-end.
Adoptable Pattern: Input/output rails + jailbreak/content checks using NeMo Guardrails and Colang - replace brittle prompt rules.
Enterprise-Ready: Works on-premises/air-gapped; supports tenant-specific policies and domain packs for SAR/optical/change-detection use cases.

speakers.name: Waku Hatakeyama
speakers.title: Executive Officer of Satellite Data Analysis AI, Principal Engineer

title: Single View X-Ray Novel View Synthesis With World Foundation Model
abstract: Explore how we specialized NVIDIA's Cosmos-Predict2 world foundation model to generate high-fidelity, novel diagnostic views from a single frontal chest X-ray. This method provides a new solution for enhancing diagnostic confidence while aiming to reduce patient radiation exposure by minimizing the need for additional scans.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, MONAI, Cosmos
key_takeaways: Discover how we specialized a physics-aware world foundation model (NVIDIA's Cosmos-Predict2) for the high-fidelity medical task of X-ray novel view synthesis.
Understand our data curation process, which uses volume rendering from 3D CT scans to create physically accurate and perfectly aligned digitally reconstructed radiographs (DRRs) for training.
Learn about the training process, which treats 360-degree views as a video and uses a phased loss function approach (EDM, reconstruction and LPIPS ) on an NVIDIA A100 GPU.

Understand how this method begins a paradigm shift from static 2D images (X-rays) to 3D representations (CT scans), aiming to reduce the high radiation dose and complex clinical workflows  associated with CT scans.

title: TuringData Multi-Tier KVCache Open Architecture: Accelerating AI Inference Performance
abstract: TuringData has introduced a unified KVCache software platform designed for large-scale AI inference. By leveraging NVIDIA H20 GPUs and high-speed InfiniBand networks, the platform seamlessly expands GPU memory and optimizes KVCache management across multi-tier storage layers, significantly boosting inference performance and resource efficiency. It enables smooth integration with mainstream inference frameworks such as vLLM and SGLang, fully utilizing existing GPU memory and NVMe resources without additional hardware. Through advanced cache-matching algorithms, TuringData KVCache delivers notable acceleration in real-world user testing, and future software upgrades with enhanced compression and caching strategies will further improve inference speed and efficiency.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, AGX, DGX Platform, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, CUDA-X, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Learn how TuringData's open multi-tier KVCache architecture extends GPU memory by transparently leveraging DRAM, NVMe, and remote storage to overcome the memory bottleneck in large-scale LLM inference.

Discover how intelligent cache matching and tiered data scheduling significantly boost cache hit rates and maintain inference accuracy, enabling higher concurrency while keeping Time-to-First-Token under two seconds.

See how integration with frameworks like vLLM and SGLang, combined with GPUDirect Storage acceleration, reduces latency, improves bandwidth, and enhances overall user experience in long-context and multi-turn dialogue scenarios.

speakers.name: Long Chen
speakers.title: NVIDIA 网络市场开发总监

title: Multi-Vector Variational Generative Framework for Personalized Cognitive Modeling and Training
abstract: We introduce an AI-driven framework that uses NVIDIA MONAI and GPU acceleration to quantitatively model brain aging and cognitive trajectories from MRI, addressing the limitations of subjective cognitive assessments. This timely research enables earlier risk prediction, personalized cognitive training, and future development of avatar-based interfaces powered by NVIDIA NeMo technologies.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
key_takeaways: Explore how NVIDIA MONAI enables quantitative MRI analysis for early detection of brain aging and cognitive decline.
Discover our MV-VAE and generative adversarial network approach for individualized brain-aging and cognitive-trajectory modeling.
See how quantitative imaging and behavioral data integration support personalized cognitive training and early-risk prediction.
Learn how GPU acceleration improved efficiency and demonstrated high predictive accuracy across multiple clinical validation sites.
Preview future integration of NVIDIA NeMo and Llama Nemotron in our avatar-based human-machine interface research.

speakers.name: Yi Tien Lee
speakers.title: Associate Research Fellow and Assistant Professor
speakers.company: Taipei Medical University Research Center for Neuroscience, TMU Ph.D. Program in Medical Neuroscience

speakers.name: Yung-Chieh Chen
speakers.title: Attending Neuroradiologist and Assistant Professor of Radiology

speakers.name: Cheng Yu Chen
speakers.title: Chief, Section of Neuroradiology, Taipei Medical University Hospital; and Director, Translational Imaging Research Center, TMUH

title: UniLEAD: A Unified and Lightweight Model for Anomaly Detection
abstract: We introduce UniLEAD, a lightweight framework that improves anomaly detection across diverse visual domains through mixture-of-adapters and deterministic routing. Explore how our approach delivers state-of-the-art accuracy with minimal parameters - making efficient, interpretable anomaly detection accessible for real-world industrial and medical applications.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: Parameter-Efficient: Achieves high accuracy with only 1.42M parameters and 1.93 GFLOPs.
Deterministic Routing: Ensures stable optimization and predictable inference cost.
Mixture-of-Adapters: Captures both global structures and fine-grained anomalies efficiently.
Interpretable Results: Produces sharp, transparent anomaly maps via feature jittering and reconstruction.
Broad Impact: Delivers state-of-the-art AUROC on MVTec AD, LOCO, and VisA datasets.

title: Conversational AI Agent for Transportation Planning
abstract: We present a hybrid AI agent that combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and LoRA-tuned models to achieve 95.7% accuracy in generating transportation planning commands from natural language, reducing technical barriers and letting planners focus on analytical insights instead of memorizing command-line interface (CLI) syntax.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU
key_takeaways: Hybrid RAG + LoRA fine-tuning outperforms either approach alone, achieving 95.7% accuracy compared to 71.3% (RAG-only) and 89.7% (fine-tuning-only) for CLI command generation.
GPU-accelerated training on eight A100s reduces development time to ~5 hours, while RTX A6000 enables real-time conversational inference with ~1.6s latency.
Multi-turn context tracking enables complex workflows where users can chain commands naturally across conversations without repeating context.
The framework generalizes to any CLI tool with documentation, requiring only synthetic training examples to adapt the approach to new domains.

title: Scene-Aware Summarization for Long and Multi-Video RAG
abstract: We unveil a novel multi-modal LLM framework designed for understanding and retrieving insights from long-form or multi-video content. Our approach introduces hierarchical scene-aware summarization, enabling faster, more coherent reasoning over extended video inputs. This method significantly enhances retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by structuring video data into narratively meaningful segments, supporting efficient and scalable downstream analysis.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
key_takeaways: Understand how multi-modal LLMs leverage summarization-based RAG to efficiently process long or multi-video inputs.
Discover a hierarchical scene-aware segmentation technique that captures both fine-grained and macro-level narrative transitions.
See how scene-level summarization leads to quantifiable gains in video understanding tasks - outperforming conventional chunk-based baselines.
Observe how scene-aware RAG enhances cross-video moment retrieval and unlocks practical applications like multi-video Q&A, narrative tracing, and ad alignment.

title: Build AI Libraries for NVIDIA GPUs in Pure Modern Java With TornadoVM
abstract:
Discover how TornadoVM opens the NVIDIA GPU ecosystem to millions of Java developers, a community where GPU adoption has traditionally been extremely low. We demonstrate how TornadoVM's hybrid API enables pure Java code to compile to PTX and interoperate with NVIDIA libraries like cuDNN, bridging GPU compute with the broader JVM ecosystem through frameworks such as GraalVM, LangChain4j, and Quarkus.

As an open-source framework, TornadoVM provides a seamless bridge between Java applications and NVIDIA's developer tooling, allowing profiling, debugging, and optimization of GPU kernels directly from Java. This work highlights how GPU acceleration can become a first-class capability for the JVM community, unlocking new opportunities for AI and high-performance computing in pure modern Java.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, cuBLAS, cuDDN, NSight Compute, NSight Systems, Blackwell
key_takeaways: NVIDIA hardware can be accessed by Java developers with minimal exposure to GPUs, all in an open-source environment.
NVIDIA tooling can work seamlessy with Java applications using TornadoVM.
Complete libraries can be built in Java, compiled to PTX directly, and integrated with the JVM ecosystem.
JIT-compiled PTX from Java can be mixed with libraries like cuDNN or cuBLAS, all within pure Java.

title: LFQUIAD: Lookup-Free Quantized Autoencoder for Few-Shot Unsupervised Industrial Anomaly Detection
abstract: Explore how we generate realistic synthetic anomalies using diffusion-based inpainting to train a quantized autoencoder without relying on large codebooks. Learn how our LFQUIAD framework improves anomaly detection and segmentation with minimal real data, offering a scalable and plug-and-play solution for industrial and medical inspection.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: Generate synthetic anomalies using diffusion-based inpainting to overcome limited or ambiguous real data.
Leverage lookup-free quantization to discretize latent features without a codebook, improving generalization and stability.
Enable few-shot unsupervised training, achieving state-of-the-art performance on MVTecAD and VisA with minimal normal samples.
Adopt a modular, plug-and-play anomaly generation module easily adaptable to other anomaly detection pipelines.
Empower industrial inspection through a lightweight, interpretable, and scalable anomaly detection solution.

title: Next-Generation Distributed Storage Architecture: Low-Latency and Network Optimization for AI Workloads
abstract: Dive deep into our next-generation distributed storage architecture, optimized for KVCache offloading and prefill-decode disaggregation. Explore key techniques for achieving ultra-low latency and high throughput, addressing critical I/O bottlenecks and North-South network efficiency in GPU-centric AI inference clusters.
key_takeaways: Learn how we achieve millisecond-level latency through a reengineered single-process I/O stack and direct app-to-disk data paths.
Discover the benefits of reengineered network stack for low-latency transmission and maximized bandwidth.
See how the Unique Network mode with Spectrum-X and BF3 adaptive routing delivers optimal throughput and dynamic congestion control.

speakers.name: Pei-Feng Liu
speakers.title: Principal Architect
speakers.company: SuperCloud Digital Technology Group Co., Ltd.

speakers.name: Rui Wu
speakers.title: R&D Director
speakers.company: SuperCloud Digital Technology Group Co., Ltd.

title: VLA-Cache: Efficient Vision-Language-Action Manipulation via Adaptive Token Caching
abstract: We present a training-free approach that accelerates multi-modal robotic control by reusing redundant visual information across frames. This work introduces adaptive token caching that cuts inference latency by up to 1.7× while preserving task accuracy, enabling faster and more efficient real-world robot actions.
key_takeaways: Reuse static visual tokens across frames to eliminate redundant computation.
Filter task-relevant regions using attention signals to maintain semantic fidelity.
Adaptively reuse tokens across layers for optimal efficiency-accuracy balance.
Achieve up to 1.7× faster inference and 15% higher control frequency.
Transfer seamlessly from simulation to real-world robotic manipulation.

title: Galaxea Open-World Dataset and G0 Dual-System VLA Model
abstract: We present the Galaxea Open-World Dataset, a large and diverse collection of robotic behaviors captured in real human environments. Demonstrations are collected with a consistent robotic embodiment and annotated with precise subtask-level language descriptions for both training and evaluation. Building on this dataset, we introduce G0, a dual-system framework combining a vision-language model (VLM) for multi-modal planning and a vision-language-action (VLA) model for fine-grained execution. G0 is trained through a three-stage curriculum: cross-embodiment pre-training, single-embodiment pre-training, and task-specific post-training. Extensive benchmarks on tabletop manipulation, few-shot learning, and long-horizon mobile manipulation validate the approach, showing that single-embodiment pre-training, together with the Galaxea Dataset, is crucial for strong overall performance.
key_takeaways: Galaxea Open-World Dataset: Real-World, Consistent, and Language-Aligned. The dataset contains 100,000 trajectories across 131 tasks, involving over 1,600 objects and 58 operational skills. And it has been downloaded over 120,000 times, ranking No. 1 globally on Hugging Face and ModelScope. This demonstrates the community's strong demand for authentic embodied data.

G0 Dual-System Framework: Slow Thinking Meets Fast Execution.  Our G0 dual system is comprised of a fast-response System-1 and a deliberative System-2. System-1 is a VLA model responsible for perceiving the environment, interpreting subtask instructions, and executing actions. Meanwhile, System-2 operates as a VLM that processes high-level natural language task instructions, understands the scene, and subsequently plans subtask instructions for System-1.

Best Practices for Reliable and Standardized Robotics Evaluation. Standardized evaluation is crucial for measuring model progress, but robotics is a highly complex system, where tiny differences in hardware or environment can lead to large variations in results. Thus, evaluation is more a matter of system engineering than model engineering. To address this, we follow three best practices: first, perform log replay on the robot before each evaluation to verify environment settings and robot poses; second, ensure high performance in open-loop evaluation, as good open-loop results increase the likelihood of success in real-world closed-loop tests; and third, conduct parallel evaluation across multiple models to minimize the impact of small experimental differences and obtain accurate model rankings.

title: AI-Driven Discovery of Novel JAK3 Inhibitors Using NVIDIA NIM-Accelerated Computational Pipeline
abstract: We accelerated JAK3 inhibitor discovery from months to hours using NVIDIA NIM microservices with GenMol and DiffDock on RTX Ada 6000 GPUs. Our pipeline identified novel drug candidates with better binding than FDA-approved treatments. This end-to-end workflow runs on a single GPU and combines NIM-accelerated AI generation with physics-based validation. Pharmaceutical companies and academic researchers can deploy this modular NIM-powered framework to dramatically reduce drug development timelines and costs for any therapeutic target.
key_takeaways: AI-driven pipeline reduces drug discovery from months to hours while maintaining validation standards, accelerating time to market.
GenMol's fragment remasking with discrete diffusion explores beyond initial chemical spaces, identifying novel scaffolds outperforming FDA-approved drugs.
NVIDIA NIM integration of GenMol and DiffDock on RTX Ada 6000 GPUs enables seamless single-GPU pipeline execution.
The approach identified four pharmacophore archetypes with validated binding modes, demonstrating AI-generated molecules maintain chemical and biological relevance.
The modular workflow allows pharmaceutical companies and academic labs to customize each stage with end-to-end GPU acceleration.

title: Efficient On-Policy Distillation for Transferring Reasoning Capabilities via Parallel and Sparse Training
abstract: We present an efficient on-policy distillation framework enhanced by the novel Zero-trick, which mitigates logit alignment gaps in sparse distillation. Our work fills the gap of open-source on-policy distillation tools and drastically reduces computational overhead compared with full-logit distillation, making advanced reasoning capabilities more accessible for practical small-scale models.
key_takeaways: Learn how our on-policy distillation framework uses student-generated data to reduce distribution shift between training and inference.
Discover how the proposed Zero-trick fixes logit alignment gaps in sparse (top-K) distillation that otherwise leave the student's generative behavior unchanged.
Access a scalable, open-source distillation tool (via Nemo-RL) that supports multi-training backends (PyTorch DTensor, Megatron), vLLM inference, and parallel training (CP, TP, PP, DP).
See how that on-policy distillation outperforms off-policy methods on downstream tasks, with the Zero-trick further boosting model performance.

title: Benchmarking CUDA-Q Emulators Across Multiple GPU Generations
abstract: Explore how the performance of CUDA-Q emulators scales across the latest NVIDIA GPU generations and between single and multi-GPU setups to guide more efficient quantum-classical workloads.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Blackwell, CUDA-Q
key_takeaways: GPUs significantly outperform CPUs for CUDA-Q state vector simulations.
The latest Blackwell GPUs deliver up to 3.36x higher performance compared to the previous Hopper generation.
Multi-GPU setups show performance advantages as qubit counts increase.
Our insights can guide researchers in selecting optimal hardware configurations for hybrid quantum-classical computing.

title: Taylor-Series-Expansion-Based Transformer Models With Once-Tuning-Multiple-Variants Recipe
abstract:
We introduce a revolutionary approach to transformer architecture that addresses one of the most pressing challenges in modern AI: efficient model deployment without sacrificing accuracy. The work presents two key innovations, a Taylor-Series-Expansion (TSE) based model structure, and a Once-Tuning-Multiple-Variants (OTMV) recipe, that fundamentally rethink how transformers store and utilize parameters.

The TSE architecture challenges conventional wisdom by storing only one copy of first-order Taylor weights in physical memory, dramatically reduces on-chip memory requirements while maintaining model expressiveness.
The OTMV recipe's ability to create multiple model variants from a single tuning process directly addresses industry demands for flexible deployment across heterogeneous hardware ecosystems.
nvidia_technology: DRIVE, Jetson, DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, cuBLAS, cuDDN, CV-CUDA
key_takeaways: Dramatic Memory Reduction: TSE architecture stores only one copy of first-order Taylor weights, with other blocks sharing via scalar coefficients.
One Tuning, Multiple Deployments: OTMV recipe enables a single training session to create models adaptable to various hardware platforms without retraining.
Targets Real Hardware Bottlenecks: Focuses on memory access efficiency rather than just theoretical FLOPs, addressing actual deployment constraints.
Maintains Accuracy: Achieves negligible accuracy drops on various vision and vision-language tasks while improving deployment efficiency.
Validated by Top Venues: Published in IEEE T-PAMI 2025 and CVPR 2025, confirming scientific rigor and impact.

title: LoBE-GS: Load-Balanced and Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Scene Reconstruction
abstract: Explore new techniques for load-balanced 3D Gaussian splatting that make large-scale scene reconstruction faster and more accessible. We introduce LoBE-GS, a framework that balances computation across partitions to achieve up to 2x faster training while maintaining quality-enabling real-time visualization and interactive exploration through Omniverse streaming.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, Omniverse, OVX, OpenGL, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Load-Balanced Partitioning: Automatically balances per-block runtime to minimize bottlenecks and accelerate large-scale 3DGS training.
Proxy-Guided Optimization: Uses a lightweight visibility-based runtime proxy to drive efficient Bayesian scene partitioning.
Faster Convergence: Achieves up to 2x end-to-end speedup over prior large-scale 3DGS methods while preserving rendering fidelity.
Scalable Deployment: Supports real-time streaming and interaction via NVIDIA Omniverse with stable 30 FPS performance on laptops and phones.
Unified Export Pipeline: Merges and prunes optimized blocks into a single USDZ-ready model for collaborative visualization and playback.

title: A Diffusion Model Framework for Solving Scientific Inverse Problems
abstract: A key challenge in many scientific tasks is inferring the underlying scientific phenomenon from sparse and corrupted measurements, also known as inverse problems. A canonical example is imaging a black hole using the Event Horizon Telescope, which collects sparse radio waves that have been corrupted by the Earth's atmosphere. We have developed a framework based on diffusion models that can solve a broad range of scientific inverse problems, with applications that span medical imaging, astronomy, seismology, and climate science.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation
key_takeaways: A Gen AI model to help scientists infer hidden phenomena from complex measures (telescopes, seismometers, etc)

Our approach has been applied to astronomy (black hole imaging from radio-wave telescopes), seismology (inferring subsurface physical properties from ground seismometers), and other scientific applications.
We have released our framework and benchmark for others to build upon.

title: Power-Efficient AI in Space: THOR-SR Mission NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX for Onboard Computing
abstract:
The THOR-SR (Terrestrial Gamma-ray Flash [TGF] and High-energy Astrophysics Observatory for gamma Rays on board Space Rider) is a high-energy astrophysics technology being developed for flight in low Earth orbit aboard the European Space Agency's Space Rider vehicle during a two-month mission planned for Q4 2027. THOR-SR employs cutting-edge Timepix semiconductor detectors to enable high-resolution observations in gamma-ray astrophysics, TGFs, space radiation, and space-weather monitoring.

We address the integration of AI in space missions, which requires HPC under stringent power and radiation constraints, with a detailed characterization of the Jetson AI platform optimized for power-limited, non-radiation-hardened environments. Such edge devices can sustain full AI workloads within a 20-25 W power envelope, providing a viable alternative for next-generation space missions.
key_takeaways: The proposed AI platform is incorporated into the THOR instrument on board ESA's Space Rider vehicle, a space mission to be launched in Q4 2027.
The developed onboard computer meets all mission performance and reliability requirements.
The processing performance of the NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX was evaluated under multiple CPU, GPU, and memory clock configurations to assess their suitability for in-mission scientific data processing.
Thermal tests confirmed stable performance within the defined thermal envelope, supported by a custom passive cooling design.
Overall, the system shows that commercial embedded platforms can be effectively adapted for space applications through careful thermal, power, and software optimization.

speakers.name: Gabriel Falcao
speakers.title: Associate Professor and Visiting Associate Professor
speakers.company: Instituto de Telecomunições, University of Coimbra, New York University Abu Dhabi

title: Parallelization of Softened Gauss on GPU
abstract: We implemented a novel CUDA algorithm for simulating the motion of stars around supermassive black holes in galactic centers, or the motion of planetesimals in solar systems. Our approach enables the evolution of such systems over millions to billions of years, achieving simulation runtimes of days to weeks on GPUs, tasks that would otherwise take prohibitively long on CPUs. Our implementation demonstrates speedups of over 10x when comparing top-tier CPUs to the leading NVIDIA GPU (B200).
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, Hopper, NSight Compute, NSight Systems, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Optimize implementation of quadratures and cubatures on GPUs
Possibility of evolving large number of particle (rings) system around central potentials to solve fundamental physics problems
Generalized implementation of N-dimensional numerical integration for a vector of functions of arbitrary size
Benefits of FP64 performance for scientific applications

title: Weight Space Learning From Hugging Face ModelZoos
abstract: The weights of neural networks have become a novel data modality, creating the field of weight space learning. Learning meaningful representations of weights typically requires large, carefully constructed collections of trained models, typically referred to as model zoos. These model zoos are often trained ad-hoc, requiring large computational resources, constraining the learned weight space representations in scale and flexibility. We drop this requirement by training a weight space learning backbone on arbitrary models downloaded from large, unstructured model repositories such as Hugging Face. Unlike curated model zoos, these repositories contain highly heterogeneous models: they vary in architecture and dataset, and are largely undocumented. Weight space representations trained on models from Hugging Face often outperform backbones trained on laboratory-generated model zoos.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Pre-Trained / Foundation Models
key_takeaways: The weights of neural networks have emerged as a novel data modality, giving rise to the field of weight space learning (WSL).
Weight space learning has a significant impact of how we analyze and train neural networks, and drastically improves training time and accuracy.

To scale weight space learning, we need to enable WSL to train from arbitrary models as found on public model hubs, such as Hugging Face, allowing WSL to distill the knowledge of potentially up to 2 million models.

title: GPU-Accelerated Simulations of Flow-Structure Interaction at Extreme Scales
abstract: Numerical simulation of the Navier-Stokes equation is among the most complex problems in computational sciences. The complexity increases tremendously when incorporating moving boundaries and modeling interactions with multiple physics, such as structural mechanics, acoustics, and more. We present a sharp-interface immersed boundary method and accelerate it using GPUs to simulate some of the most complex problems currently beyond the capabilities of many industrial solvers.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, HPC SDK, Hopper, cuBLAS, CUDA-X
key_takeaways: Computational fluid dynamics with moving bodies for various flow regimes is extremely hard. We present a sharp-interface immersed boundary method to make it possible.
The method involves highly sequential algorithms, and we present its acceleration on multi-GPU clusters along with gains over CPU setups.
This makes simulating complex problems possible, impacting industries such as design, healthcare, aerospace, automative and ecology.

title: HyperSack: Resource-Aware Distributed Hyperparameter Optimization for Lightweight Vision and Language Models on GPUs
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization (HPO) can unlock the full potential of deep learning (DL) models, but it's one of the most compute-intensive tasks in DL. Existing HPO parallelization strategies underutilize powerful GPUs, especially for training lightweight deep neural networks. Resource-sharing mechanisms such as multi-process service can improve GPU utilization, but naive adaptations for HPO workloads lead to poor performance. We present HyperSack: a distributed HPO framework for dynamic and resource-aware scheduling on GPU-based HPC systems. HyperSack cuts the execution time of HPO workloads by orchestrating efficient placement of DL training jobs. Our tests on vision and language models HPO workloads show up to 2.8x performance improvement in execution time on AI00s, 4.0x on HI00s, and 3.9x on a combination of AI00s and HI00s using HyperSack over standard HPO parallelization methods.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, cuDDN, Interconnect Networking, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NSight Systems
key_takeaways: HyperSack is a distributed HPO framework that enables dynamic, resource-aware scheduling on heterogeneous GPU-based HPC systems.
Define the GPU assignment problem (GAP) by modeling GPU compute, GPU memory, and CPU compute constraints to guide efficient workload placement
Explore scheduling policies that map GAP to the multiple knapsack problem (MKP) to maximize GPU utilization during HPO
Implement a profiling and history-based scheduling scheme that uses utilization metrics and prior job data to optimize runtime decisions
Demonstrate up to 4x faster HPO execution on modern GPU clusters, improving performance across A100, H100, and mixed GPU environments

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.title: Professor and Distinguished Scholar of Computer Science

title: Accelerate Robotic Grinding With Physics-Based Simulation and Synthetic Data
abstract: To solve the slow, manual tuning and costly physical trials hindering robotic grinding, we present HolonCloud: a physics-based digital twin powered by NVIDIA Isaac Sim. This framework leverages synthetic data and material simulation to accurately predict results, eliminating physical validation and enabling an autonomous, reinforcement learning-driven sim-to-real workflow.
key_takeaways: Our HolonOS platform addresses time-consuming manual setup, a key industry challenge, by using 3D vision and a no-code, GUI-driven engine for path generation and adjustment.

The HolonCloud framework accelerates optimization by using a simulation engine to train an reinforcement learning agent, allowing it to autonomously discover optimal parameters and eliminate costly physical trials.
We leverage NVIDIA Isaac Sim to build a high-fidelity digital twin that accurately simulates grinding results by combining physics-based engines with real-world data for calibration.

title: Manufacturing AI Agent and Explainable AI for Human-in-the-Loop Knowledge Transfer
abstract: Explore a Multi-Tool ReAct AI Agent that integrates XGBoost, GPU-accelerated SHAP, and LLM reasoning on NVIDIA NIM to preserve and scale tacit manufacturing expertise. We demonstrate how this agentic AI framework improves optimization, interpretability, and operational decision-making, helping manufacturers accelerate digital transformation through explainable, data-driven intelligence.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: RAPIDS, cuDF, cuML, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, cuVS
key_takeaways: Accelerates digital transformation in manufacturing, turning tacit expert knowledge into scalable, data-driven intelligence with AI agent
Demonstrates how agentic AI bridges explainability, optimization, and deployment in real manufacturing workflows
Multi-Tool ReAct AI Agent integrates XGBoost, GPU-accelerated SHAP, and LLM reasoning within the NVIDIA NIM framework
Provides a scalable agentic XAI framework for industrial digital transformation, bridging human reasoning and machine intelligence
Understand how this approach establishes a scalable, transparent foundation for next-generation smart manufacturing systems

title: Rapid VSOP Animation Generation via Vision‐Language Models and Trajectory Prediction
abstract: We demonstrate a vision‐language‐model‐driven pipeline that turns a few field image uploads and text instructions into accurate visual standard operating procedure (VSOP) animations in just minutes, achieving visual quality indistinguishable from expert‐crafted 3D animations. By eliminating hours to days of manual authoring and the need for specialist 3D animation skills, our system makes VSOP creation dramatically faster and accessible to anyone.
key_takeaways: Rapid Authoring: The end‐to‐end workflow reduces VSOP creation time by ~93 % (from an hour-and-a-half to seven minutes).
Low‐Skill Operation: Junior engineers or non‐technical staff can produce production‐grade animations without any 3D‐animation expertise, dramatically lowering labor cost.
Semantic‐to‐Geometric Bridge: Trajectory‐Prediction Module translates VLM‐generated textual directions into deterministic 3D motion paths.
Human‐Level Quality: Domain experts rated the automatically generated VSOPs as visually identical to manually created ones.

title: SmartEyes: Efficient AI for Real-Time Retail Loss Prevention
abstract: We introduce SmartEyes, a plug-and-play AI system that improves retail loss prevention by reducing false alerts and running in real time on a single GPU. With NVIDIA NeMo enabling PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA adaptation of Qwen-2.5VL (7B), SmartEyes runs in real time on a single RTX 4080, boosting throughput from 16 to 46 FPS. On the MERL Shopping dataset, accuracy rises from 38% to 82%, with false positives cut from 52% to 16%. Live demos across "put back," "pretend to take," and "take item" scenarios validate robust, low-hallucination alerts you can adapt to any surveillance setting.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks
key_takeaways: Improve performance by focusing on what matters: our perception-cognition focusing method cuts redundant inference and reduces hallucinations in surveillance video.
Boost Real-Time Efficiency: SmartEyes runs on a single RTX 4080, increasing throughput from 16 to 46 FPS while maintaining fast alerts within 1-2 seconds.
Enhance Accuracy in Real Scenarios: On the MERL Shopping dataset, accuracy jumps from 38% to 82%, with false positives reduced from 52% to 16% and false negatives from 10% to 2%.
Adapt Seamlessly with Efficient Fine-Tuning: NVIDIA NeMo enables PEFT adaptation of Qwen-2.5VL (7B) for practical, resource-friendly deployment.

title: Recon: A Real-Time Agentic AI Platform for Intelligent Traffic Incident Detection
abstract: Dive into Recon, a real-time agentic AI platform built on NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin that fuses TAO-based multi-object detection, VILA vision-language reasoning, and NeMo LLM-driven alert synthesis for open-world traffic incident detection. We explore how integrating multi-modal reasoning pipelines enables dynamic, zero-rule anomaly understanding - pushing the boundaries of intelligent video analytics for autonomous infrastructure monitoring. All while being completely on the edge!
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, CUDA, DeepStream, TensorRT, Metropolis, JetPack, NeMo, TAO Toolkit
key_takeaways: Learn how to deploy vision language models (VLMs) like NVIDIA VILA in production on edge devices like Jetson AGX Orin.
Explore how to combine multiple models like VLM (NVIDIA VILA) and LLM (Nemotron) to provide strong zero-shot classification (alerts) grounded by domain knowledge base.
Vision-language models detect new incident types without retraining by understanding scenes through natural language instead of fixed rules.
Real-world highway deployment proves agentic AI can run autonomously, without constant human monitoring, while processing live video feeds.

title: RAPIDS: Real-Time Accelerated Pipeline for Intelligent Dental Segmentation
abstract: We present RAPIDS, a GPU-accelerated dual-stage deep learning framework that eliminates the dental AI annotation bottleneck by transforming 3,000 polygon annotations into 18,131 pixel-wise training samples, reducing annotation costs. Using NVIDIA TensorRT optimization, we achieve 47ms inference latency on panoramic radiographs, which is 4x faster than baseline, enabling population-scale caries screening at 680 patients per hour on a single A100 GPU. Our framework achieves superior diagnostic accuracy with IoU of 0.9013 and Dice coefficient of 0.9482 while reducing false positive rates from 15-20% to just 2.26%. Mixed precision training on V100 GPUs completes in 4.5 hours, which is 6.2x faster than CPU, cutting research cycles from weeks to days. NVIDIA GPU acceleration transforms dental AI from expensive research into practical, affordable tools for resource-limited settings worldwide.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
key_takeaways: Automated annotation pipeline converts polygon-based labels into pixel-wise segmentation masks using GPU acceleration, reducing annotation costs.
TensorRT optimization delivers 4x inference speedup (189ms → 47ms) and 4x model compression (245MB → 61MB), enabling real-time chairside diagnostic  support with sub-100ms latency requirements.
Mixed precision training on NVIDIA V100 GPUs achieves 6.2x faster training (28 hours → 4.5 hours) compared to CPU baseline, accelerating research iteration cycles from weeks to days.

title: GLARE: GPU-Accelerated 3D CT Vision-Language Reasoning via Efficient Anatomical Boundary Guidance
abstract: Interpreting volumetric CT scans using vision-language models requires significant GPU resources and robust spatial focus. GLARE introduces slice-wise visual-instruction prompting, overlaying explicit blue contours generated by NVIDIA VISTA-3D on every axial CT slice to direct model attention to clinically relevant regions. Combined with a compact Qwen-2.5 decoder, this design enables high-performance medical reasoning with reduced VRAM footprint and inference time, delivering state-of-the-art VQA accuracy on medical benchmarks.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI
nvidia_technology: CUDA, TensorRT, cuDDN, MONAI, NCCL, NeMo
key_takeaways: Slice-wise visual prompting enforces explicit spatial grounding for clinical queries in 3D CT.
Efficient Qwen-2.5 language model achieves up to 6.8GB GPU memory reduction versus standard 7B decoders.
Automatic organ segmentation and contour generation using NVIDIA VISTA-3D
Significant gains in VQA metrics (BLEU-4, ROUGE-L, METEOR) and closed accuracy with visual prompting
Enables real-time, high-fidelity CT reasoning on a single 24GB GPU for clinical use.

speakers.name: Eunseob Choi
speakers.title: Undergraduate Researcher
speakers.company: Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology

title: SAKE: Toward Editing Auditory Attribute Knowledge of Large Audio-Language Models
abstract: We present the first benchmark and study on applying knowledge editing techniques to modify auditory attribute knowledge in large audio-language models (LALMs). This direction is both timely and important, as knowledge editing has shown promise in mitigating bias and enabling personalization in large language models (LLMs). Extending these capabilities to auditory knowledge could open new possibilities for achieving similar goals in multi-modal and speech-based systems.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Audio / Speech Foundation Models
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, cuDDN, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NVLink / NVSwitch, Triton
key_takeaways: Editing auditory attribute knowledge remains difficult for existing knowledge editing methods, underscoring the need for more advanced and modality-aware approaches.
Fine-tuning the modality connector remains a strong baseline compared with meta-learning-based knowledge editing methods.
In-context editing is ineffective in LALMs, showing their limited in-context learning capability.
Preserving unrelated knowledge, especially auditory knowledge, remains a significant challenge.
When multiple edits are applied in sequence, most methods quickly lose previously edited knowledge.

title: ProFusion: 3D Reconstruction of Protein Complex Structures from Multi-View AFM Images
abstract: We present ProFusion, a framework that combines atomic force microscopy (AFM) with generative AI to predict 3D structures of proteins and protein complexes. While models like AlphaFold excel at individual proteins, they struggle with large complexes. Conversely, techniques like Cryo-EM offer high accuracy, but are costly and time-intensive. ProFusion bridges this gap by coupling AFM's multi-view imaging with a diffusion-based neural radiance field (NeRF) model, trained on a virtual AFM dataset of ~542,000 proteins with synthetic multi-view images. This new paradigm for protein structure prediction bridges the gap between AI-based methods and experimental validation. By enabling accurate, rapid, and cost-effective 3D reconstruction of protein complexes, ProFusion opens opportunities for advancing structural biology, speeding drug discovery, and supporting iterative experimental design.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
key_takeaways: A novel, cost-effective method for 3D protein complex structure prediction using unposed multi-view AFM images
A virtual AFM simulation framework that produces multi-view image datasets for 542,000 proteins, eliminating the dependence on costly and time-intensive experimental data collection
Extensive validation on experimental AFM images of diverse protein complexes (EIN-HPr, WRC-Rac1, CasB-E), achieving reconstruction fidelity within AFM imaging resolution

title: PinRec: Outcome-Conditioned, Multi-Token Generative Retrieval for Industry-Scale Recommendation Systems
abstract: Our poster introduces PinRec, a system that fundamentally redesigns recommendation retrieval using an LLM-like architecture to generate a sequence of items a Pinterest user is likely to engage with, unlike a more standard two-tower retrieval architecture. Deployed on NVIDIA L40S GPUs with innovations such as outcome-conditioning, multi-token generation, and a fully torch.compiled architecture, PinRec boosted key online metrics by 2-4% or more for hundreds of million users, making it one of the first industrial-scale production systems for generative retrieval.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, NSight Systems, Triton
key_takeaways: Outcome‐conditioned generation lets us set "budgets" for saves, clicks, or other interactions, and produces statistically significant lifts, e.g., +4.01% Homefeed Grid Clicks, +4.21% Search Saves, and +0.63% Sitewide Saves in a Related Pins experiment (p=0.05).

Multi‐token generation can predict several future items in parallel, improving diversity and recall while reducing decode steps, with a Recall@10 of 0.676/0.631/0.450 on Homefeed/Related Pins/Search respectively.

Efficient serving on L40S GPUs with NVIDIA Triton Inference Server Python backend, maximally torch.compiled code with Torch inductor, and KV cache. These all help us achieve high throughputs per GPU, high SM activities, and p50 latencies well under 100ms.
By directly generating sequences from user history, and including outcome conditioning levers, we allow for more product iteration compared to heuristic-based stacks.
Benchmarking shows significant improvements in both large‐scale online A/B tests and offline comparisons against baselines such as TIGER, SASRec, and HSTU.

title: Low-Cost Motion Learning Pipeline for Industrial Humanoids on NVIDIA Jetson Platforms
abstract: In real factories, safety regulations, security restrictions, and a lack of skilled operators make it difficult to collect and deploy motion data at scale. To address these challenges, we developed an intuitive teleoperation pipeline using a single consumer-grade head-mounted display (HMD), removing the need for external trackers or multi-camera systems. The system integrates Jetson-based inference with an RTOS-driven dual-network control architecture-EtherCAT for ALICE-M1 and RS-485 for ALICE4-enabling deterministic real-time operation. A GR00T-Mimic-inspired data augmentation method further diversifies robot trajectories without additional data collection, enhancing robustness across manipulation tasks. Experiments in industrial scenarios show stable autonomous execution and confirm the feasibility of deploying Jetson-based humanoid systems for real-world industrial applications.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, AGX, DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, Isaac, Omniverse, cuDDN, JetPack, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Demonstrate a low-cost data collection pipeline using only a single consumer-grade HMD without external trackers or mocap systems.
Validate the system through autonomous multi-robot coordination tasks in real industrial scenarios, demonstrating scalable humanoid deployment.
Understand our GR00T Mimic-inspired trajectory augmentation that turns one teleoperation demo into 100 trajectories in under 30 minutes, compared with about four hours for traditional collection.

title: NanoViT: Basis-Aligned Hybrid Neural Network for Jetson Devices
abstract: Computer vision tasks often demand intensive computation, even on GPU-equipped devices. To address this challenge, we present NanoViT, a hybrid architecture that integrates convolutional layers with transformers, achieving inference speeds comparable to conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) on NVIDIA GPUs. Unlike prior work that relies heavily on empirical experimentation, NanoViT is grounded in new theoretical insights into the role and behavior of convolution in visual classification.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Jetson, DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, JetPack, DGX Station
key_takeaways: NanoViT is a hybrid model combining basis-aligned CNNs with Transformers.
NanoViT runs as fast as conventional CNNs on NVIDIA GPUs and Jetson devices.
NanoViT is the first network to reach 70% accuracy on ImageNet with less than 1 million parameters (0.99M).
The poster highlights the theory of superposition and basis-aligned features in a practical neural network architecture.

speakers.name: Lucas Mohimont
speakers.title: Postdoctoral Researcher
speakers.company: Laboratoire d'Informatique en Calcul Intensif et Image pour la Simulation / LRC DIGIT URCA-CEA

speakers.name: Luiz Angelo Steffenel

speakers.name: Lilian Hollard
speakers.title: Ph.D. Candidate
speakers.company: Laboratoire d'Informatique en Calcul Intensif et Image pour la Simulation / LRC DIGIT URCA-CEA

title: ColonVR: Enhancing Colonography Through Artificial Intelligence and Virtual Reality
abstract:
Colorectal cancer is a leading cause of cancer-related deaths. While virtual colonoscopy provides a noninvasive alternative, current approaches depend on manual segmentation and lack immersive exploration. We present ColonVR, a hybrid AI and virtual reality framework that combines NVIDIA MONAI with TotalSegmentator for automated colon reconstruction from unprepared CT scans. Our hybrid pipeline was benchmarked against NVIDIA VISTA 3D, MONAI, CLEAR, and TotalSegmentator, showing improved anatomical accuracy and surface continuity.

In a study with 30 participants, users searched for polyps inside a reconstructed colon in VR using three navigation modes: Automatic, Flythrough, and Teleport. Automatic navigation delivered the best trade-off between efficiency, accuracy, and comfort, highlighting how AI-driven reconstruction and immersive VR can advance diagnostic training.
key_takeaways: We developed ColonVR, a hybrid framework combining NVIDIA MONAI-based AI reconstruction with immersive VR exploration.
We created a hybrid segmentation pipeline benchmarked against VISTA 3D, TotalSegmentator, MONAI, and CLEAR, achieving higher anatomical accuracy and continuity.
We conducted a within-subjects user study with 30 participants tasked with finding polyps in the reconstructed colon using three navigation modes.
We measured coverage, detection accuracy, task duration, workload, usability, and simulator sickness to assess performance and comfort.
Automatic navigation provided the best balance of accuracy, efficiency, and user comfort, demonstrating how AI and VR can enhance diagnostic training.

speakers.name: Joaquim Jorge
speakers.title: Professor
speakers.company: Instituto Superior Tecnico, University of Lisbon

title: GRAVEX: GPU-Accelerated GAN-Diffusion Representation for Counterfeit Pattern Analysis With MONAI-Based Validation
abstract: We demonstrate how systematically reusing and fine-tuning discriminators from generative models yields robust deepfake detection across diverse synthesis methods. By integrating scale-adaptive features from ProGAN discriminators with temporal-consistency features from diffusion models, our GRAVEX framework achieves 74.33% F1-score across nine state-of-the-art generators-outperforming conventional ViT-based detectors by over 30% F1, on average. Implemented using NVIDIA MONAI for streamlined training and deployment, this approach addresses the critical challenge of detecting AI-generated faces as generative AI becomes increasingly sophisticated. On challenging datasets like CIFAKE, where baseline detectors fail completely, GRAVEX maintains 88% F1-score, demonstrating exceptional robustness and validating discriminator reuse as a promising path toward production-ready detection systems.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: GRAVEX achieves superior cross-generator performance by systematically reusing discriminators trained for image generation, reaching 74.33% F1-score across nine diverse synthesis methods and outperforming existing ViT-based detectors by over 30% F1 on average.

The hybrid framework integrates hierarchical spatial features from ProGAN discriminators with temporal-consistency features from diffusion models, producing generation-agnostic representations that maintain 88% F1-score on challenging datasets where conventional detectors achieve near-zero performance.

Built entirely within NVIDIA MONAI ecosystem, GRAVEX provides a reproducible, deployment-ready pipeline with standardized data transforms, efficient distributed training, and seamless integration with MONAI Deploy App SDK and Triton Inference Server for production-scale deepfake detection.

speakers.name: Eunseob Choi
speakers.title: Undergraduate Researcher
speakers.company: Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology

title: AI Inference on NVIDIA Jetson Orin AGX GPU for Real-Time Arthroscopic Surgery Assistance
abstract:
AI is transforming medical imaging and surgical navigation by enhancing precision, reducing human error, and improving patient outcomes. Yet, its integration in operating rooms is challenging due to stringent real-time and safety demands.

In minimally invasive procedures such as arthroscopy, limited maneuverability and indirect visualization make tasks like ACL reconstruction technically demanding. AI-driven video-based surgical navigation in edge computing systems offer improved accuracy and efficiency without high-end workstations.

Our recent collaboration with Smith & Nephew features an edge AI surgical assistance pipeline built on the NVIDIA Holoscan framework and Jetson AGX Orin hardware. We optimized three AI subsystems - segmentation, classification, and lens mark detection - validated on cadaveric datasets, achieving real-time performance with dozens of frames per second.
nvidia_technology: Jetson, CUDA, TensorRT, HPC SDK, cuBLAS, cuDDN, NSight Graphics, NVLink / NVSwitch, Optical Flow SDK, Video Codec SDK
key_takeaways: A new edge AI surgical assistance pipeline using NVIDIA Holoscan framework and Jetson AGX Orin hardware
Implementation and optimization of three AI subsystems: segmentation, classification, and lens mark detection models
Real-time AI performance validated on cadaveric datasets
Real-time surgical guidance requirements achieved
Surgical safety and efficiency enhanced

speakers.name: Gabriel Falcao
speakers.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
speakers.company: Instituto de Telecomunicações, University of Coimbra, New York University Abu Dhabi

title: GPU-Accelerated SIP/RTP Real-Time Voice Customer Service System
abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated real-time voice interaction system built on NVIDIA NeMo, TensorRT-LLM, and Riva, capable of running directly over standard SIP/RTP telephony. Using only one RTX 5070 Ti or RTX 5090 GPU, the system achieves sub-600 ms end-to-end conversational latency, performing automatic speech recognition (ASR), LLM inference, and text-to-speech (TTS) fully on-device. This work demonstrates how small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) can deploy on-premises AI voice agents using consumer-grade GPUs, reducing cloud costs and ensuring secure, low-latency communication.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation
key_takeaways: Demonstrates real-time AI voice processing using RTX 5070 Ti / RTX 5090 GPU
Achieves 530 ms end-to-end latency, providing natural, human-like responses over SIP/RTP.
Implements a nonintrusive "bypass" RTP mirroring architecture for zero-disruption AI monitoring
Integrates NeMo ASR, TensorRT-LLM, and Riva TTS into a unified GPU pipeline optimized for telephony
Enables cost-effective, private AI deployment for SMEs without relying on cloud infrastructure

title: Characterizing Communication Patterns in Distributed LLM Inference
abstract: This work investigates communication dynamics in distributed LLM serving-analyzing how various parallelization approaches coordinate data exchange between GPU workers during inference. We study transformer-based models as representative examples of contemporary architectures widely used in operational deployments. Our work combines detailed profiling measurements with predictive analytical models to characterize communication behavior across different parallelization configurations.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Interconnect Networking, NCCL, NSight Systems, NVLink / NVSwitch
key_takeaways: LLM inference is communication-bound: the decode stage dominates runtime with high-frequency, moderate-sized collective operations.
Tensor parallelism delivers low latency, while pipeline and hybrid schemes trade performance for bandwidth efficiency.
Tensor parallelism suits short, interactive workloads; pipeline parallelism excels in long-form or bandwidth-limited settings.
Analytical models and profiling insights on GPU communication help practitioners select the optimal parallelism strategy for real-world LLM deployments.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.title: Professor and Distinguished Scholar of Computer Science

title: Multi-Modal AI Biomarker for Early Cancer Cachexia Detection Integrates Clinical, Radiologic, and Laboratory Indicators
abstract: Cancer cachexia is a multifactorial metabolic syndrome marked by substantial weight loss, skeletal muscle wasting, and systemic inflammation contributing to poor quality of life and increased mortality. No definitive biomarker exists and commonly used serum indicators, skeletal muscle metrics, and anthropometric measures overlap with other conditions, limiting diagnostic specificity. Existing composite indices (Cancer Cachexia Index and Cachexia Score) integrate multiple symptomatic factors but lack standardized thresholds and are rarely scalable for clinical use. We present a multi-modal AI biomarker for early cancer cachexia detection, integrating diverse mechanistic signals from routinely available patient data, including clinical data, inflammatory and nutritional lab markers, skeletal muscle metrics (CT scans), and cachexia symptoms extracted from clinical notes using LLMs.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, DGX Platform, CUDA, TensorRT, RAPIDS, Hopper, cuBLAS, cuDDN, cuDF, cuML, MONAI, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Multi-modal AI framework that integrates routinely collected patient data, including demographics, anthropometric measures, cancer staging, radiologic skeletal muscle metrics, laboratory biomarkers, and cachexia-related symptoms extracted from unstructured clinical notes, for early detection of cancer cachexia at the time of cancer diagnosis

Application of LLMs to transform unstructured clinical notes into structured symptom features, enabling seamless incorporation of text-derived information into predictive pipelines and improving model performance
The AI biomarker adapts to patient characteristics, enabling generalization across cancer types and patient populations.
Step-wise addition of data modalities improves cancer cachexia and survival prediction accuracy

Uncertainty-aware AI framework, capable of accommodating missing modalities, supporting human-in-the-loop decision-making, and facilitating integration into real-world oncology workflows to enable timely interventions and personalized care

speakers.name: Matthew Schabath
speakers.title: Sr. Member
speakers.company: H. Lee Moffitt Cancer Center and Research Institute

speakers.name: Ghulam Rasool
speakers.title: Assistant Member
speakers.company: H. Lee Moffitt Cancer Center and Research Institute

title: An Ecosystem for End-to-End Multi-Modal Data Curation
abstract:
Multi-modal data from diverse domains is the lifeblood of the Gen AI revolution, but curating and discovering datasets is expensive and time consuming. With the scarcity of efficient tools to unify data discovery, debiasing, and blending multi-modal human-labeled datasets for reuse in dynamic downstream model development tasks, there is a tremendous opportunity to drastically reduce data annotation cost and data pre-processing compute.

We present inventions utilizing technologies such as agentic AI, synthetic data generation, and multi-modal embedding models closely integrated with the NVIDIA tool suite. Our end-to-end ecosystem exhibits downstream performance improvements on multiple task types (pre-training, post-training). These tools can rapidly cut down cycle times for foundation model teams, and power smarter and more aligned Gen AI models across numerous industries.
nvidia_technology: Riva, cuML, NeMo, TAO Toolkit, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Learn about our approach to semantic curation  for human-labeled data powered by smart subsampling and mixture-of-experts-style multi-modal embedding models.
Learn how to use NVIDIA's technologies to build an agentic dataset blending flywheel for intelligent and efficient multi-modal data processing.
Learn how we championed integration of our data discovery technologies to break down silos in real-world data pipelines for our flagship Gen AI models.

title: Agentic AutoML with RAPIDS-Accelerated AutoGluon and NeMo Agent Toolkit
abstract:
We introduce NeMo-RAPIDS AutoML Agent, an agentic AutoML framework designed for tabular and time-series tasks. Unlike prior LLM-based AutoML systems (e.g., AutoML-Agent), our system integrates NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit for multi-agent orchestration and RAPIDS for GPU-accelerated AutoML.

Through tool-augmented reasoning, LLM agents invoke high-level APIs (AutoGluon, RAPIDS) instead of writing raw code, enabling both autonomy and performance. Experiments on public datasets demonstrate up to 13% improvement in quality and 5-10x faster runtime, proving that agentic orchestration plus GPU acceleration can make LLM-driven AutoML efficient, scalable, and enterprise-ready.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, RAPIDS, cuDF, NeMo, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Agentic Orchestration: Multi-agent collaboration via NeMo Agent Toolkit, featuring orchestration, telemetry, and adaptive workflows.
Tool-Augmented Reasoning: LLM agents use APIs (AutoGluon, RAPIDS) instead of manual coding, improving robustness and modularity.
GPU-Accelerated AutoML: RAPIDS integration achieves 5-10x faster runtime without compromising accuracy.
Performance Gains: 13% improvement in prediction quality across tabular and text tasks versus baseline AutoML-Agent.
Enterprise Scalability: Architecture supports full-life cycle MLOps-from data prep to model deployment (TensorRT-ready).

title: A Multi-Step Approach to Customize Small Language Models for Enterprise AI Applications
abstract: Many enterprise generative AI use cases could benefit from more effectively utilizing user preference data. In this poster, we demonstrate an affective architecture for fine-tuning small language models using this preference data, built on NVIDIA NeMo, and share results from the model performance gained.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: CUDA, Hopper, LaunchPad, Base Command Manager, NeMo, DGX Spark
key_takeaways: Open-source small language models are effective for many enterprise use cases where customization is critical.
User preference data contains a valuable signal that can be used to improve small language models through fine-tuning.
NVIDIA NeMo provides an effective toolset for implementing this kind of fine-tuning.
NeMotron Nano 2 provides computational benefits in the form of boosted throughput and lowered memory footprint.

title: Agentic AI for DAG Reasoning: Build Deterministic Data Engineering Pipelines Through Human-Machine Collaboration
abstract: Data engineering can dramatically benefit from agentic assistance. However, agents are not well suited to many standard data integration tasks, as hallucinations during pipeline execution can produce non-deterministic outcomes, and operations rarely merit the expense of AI at scale. We demonstrate a system that leverages agents to help design abstract data pipelines as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs), then translate those pipelines into code that can be deployed in existing data platforms. This ensures deterministic, traceable, and affordable data workflows, while still benefiting from the huge advantages agents offer in accelerating building pipelines. A combination graph and chat based user interface is demonstrated allowing efficient human-machine teaming for pipeline build-out.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: AI agents to build, code, and deploy data integration pipelines (Directed Acyclic Graphs)
Human-machine collaboration for efficient pipeline design
Single abstract pipeline can be compiled for any data integration platform, simplifying migration
Deploying deterministic pipelines designed by agents mitigates AI challenges in operations

title: Accelerate Predictive Water Safety via Synthetic Data and Continuous Learning
abstract: We demonstrate how synthetic data generation and continuous learning solve the critical deployment barriers in water safety AI-reducing detection latency by 37% and cutting multi-site onboarding from two months to five days. Explore how generative AI mitigates model drift across diverse aquatic environments where labeled real-world data is scarce and costly.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, cuDDN, CV-CUDA, JetPack, Omniverse Replicator, Cosmos
key_takeaways: Model drift and coverage gaps make traditional computer vision unreliable for water safety AI across diverse environments-requiring a fundamentally different approach to real-time detection.
Per-facility onboarding time collapses from ~two months to five days (90% reduction), reducing engineering hours and enabling rapid scaling across multiple aquatic venues globally.

This data-agnostic pipeline shifts water safety from reactive monitoring to predictive prevention-reducing false alarms, operational friction, and liability costs while making AI-powered lifeguarding accessible to under-resourced facilities.

title: Can Large Language Models Explain Themselves? A Study of LLM-Generated Self-Explanations
abstract: LLMs excel at diverse natural language processing (NLP) tasks, from sentiment analysis to reasoning and summarization. Because they are instruction-tuned to produce helpful responses, they often generate self-explanations - rationales accompanying their outputs. For instance, when analyzing a movie review, an LLM may identify sentiment-laden words like "fantastic" or "memorable." We investigate such self-explanations in sentiment analysis, focusing on feature attribution explanations. We explore different elicitation strategies, assess their faithfulness, and compare them with traditional methods like occlusion and LIME. Our experiments show that LLM self-explanations are comparably faithful, differ notably from traditional ones, and are far cheaper to obtain. These findings invite a reconsideration of current interpretability practices in the era of instruction-tuned LLMs.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA
key_takeaways: LLM self-explanations match the faithfulness performance of traditional methods like LIME and occlusion, but are much cheaper to produce since they are generated simultaneously with the prediction.

The LLM's insensitivity to single-word removal severely affects traditional faithfulness metrics like DFMIT and RankDel, suggesting that the classic interpretability pipeline is ill-suited for evaluating these human-like models and must be rethought.

LLM-generated word attribution and prediction confidence values are characteristically sparse and "well-rounded," a qualitative difference believed to reflect ChatGPT's alignment towards mimicking human-like, non-fine-grained reasoning.

Requiring LLMs to generate feature attribution explanations consistently results in a drop in prediction accuracy compared to prediction-only models, supporting an accuracy-interpretability trade-off.

title: Automate Enterprise Day-to-Days With AI Personal Assistant
abstract:
NVIDIA employees face the real problem of information overload on a daily basis. Communication, project updates, knowledge sharing, and cross-functional collaborations are spread across multiple third-party enterprise cloud providers such as Teams, Outlook, Confluence, Jira, Slack, etc. People spend a lot of time sifting through noisy information.

We propose an AI-based "Personal Assistant" to automatically connect to people's information sources, leveraging AI to identify people's ongoing tasks, to-dos, recent accomplishments, etc. We propose an MCP-based architecture for our app, where the Personal Assistant operates as an MCP client, while leveraging native or partner-hosted MCP servers to tackle various daily task that requires various data access. We also set ourselves as an example of standard MCP-based agentic architecture with a high standard on security.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: TensorRT, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, cuVS, Blueprint
key_takeaways: MCP-based architecture provides a solid and powerful foundation for enterprise agentic AI.
Connecting agentic AI with multiple enterprise data sources has shown great promise in enhancing employee productivity.
Integrating agentic AI seamlesly into users' daily workflow is key for a successful enterprise productivity product.

title: Natural Language to 3D Geometric CAD: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Design Synthesis Using Hierarchical Decomposition
abstract: We present a multi-agent LLM framework that automatically converts natural language descriptions into parametric 3D CAD models through hierarchical decomposition. Our system uses specialized AI agents for different stages of the process, including reasoning, planning, compiling, and self-repair. The system achieves better-than 90% success rates in generating parametric CAD models with preserved dependencies, enabling the generation of editable designs. Our approach empowers domain experts to express requirements in natural language and automatically generate 3D models, accelerating the design process and democratizing CAD access.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation
key_takeaways: Hierarchical assembly decomposition automatically breaks complex designs into components with maintained constraints, accelerating design-to-manufacturing workflows by 10-100x for common mechanical parts.

Multi-agent architecture outperforms monolithic models: specialized Reasoner, Planner, Compiler, and Repair agents collaborate through LangGraph orchestration to generate geometrically valid, parametric CAD models from conversational input.

JSON schema-driven intermediate representation serves as validation guardrail and human-interpretable specification, bridging the semantic gap between design intent and formal geometric representations.

Achieves 94% schema validation and 89% OpenSCAD compilation success after iterative repair, with 91% accuracy in preserving parametric dependencies - enabling rapid design iterations through adjustable parameters.

Open-source framework democratizes CAD design, enabling domain experts to generate 3D models through natural language without manual drafting expertise - lowering barriers for prototyping and education.

title: Building Industrial Automation Systems, From Simulation to Production
abstract: Our framework accelerates development, training, and deployment of intelligent robotic systems by integrating digital model creation, synthetic data generation, real-time scene adaptation, LLMs, and vision language models (VLMs). Uing NVIDIA technologies for high-performance simulation and inference enables realistic training and testing of vision models and overall system performance. LLM and VLM integration allows the system to interpret user commands, generate executable code, and enhance scene understanding for intuitive human-machine collaboration. A custom vision system provides 3D data coordinates for robot manipulation in the simulation. The system is already used for industrial purposes, including high-fidelity perception for manufacturing, quality control, and surveillance. Results from simulated and real-world deployments demonstrate the framework's adaptability and potential.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, Isaac, cuDDN, Triton
key_takeaways: The framework facilitates the generation of synthetic data to train and improve computer vision models.
The framework provides a controlled simulation environment for validating and analyzing the performance of existing computer vision models on real-world use cases.
The framework enables natural language interaction with the entirety of the simulated environment through the use of LLMs and VLMs.

speakers.name: Nikolina Roso
speakers.title: Academic Assistant
speakers.company: University of Zagreb Faculty of Electrical Engineering and Computing

title: Groove Intelligence: Reviving Vinyl Through Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Explore how AI and computer vision can read sound visually. This project presents a digital turntable that plays vinyl records using only a 4K camera and deep learning to reconstruct audio from microscopic groove images in real time. Using NVIDIA GPU acceleration, we merge art and technology to preserve fragile analog media and reveal new possibilities for sound restoration, cultural heritage, and multi-modal AI - where image and sound become one expressive language.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
key_takeaways: Demonstrates how AI and computer vision can visually decode sound from vinyl grooves.
Uses a 4K/60 fps camera and deep learning instead of a traditional needle to reconstruct audio.
Leverages NVIDIA GPU acceleration for real-time processing and precision signal recovery.
Enables non-contact playback that preserves and restores fragile analog media.
Bridges art, science, and technology to explore new multi-modal AI expressions linking image and sound.

title: Auto Help: Transforming NVIDIA IT Support Through AI-Powered Ticket Resolution
abstract: Thousands of inquiry tickets received annually by IT support can be resolved using existing knowledge bases but require manual effort, prolonging resolution times. The Auto Help System automates this process using an LLM-powered architecture with intelligent classification agent and a context-aware, retrieval augmented generation (RAG)-based generator that crafts human-like, accurate responses. A multi-layered guardrail ensures safe and relevant outputs, while an automated workflow updates tickets, notifies users via Slack and email, and escalates when needed. We achieved 89% accuracy where the system delivers a median time savings of 39 minutes per ticket, and on average two days of fulfillment time for customers, and is projected to self-resolve over 8,000 tickets annually, resulting in an estimated $880,000 in savings while enhancing user satisfaction and operational efficiency.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: See measurable business impact with 89% accuracy, 8,000+ tickets self-resolved yearly, and $880,000 in cost savings.
Understand how intelligent classification and RAG-based generation ensure accurate, human-like ticket responses.
Discover how multi-layered guardrails maintain safety, relevance, and factual precision in AI-driven workflows.
Learn how LLM-powered automation transforms IT inquiry handling by enabling instant, self-service resolutions.
Explore how scalable AI support frameworks free IT teams to focus on higher-value, complex problem-solving.

title: AI-Driven Industrial Safety Monitoring: Real-Time Detection and Physics-Informed Safety Risk Reasoning
abstract: We present an AI-based industrial safety monitoring system that performs real-time personal protective equipment (PPE) detection and worker action recognition. Using physics-informed risk reasoning, the system generates automated safety summaries and actionable recommendations, supporting evidence-based decision-making. Deployed on server and edge platforms, it significantly reduces PPE noncompliance and provides timely insights for operators and managers in complex industrial environments.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition
nvidia_technology: Jetson, RTX GPU, TensorRT, Omniverse, Cosmos
key_takeaways: Domain Generalization with Synthetic Data: Simulation-based datasets from NVIDIA Omniverse improve model robustness across diverse lighting, camera angles, and occlusions.
Physics-Informed Risk Reasoning: Cosmos Reason1 integrates spatio-temporal and physical reasoning to assess hazards, generate safety summaries, and provide proactive recommendations.
Measurable Safety Impact: Six months of deployment showed a 57.8% reduction in PPE noncompliance and no observed worker injuries.
Scalable and Edge-Ready Implementation: Optimized models run on both server (RTX 4080 SUPER) and edge (Jetson Orin Nano) platforms at 30 FPS per camera.

title: Secure the AI Factory With NVIDIA BlueField and Palo Alto Networks Prisma AIRS
abstract: This poster showcases the integration of Palo Alto Networks Prisma AIRS security on NVIDIA BlueField DPUs to secure modern AI factories. As AI adoption accelerates, this approach delivers essential zero-trust protection and real-time threat detection directly within the infrastructure, ensuring data integrity and high performance for critical AI workloads without compromise.
