topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI
key_takeaways: Secure AI factories by integrating Prisma AIRS security onto NVIDIA BlueField DPUs, isolating security functions to maintain peak workload performance.
Enable zero-trust principles for AI infrastructure, ensuring data integrity and securing capable devices at the edge with zero compute impact on host workloads.
Integrate NVIDIA DOCA alerts directly with Cortex XSIAM for enhanced visibility into workload security, and enable automated remediation action.
Deliver high-performance, real-time threat detection and mitigation against application-level attacks and evolving AI threats within the NVIDIA AI Factory.

title: MATATA: Weakly Supervised End-to-End Mathematical Tool-Augmented Reasoning for Tabular Applications
abstract: We introduce MATATA, a new way to train agentic small language models to reason through complex business documents with tables and numbers - without expensive human annotation or large models. Discover how our weakly supervised, end-to-end approach improves accuracy and computational efficiency for document understanding and reasoning tasks.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Novel method for end-to-end weak supervision fine-tuning of tool-augmented reasoning agents
Lean, scalable, efficient models with reduced computational and GPU footprint
Preserve privacy and security with open-source models when operating on sensitive information without trade-offs on accuracy

title: Accessible Quantum Reinforcement Learning for Finance: Benchmarking on NVIDIA RTX GPUs
abstract: Explore how we make quantum reinforcement learning for finance truly accessible using everyday GPUs. This poster demonstrates how you can train hybrid quantum-classical financial agents on NVIDIA RTX 3090s in just 20 hours, benchmarked on 18 years of Taiwan stock data, revealing how GPU-accelerated quantum simulation unlocks faster, fairer, and more inclusive financial AI research.
key_takeaways: Accessible Quantum Finance Training: Hybrid quantum-classical reinforcement learning models can be trained on an affordable NVIDIA RTX 3090 GPU within ~20 hours, proving large-scale quantum finance experiments no longer require supercomputers.

Real-World Benchmark on 18 Years of Data: Using 2,646 Taiwan-listed firms across 47 sectors (2007-25), our setup successfully backtested five quantum reinforcement learning models, ensuring credible, high-resolution financial evaluations.

Identified the True Bottleneck: Despite 85-95% GPU utilization, CPU-based quantum circuit simulation still consumed 60-70% of training time, highlighting the need for full-stack GPU acceleration via NVIDIA cuQuantum.

Quantum Attention Innovations: We integrated variational quantum circuits (VQC) into RWKV and self-qttention architectures, achieving richer policy exploration and improved reward alignment for volatile markets.

Scalable GPU Pathway for Research: Upgrading from RTX 3090 → A100 → H100 cuts model training from 20 hours to 0.5 hours, enabling multi-market, multi-reward experiments and accelerating the democratization of quantum AI for finance.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.title: AI Research Assistant, Harvard Medical School, and AI Trainer, OpenAI
speakers.company: Harvard Medical School/Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.title: Engineer
speakers.company: Department of Information and Communications Research Laboratories, Industrial Technology Research Institute

title: Deploy One-Click Agent Blueprints With NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit on On-Prem AI Infrastructure
abstract: Astra Blueprints enables one-click deployment of agentic AI workflows on on-premises NVIDIA infrastructure using the NeMo Agent Toolkit. The framework provides blueprint-based templates to standardize workflow creation and configuration while supporting customization across use cases. With continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) and GitOps automation, Astra orchestrates code, image, and deployment life cycles via the Astra Console, integrating with Vault, ArgoCD, and JFrog Artifactory. OpenTelemetry ensures unified observability with real-time metrics, traces, and logs. By abstracting infrastructure complexity and ensuring enterprise-grade scalability, privacy, and compliance, Astra Blueprints accelerates the transition from prototype to production-empowering teams to deploy and monitor multi-agent AI systems efficiently.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: HGX, NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Blueprint
key_takeaways: One-Click Agentic Deployment: Rapidly launch agentic AI workflows on on-premises NVIDIA infrastructure using NeMo Agent Toolkit.
Blueprint-Driven Standardization: Reusable templates simplify configuration while preserving flexibility across diverse enterprise use cases.
Fully Automated Pipeline: CI/CD and GitOps automate code, image, and deployment life cycles via Astra Console integration.
Unified Observability: OpenTelemetry delivers real-time metrics, logs, and traces across agents, models, and systems.
Enterprise-Ready Framework: Built-in single sign-on, security, and compliance ensure scalable, reproducible, and privacy-preserving deployments.

title: GPU-Accelerated Chinese Brain-to-Text Interface Powered by NVIDIA H100 80GB
abstract: This poster presents the first GPU-accelerated EEG-to-Chinese text generation framework, enabling brain-to-text communication for Chinese speakers with speech disorders. By leveraging the NVIDIA H100's Transformer Engine and mixed-precision training, the system achieves threefold faster training and 60 TFLOPS performance, making real-time decoding feasible. The model decodes 128-channel EEG signals into open-vocabulary Chinese text, addressing a long-standing gap in multilingual brain-computer interface (BCI) research. This work empowers inclusive assistive communication and sets a new foundation for large-scale, clinically deployable Chinese brain-computer interfaces.
key_takeaways: First Chinese EEG-to-Text System:  Achieved 6.38% BLEU-1 score on ChineseEEG dataset, establishing the first baseline for logographic language brain-to-text decoding.

First Chinese EEG-to-Text Framework: Achieved open-vocabulary Chinese text generation directly from 128-channel EEG signals using a 50,000+ sample dataset, marking the first large-scale brain-to-text model for Chinese speakers.
GPU-Accelerated Efficiency: Leveraged NVIDIA H100 80GB with 60 TFLOPS Transformer performance, enabling 3x faster FP16 training and completing full model convergence within 50 epochs on a single GPU.

Superior Decoding Accuracy: Integrated contrastive and cross-entropy objectives, improving BLEU-based decoding performance over prior EEG-to-text baselines by ~25-30% in Chinese language reconstruction.

Open and Reproducible BCI Platform: Released an open-source PyTorch 2.x implementation, lowering computational barriers and promoting multilingual BCI research for the 7.5 million Chinese speakers with communication disorders.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.title: AI Research Assistant, Harvard Medical School, and AI Trainer, OpenAI
speakers.company: Harvard Medical School/Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.title: Engineer
speakers.company: Department of Information and Communications Research Laboratories, Industrial Technology Research Institute

title: A Vision Language Foundation Model for MRI Standarization
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is widely used in both clinical and research settings. However, there is no standard system for naming or categorizing MRI sequences. This inconsistency is due to differences in imaging protocols, scanner vendors, and institutional practices. As a result, MRI sequences often vary in appearance, and DICOM metadata can be unreliable or conflicting. Clinician oversight is frequently required to verify the correct sequence type. This manual process is time-consuming and prone to error, especially when dealing with large datasets. The goal of this study is to create an efficient streamlined method to standardize the MRI sequences. In this project, we introduce the first foundation model that standardize MRI naming convention across nearly all sequence and anatomy in routine scans.
key_takeaways: This is the first vision language model to standardize MRI naming convention.
The proposed harmonization method can standardize the MRI naming convention and significantly facilitate the workflow for the radiologist.
The proposed method can handle nearly all common rountine MRI scan include 15 sequence types and 15 anatomy types.

title: Efficient SubtleHD-Enhanced MR Images for Improved Dementia Classification
abstract: Our poster demonstrates new solutions for robust, accelerated dementia classification by training a 3D ResNet34 model on U.S. Food and Drug Administration-cleared SubtleHD enhanced MR images, and explores how to improve performance by accelerating the ConvNeXt enhancement pipeline using NVIDIA TensorRT on various GPUs. This work is timely because it provides a validated, high-efficiency pipeline that resolves the critical trade-off between rapid MR scanning and reliable AI-guided diagnosis in neurology.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
nvidia_technology: RTX GPU, CUDA, TensorRT, DGX Station
key_takeaways: SubtleHD-Trained Model Superiority: The 3D dementia classifier achieved superior robustness and accuracy across both original and SubtleHD-enhanced test sets when trained on the enhanced (higher quality) images.

ConvNeXt Acceleration with TensorRT: The ConvNeXt-based SubtleHD enhancement pipeline achieved significant inference speedup using NVIDIA TensorRT on both the cost-effective RTX 2080 Ti and the advanced A100, proving high-speed clinical viability.

Resolution of the Speed-Quality Trade-off: The pipeline effectively resolves the conflict between fast MR scanning and diagnostic reliability by restoring diagnostic-grade image quality from accelerated scans via SubtleHD pre-processing.

Quantifying Qualitative Impact: This study successfully translates the qualitative benefit of image enhancement into a quantifiable improvement in AI classification performance for the critical three-class dementia task (CN, MCI, AD).

Effective Data Augmentation Strategy: Training the 3D ResNet34 model on a combination of both (Original + SubtleHD) images demonstrably outperformed training on Original images alone, highlighting the value of the enhancement for model generalization.

title: Efficient Iterative QPE Simulations for Quantum Chemistry Using Distributed State-Vector Methods
abstract: We demonstrate Iterative quantum phase estimation (QPE) simulations for quantum chemistry up to 31 qubits on 64 GPUs using chemqulacs-gpu, our distributed simulator with NVIDIA cuQuantum backend. Through memory-efficient algorithms and optimized communication strategies, we achieve strong scalability for evaluating quantum algorithms toward fault-tolerant quantum computing.
nvidia_technology: DGX Platform, HPC SDK, Infiniband Networking, Hopper, Interconnect Networking
key_takeaways: Explore quantum chemistry algorithms using GPU supercomputers to evaluate their performance before fault-tolerant quantum computers become practically available.
Learn how intelligent qubit placement and data distribution strategies minimize communication overhead to achieve efficient scaling across multiple GPUs.
Discover how IQPE's shallow circuits and minimal qubit requirements make it a promising approach for the upcoming early fault-tolerant quantum computing era.
Demonstrate successful molecular simulations up to 31+1 qubits on 64 GPUs, showing strong scalability for realistic chemical systems.
Provide open-source tools and methods that advance the research community's ability to explore quantum chemistry simulations at unprecedented scales.

title: Automated, DL-Powered Edge-Based, Point-of-Care Ultrasound Tool for Improved CVD Risk Screening
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) contribute to over one-fourth of all deaths in India, making early and equitable risk screening a priority. Screening methods relying on blood pressure and risk charts have low sensitivity and detect disease late, while advanced ultrasound tools are confined to tertiary centers by high cost, complexity, and legal constraints. We propose a low-cost, ultrafast A-mode ultrasound edge system powered by automated deep learning (DL) algorithms for arterial wall detection and motion tracking. The system enables multi-marker vascular assessment - including arterial stiffness, endothelial function, and calibration-free blood pressure - at the point of care. Compact, intelligent, and easy to use, the technology promotes field-deployable cardiovascular screening while building indigenous vascular phenotyping datasets to drive personalized and preventive healthcare.
key_takeaways: Demonstrates a low-cost, ultrafast A-mode ultrasound edge system for vascular health assessment
Introduces automated deep learning algorithms for real-time arterial wall detection and motion tracking
Ultrafast scan rates enable multi-marker evaluation including arterial stiffness, endothelial function, and calibration-free blood pressure
Achieves high scalability and accessibility, bringing tertiary-grade cardiovascular screening to the point of care
Contributes to India's first indigenous vascular phenotyping dataset, supporting population-specific preventive care

speakers.name: Mohanasankar Sivaprakasam
speakers.title: Professor, Head of Brain Research Center

title: Sherlock: Generative AI for Automated Content Security
abstract: Explore how Sherlock, a generative AI-powered system, automates enterprise document security at scale. We present new techniques for classifying sensitive content, detecting false positives, and improving accuracy through a data-centric feedback loop, without retraining large language models. As enterprises increasingly adopt retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, Sherlock offers a timely and practical solution to safeguard confidential data while enabling secure, AI-driven productivity.
key_takeaways: Showcase how generative AI automates document sensitivity classification, reducing manual review and preventing data leakage.
Present a multi-agent LLM architecture that detects false positives and identifies confidential content with high precision.
Enable secure adoption of RAG and MCP systems by ensuring AI tools access only properly cleared information.

title: NetMind: Telecom Agentic AI Assistant Toward Level-4 Autonomous Networks
abstract: NetMind platform accelerates the journey to Level-4 autonomous networks, solving multiple problems related to network information retrieval, fault management, self-optimization, and energy efficiency. By integrating LLM, multi-agent, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with real-time telemetry, it delivers autonomous intelligence with proven results: 95% faster fault resolution, 30% peak-load reduction, and 15% energy savings. This highly scalable solution is ready for deployment across 10 international markets.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, DGX Platform, TensorRT, NeMo, Triton, NVIDIA NIM
key_takeaways: Application of Agentic AI in high-value scenarios such as network information retrieval, fault management, self-optimization, and energy efficiency.
NetMind architecture integrates LLM + multi-agent + RAG + real-time telemetry to enable autonomous network intelligence.
Inference and automation performance accelerated by the NVIDIA AI stack.
Real-world results: 50% faster data query, 40% faster data retrieval, up to 95% reduction in fault resolution time, 30% peak-load reduction, 15% energy savings.
Viettel Networks' strategic roadmap toward achieving Level-4 autonomous networks. Scalability potential for deployment across 10 international markets

title: GPU-Accelerated Scalable Domain-Specific Data Curation Using VLM
abstract: We will explain how to implement data curation specialized for a specific domain (e.g., autonomous driving) by linking NVIDIA NeMo Curator and vision language models and utilizing image caption information. Solving the duplicate data problem, which is a bottleneck in AI development, and creating highly accurate datasets that are impossible to do manually are essential to improving model performance. This poster provides cutting-edge, practical insights that will dramatically improve AI learning efficiency and quality.
key_takeaways: improve Dataset Quality and Diversity: See how this technique effectively removes redundant images to build more-robust and diverse datasets for AI training.

Learn a Novel Method for Targeted Data Curation: Discover how to use vision language models (VLMs) to guide NVIDIA's NeMo Curator, focusing on specific semantic features rather than just visual similarity.
Achieve Cost-Efficient Data Processing: Understand the practical application and minimal cost of this advanced curation method, confirming it as a scalable solution for large-scale projects.

speakers.name: 山田 直
speakers.title: プラットフォーム開発本部 開発部 MLエンジニアリンググループ Physical AI 技術開発リーダー

title: CAPA: Cross-View Automated Part Alignment via SAM-Guided Point Sampling for 3D Part Segmentation
abstract: The limited part-level understanding capability of existing 2D semantic segmentation models is a bottleneck in 2D-to-3D part segmentation. So we introduce cross-view automated part alignment (CAPA), a novel zero-shot multi-view framework that systematically unifies state-of-the-art 2D and 3D models to achieve superior, class-agnostic 3D part segmentation. It utilizes ControlNet and Semantic-SAM to generate and segment 36 geometrically consistent, high-fidelity 2D part masks across multiple views. Next, it employs PTv3 for feature extraction and a novel Wasserstein distance-based clustering approach for cross-view integration, ensuring consistent 3D part definition. CAPA achieved 15% class-agnostic mIoU improvement over existing methods on challenging benchmarks: PartObjaverse-Tiny and PartNetE. And it only requires 5-9 minutes per object on an RTX 3090 (vs. 15-20 min for other models).
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation
key_takeaways: A novel zero-shot multi-view framework that systematically unifies state-of-the-art 2D and 3D models to achieve superior, class-agnostic 3D part segmentation
CAPA significantly improves zero-shot 3D part segmentation. It achieved 15% class-agnostic mIoU improvement over existing state-of-the-art methods.
CAPA is also computationally superior, requiring 5-9 minutes per object on an RTX 3090 (vs. 15-20 min for comparison models).

title: Safety for Agentic AI: Safeguard Agentic AI Systems With End-to-End Workflow
abstract: Explore our new open-source, end-to-end workflow designed to safeguard agentic AI systems against a wide range of risks. We provide a complete recipe to improve model resilience against attacks and enable seamless, one-click deployment for safe enterprise adoption.
topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI
key_takeaways: Learn our end-to-end training recipe to benchmark, train, and deploy safer open-source software models.
Discover how to curate custom safety data by generating on-policy responses and filtering them with a guard model.
See how our fine-tuned models show improved resilience to security attacks while avoiding significant accuracy degradation.

title: Agility With the Speed of Light, Powered by NeMo Agentic Toolkit
abstract:
The Agentic Scrum Master (ASM) is a multi-service platform using the NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit to orchestrate workflows autonomously.

Its intelligent core integrates five applications/systems that provide proactive insights like predictive risk assessment and Smart assignee recommendations.

ASM achieves true portfolio alignment via full bidirectional Jira-Jira Align linking. This autonomously manages epic-to-capability linking and feature hierarchies, solving a major enterprise coordination problem. ASM's development was lead by a Scrum master in the Agentic AI Hackathon at NVIDIA in 2025. A non-developer picked it up and enhanced it further, inspiring others use it to reduce the admin churn and move from reactive scrum mastering to proactive strategic leadership to deliver highest value to customers.
key_takeaways: Scrum masters become strategic leaders when empowered with ASM's proactive, intelligent reports with leading versus lagging indicators - enabling strategic planning, executional excellence, and delivery.
AI Intelligence * Assignee Recommendations * Story Point Estimation * Natural Language Queries * Guided Issue creation
Jira Align Integration * Epic-to-Capability Linking * Workstream Mapping * Portfolio Management * Bi-directional Sync

Assignee Recommendations * Velocity Forecasting * Story Point Estimation * Bandwidth Analysis * Natural Language Queries * Sprint End Reports * Guided Issue creation * Feasibility Checking    Jira Align Integration  User Interfaces * Epic-to-Capability Linking * Web Dashboard (Port 8000) * Workstream Mapping * AI Chatbot (Port 8001) * Portfolio Management * React UI (Port 3000) * Bi-directional Sync * REST API with Swagger *  Risk Management * Jira Operations * Smart Sprint Monitor * Bulk Ticket Creation * Sprint Risk Analyzer * Sprint Planning, Management
Proactive Risk Alerts * Issue Search & Query * AI Intelligence * Sprint Analytics

title: Latency Optimization in BugNeMo
abstract: High query latency hindered NVIDIA's BugNeMo Text2SQL co-pilot operating on millions of records. We identified and resolved three key bottlenecks-table hint injection, inefficient LIKE searches, and missing indexes-through AST-based query rewriting, full-text indexing, and targeted index creation. These optimizations reduced median latency from 26.9 to 7.8 seconds, vastly improving responsiveness and scalability of the Gen AI-powered platform.
key_takeaways: Production p50 latency improved from 26.9 s to 7.8 s with zero regressions.
Automated table hint injection via AST manipulation saves 4.2s per query.
Full-Text Index (FTI) integration optimize timeout queries within five seconds.

title: From Data to Decisions: Accelerate Supply Chain Planning With Agentic AI (Talk to Your Supply Chain)
abstract: This poster presents an agentic AI system that enables supply chain planners to ask complex operational questions in natural language and receive accurate, validated answers sourced directly from enterprise datasets. By combining entity normalization, Text2SQL generation, and a reflection-based self-correction loop, the system eliminates manual data stitching across SAP/HANA/Databricks tables and significantly reduces time-to-insight. Deployed across NVIDIA's Operations Data Platform, the solution has already delivered measurable value with over 3,800 invocations and 1,900-plus planner-hours saved in just three months, demonstrating strong business adoption and scalability. The architecture is designed for extensibility, enabling future multi-retriever reasoning across bill of materials (BOM), logistics, supplier risk, and material readiness workflows.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Agentic AI + Text2SQL enables planners to query enterprise supply chain data using natural language with high accuracy.
The system performs entity correction and schema reasoning before execution, eliminating manual SQL lookups and subject-matter expert dependency.
Reflection-based validation allows the model to self-correct errors, improving reliability in real-world usage.
Adoption is strong, with 3,800+ queries and 1,900+ hours saved in three months (~0.92 FTE gained).
Designed for Extensibility: Architecture can support multi-retriever reasoning across BOM, logistics, supplier risk, and build readiness.

title: DeepFrame: Scalable Long-Context Video Understanding With Omnimodal Fusion
abstract: We present DeepFrame, an end-to-end framework for processing multi-hour videos using omnimodal fusion (video+audio+text) across 128k-256k tokens. Our hybrid GPU infrastructure leverages L40S/H100/B200 for data generation and training, with NVIDIA NeMo Curator for automated video annotation. The system employs Flash Attention 2 (40% VRAM reduction) and mixed precision training (2-3x speedup) on AWS ParallelCluster. We evaluate on LVBench, WorldSense, and custom NARU benchmarks for narrative intelligence. Applications include media moderation, education, security, and retail analytics. Results were announced in January 2026. This work demonstrates breakthrough capabilities in long-form video understanding using NVIDIA's hybrid GPU infrastructure for large-scale AI development.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics
key_takeaways: Breakthrough Long-Context: First framework for 128k-256k tokens, enabling multi-hour video analysis (30+ min to hours)
Omnimodal Fusion: Novel architecture integrating video+audio+text with cross-modal attention for temporal reasoning
NVIDIA Infrastructure: Hybrid L40S/H100/B200 clusters with NeMo Curator for scalable data generation
Comprehensive Evaluation: LVBench, WorldSense, NARU benchmarks across media/education/security/retail

title: Nemotron Safety Guard Multi-Modal
abstract:
As more and more contemporary LLMs exhibit multi-modal capabilities, comprehensive safety evaluation becomes critical for reputational, regulatory, and ethical compliance. Rules and regulations such as EU AI Act have stiff penalties for violations.

Existing vision language model (VLM) safety benchmarks typically cover limited safety categories such as violence, sexual content, and hate speech, and rely on third-party judge models like GPT-4 that may not align with NVIDIA safety standards. Consequently, there is a compelling need for a dedicated safety guard model and an accompanying benchmark that closely adheres to NVIDIA's internal safety policy.
topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI
nvidia_technology: CUDA, Infiniband Networking, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NVLink / NVSwitch, NVIDIA NIM, DGX Cloud
key_takeaways: Content safety evaluation of VLMs is critical for reputational, regulatory, and ethical compliance.
Existing VLM benchmarks focus on just a few aspects of content safety, such as hate and sexual content, and often have safety policy that doesn't align with NVIDIA's.
There is a dearth of open-source software content safety guard models that come with an evaluation dataset.

title: AI4S Machine Learning Potential: Integrated Workflow With Active Learning, Model Distillation, and Multi-GPU Simulation
abstract: Our GPU-accelerated pipeline bridges first-principles data generation, neural potential training, and large-scale atomistic simulation. Starting from automated data generation, representative chemical systems are selected and explored through DFT calculations under an active-learning strategy. Then neural interatomic potentials are trained from scratch or refined via multi-head fine-tuning and model distillation, leveraging GPU-optimized cuequivariant architectures for efficient learning. The trained models are deployed in LAMMPS-MLIAP for massive-scale molecular dynamics, achieving 300,000 atom simulations with full GPU acceleration. This framework unifies data, training, and simulation within a single reproducible ecosystem-transforming traditional materials modeling into a scalable, automated workflow capable of continuously improving its accuracy through iterative GPU-based learning.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science
key_takeaways: GPU-Driven End-to-End Framework for Machine-Learning Potentials
Entirely GPU-Accelerated Active Learning
Model Distillation and Multi-GPU Simulation

title: Vision Transformers and Drones for Antarctic Hyperspectral Vegetation Mapping
abstract: We demonstrate how GPU-accelerated vision transformers with drone hyperspectral data are used to produce high-precision, 8-class maps of Antarctic vegetation, and how this approach improves inference quality and robustness on unseen scenes. We show practical, NVIDIA-ready workflows (transformers, convolutional neural networks (CNNs), gradient boosting) that turn large spectral cubes into reliable maps for fast, data-driven decisions.
key_takeaways: Explore vision transformer (ViT) models for hyperspectral imaging including SpectralFormer and PatchOut, and observe why attention over spectral-spatial context yields cleaner inference maps than CNN-only baselines.
Use GPU-optimized training and inference using CUDA and RAPIDS to handle large hyperspectral datasets efficiently without sacrificing segmentation fidelity.

Adopt three input setups (raw spectra, full features, vegetation-focused features) to balance accuracy, speed, and memory and learn when each setup is the pragmatic choice on NVIDIA hardware and input data.
Improve generalization on unseen transects by combining transformer attention with targeted vegetation features, reducing artifacts in low-light or shadowed areas.
Scale up confidently with chunked I/O and GPU-friendly preprocessing so your pipeline remains responsive from lab to field deployments.

speakers.name: Juan Sandino
speakers.title: Research Fellow
speakers.company: Securing Antarctica's Environmental Future, Queensland University of Technology (QUT)

title: Accelerate Speed to Insight: Harness BERTopic and NVIDIA Technology
abstract: Explore how we accelerated insight extraction on Adoreboard by combining BERTopic with NVIDIA GPU technology. This approach reduces analysis time by 83% and theme overload by two-thirds, delivering faster, clearer, and more actionable insights. Using CUDA, RAPIDS, and containerized pipelines, we enable real-time topic modeling across massive datasets-empowering teams to move from hours of manual analysis to minutes of automated understanding.
key_takeaways: Discover how combining BERTopic with NVIDIA GPU acceleration cuts analysis time by 83%, transforming insight generation from hours to minutes.
Learn how advanced topic modeling reduces "theme overload" from over 100 vague topics to 35 clear, actionable themes with rich subtopics.
See how CUDA, RAPIDS, and containerized pipelines deliver real-time inference and seamless scalability across large, complex datasets
Understand how locally hosted LLMs enhance interpretability and labeling accuracy while maintaining data security.
Explore how accelerated, human-validated insights empower teams to make faster, more informed decisions at scale.

title: AI Planner: An Agentic Workflow for Supply Chain Optimization
abstract: AI Planner demonstrates an agentic framework for supply chain optimization and is aimed at democratizing operations research to business supply chain planners. We combined discrete optimization with the power of agentic AI and created an end-to-end decision management solution.
key_takeaways: Learn the power of LLMs for supply chain optimization.
Learn how AI Planner is proving value in two critical NVIDIA use cases for our operations and business planners.
Understand how LLMs can orchestrate end-to-end decision-making at the time of disruptions.

speakers.name: Anthony Noguera
speakers.title: Director IT - AI For Business Applications

title: Real-Time 3D Video Generation in Neurosurgery and Future Directions for Physical AI
abstract: To convert a single monocular surgical feed into live stereoscopic video for assistant monitors and remote viewers, we train a depth-estimation network on paired stereo data, convert depth to point clouds, and synthesize the second-eye view for consistent depth cues. Explore new techniques for edge optimization on NVIDIA Holoscan/IGX using CUDA Graphs and GPUDirect to minimize latency. Dive deep into system-level trade-offs, safety-ready verification, and pathways to integrate stereo output with robot assistance, multi-modal sensing, and low-cost clinical deployment so you can improve teamwork, instrument hand-offs, and remote training.
topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging
key_takeaways: Real-Time Stereoscopic Conversion: How to convert a single monocular surgical feed into live left/right views that restore depth cues for all OR displays.
Edge-Optimized Pipeline: Improve latency and throughput by deploying depth estimation, point-cloud reconstruction, and view synthesis on NVIDIA Holoscan/IGX with CUDA Graphs and GPUDirect.
Clinical Impact: New solution for enhancing teamwork, instrument hand-offs, and remote training through consistent stereoscopic visualization.
Pathways to Physical AI: Explore integration with robot-assisted control, multi-modal sensing, and safety-ready verification to enable deterministic, low-cost clinical deployments.

speakers.name: Takahashi Junichiro
speakers.title: Associate Professor
speakers.company: Brain Function Analysis & Digital Medicine Research Institute, Showa Medical University

speakers.name: Kosuke Tanaka
speakers.title: Assistant Professor
speakers.company: Brain Function Analysis & Digital Medicine Research Institute, Showa Medical University

title: PyG-RAG: Double LLM RAG Accuracy by Adding GNN
abstract: This poster covers how graph neural networks (GNNs) can be used to enhance LLMs using PyG to improve accuracy for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)-like tasks, across any kind of data domain, by up to 2x! This will include examples on real-world data. We'll also cover how LLMs can be used to enhance GNNs for graph machine learning tasks, including a link to a full one-hour talk given at Stanford University on the topic.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
key_takeaways: Adding structured data (graphs) to AI systems enables a whole new world of data.
Graph Modeling (GNNs and graph transformers) are the ideal model to handle structured data.
This enables up to 2x boost in accuracy over purely unstructured data methods (LLM).

speakers.name: Rishi Puri
speakers.title: Sr. Deep Learning Engineer and Lead for PyTorch Geometric

title: Benchmarking GDS: From Synthetic Loads to Real AI Workflows
abstract: This poster emphasizes performance benefits of GPU direct storage. We ran the TRT LLM and compared it with and without GPUDirect Storage (GDS), and got better performance with GDS. This solves the major problem of loading data into GPU memory and avoids bounce buffer through CPU. Other GPU-accelerated applications can make use of a similar approach.
key_takeaways: GDS removes CPU I/O bottlenecks by letting data flow directly between the filesystem and GPU memory, avoiding extra copies through host DRAM and reducing CPU involvement.
On synthetic benchmarks (gdsio), GDS achieves about 3x higher efficiency than POSIX I/O when efficiency is measured as GB/s per percent of CPU usage.

For TensorRT‐LLM with Llama‐3.1‐70B, GDS consistently lowers time‐to‐first‐token (TTFT) versus POSIX and "no offload" configurations across a wide range of input sequence lengths, including reuse scenarios.
For Qwen3‐8B, GDS again yields lower TTFT in both cold and reuse cases, outperforming POSIX, DRAM offload, and no‐offload setups, especially as total token counts grow.

Using a DGX B300 with local NVMe, the study shows that GDS advantages are not just synthetic-they translate into real AI workloads, improving both performance and CPU efficiency for large language model inference.

title: AI-Augmented RFP Benchmarking Suite: Intelligent Performance Prediction for Heterogeneous Supercomputing Clusters
abstract: We present an AI-assisted benchmarking method that predicts multi-node HPL performance using minimal measurements. The model achieves <3% error up to 32 nodes and detects uncertainty at higher scales, enabling faster and more efficient cluster sizing and request for proposal (RFP) evaluation.
key_takeaways: Predict cluster performance with <3% error using minimal benchmark data.
Benchmark once, and extrapolate confidently up to 32+ nodes.
Automatically detect bottlenecks from topology, PCIe, and network behavior.
Reduce benchmarking time and cost for cluster sizing and RFP evaluation.
Combine physics-based scaling with AI for accurate multi-node prediction.

title: Tera-Scale, Multi-Modal Human Brain Visualization Pipeline With NVIDIA Acceleration
abstract: We present our pipeline for whole-brain high-resolution rendering, modeling, and visualization using NVIDIA technologies, from data to visualization. This work builds on the dataset released by IIT Madras (DHARANI), where we digitized more than 5,000 fetal brain sections at cellular resolution via a custom high-throughput pipeline. The resulting 3D volumetric atlas - freely available worldwide - fills a critical gap: until now, high-resoution, whole-brain 3D data across prenatal development was largely unavailable, or limited. By leveraging GPU-accelerated processing, we provide an end-to-end scalable solution from raw data to immersive visualization - paving the way for reproducible brain mapping, cross-modal research, and brain-inspired computational models.
topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Rendering Engines / Pipelines / Tools
key_takeaways: Tera-voxel volumetric rendering
Zero-footprint remote rendering for collaborating neuroscientists located all over the world
Rich immersive experience and interactions

title: Dynamic Rate Limiting for Multi-Tenant GPU Inference in AI Factories
abstract: This poster shows how dynamic, priority‐aware rate limiting turns AI factories into efficient, shared GPU inference plants that keep critical workloads fast while boosting overall utilization.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, NVIDIA Run:ai
key_takeaways: Priority‐based rate limiting enables true multi‐tenancy on shared GPU inference clusters while preserving service-level agreements for critical applications.
Dynamic quota sharing lets low‐priority tenants borrow unused capacity, significantly increasing overall GPU utilization compared to static limits.
Under saturation, the system enforces guaranteed minimum throughput for high‐priority apps and limits others with clear HTTP 429 responses.
Real production‐like scenarios demonstrate how this approach mitigates noisy neighbors and smooths degradation rather than failing unpredictably.
The design integrates cleanly into existing inference platforms and observability stacks, enabling GPU‐aware autoscaling and cost attribution per tenant.

title: LAMMPS at Mixed Precision: Speeding Up Material Science Simulations
abstract: By implementing reduced precision, we improved end-to-end simulation throughput by factors ranging from 1.8x to ~12x across a variety of interatomic potentials in the LAMMPS molecular dynamics code, as measured on an RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPU.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, CUDA, Hopper, cuFFT, NSight Compute, Blackwell, DGX Station
key_takeaways: Materials science simulations using molecular dynamics can benefit from reduced-precision methods, as already demonstrated for simulations of biological systems.

Adding reduced precision in LAMMPS required a massive code refactor spanning ~13,000 lines of code across ~350 files. A novel construct called TransformView was created to provide seamless interoperability between reduced-precision data on the GPU and "legacy" double-precision data on the host CPU.

Profiler-guided code investigations are critical for implementing reduced-precision methods in existing double-precision code because the C/C++ permits silent conversions between FP64 and FP32 datatypes.
The proper implementation of reduced-precision methods provides performance improvements on both data center and workstation GPUs.
LAMMPS potentials with higher arithmetic intensity see a larger relative benefit on GPUs with lower double-precision throughput.

title: Fast High-Fidelity Urban Airflow Emulator With Explainable Optimized Sensor Placement
abstract: Explore how we enable real-time, near-DNS-quality reconstruction of urban airflow using only sparse, strategically optimized sensors, along with interpretable sensor layouts that cities can actually deploy. Our approach offers new tools for understanding how wind moves pollutants and heat through dense urban spaces, helping communities make faster and better-informed decisions. This work is especially timely as the World Health Organization reports that air pollution contributes to 7 million premature deaths every year, underscoring the urgent need for accurate, scalable monitoring solutions that support healthier and more resilient cities.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, CUDA, PhysicsNeMo, NeMo, NSight Systems
key_takeaways: See how diffusion-based generative models enable near-DNS-quality flow predictions in real time, supporting rapid environmental decision-making.
Discover how explainable, Shapley-value sensor attribution identifies compact, practical sensor regions that match real-world deployment constraints.
Explore how scalable GPU-accelerated modeling brings city-wide environmental monitoring closer to reality at a fraction of traditional computational cost.

title: Audio Flamingo: Advancing Audio Intelligence With Open and Powerful Large Audio-Language Models
abstract: We present new audio-first AI techniques that help language models truly listen-improving how they understand sounds, speech, music, and complex real-world scenes. You'll discover how large audio-language models can boost perception, reasoning, and creative applications, offering timely solutions for building more natural, multi-modal AI systems.
key_takeaways: Learn how large audio-language models can significantly improve AI perception by deeply understanding sounds, speech, and music in real-world environments.
See new techniques that enable AI to reason over audio-including multi-audio comparison, long-audio understanding, and step-by-step thinking over complex auditory scenes.
Explore scalable approaches for building audio-first systems, including dataset expansion strategies that grow high-quality audio examples to tens of millions.
Discover how audio-driven dialogue, voice chat, and music-aware interaction unlock more natural and intuitive multi-modal user experiences.
Understand how open, high-performance audio models can accelerate your development of audio, video, and multi-modal applications across research and industry.

title: Generate Rare Driving Scenarios Using World Foundational Models Plus Omniverse
abstract: Design a world‐model‐driven framework that automatically generates diverse, realistic, and targeted rare driving scenarios, and quantify its impact on downstream perception and planning performance.
topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation
key_takeaways: End‐to‐end autonomous vehicle development with rare‐event generation
Reduced testing costs
Improved safety assurance
Domain adaptation

title: Building Safer Physical AI: Certification, Cybersecurity, and Agentic Control
abstract:
Safety, cybersecurity, and AI safety are interconnected mission-critical properties of physical AI. As autonomous systems move into the real world, they require a foundation of trust spanning from sensors and hardware platforms to operating systems, reasoning models, and agentic functions. 

Join us to explore the NVIDIA Halos safety framework and learn how to architect certifiable systems for Level 4 autonomous vehicles and robotics. We'll examine the design of robust hardware and software safety foundations-integrating hardware platform and operating system-to ensure rigorous safety and systematic cybersecurity by design across the entire stack.  You'll learn how to leverage NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion for automotive, and NVIDIA IGX for robotics, to implement advanced inside-out and outside-in functional safety use cases for scalable autonomy.
nvidia_technology: DRIVE, CUDA, DRIVE SDK, Isaac, Omniverse, Metropolis, OVX, Clara Holoscan, DRIVE AV, IGX, Blackwell, Cosmos
key_takeaways: Architect hardware and software safety foundations spanning the hardware platform, operating system, and safety functions.
Minimize exploitable system weaknesses by integrating the systematic cybersecurity strategies of NVIDIA Halos across hardware and software.
Accelerate physical AI certification by combining AI safety and sensor safety into a unified compliance workflow.
Deploy Halos outside-in safety agents (HOISA) to extend perception beyond the robot, creating proactive safety zones in industrial environments.

title: Claws: How to Build Safe and Secure Long Running Agents
abstract:
Running today’s self-evolving AI agents is fundamentally insecure. Agents such as OpenClaw operate with real system access but lack a robust, infrastructure-level security model. Tool-level restrictions can be bypassed when agents discover alternate execution paths, and container or prompt-based guardrails are insufficient for long-running systems. In this session, experts will discuss infrastructure-level security and runtime platforms for deploying autonomous agents more securely by default.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: Understand why long-running agents introduce new security risks beyond traditional model deployments.
Learn the core design principles for safe agent systems, including permissions, isolation, and controlled execution.
Recognize common failure modes, such as tool bypass and unintended system access, and how to counteract such failure modes.
Explore practical approaches to sandboxing, access control, and runtime enforcement.
Gain insight into monitoring, auditing, and governing agents operating across extended, multi-step workflows.

title: Ask the NVCC Experts: CUDA Compiler Language, Performance, and Tuning
abstract:
Join us for an interactive Connect with Experts session focused on the NVCC CUDA Compiler Toolchain—the core engine behind CUDA C++ language support, compiler functionality, and performance optimization on NVIDIA platforms.

NVCC compiler experts will be available to discuss topics including CUDA language features, compilation workflows, optimization strategies, and performance tuning considerations across different workloads and architectures. Bring real-world questions, challenges, or areas of curiosity: from understanding compilation decisions and generated code to navigating advanced optimization and debugging scenarios.
topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers
key_takeaways: Learn how compiler decisions impact performance, and what to consider when authoring and tuning CUDA applications.
Gain a clearer understanding of the NVCC CUDA compiler toolchain including language support, core functionality, and compilation flow.
Get direct insights from CUDA and NVCC experts on common developer questions, challenges, and real‑world scenarios.
Explore best practices for working with CUDA language features, diagnostics, and optimization techniques.

title: Optimize Deep Learning Kernels on NVIDIA GPUs With FlashInfer and cuDNN
abstract:
Modern AI workloads live or die by the performance of a handful of critical GPU kernels. In this Connect With the Experts session, there are no slides—just open, in-depth discussions with NVIDIA engineers about FlashInfer, cuDNN, and the optimized kernels that power today’s models. Bring your hardest performance questions, unusual model shapes, and kernel-level challenges to talk through how choices around attention, Tensor operations, memory access patterns, fusion, and precision affect behavior on current GPU architectures. The conversation will center on your real-world scenarios: what limits you’re hitting, what you’ve already tried, and which directions are most promising to explore next. You’ll walk away with concrete ideas and heuristics you can apply later with your own tools and workflows, plus pointers to resources for going deeper.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.
key_takeaways: Understand how FlashInfer and cuDNN optimize critical deep learning kernels.
Diagnose GPU performance bottlenecks using attention, Tensor, and memory insights.
Design kernel fusion and precision strategies for demanding model architectures.

title: Empowering Human Creativity: A Discussion With Paramount Skydance’s Dane Glasgow
abstract: Whether it was the transition from black-and-white to color or from linear to streaming, the entertainment industry is well-versed in innovation, transformation, and disruption. As content produced by AI models becomes more widespread, media companies face a unique set of issues as the industry works to protect its intellectual property and maintain the trust of its creatives and audiences. In a fireside chat with Paramount’s Chief Product Officer Dane Glasgow, we will hear how he and his company leverage new and emerging technologies in support of their employees and the creative community, while taking definitive measures to protect its IP and storytelling.
topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Media Streaming / Data Streaming
key_takeaways: Understand how Hollywood executives and leaders in the creative economy view AI and its applications.
Learn about the implications AI has on issues related to intellectual property, identity, and trust in media.
Hear how major publicly traded companies are creating long-term value by upgrading their tech stacks and implementing other available technologies.

title: Accelerating Scientific Discovery Through Global Innovation
abstract: Join Undersecretary Dario Gil for a forward-looking update on the Genesis Mission—a bold initiative redefining how science, technology, and global collaboration drive scientific discovery. This session will showcase early successes of NVIDIA’s long-standing collaborations with the U.S. Department of Energy and ongoing work with leading research centers such as the Argonne, Los Alamos, and Oak Ridge national laboratories. Discover how these collaborations are advancing breakthroughs across energy, quantum technology, and materials science—and learn how developers, researchers, and business leaders alike can help shape the next frontier of scientific innovation.
key_takeaways: Understand the Genesis Mission’s vision, goals, and milestones for global scientific collaboration with industry partners.
Explore real-world success stories demonstrating the mission’s impact on research and innovation.
Learn where Genesis is focusing its research efforts—energy, quantum computing, and materials science—and how to engage in these initiatives.

title: Live Agent Gauntlet
abstract:
Put your AI agent to the test in a competitive, benchmark-driven challenge. In this esports-style hackathon, teams are given the challenge in advance and have dedicated time to build and optimize their agents before the event.

On the day of the competition, teams will load their agents live and evaluate performance head-to-head on standardized benchmarks. Results will be tracked in real time on a public leaderboard, showcasing the strongest approaches and optimizations.

title: Vibe Hack: Rapid Prototyping, From Idea to Reality
abstract:
Experience the power of "Vibe Coding"—where momentum drives creation. In this accessible three-hour session, we are removing complex roadmaps to focus on rapid ideation and execution. Whether you are a business leader, a student, or a seasoned developer, you will use NVIDIA cutting-edge AI models to turn concepts into working applications in real time. Collaborate with others, meet community members, and learn from others by building together. All teams will compete for the main prizes, with a special bounty category dedicated to best use of NVIDIA Nemotron models.

All entries are eligible to be featured in a Developer Showcase.

title: Safety in the OpenClaw Era
abstract:
AI has crossed a threshold. Autonomous agents like OpenClaw, no longer just assist but hold credentials, persist context across sessions, spawn subagents, and execute continuously against production systems. That's a fundamentally different threat model than anything enterprise security was built to handle. The attack surface is real. Every prompt injection is a potential credential leak. Every third-party skill is an unreviewed binary. Every major enterprise platform is becoming an agent platform, and the governance infrastructure to match that reality doesn't exist at scale yet.
This panel brings together security and AI leaders to ask the hard questions: what does a real security model for autonomous agents look like? 
The agents are ready. The infrastructure to trust them is what we're here to discuss.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: How AI is shifting from copilots that assist to autonomous agents that act, learn, and adapt independently.
How autonomous systems can run continuously across cloud and local environments, managing end-to-end workflows.
What OpenClaw and similar platforms reveal about deploying autonomous agents safely and at scale.
What new security, governance, and observability models are needed to oversee self-directed systems.
How the rise of the “agentic operating layer” is transforming human–AI collaboration into continuous partnership.

speakers.name: Francis deSouza
speakers.title: Chief Operating Officer and President, Security Products

title: Science at the Speed of Light: The Genesis Mission Across DoE Labs
abstract: Panelists will discuss the key areas of the Genesis Mission and how the private-public partnership is working, including such key areas as energy, quantum, and open models.
nvidia_technology: BlueField DPU, CUDA, Omniverse, PhysicsNeMo, cuQuantum, BioNeMo, Clara Holoscan, cuOPT, NeMo, NVLink / NVSwitch, CUDA Quantum, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, CUDA-Q, NVIDIA Run:ai, Nemotron
key_takeaways: Genesis Mission Goals...and how we measure success for the nation
Progress in the Energy Space...and what it means for powering AI
Quantum Work at the U.S. Department of Energy (DoE)... and the next frontier of computing
Open Models...and what is needed

title: Physical AI in Enterprises: What’s Real, What Scales, What’s Next (Presented by Deloitte)
abstract:
Top leaders from Siemens and Volkswagen come together to explore how physical AI, Siemens’ industrial digitalization portfolio, and digital twins built on OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse libraries are redefining the future of automated virtual commissioning. This executive conversation highlights how physics-accurate digital twins, real-time interoperability, and closed-loop automation are enabling faster engineering cycles, higher production quality, and more resilient manufacturing operations. 

The panel will examine how combining Siemens’ end-to-end automation and life-cycle platforms with real-time simulation, powered by NVIDIA libraries and accelerated AI infrastructure, creates a unified environment where design, controls, and operations continuously learn from each other. Together, Siemens and Volkswagen will share strategic insights on how these technologies are accelerating the shift from simulation to scalable, AI-driven production systems across global manufacturing networks.
key_takeaways: Tuesday, March 17  |  4:00 p.m. - 4:40 p.m. PDT
SJCC 230C (L2)
Explore More Great Sessions
Connect with the Experts
In-Person

Tue, Mar 17 | 2:00 p.m. PDT
Brian Harrison | NVIDIA
Stephanie Rubenstein | NVIDIA

Join this interactive Q&A session to get direct, actionable advice from NVIDIA's core engineering teams on overcoming the critical developer friction points in building large-scale, physically accurate digital twins for robotics, autonomous vehicles, and manufacturing. This is your unique opportunity to discuss your specific use cases and technical challenges directly with the experts who develop and maintain the core Omniverse libraries and technologies for world simulation, including OpenUSD, PhysX, RTX, and more. Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Mark Hindsbo

title: Open Models: Where We Are and Where We’re Headed
abstract:
Doors open at 11:30 a.m. Session begins at 12:30 p.m.

One of the most important shifts in AI over the past year has been the rapid advancement of open frontier models. Open innovation is accelerating progress across every company and every industry, making it clear that AI will proliferate everywhere. 

 NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang hosts industry leaders for a candid discussion on the state of the art in open frontier models and what comes next.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

title: GTC Live 2026 Keynote Pregame
abstract: Catch the excitement of the keynote pregame.  Be there for the live conversations between the hosts and industry leader panelists as they examine accelerated computing beyond AI, NVIDIA’s role as a full-stack infrastructure company, and the five-layer foundation behind one of the largest infrastructures build-outs in history — and the ecosystem required to deliver it worldwide.

title: 从 M2 到 M2.5：以更小的 (Mini) 参数释放最大的 (Max) 普惠智能
abstract: 本次分享将系统介绍 MiniMax M2 系列如何在仅 10B 激活参数规模下，实现 SOTA 级别的 coding 与 agentic co-work 性能。我们将深入解析在训练资源受限条件下的模型架构取舍、agentic 数据与环境的大规模扩展方法，以及如何基于 NVIDIA 软硬件全栈能力实现高效训练、强化学习与高吞吐推理落地。
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation
key_takeaways: 架构取舍：在真实任务约束下选择 Full Attention 的工程逻辑。我们将解析为何在复杂推理与 coding 任务中坚持采用 full attention 而非 linear attention，以及如何在 NVIDIA GPU 加速与高效算子优化支持下控制训练与推理成本，实现性能与效率的平衡。
Agentic 数据与环境扩展：从单任务能力到系统能力提升。介绍如何构建大规模、多样化的 agentic 交互环境与工具链数据，通过环境扩展驱动模型在真实下游复杂任务中的泛化能力提升。
原生大规模 Agent 强化学习框架 Forge。分享MiniMax M2系列模型的原生 agent 强化学习框架 Forge，如何结合 Megatron-LM 分布式并行训练体系，以及 NVIDIA 的 GPU 集群与系统级优化能力，实现白盒与黑盒 agent 的大规模强化学习训练，在稳定性、吞吐与资源效率之间取得工程级平衡。

title: UK AI Minister: AI Growth Zones and the UK’s AI Opportunities Action Plan
abstract: Join this fireside chat with Kanishka Narayan, MP and U.K. AI Minister, and Bea Longworth, Director of Government Relations, EMEA to examine how the U.K. Government is reshaping the country's global competitiveness in AI through massive infrastructure investment and innovative policy frameworks. You will leave with a clear understanding of the U.K. government's roadmap for AI leadership and how countries can unlock new economic value for enterprises, governments, scientists, and citizens around the world.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Hopper, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Unlock Infrastructure ROI: Discover how the U.K.’s "Growth Zones" model is fast-tracking data center construction and grid connectivity to solve the energy bottleneck for enterprise AI scaling.

Build Sovereign Capability: Understand the strategic shift from "adopting" to "making" AI, and how the Sovereign AI Unit’s investment roadmap creates a blueprint for national and corporate competitive advantage.

Drive Public-Private Value: See real-world examples of how targeted government investment is de-risking private R&D, creating a multiplier effect that accelerates economic impact for global organizations.

speakers.name: Kanishka Narayan
speakers.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State

title: Scale Marketing Content Automation With 3D Digital Twin and Generative AI
abstract:
As brands scale personalized campaigns across channels and regions, traditional production models break down. This session explores how teams automate content creation with 3D digital twins and generative AI—transforming physical products into reusable systems that power high-volume pipelines.

Learn how automated workflows generate packshots, composite imagery, and product configurations at scale, replacing manual photoshoots with cloud-native, production-ready content pipelines.
topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Image / Photo Editing
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Omniverse Replicator, RTX Remix, NVIDIA AI Enterprise
key_takeaways: Automating Content Production with Digital Twins: Learn how 3D digital twins become reusable systems that drive automated generation of marketing imagery across formats, regions, and campaigns.
Simulated Content Pipelines at Scale: See how 3D digital twins can replace manual photoshoots with scalable, automated content generation.

From Creative Assets to Operational Systems: Understand how Adobe’s content platform operationalizes digital twins across the marketing life cycle—powering production, activation, and performance measurement with automation and governance.

title: Real-Time Generative Video Re-Styling for Gaming in the Next-Generation Datacenter
abstract: Decart’s Lucy Restyle is a World Transformation Model that reimagines any video, game, or camera feed into a new digital world in real time. We will dive into how Decart and NVIDIA optimized Lucy Restyle inference for low-latency, cost-efficient cloud deployment on NVIDIA B40 GPUs through GeForce NOW (GFN). Finally, we’ll demonstrate how to harness Lucy Restyle to Lucy Restyle completely restyle titles via the ultimate modding platform: RTX Remix.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, RTX Remix, Blackwell
key_takeaways: Learn how the latest generative models can transform video, with fine grain control over the output style & look.
Discover how GPU experts at Decart and NVIDIA optimized the Lucy Restyle model to run in the cloud on GDN datacenters, precision optimized for performance and ultra-low latency.
Learn how GDN SDK integration enabled Lucy Restyle to stream from the cloud to reach a range of consumer devices.
See how Video to Video models can be plumbed into a game rendering pipeline for real-time frame buffer modification.

speakers.name: Alex Dunn
speakers.title: Director of Engineering at NVIDIA Lightspeed Studios,

title: How NVIDIA Tapped Its Unstructured Data for AI Insights
abstract: Enterprises sit on a treasure trove of knowledge, the biproduct of years of digital operations, locked away in silos. In this session, NVIDIA IT, along with ecosystem partners, will walk through the architectural patterns, governance models, and “vendor lab” environment NVIDIA created to unlock the value of this data for AI developers and agents.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, RTX GPU, LaunchPad, Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), NeMo, NVIDIA NIM, Blackwell, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Blueprint
key_takeaways: Design patterns for overcoming data silos, including dedicated “vendor lab” environments to onboard and test agents safely
How to use eval-driven workflows to compare, select, and tune agents to minimize risk while maximizing return on investment
Practical guidance for defining access controls and data governance so ML engineers can move quickly
Lessons learned on what actually drives agent adoption (hint: performance and alignment to user needs, not hype)

title: Continuous Inference Benchmarking of the AI Frontier
abstract:
As large language model architectures evolve, traditional peak performance metrics are becoming less predictive of real-world efficiency. Effective inference benchmarking now requires a multidimensional approach that balances raw throughput against energy consumption, cost-per-token, and the critical "interactivity" thresholds required for user-facing applications.

This session introduces InferenceX, SemiAnalysis’ open-source benchmark suite designed to navigate this complexity by sweeping the latest software optimizations across leading AI accelerators. It moves beyond static datasheets to examine nightly, reproducible results that reveal how frontier models perform under variable production workloads. Learn how the InferenceX benchmarks are designed, how to run them, and how to interpret the results to make informed decisions.
key_takeaways: Learn how InferenceXv2 extends the original InferenceMAX benchmark with new metrics, workflows, and real-world use cases you can apply immediately.
Discover how to balance throughput, power efficiency, and cost-per-token to benchmark AI performance that actually matters in production.
Explore how interactivity thresholds shape real-world latency and user experience—and how to measure them effectively.
Learn how to compare software optimizations across top AI accelerators in your own environment.
Gain the skills to interpret nightly, reproducible benchmark data and make smarter hardware and deployment decisions based on real production behavior.

title: Co-Engineering Grace CPU Platforms for Hyperscale
abstract: Meta operates one of the world’s largest and most diverse computing fleets, spanning AI training and inference, web services, ranking, caching, analytics, and video processing. In this technical session, leaders from Meta and NVIDIA share how they co-engineer NVIDIA Grace CPU–based platforms end‑to‑end to meet the performance, efficiency, and scale demands of hyperscale fleets. You’ll see how joint work across silicon, platform design, and infrastructure integration improves performance per watt, rack density, and overall fleet efficiency, and how these platforms enable the next generation of hyperscale infrastructure for AI and large‑scale services.
nvidia_technology: Cloud / Data Center GPU, Grace CPU, MGX, NSight Compute
key_takeaways: Understand why hardware–software co‑design is critical for large‑scale, production deployments.
Learn concrete optimization strategies to deliver world‑class performance and efficiency across diverse AI and cloud workloads.

See how end‑to‑end co‑engineering from Meta and NVIDIA—from silicon to data center integration—is shaping the next generation of data center compute for AI, cloud services, and large‑scale distributed systems.

speakers.name: Ian Finder
speakers.title: Head of Product, Data Center CPUs

title: Computing on the Road: Scaling the Waymo Driver for the Real World
abstract: Autonomous ride-hailing is increasingly a daily reality for more people with Waymo. As the most mature manifestation of AI in the physical world, Waymo’s path has been defined by 200 million miles and over 20 million trips completed without a human specialist present. Drawing on this unparalleled scale of experience, we will explore Waymo’s approach to developing its own custom hardware, the vital role AI compute plays in enabling it, and how this integrated foundation makes our demonstrably safe AI architecture possible.

title: Transforming how Vehicles are Designed, Bought, and Serviced With Agentic AI (Presented by AWS)
abstract:
AWS and Cox Automotive, powered by NVIDIA, demonstrate how AI agents are transforming vehicle operations from experimental tools into foundational business infrastructure. Cox Automotive built an agentic workflow in just four days using proprietary data and multi-agent orchestration to automate vehicle inspections, ensure accurate billing, and deliver consistent recommendations. By deploying real-time, collaborative AI agents, they transformed a manual, technician-driven process into a production-ready solution—reducing turnaround time from two days to under 30 minutes while boosting satisfaction for both customers and technicians.

This session showcases how AWS and NVIDIA enable rapid deployment of intelligent automation that delivers measurable business outcomes: faster cycle times, improved accuracy, and enhanced customer experience. AWS is built for industry.
key_takeaways: AI agents are moving from small experiments to core business infrastructure for vehicle operations.
Cox Automotive quickly built an agentic workflow in four days by combining its proprietary data with multi-agent orchestration.
The AI agents now automate vehicle inspections, enforce accurate billing, and standardize recommendations that were previously manual.
Real-time, collaborative AI agents replaced a technician-driven process with a production-ready solution.
Turnaround time dropped from two days to under 30 minutes, improving satisfaction for both customers and technicians.

title: From Benchmark Silos to an Interoperable AI Evaluation Ecosystem
abstract: As agent benchmarks multiply, the AI research community is paying a growing integration tax, with each new benchmark requiring custom infrastructure and tooling. This session introduces Common Unified Benchmark Environments (CUBE), a universal benchmarking protocol built on MCP and Gym that defines shared APIs for tasks, benchmarks, packages, and registries. We explore CUBE’s design and show how it integrates with existing evaluation workflows, including early reference integrations validated in collaboration with NVIDIA using NeMo Gym and NeMo Evaluator. Learn how to adopt CUBE, integrate it into your own tooling, and contribute to a more interoperable and scalable benchmarking ecosystem.
key_takeaways: Understand why fragmented agent benchmarks create significant integration and maintenance overhead.
Learn the core design principles behind CUBE, and how its unified APIs standardize evaluation workflows.
Explore how CUBE integrates with existing agent frameworks and evaluation tools.
Apply practical steps to adopt interoperable benchmarks in your own research or development environment.
Contribute to a shared benchmarking ecosystem that scales with the pace of agentic AI innovation.

title: Build-a-Claw Event
abstract:
Stop by NVIDIA’s build-a-claw event in the GTC Park running all week to customize and deploy a proactive, always-on AI assistant.
With support from NVIDIA experts, GTC attendees can quickly have an AI agent at their fingertips. Whether technical or just curious, participants will name an agent, define its personality and grant it access to the tools it needs. Think of it as the personal assistant you’ve always wanted, reachable via your preferred messaged app. 
To run the custom agent, bring your own NVIDIA DGX Spark. Or buy one at the Gear Store (SJCC street level) or MicroCenter pop-up (GTC Park).
To run the custom agent, use cloud compute provided onsite, or harness local accelerated computing by bringing your NVIDIA DGX Spark or GeForce RTX laptop, with no personal data on the device. NVIDIA hardware — including DGX Spark and Jetson modules — will also be available to buy on site at Gear Store (SJCC street level) and Micro Center (pop-up at GTC Park).
Helpful material to review before you arrive:
Install & Demo Guide | Notice & Disclaimers

topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

title: The Next Era of Surgery: AI-Driven Innovation in the Operating Room
abstract: Explore how Barco’s newest AI-based surgical products, powered by NVIDIA AI infrastructure and the NVIDIA Holoscan platform, transform the operating room into a software-defined space. You will discover how generative AI improves surgical precision and patient safety, and simplifies the clinical workflow. Combined with NVIDIA edge computing solutions, we can run real-time AI models that act as a surgical copilot.
key_takeaways: Explore how generative AI enables hands-free control of surgical displays, allowing clinicians to manage data without breaking the sterile field.
Transition to a software-defined operating room by running multiple AI applications simultaneously.
Boost surgical performance with real-time clinical guidance and edge compute built directly into the surgical workflow.

title: Always-On Autonomous AI Agents: Exploring the OpenClaw Abstraction
abstract:
As agents evolve from basic conversational interfaces to personal complex task navigators, the primary challenge shifts from writing context strategies to designing robust systems. This hands-on workshop explores the engineering needed to manage persistent agent state, secure tool execution, and complex workflow creation in a low-code, high-capacity context. 

Using OpenClaw, a popular open-source agent gateway, we illustrate how a single daemon can manage sessions across multiple applications, maintain long-term interpretable memory, and safely execute tools in a virtual user-centric environment. We emphasize low-code abstractions and out-of-the-box capabilities while maintaining enough rigor to motivate effective and secure best practices. You'll be able to expand on these ideas and improve your agents' capabilities as new innovations arrive and the field continues to evolve.
topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation
key_takeaways: An understanding of the separation between the Gateway (the control plane) and the Agent (the reasoning loop), and why this decoupling is essential for multi-channel resilience
The use of OpenClaw’s "File-system-as-Context" pattern to define agent personas and enable autonomous action planning
An intuition for OpenClaw’s Heartbeat mechanism and its use in live workspace monitoring and coordination
Enough knowledge to implement robust skill specifications, human-in-the-loop gates, and observability patterns for high-risk (like sending emails) and autonomous low-risk actions (like reading docs)
A rationale for component capabilities and costs to propose feasible agentic setups for personal, multi-user, and enterprise deployments

title: Watch Party: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 1: Simulation-First Design and Accelerated Development
abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。
会议主持人：
Wei Yan, Solution Architect, NVIDIA
Holly Ye, Account Manager, NVIDIA
Join Part 1 of a two-part curated session on how simulation-first design is changing the way healthcare robots are built. You’ll see how teams are using NVIDIA Physical AI—spanning world foundation models, physics-based simulation, synthetic data, and real-time sensor-to-action deployment—to move faster with less risk. This session focuses on how simulation and digital twins are being used in practice to speed up development, test decisions earlier, and optimize systems long before they reach the operating room

How Simulation-First Design Accelerates Healthcare Robotic Development: Learn how teams use digital twins, physics-based environments, and generative simulation to rapidly design, test, and refine robotic systems before they touch the real world.
How World Foundation Models and Synthetic Data Expand Capability and Coverage: See how specialized foundation models and SDG pipelines create rich scenario diversity, improve training efficiency, and strengthen early-stage algorithm performance.
How Developers are Building Next-Generation Devices With NVIDIA Physical AI: Hear from industry leaders using NVIDIA’s simulation and modeling technologies to optimize planning, validate concepts, and shorten product development cycles.

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

title: Watch Party: Artist to Audience: The Developer Platform for Real-Time AI in Media
abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。
会议主持人：
Maggie Jiang, Regional Industry Sales Lead, NVIDIA
In this session, we’ll show how real-time AI is becoming the new fabric of modern media—connecting artist workflows to studio pipelines to distribution at global scale. You’ll learn the reference architecture behind an AI-native media stack: accelerated compute, low-latency networking, and developer-ready SDKs and APIs that let teams build, integrate, and ship AI into real production systems. We’ll walk through practical examples across creation, live production, enhancement, understanding, and delivery—focusing on what it takes to move from demos to deployed workflows, with measurable gains in speed, quality, and cost.

Learn how AI-powered tools and generative agents are transforming media production workflows, enabling faster, more creative, and cost-effective content creation across multiple formats.
Discover how AI-driven recommendation systems and dynamic content optimization are enhancing audience engagement and discoverability across platforms.
Gain insights into how AI enables more targeted ads, microtransactions, and tailored subscription services, driving more efficient and sustainable revenue streams.

title: Watch Party: Evaluation-Driven Development: Best Practices for Building Reliable Agents
abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。
会议主持人：
Yipeng Li, Developer Community Manager, NVIDIA
Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard. As workflows shift to complex long-horizon tasks, traditional testing fails. How do you debug and improve non-deterministic behavior?

This session presents a framework for evaluation-driven development. We move beyond “vibe eval” to disciplined engineering covering ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns. Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate how to build pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. You'll leave with a workflow for implementing evaluation methods that take agents from prototype to scalable deployment using a suite of tooling enabled by NVIDIA NeMo and open NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning.

Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard—especially for long-horizon, non-deterministic tasks where traditional testing fails. This session introduces a disciplined framework for evaluation-driven development, moving beyond “vibe eval” to structured methods like ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns.
Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. Learn how to implement evaluation workflows that scale agents from prototype to production.
The workflow leverages NVIDIA NeMo Evaluator for reproducible benchmarking, NeMo Guardrails for safety, NeMo Agent Toolkit for orchestration, and NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning. We’ll show how these tools integrate to enable robust, scalable agent development.

abstract:
